ayahs.t: In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful.
ayahs.n: in the name of allah the entirely merciful the especially merciful

ayahs.t: [All] praise is [due] to Allah, Lord of the worlds -
ayahs.n: all praise is due to allah lord of the worlds

ayahs.t: The Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful,
ayahs.n: the entirely merciful the especially merciful

ayahs.t: Sovereign of the Day of Recompense.
ayahs.n: sovereign of the day of recompense

ayahs.t: It is You we worship and You we ask for help.
ayahs.n: it is you we worship and you we ask for help

ayahs.t: Guide us to the straight path -
ayahs.n: guide us to the straight path

ayahs.t: The path of those upon whom You have bestowed favor, not of those who have evoked [Your] anger or of those who are astray.
ayahs.n: the path of those upon whom you have bestowed favor not of those who have evoked your anger or of those who are astray

ayahs.t: Alif, Lam, Meem.
ayahs.n: alif lam meem

ayahs.t: This is the Book about which there is no doubt, a guidance for those conscious of Allah -
ayahs.n: this is the book about which there is no doubt a guidance for those conscious of allah

ayahs.t: Who believe in the unseen, establish prayer, and spend out of what We have provided for them,
ayahs.n: who believe in the unseen establish prayer and spend out of what we have provided for them

ayahs.t: And who believe in what has been revealed to you, [O Muhammad], and what was revealed before you, and of the Hereafter they are certain [in faith].
ayahs.n: and who believe in what has been revealed to you o muhammad and what was revealed before you and of the hereafter they are certain in faith

ayahs.t: Those are upon [right] guidance from their Lord, and it is those who are the successful.
ayahs.n: those are upon right guidance from their lord and it is those who are the successful

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who disbelieve - it is all the same for them whether you warn them or do not warn them - they will not believe.
ayahs.n: indeed those who disbelieve it is all the same for them whether you warn them or do not warn them they will not believe

ayahs.t: Allah has set a seal upon their hearts and upon their hearing, and over their vision is a veil. And for them is a great punishment.
ayahs.n: allah has set a seal upon their hearts and upon their hearing and over their vision is a veil and for them is a great punishment

ayahs.t: And of the people are some who say, "We believe in Allah and the Last Day," but they are not believers.
ayahs.n: and of the people are some who say we believe in allah and the last day but they are not believers

ayahs.t: They [think to] deceive Allah and those who believe, but they deceive not except themselves and perceive [it] not.
ayahs.n: they think to deceive allah and those who believe but they deceive not except themselves and perceive it not

ayahs.t: In their hearts is disease, so Allah has increased their disease; and for them is a painful punishment because they [habitually] used to lie.
ayahs.n: in their hearts is disease so allah has increased their disease and for them is a painful punishment because they habitually used to lie

ayahs.t: And when it is said to them, "Do not cause corruption on the earth," they say, "We are but reformers."
ayahs.n: and when it is said to them do not cause corruption on the earth they say we are but reformers

ayahs.t: Unquestionably, it is they who are the corrupters, but they perceive [it] not.
ayahs.n: unquestionably it is they who are the corrupters but they perceive it not

ayahs.t: And when it is said to them, "Believe as the people have believed," they say, "Should we believe as the foolish have believed?" Unquestionably, it is they who are the foolish, but they know [it] not.
ayahs.n: and when it is said to them believe as the people have believed they say should we believe as the foolish have believed unquestionably it is they who are the foolish but they know it not

ayahs.t: And when they meet those who believe, they say, "We believe"; but when they are alone with their evil ones, they say, "Indeed, we are with you; we were only mockers."
ayahs.n: and when they meet those who believe they say we believe but when they are alone with their evil ones they say indeed we are with you we were only mockers

ayahs.t: [But] Allah mocks them and prolongs them in their transgression [while] they wander blindly.
ayahs.n: but allah mocks them and prolongs them in their transgression while they wander blindly

ayahs.t: Those are the ones who have purchased error [in exchange] for guidance, so their transaction has brought no profit, nor were they guided.
ayahs.n: those are the ones who have purchased error in exchange for guidance so their transaction has brought no profit nor were they guided

ayahs.t: Their example is that of one who kindled a fire, but when it illuminated what was around him, Allah took away their light and left them in darkness [so] they could not see.
ayahs.n: their example is that of one who kindled a fire but when it illuminated what was around him allah took away their light and left them in darkness so they could not see

ayahs.t: Deaf, dumb and blind - so they will not return [to the right path].
ayahs.n: deaf dumb and blind so they will not return to the right path

ayahs.t: Or [it is] like a rainstorm from the sky within which is darkness, thunder and lightning. They put their fingers in their ears against the thunderclaps in dread of death. But Allah is encompassing of the disbelievers.
ayahs.n: or it is like a rainstorm from the sky within which is darkness thunder and lightning they put their fingers in their ears against the thunderclaps in dread of death but allah is encompassing of the disbelievers

ayahs.t: The lightning almost snatches away their sight. Every time it lights [the way] for them, they walk therein; but when darkness comes over them, they stand [still]. And if Allah had willed, He could have taken away their hearing and their sight. Indeed, Allah is over all things competent.
ayahs.n: the lightning almost snatches away their sight every time it lights the way for them they walk therein but when darkness comes over them they stand still and if allah had willed he could have taken away their hearing and their sight indeed allah is over all things competent

ayahs.t: O mankind, worship your Lord, who created you and those before you, that you may become righteous -
ayahs.n: o mankind worship your lord who created you and those before you that you may become righteous

ayahs.t: [He] who made for you the earth a bed [spread out] and the sky a ceiling and sent down from the sky, rain and brought forth thereby fruits as provision for you. So do not attribute to Allah equals while you know [that there is nothing similar to Him].
ayahs.n: he who made for you the earth a bed spread out and the sky a ceiling and sent down from the sky rain and brought forth thereby fruits as provision for you so do not attribute to allah equals while you know that there is nothing similar to him

ayahs.t: And if you are in doubt about what We have sent down upon Our Servant [Muhammad], then produce a surah the like thereof and call upon your witnesses other than Allah, if you should be truthful.
ayahs.n: and if you are in doubt about what we have sent down upon our servant muhammad then produce a surah the like thereof and call upon your witnesses other than allah if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: But if you do not - and you will never be able to - then fear the Fire, whose fuel is men and stones, prepared for the disbelievers.
ayahs.n: but if you do not and you will never be able to then fear the fire whose fuel is men and stones prepared for the disbelievers

ayahs.t: And give good tidings to those who believe and do righteous deeds that they will have gardens [in Paradise] beneath which rivers flow. Whenever they are provided with a provision of fruit therefrom, they will say, "This is what we were provided with before." And it is given to them in likeness. And they will have therein purified spouses, and they will abide therein eternally.
ayahs.n: and give good tidings to those who believe and do righteous deeds that they will have gardens in paradise beneath which rivers flow whenever they are provided with a provision of fruit therefrom they will say this is what we were provided with before and it is given to them in likeness and they will have therein purified spouses and they will abide therein eternally

ayahs.t: Indeed, Allah is not timid to present an example - that of a mosquito or what is smaller than it. And those who have believed know that it is the truth from their Lord. But as for those who disbelieve, they say, "What did Allah intend by this as an example?" He misleads many thereby and guides many thereby. And He misleads not except the defiantly disobedient,
ayahs.n: indeed allah is not timid to present an example that of a mosquito or what is smaller than it and those who have believed know that it is the truth from their lord but as for those who disbelieve they say what did allah intend by this as an example he misleads many thereby and guides many thereby and he misleads not except the defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: Who break the covenant of Allah after contracting it and sever that which Allah has ordered to be joined and cause corruption on earth. It is those who are the losers.
ayahs.n: who break the covenant of allah after contracting it and sever that which allah has ordered to be joined and cause corruption on earth it is those who are the losers

ayahs.t: How can you disbelieve in Allah when you were lifeless and He brought you to life; then He will cause you to die, then He will bring you [back] to life, and then to Him you will be returned.
ayahs.n: how can you disbelieve in allah when you were lifeless and he brought you to life then he will cause you to die then he will bring you back to life and then to him you will be returned

ayahs.t: It is He who created for you all of that which is on the earth. Then He directed Himself to the heaven, [His being above all creation], and made them seven heavens, and He is Knowing of all things.
ayahs.n: it is he who created for you all of that which is on the earth then he directed himself to the heaven his being above all creation and made them seven heavens and he is knowing of all things

ayahs.t: And [mention, O Muhammad], when your Lord said to the angels, "Indeed, I will make upon the earth a successive authority." They said, "Will You place upon it one who causes corruption therein and sheds blood, while we declare Your praise and sanctify You?" Allah said, "Indeed, I know that which you do not know."
ayahs.n: and mention o muhammad when your lord said to the angels indeed i will make upon the earth a successive authority they said will you place upon it one who causes corruption therein and sheds blood while we declare your praise and sanctify you allah said indeed i know that which you do not know

ayahs.t: And He taught Adam the names - all of them. Then He showed them to the angels and said, "Inform Me of the names of these, if you are truthful."
ayahs.n: and he taught adam the names all of them then he showed them to the angels and said inform me of the names of these if you are truthful

ayahs.t: They said, "Exalted are You; we have no knowledge except what You have taught us. Indeed, it is You who is the Knowing, the Wise."
ayahs.n: they said exalted are you we have no knowledge except what you have taught us indeed it is you who is the knowing the wise

ayahs.t: He said, "O Adam, inform them of their names." And when he had informed them of their names, He said, "Did I not tell you that I know the unseen [aspects] of the heavens and the earth? And I know what you reveal and what you have concealed."
ayahs.n: he said o adam inform them of their names and when he had informed them of their names he said did i not tell you that i know the unseen aspects of the heavens and the earth and i know what you reveal and what you have concealed

ayahs.t: And [mention] when We said to the angels, "Prostrate before Adam"; so they prostrated, except for Iblees. He refused and was arrogant and became of the disbelievers.
ayahs.n: and mention when we said to the angels prostrate before adam so they prostrated except for iblees he refused and was arrogant and became of the disbelievers

ayahs.t: And We said, "O Adam, dwell, you and your wife, in Paradise and eat therefrom in [ease and] abundance from wherever you will. But do not approach this tree, lest you be among the wrongdoers."
ayahs.n: and we said o adam dwell you and your wife in paradise and eat therefrom in ease and abundance from wherever you will but do not approach this tree lest you be among the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: But Satan caused them to slip out of it and removed them from that [condition] in which they had been. And We said, "Go down, [all of you], as enemies to one another, and you will have upon the earth a place of settlement and provision for a time."
ayahs.n: but satan caused them to slip out of it and removed them from that condition in which they had been and we said go down all of you as enemies to one another and you will have upon the earth a place of settlement and provision for a time

ayahs.t: Then Adam received from his Lord [some] words, and He accepted his repentance. Indeed, it is He who is the Accepting of repentance, the Merciful.
ayahs.n: then adam received from his lord some words and he accepted his repentance indeed it is he who is the accepting of repentance the merciful

ayahs.t: We said, "Go down from it, all of you. And when guidance comes to you from Me, whoever follows My guidance - there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve.
ayahs.n: we said go down from it all of you and when guidance comes to you from me whoever follows my guidance there will be no fear concerning them nor will they grieve

ayahs.t: And those who disbelieve and deny Our signs - those will be companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally."
ayahs.n: and those who disbelieve and deny our signs those will be companions of the fire they will abide therein eternally

ayahs.t: O Children of Israel, remember My favor which I have bestowed upon you and fulfill My covenant [upon you] that I will fulfill your covenant [from Me], and be afraid of [only] Me.
ayahs.n: o children of israel remember my favor which i have bestowed upon you and fulfill my covenant upon you that i will fulfill your covenant from me and be afraid of only me

ayahs.t: And believe in what I have sent down confirming that which is [already] with you, and be not the first to disbelieve in it. And do not exchange My signs for a small price, and fear [only] Me.
ayahs.n: and believe in what i have sent down confirming that which is already with you and be not the first to disbelieve in it and do not exchange my signs for a small price and fear only me

ayahs.t: And do not mix the truth with falsehood or conceal the truth while you know [it].
ayahs.n: and do not mix the truth with falsehood or conceal the truth while you know it

ayahs.t: And establish prayer and give zakah and bow with those who bow [in worship and obedience].
ayahs.n: and establish prayer and give zakah and bow with those who bow in worship and obedience

ayahs.t: Do you order righteousness of the people and forget yourselves while you recite the Scripture? Then will you not reason?
ayahs.n: do you order righteousness of the people and forget yourselves while you recite the scripture then will you not reason

ayahs.t: And seek help through patience and prayer, and indeed, it is difficult except for the humbly submissive [to Allah]
ayahs.n: and seek help through patience and prayer and indeed it is difficult except for the humbly submissive to allah

ayahs.t: Who are certain that they will meet their Lord and that they will return to Him.
ayahs.n: who are certain that they will meet their lord and that they will return to him

ayahs.t: O Children of Israel, remember My favor that I have bestowed upon you and that I preferred you over the worlds.
ayahs.n: o children of israel remember my favor that i have bestowed upon you and that i preferred you over the worlds

ayahs.t: And fear a Day when no soul will suffice for another soul at all, nor will intercession be accepted from it, nor will compensation be taken from it, nor will they be aided.
ayahs.n: and fear a day when no soul will suffice for another soul at all nor will intercession be accepted from it nor will compensation be taken from it nor will they be aided

ayahs.t: And [recall] when We saved your forefathers from the people of Pharaoh, who afflicted you with the worst torment, slaughtering your [newborn] sons and keeping your females alive. And in that was a great trial from your Lord.
ayahs.n: and recall when we saved your forefathers from the people of pharaoh who afflicted you with the worst torment slaughtering your newborn sons and keeping your females alive and in that was a great trial from your lord

ayahs.t: And [recall] when We parted the sea for you and saved you and drowned the people of Pharaoh while you were looking on.
ayahs.n: and recall when we parted the sea for you and saved you and drowned the people of pharaoh while you were looking on

ayahs.t: And [recall] when We made an appointment with Moses for forty nights. Then you took [for worship] the calf after him, while you were wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: and recall when we made an appointment with moses for forty nights then you took for worship the calf after him while you were wrongdoers

ayahs.t: Then We forgave you after that so perhaps you would be grateful.
ayahs.n: then we forgave you after that so perhaps you would be grateful

ayahs.t: And [recall] when We gave Moses the Scripture and criterion that perhaps you would be guided.
ayahs.n: and recall when we gave moses the scripture and criterion that perhaps you would be guided

ayahs.t: And [recall] when Moses said to his people, "O my people, indeed you have wronged yourselves by your taking of the calf [for worship]. So repent to your Creator and kill yourselves. That is best for [all of] you in the sight of your Creator." Then He accepted your repentance; indeed, He is the Accepting of repentance, the Merciful.
ayahs.n: and recall when moses said to his people o my people indeed you have wronged yourselves by your taking of the calf for worship so repent to your creator and kill yourselves that is best for all of you in the sight of your creator then he accepted your repentance indeed he is the accepting of repentance the merciful

ayahs.t: And [recall] when you said, "O Moses, we will never believe you until we see Allah outright"; so the thunderbolt took you while you were looking on.
ayahs.n: and recall when you said o moses we will never believe you until we see allah outright so the thunderbolt took you while you were looking on

ayahs.t: Then We revived you after your death that perhaps you would be grateful.
ayahs.n: then we revived you after your death that perhaps you would be grateful

ayahs.t: And We shaded you with clouds and sent down to you manna and quails, [saying], "Eat from the good things with which We have provided you." And they wronged Us not - but they were [only] wronging themselves.
ayahs.n: and we shaded you with clouds and sent down to you manna and quails saying eat from the good things with which we have provided you and they wronged us not but they were only wronging themselves

ayahs.t: And [recall] when We said, "Enter this city and eat from it wherever you will in [ease and] abundance, and enter the gate bowing humbly and say, 'Relieve us of our burdens.' We will [then] forgive your sins for you, and We will increase the doers of good [in goodness and reward]."
ayahs.n: and recall when we said enter this city and eat from it wherever you will in ease and abundance and enter the gate bowing humbly and say relieve us of our burdens we will then forgive your sins for you and we will increase the doers of good in goodness and reward

ayahs.t: But those who wronged changed [those words] to a statement other than that which had been said to them, so We sent down upon those who wronged a punishment from the sky because they were defiantly disobeying.
ayahs.n: but those who wronged changed those words to a statement other than that which had been said to them so we sent down upon those who wronged a punishment from the sky because they were defiantly disobeying

ayahs.t: And [recall] when Moses prayed for water for his people, so We said, "Strike with your staff the stone." And there gushed forth from it twelve springs, and every people knew its watering place. "Eat and drink from the provision of Allah, and do not commit abuse on the earth, spreading corruption."
ayahs.n: and recall when moses prayed for water for his people so we said strike with your staff the stone and there gushed forth from it twelve springs and every people knew its watering place eat and drink from the provision of allah and do not commit abuse on the earth spreading corruption

ayahs.t: And [recall] when you said, "O Moses, we can never endure one [kind of] food. So call upon your Lord to bring forth for us from the earth its green herbs and its cucumbers and its garlic and its lentils and its onions." [Moses] said, "Would you exchange what is better for what is less? Go into [any] settlement and indeed, you will have what you have asked." And they were covered with humiliation and poverty and returned with anger from Allah [upon them]. That was because they [repeatedly] disbelieved in the signs of Allah and killed the prophets without right. That was because they disobeyed and were [habitually] transgressing.
ayahs.n: and recall when you said o moses we can never endure one kind of food so call upon your lord to bring forth for us from the earth its green herbs and its cucumbers and its garlic and its lentils and its onions moses said would you exchange what is better for what is less go into any settlement and indeed you will have what you have asked and they were covered with humiliation and poverty and returned with anger from allah upon them that was because they repeatedly disbelieved in the signs of allah and killed the prophets without right that was because they disobeyed and were habitually transgressing

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who believed and those who were Jews or Christians or Sabeans [before Prophet Muhammad] - those [among them] who believed in Allah and the Last Day and did righteousness - will have their reward with their Lord, and no fear will there be concerning them, nor will they grieve.
ayahs.n: indeed those who believed and those who were jews or christians or sabeans before prophet muhammad those among them who believed in allah and the last day and did righteousness will have their reward with their lord and no fear will there be concerning them nor will they grieve

ayahs.t: And [recall] when We took your covenant, [O Children of Israel, to abide by the Torah] and We raised over you the mount, [saying], "Take what We have given you with determination and remember what is in it that perhaps you may become righteous."
ayahs.n: and recall when we took your covenant o children of israel to abide by the torah and we raised over you the mount saying take what we have given you with determination and remember what is in it that perhaps you may become righteous

ayahs.t: Then you turned away after that. And if not for the favor of Allah upon you and His mercy, you would have been among the losers.
ayahs.n: then you turned away after that and if not for the favor of allah upon you and his mercy you would have been among the losers

ayahs.t: And you had already known about those who transgressed among you concerning the sabbath, and We said to them, "Be apes, despised."
ayahs.n: and you had already known about those who transgressed among you concerning the sabbath and we said to them be apes despised

ayahs.t: And We made it a deterrent punishment for those who were present and those who succeeded [them] and a lesson for those who fear Allah.
ayahs.n: and we made it a deterrent punishment for those who were present and those who succeeded them and a lesson for those who fear allah

ayahs.t: And [recall] when Moses said to his people, "Indeed, Allah commands you to slaughter a cow." They said, "Do you take us in ridicule?" He said, "I seek refuge in Allah from being among the ignorant."
ayahs.n: and recall when moses said to his people indeed allah commands you to slaughter a cow they said do you take us in ridicule he said i seek refuge in allah from being among the ignorant

ayahs.t: They said, "Call upon your Lord to make clear to us what it is." [Moses] said, "[Allah] says, 'It is a cow which is neither old nor virgin, but median between that,' so do what you are commanded."
ayahs.n: they said call upon your lord to make clear to us what it is moses said allah says it is a cow which is neither old nor virgin but median between that so do what you are commanded

ayahs.t: They said, "Call upon your Lord to show us what is her color." He said, "He says, 'It is a yellow cow, bright in color - pleasing to the observers.' "
ayahs.n: they said call upon your lord to show us what is her color he said he says it is a yellow cow bright in color pleasing to the observers

ayahs.t: They said, "Call upon your Lord to make clear to us what it is. Indeed, [all] cows look alike to us. And indeed we, if Allah wills, will be guided."
ayahs.n: they said call upon your lord to make clear to us what it is indeed all cows look alike to us and indeed we if allah wills will be guided

ayahs.t: He said, "He says, 'It is a cow neither trained to plow the earth nor to irrigate the field, one free from fault with no spot upon her.' " They said, "Now you have come with the truth." So they slaughtered her, but they could hardly do it.
ayahs.n: he said he says it is a cow neither trained to plow the earth nor to irrigate the field one free from fault with no spot upon her they said now you have come with the truth so they slaughtered her but they could hardly do it

ayahs.t: And [recall] when you slew a man and disputed over it, but Allah was to bring out that which you were concealing.
ayahs.n: and recall when you slew a man and disputed over it but allah was to bring out that which you were concealing

ayahs.t: So, We said, "Strike the slain man with part of it." Thus does Allah bring the dead to life, and He shows you His signs that you might reason.
ayahs.n: so we said strike the slain man with part of it thus does allah bring the dead to life and he shows you his signs that you might reason

ayahs.t: Then your hearts became hardened after that, being like stones or even harder. For indeed, there are stones from which rivers burst forth, and there are some of them that split open and water comes out, and there are some of them that fall down for fear of Allah. And Allah is not unaware of what you do.
ayahs.n: then your hearts became hardened after that being like stones or even harder for indeed there are stones from which rivers burst forth and there are some of them that split open and water comes out and there are some of them that fall down for fear of allah and allah is not unaware of what you do

ayahs.t: Do you covet [the hope, O believers], that they would believe for you while a party of them used to hear the words of Allah and then distort the Torah after they had understood it while they were knowing?
ayahs.n: do you covet the hope o believers that they would believe for you while a party of them used to hear the words of allah and then distort the torah after they had understood it while they were knowing

ayahs.t: And when they meet those who believe, they say, "We have believed"; but when they are alone with one another, they say, "Do you talk to them about what Allah has revealed to you so they can argue with you about it before your Lord?" Then will you not reason?
ayahs.n: and when they meet those who believe they say we have believed but when they are alone with one another they say do you talk to them about what allah has revealed to you so they can argue with you about it before your lord then will you not reason

ayahs.t: But do they not know that Allah knows what they conceal and what they declare?
ayahs.n: but do they not know that allah knows what they conceal and what they declare

ayahs.t: And among them are unlettered ones who do not know the Scripture except in wishful thinking, but they are only assuming.
ayahs.n: and among them are unlettered ones who do not know the scripture except in wishful thinking but they are only assuming

ayahs.t: So woe to those who write the "scripture" with their own hands, then say, "This is from Allah," in order to exchange it for a small price. Woe to them for what their hands have written and woe to them for what they earn.
ayahs.n: so woe to those who write the scripture with their own hands then say this is from allah in order to exchange it for a small price woe to them for what their hands have written and woe to them for what they earn

ayahs.t: And they say, "Never will the Fire touch us, except for a few days." Say, "Have you taken a covenant with Allah? For Allah will never break His covenant. Or do you say about Allah that which you do not know?"
ayahs.n: and they say never will the fire touch us except for a few days say have you taken a covenant with allah for allah will never break his covenant or do you say about allah that which you do not know

ayahs.t: Yes, whoever earns evil and his sin has encompassed him - those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally.
ayahs.n: yes whoever earns evil and his sin has encompassed him those are the companions of the fire they will abide therein eternally

ayahs.t: But they who believe and do righteous deeds - those are the companions of Paradise; they will abide therein eternally.
ayahs.n: but they who believe and do righteous deeds those are the companions of paradise they will abide therein eternally

ayahs.t: And [recall] when We took the covenant from the Children of Israel, [enjoining upon them], "Do not worship except Allah; and to parents do good and to relatives, orphans, and the needy. And speak to people good [words] and establish prayer and give zakah." Then you turned away, except a few of you, and you were refusing.
ayahs.n: and recall when we took the covenant from the children of israel enjoining upon them do not worship except allah and to parents do good and to relatives orphans and the needy and speak to people good words and establish prayer and give zakah then you turned away except a few of you and you were refusing

ayahs.t: And [recall] when We took your covenant, [saying], "Do not shed each other's blood or evict one another from your homes." Then you acknowledged [this] while you were witnessing.
ayahs.n: and recall when we took your covenant saying do not shed each others blood or evict one another from your homes then you acknowledged this while you were witnessing

ayahs.t: Then, you are those [same ones who are] killing one another and evicting a party of your people from their homes, cooperating against them in sin and aggression. And if they come to you as captives, you ransom them, although their eviction was forbidden to you. So do you believe in part of the Scripture and disbelieve in part? Then what is the recompense for those who do that among you except disgrace in worldly life; and on the Day of Resurrection they will be sent back to the severest of punishment. And Allah is not unaware of what you do.
ayahs.n: then you are those same ones who are killing one another and evicting a party of your people from their homes cooperating against them in sin and aggression and if they come to you as captives you ransom them although their eviction was forbidden to you so do you believe in part of the scripture and disbelieve in part then what is the recompense for those who do that among you except disgrace in worldly life and on the day of resurrection they will be sent back to the severest of punishment and allah is not unaware of what you do

ayahs.t: Those are the ones who have bought the life of this world [in exchange] for the Hereafter, so the punishment will not be lightened for them, nor will they be aided.
ayahs.n: those are the ones who have bought the life of this world in exchange for the hereafter so the punishment will not be lightened for them nor will they be aided

ayahs.t: And We did certainly give Moses the Torah and followed up after him with messengers. And We gave Jesus, the son of Mary, clear proofs and supported him with the Pure Spirit. But is it [not] that every time a messenger came to you, [O Children of Israel], with what your souls did not desire, you were arrogant? And a party [of messengers] you denied and another party you killed.
ayahs.n: and we did certainly give moses the torah and followed up after him with messengers and we gave jesus the son of mary clear proofs and supported him with the pure spirit but is it not that every time a messenger came to you o children of israel with what your souls did not desire you were arrogant and a party of messengers you denied and another party you killed

ayahs.t: And they said, "Our hearts are wrapped." But, [in fact], Allah has cursed them for their disbelief, so little is it that they believe.
ayahs.n: and they said our hearts are wrapped but in fact allah has cursed them for their disbelief so little is it that they believe

ayahs.t: And when there came to them a Book from Allah confirming that which was with them - although before they used to pray for victory against those who disbelieved - but [then] when there came to them that which they recognized, they disbelieved in it; so the curse of Allah will be upon the disbelievers.
ayahs.n: and when there came to them a book from allah confirming that which was with them although before they used to pray for victory against those who disbelieved but then when there came to them that which they recognized they disbelieved in it so the curse of allah will be upon the disbelievers

ayahs.t: How wretched is that for which they sold themselves - that they would disbelieve in what Allah has revealed through [their] outrage that Allah would send down His favor upon whom He wills from among His servants. So they returned having [earned] wrath upon wrath. And for the disbelievers is a humiliating punishment.
ayahs.n: how wretched is that for which they sold themselves that they would disbelieve in what allah has revealed through their outrage that allah would send down his favor upon whom he wills from among his servants so they returned having earned wrath upon wrath and for the disbelievers is a humiliating punishment

ayahs.t: And when it is said to them, "Believe in what Allah has revealed," they say, "We believe [only] in what was revealed to us." And they disbelieve in what came after it, while it is the truth confirming that which is with them. Say, "Then why did you kill the prophets of Allah before, if you are [indeed] believers?"
ayahs.n: and when it is said to them believe in what allah has revealed they say we believe only in what was revealed to us and they disbelieve in what came after it while it is the truth confirming that which is with them say then why did you kill the prophets of allah before if you are indeed believers

ayahs.t: And Moses had certainly brought you clear proofs. Then you took the calf [in worship] after that, while you were wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: and moses had certainly brought you clear proofs then you took the calf in worship after that while you were wrongdoers

ayahs.t: And [recall] when We took your covenant and raised over you the mount, [saying], "Take what We have given you with determination and listen." They said [instead], "We hear and disobey." And their hearts absorbed [the worship of] the calf because of their disbelief. Say, "How wretched is that which your faith enjoins upon you, if you should be believers."
ayahs.n: and recall when we took your covenant and raised over you the mount saying take what we have given you with determination and listen they said instead we hear and disobey and their hearts absorbed the worship of the calf because of their disbelief say how wretched is that which your faith enjoins upon you if you should be believers

ayahs.t: Say, [O Muhammad], "If the home of the Hereafter with Allah is for you alone and not the [other] people, then wish for death, if you should be truthful.
ayahs.n: say o muhammad if the home of the hereafter with allah is for you alone and not the other people then wish for death if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: But they will never wish for it, ever, because of what their hands have put forth. And Allah is Knowing of the wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: but they will never wish for it ever because of what their hands have put forth and allah is knowing of the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: And you will surely find them the most greedy of people for life - [even] more than those who associate others with Allah. One of them wishes that he could be granted life a thousand years, but it would not remove him in the least from the [coming] punishment that he should be granted life. And Allah is Seeing of what they do.
ayahs.n: and you will surely find them the most greedy of people for life even more than those who associate others with allah one of them wishes that he could be granted life a thousand years but it would not remove him in the least from the coming punishment that he should be granted life and allah is seeing of what they do

ayahs.t: Say, "Whoever is an enemy to Gabriel - it is [none but] he who has brought the Qur'an down upon your heart, [O Muhammad], by permission of Allah, confirming that which was before it and as guidance and good tidings for the believers."
ayahs.n: say whoever is an enemy to gabriel it is none but he who has brought the quran down upon your heart o muhammad by permission of allah confirming that which was before it and as guidance and good tidings for the believers

ayahs.t: Whoever is an enemy to Allah and His angels and His messengers and Gabriel and Michael - then indeed, Allah is an enemy to the disbelievers.
ayahs.n: whoever is an enemy to allah and his angels and his messengers and gabriel and michael then indeed allah is an enemy to the disbelievers

ayahs.t: And We have certainly revealed to you verses [which are] clear proofs, and no one would deny them except the defiantly disobedient.
ayahs.n: and we have certainly revealed to you verses which are clear proofs and no one would deny them except the defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: Is it not [true] that every time they took a covenant a party of them threw it away? But, [in fact], most of them do not believe.
ayahs.n: is it not true that every time they took a covenant a party of them threw it away but in fact most of them do not believe

ayahs.t: And when a messenger from Allah came to them confirming that which was with them, a party of those who had been given the Scripture threw the Scripture of Allah behind their backs as if they did not know [what it contained].
ayahs.n: and when a messenger from allah came to them confirming that which was with them a party of those who had been given the scripture threw the scripture of allah behind their backs as if they did not know what it contained

ayahs.t: And they followed [instead] what the devils had recited during the reign of Solomon. It was not Solomon who disbelieved, but the devils disbelieved, teaching people magic and that which was revealed to the two angels at Babylon, Harut and Marut. But the two angels do not teach anyone unless they say, "We are a trial, so do not disbelieve [by practicing magic]." And [yet] they learn from them that by which they cause separation between a man and his wife. But they do not harm anyone through it except by permission of Allah. And the people learn what harms them and does not benefit them. But the Children of Israel certainly knew that whoever purchased the magic would not have in the Hereafter any share. And wretched is that for which they sold themselves, if they only knew.
ayahs.n: and they followed instead what the devils had recited during the reign of solomon it was not solomon who disbelieved but the devils disbelieved teaching people magic and that which was revealed to the two angels at babylon harut and marut but the two angels do not teach anyone unless they say we are a trial so do not disbelieve by practicing magic and yet they learn from them that by which they cause separation between a man and his wife but they do not harm anyone through it except by permission of allah and the people learn what harms them and does not benefit them but the children of israel certainly knew that whoever purchased the magic would not have in the hereafter any share and wretched is that for which they sold themselves if they only knew

ayahs.t: And if they had believed and feared Allah, then the reward from Allah would have been [far] better, if they only knew.
ayahs.n: and if they had believed and feared allah then the reward from allah would have been far better if they only knew

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, say not [to Allah 's Messenger], "Ra'ina" but say, "Unthurna" and listen. And for the disbelievers is a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed say not to allah s messenger raina but say unthurna and listen and for the disbelievers is a painful punishment

ayahs.t: Neither those who disbelieve from the People of the Scripture nor the polytheists wish that any good should be sent down to you from your Lord. But Allah selects for His mercy whom He wills, and Allah is the possessor of great bounty.
ayahs.n: neither those who disbelieve from the people of the scripture nor the polytheists wish that any good should be sent down to you from your lord but allah selects for his mercy whom he wills and allah is the possessor of great bounty

ayahs.t: We do not abrogate a verse or cause it to be forgotten except that We bring forth [one] better than it or similar to it. Do you not know that Allah is over all things competent?
ayahs.n: we do not abrogate a verse or cause it to be forgotten except that we bring forth one better than it or similar to it do you not know that allah is over all things competent

ayahs.t: Do you not know that to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and [that] you have not besides Allah any protector or any helper?
ayahs.n: do you not know that to allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and that you have not besides allah any protector or any helper

ayahs.t: Or do you intend to ask your Messenger as Moses was asked before? And whoever exchanges faith for disbelief has certainly strayed from the soundness of the way.
ayahs.n: or do you intend to ask your messenger as moses was asked before and whoever exchanges faith for disbelief has certainly strayed from the soundness of the way

ayahs.t: Many of the People of the Scripture wish they could turn you back to disbelief after you have believed, out of envy from themselves [even] after the truth has become clear to them. So pardon and overlook until Allah delivers His command. Indeed, Allah is over all things competent.
ayahs.n: many of the people of the scripture wish they could turn you back to disbelief after you have believed out of envy from themselves even after the truth has become clear to them so pardon and overlook until allah delivers his command indeed allah is over all things competent

ayahs.t: And establish prayer and give zakah, and whatever good you put forward for yourselves - you will find it with Allah. Indeed, Allah of what you do, is Seeing.
ayahs.n: and establish prayer and give zakah and whatever good you put forward for yourselves you will find it with allah indeed allah of what you do is seeing

ayahs.t: And they say, "None will enter Paradise except one who is a Jew or a Christian." That is [merely] their wishful thinking, Say, "Produce your proof, if you should be truthful."
ayahs.n: and they say none will enter paradise except one who is a jew or a christian that is merely their wishful thinking say produce your proof if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: Yes [on the contrary], whoever submits his face in Islam to Allah while being a doer of good will have his reward with his Lord. And no fear will there be concerning them, nor will they grieve.
ayahs.n: yes on the contrary whoever submits his face in islam to allah while being a doer of good will have his reward with his lord and no fear will there be concerning them nor will they grieve

ayahs.t: The Jews say "The Christians have nothing [true] to stand on," and the Christians say, "The Jews have nothing to stand on," although they [both] recite the Scripture. Thus the polytheists speak the same as their words. But Allah will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ.
ayahs.n: the jews say the christians have nothing true to stand on and the christians say the jews have nothing to stand on although they both recite the scripture thus the polytheists speak the same as their words but allah will judge between them on the day of resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ

ayahs.t: And who are more unjust than those who prevent the name of Allah from being mentioned in His mosques and strive toward their destruction. It is not for them to enter them except in fear. For them in this world is disgrace, and they will have in the Hereafter a great punishment.
ayahs.n: and who are more unjust than those who prevent the name of allah from being mentioned in his mosques and strive toward their destruction it is not for them to enter them except in fear for them in this world is disgrace and they will have in the hereafter a great punishment

ayahs.t: And to Allah belongs the east and the west. So wherever you [might] turn, there is the Face of Allah. Indeed, Allah is all-Encompassing and Knowing.
ayahs.n: and to allah belongs the east and the west so wherever you might turn there is the face of allah indeed allah is all encompassing and knowing

ayahs.t: They say, "Allah has taken a son." Exalted is He! Rather, to Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. All are devoutly obedient to Him,
ayahs.n: they say allah has taken a son exalted is he rather to him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth all are devoutly obedient to him

ayahs.t: Originator of the heavens and the earth. When He decrees a matter, He only says to it, "Be," and it is.
ayahs.n: originator of the heavens and the earth when he decrees a matter he only says to it be and it is

ayahs.t: Those who do not know say, "Why does Allah not speak to us or there come to us a sign?" Thus spoke those before them like their words. Their hearts resemble each other. We have shown clearly the signs to a people who are certain [in faith].
ayahs.n: those who do not know say why does allah not speak to us or there come to us a sign thus spoke those before them like their words their hearts resemble each other we have shown clearly the signs to a people who are certain in faith

ayahs.t: Indeed, We have sent you, [O Muhammad], with the truth as a bringer of good tidings and a warner, and you will not be asked about the companions of Hellfire.
ayahs.n: indeed we have sent you o muhammad with the truth as a bringer of good tidings and a warner and you will not be asked about the companions of hellfire

ayahs.t: And never will the Jews or the Christians approve of you until you follow their religion. Say, "Indeed, the guidance of Allah is the [only] guidance." If you were to follow their desires after what has come to you of knowledge, you would have against Allah no protector or helper.
ayahs.n: and never will the jews or the christians approve of you until you follow their religion say indeed the guidance of allah is the only guidance if you were to follow their desires after what has come to you of knowledge you would have against allah no protector or helper

ayahs.t: Those to whom We have given the Book recite it with its true recital. They [are the ones who] believe in it. And whoever disbelieves in it - it is they who are the losers.
ayahs.n: those to whom we have given the book recite it with its true recital they are the ones who believe in it and whoever disbelieves in it it is they who are the losers

ayahs.t: O Children of Israel, remember My favor which I have bestowed upon you and that I preferred you over the worlds.
ayahs.n: o children of israel remember my favor which i have bestowed upon you and that i preferred you over the worlds

ayahs.t: And fear a Day when no soul will suffice for another soul at all, and no compensation will be accepted from it, nor will any intercession benefit it, nor will they be aided.
ayahs.n: and fear a day when no soul will suffice for another soul at all and no compensation will be accepted from it nor will any intercession benefit it nor will they be aided

ayahs.t: And [mention, O Muhammad], when Abraham was tried by his Lord with commands and he fulfilled them. [Allah] said, "Indeed, I will make you a leader for the people." [Abraham] said, "And of my descendants?" [Allah] said, "My covenant does not include the wrongdoers."
ayahs.n: and mention o muhammad when abraham was tried by his lord with commands and he fulfilled them allah said indeed i will make you a leader for the people abraham said and of my descendants allah said my covenant does not include the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: And [mention] when We made the House a place of return for the people and [a place of] security. And take, [O believers], from the standing place of Abraham a place of prayer. And We charged Abraham and Ishmael, [saying], "Purify My House for those who perform Tawaf and those who are staying [there] for worship and those who bow and prostrate [in prayer]."
ayahs.n: and mention when we made the house a place of return for the people and a place of security and take o believers from the standing place of abraham a place of prayer and we charged abraham and ishmael saying purify my house for those who perform tawaf and those who are staying there for worship and those who bow and prostrate in prayer

ayahs.t: And [mention] when Abraham said, "My Lord, make this a secure city and provide its people with fruits - whoever of them believes in Allah and the Last Day." [Allah] said. "And whoever disbelieves - I will grant him enjoyment for a little; then I will force him to the punishment of the Fire, and wretched is the destination."
ayahs.n: and mention when abraham said my lord make this a secure city and provide its people with fruits whoever of them believes in allah and the last day allah said and whoever disbelieves i will grant him enjoyment for a little then i will force him to the punishment of the fire and wretched is the destination

ayahs.t: And [mention] when Abraham was raising the foundations of the House and [with him] Ishmael, [saying], "Our Lord, accept [this] from us. Indeed You are the Hearing, the Knowing.
ayahs.n: and mention when abraham was raising the foundations of the house and with him ishmael saying our lord accept this from us indeed you are the hearing the knowing

ayahs.t: Our Lord, and make us Muslims [in submission] to You and from our descendants a Muslim nation [in submission] to You. And show us our rites and accept our repentance. Indeed, You are the Accepting of repentance, the Merciful.
ayahs.n: our lord and make us muslims in submission to you and from our descendants a muslim nation in submission to you and show us our rites and accept our repentance indeed you are the accepting of repentance the merciful

ayahs.t: Our Lord, and send among them a messenger from themselves who will recite to them Your verses and teach them the Book and wisdom and purify them. Indeed, You are the Exalted in Might, the Wise."
ayahs.n: our lord and send among them a messenger from themselves who will recite to them your verses and teach them the book and wisdom and purify them indeed you are the exalted in might the wise

ayahs.t: And who would be averse to the religion of Abraham except one who makes a fool of himself. And We had chosen him in this world, and indeed he, in the Hereafter, will be among the righteous.
ayahs.n: and who would be averse to the religion of abraham except one who makes a fool of himself and we had chosen him in this world and indeed he in the hereafter will be among the righteous

ayahs.t: When his Lord said to him, "Submit", he said "I have submitted [in Islam] to the Lord of the worlds."
ayahs.n: when his lord said to him submit he said i have submitted in islam to the lord of the worlds

ayahs.t: And Abraham instructed his sons [to do the same] and [so did] Jacob, [saying], "O my sons, indeed Allah has chosen for you this religion, so do not die except while you are Muslims."
ayahs.n: and abraham instructed his sons to do the same and so did jacob saying o my sons indeed allah has chosen for you this religion so do not die except while you are muslims

ayahs.t: Or were you witnesses when death approached Jacob, when he said to his sons, "What will you worship after me?" They said, "We will worship your God and the God of your fathers, Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac - one God. And we are Muslims [in submission] to Him."
ayahs.n: or were you witnesses when death approached jacob when he said to his sons what will you worship after me they said we will worship your god and the god of your fathers abraham and ishmael and isaac one god and we are muslims in submission to him

ayahs.t: That was a nation which has passed on. It will have [the consequence of] what it earned, and you will have what you have earned. And you will not be asked about what they used to do.
ayahs.n: that was a nation which has passed on it will have the consequence of what it earned and you will have what you have earned and you will not be asked about what they used to do

ayahs.t: They say, "Be Jews or Christians [so] you will be guided." Say, "Rather, [we follow] the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth, and he was not of the polytheists."
ayahs.n: they say be jews or christians so you will be guided say rather we follow the religion of abraham inclining toward truth and he was not of the polytheists

ayahs.t: Say, [O believers], "We have believed in Allah and what has been revealed to us and what has been revealed to Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob and the Descendants and what was given to Moses and Jesus and what was given to the prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them, and we are Muslims [in submission] to Him."
ayahs.n: say o believers we have believed in allah and what has been revealed to us and what has been revealed to abraham and ishmael and isaac and jacob and the descendants and what was given to moses and jesus and what was given to the prophets from their lord we make no distinction between any of them and we are muslims in submission to him

ayahs.t: So if they believe in the same as you believe in, then they have been [rightly] guided; but if they turn away, they are only in dissension, and Allah will be sufficient for you against them. And He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
ayahs.n: so if they believe in the same as you believe in then they have been rightly guided but if they turn away they are only in dissension and allah will be sufficient for you against them and he is the hearing the knowing

ayahs.t: [And say, "Ours is] the religion of Allah. And who is better than Allah in [ordaining] religion? And we are worshippers of Him."
ayahs.n: and say ours is the religion of allah and who is better than allah in ordaining religion and we are worshippers of him

ayahs.t: Say, [O Muhammad], "Do you argue with us about Allah while He is our Lord and your Lord? For us are our deeds, and for you are your deeds. And we are sincere [in deed and intention] to Him."
ayahs.n: say o muhammad do you argue with us about allah while he is our lord and your lord for us are our deeds and for you are your deeds and we are sincere in deed and intention to him

ayahs.t: Or do you say that Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob and the Descendants were Jews or Christians? Say, "Are you more knowing or is Allah?" And who is more unjust than one who conceals a testimony he has from Allah? And Allah is not unaware of what you do.
ayahs.n: or do you say that abraham and ishmael and isaac and jacob and the descendants were jews or christians say are you more knowing or is allah and who is more unjust than one who conceals a testimony he has from allah and allah is not unaware of what you do

ayahs.t: That is a nation which has passed on. It will have [the consequence of] what it earned, and you will have what you have earned. And you will not be asked about what they used to do.
ayahs.n: that is a nation which has passed on it will have the consequence of what it earned and you will have what you have earned and you will not be asked about what they used to do

ayahs.t: The foolish among the people will say, "What has turned them away from their qiblah, which they used to face?" Say, "To Allah belongs the east and the west. He guides whom He wills to a straight path."
ayahs.n: the foolish among the people will say what has turned them away from their qiblah which they used to face say to allah belongs the east and the west he guides whom he wills to a straight path

ayahs.t: And thus we have made you a just community that you will be witnesses over the people and the Messenger will be a witness over you. And We did not make the qiblah which you used to face except that We might make evident who would follow the Messenger from who would turn back on his heels. And indeed, it is difficult except for those whom Allah has guided. And never would Allah have caused you to lose your faith. Indeed Allah is, to the people, Kind and Merciful.
ayahs.n: and thus we have made you a just community that you will be witnesses over the people and the messenger will be a witness over you and we did not make the qiblah which you used to face except that we might make evident who would follow the messenger from who would turn back on his heels and indeed it is difficult except for those whom allah has guided and never would allah have caused you to lose your faith indeed allah is to the people kind and merciful

ayahs.t: We have certainly seen the turning of your face, [O Muhammad], toward the heaven, and We will surely turn you to a qiblah with which you will be pleased. So turn your face toward al-Masjid al-Haram. And wherever you [believers] are, turn your faces toward it [in prayer]. Indeed, those who have been given the Scripture well know that it is the truth from their Lord. And Allah is not unaware of what they do.
ayahs.n: we have certainly seen the turning of your face o muhammad toward the heaven and we will surely turn you to a qiblah with which you will be pleased so turn your face toward al masjid al haram and wherever you believers are turn your faces toward it in prayer indeed those who have been given the scripture well know that it is the truth from their lord and allah is not unaware of what they do

ayahs.t: And if you brought to those who were given the Scripture every sign, they would not follow your qiblah. Nor will you be a follower of their qiblah. Nor would they be followers of one another's qiblah. So if you were to follow their desires after what has come to you of knowledge, indeed, you would then be among the wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: and if you brought to those who were given the scripture every sign they would not follow your qiblah nor will you be a follower of their qiblah nor would they be followers of one anothers qiblah so if you were to follow their desires after what has come to you of knowledge indeed you would then be among the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: Those to whom We gave the Scripture know him as they know their own sons. But indeed, a party of them conceal the truth while they know [it].
ayahs.n: those to whom we gave the scripture know him as they know their own sons but indeed a party of them conceal the truth while they know it

ayahs.t: The truth is from your Lord, so never be among the doubters.
ayahs.n: the truth is from your lord so never be among the doubters

ayahs.t: For each [religious following] is a direction toward which it faces. So race to [all that is] good. Wherever you may be, Allah will bring you forth [for judgement] all together. Indeed, Allah is over all things competent.
ayahs.n: for each religious following is a direction toward which it faces so race to all that is good wherever you may be allah will bring you forth for judgement all together indeed allah is over all things competent

ayahs.t: So from wherever you go out [for prayer, O Muhammad] turn your face toward al- Masjid al-Haram, and indeed, it is the truth from your Lord. And Allah is not unaware of what you do.
ayahs.n: so from wherever you go out for prayer o muhammad turn your face toward al masjid al haram and indeed it is the truth from your lord and allah is not unaware of what you do

ayahs.t: And from wherever you go out [for prayer], turn your face toward al-Masjid al-Haram. And wherever you [believers] may be, turn your faces toward it in order that the people will not have any argument against you, except for those of them who commit wrong; so fear them not but fear Me. And [it is] so I may complete My favor upon you and that you may be guided.
ayahs.n: and from wherever you go out for prayer turn your face toward al masjid al haram and wherever you believers may be turn your faces toward it in order that the people will not have any argument against you except for those of them who commit wrong so fear them not but fear me and it is so i may complete my favor upon you and that you may be guided

ayahs.t: Just as We have sent among you a messenger from yourselves reciting to you Our verses and purifying you and teaching you the Book and wisdom and teaching you that which you did not know.
ayahs.n: just as we have sent among you a messenger from yourselves reciting to you our verses and purifying you and teaching you the book and wisdom and teaching you that which you did not know

ayahs.t: So remember Me; I will remember you. And be grateful to Me and do not deny Me.
ayahs.n: so remember me i will remember you and be grateful to me and do not deny me

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, seek help through patience and prayer. Indeed, Allah is with the patient.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed seek help through patience and prayer indeed allah is with the patient

ayahs.t: And do not say about those who are killed in the way of Allah, "They are dead." Rather, they are alive, but you perceive [it] not.
ayahs.n: and do not say about those who are killed in the way of allah they are dead rather they are alive but you perceive it not

ayahs.t: And We will surely test you with something of fear and hunger and a loss of wealth and lives and fruits, but give good tidings to the patient,
ayahs.n: and we will surely test you with something of fear and hunger and a loss of wealth and lives and fruits but give good tidings to the patient

ayahs.t: Who, when disaster strikes them, say, "Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return."
ayahs.n: who when disaster strikes them say indeed we belong to allah and indeed to him we will return

ayahs.t: Those are the ones upon whom are blessings from their Lord and mercy. And it is those who are the [rightly] guided.
ayahs.n: those are the ones upon whom are blessings from their lord and mercy and it is those who are the rightly guided

ayahs.t: Indeed, as-Safa and al-Marwah are among the symbols of Allah. So whoever makes Hajj to the House or performs 'umrah - there is no blame upon him for walking between them. And whoever volunteers good - then indeed, Allah is appreciative and Knowing.
ayahs.n: indeed as safa and al marwah are among the symbols of allah so whoever makes hajj to the house or performs umrah there is no blame upon him for walking between them and whoever volunteers good then indeed allah is appreciative and knowing

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who conceal what We sent down of clear proofs and guidance after We made it clear for the people in the Scripture - those are cursed by Allah and cursed by those who curse,
ayahs.n: indeed those who conceal what we sent down of clear proofs and guidance after we made it clear for the people in the scripture those are cursed by allah and cursed by those who curse

ayahs.t: Except for those who repent and correct themselves and make evident [what they concealed]. Those - I will accept their repentance, and I am the Accepting of repentance, the Merciful.
ayahs.n: except for those who repent and correct themselves and make evident what they concealed those i will accept their repentance and i am the accepting of repentance the merciful

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who disbelieve and die while they are disbelievers - upon them will be the curse of Allah and of the angels and the people, all together,
ayahs.n: indeed those who disbelieve and die while they are disbelievers upon them will be the curse of allah and of the angels and the people all together

ayahs.t: Abiding eternally therein. The punishment will not be lightened for them, nor will they be reprieved.
ayahs.n: abiding eternally therein the punishment will not be lightened for them nor will they be reprieved

ayahs.t: And your god is one God. There is no deity [worthy of worship] except Him, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful.
ayahs.n: and your god is one god there is no deity worthy of worship except him the entirely merciful the especially merciful

ayahs.t: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and earth, and the alternation of the night and the day, and the [great] ships which sail through the sea with that which benefits people, and what Allah has sent down from the heavens of rain, giving life thereby to the earth after its lifelessness and dispersing therein every [kind of] moving creature, and [His] directing of the winds and the clouds controlled between the heaven and the earth are signs for a people who use reason.
ayahs.n: indeed in the creation of the heavens and earth and the alternation of the night and the day and the great ships which sail through the sea with that which benefits people and what allah has sent down from the heavens of rain giving life thereby to the earth after its lifelessness and dispersing therein every kind of moving creature and his directing of the winds and the clouds controlled between the heaven and the earth are signs for a people who use reason

ayahs.t: And [yet], among the people are those who take other than Allah as equals [to Him]. They love them as they [should] love Allah. But those who believe are stronger in love for Allah. And if only they who have wronged would consider [that] when they see the punishment, [they will be certain] that all power belongs to Allah and that Allah is severe in punishment.
ayahs.n: and yet among the people are those who take other than allah as equals to him they love them as they should love allah but those who believe are stronger in love for allah and if only they who have wronged would consider that when they see the punishment they will be certain that all power belongs to allah and that allah is severe in punishment

ayahs.t: [And they should consider that] when those who have been followed disassociate themselves from those who followed [them], and they [all] see the punishment, and cut off from them are the ties [of relationship],
ayahs.n: and they should consider that when those who have been followed disassociate themselves from those who followed them and they all see the punishment and cut off from them are the ties of relationship

ayahs.t: Those who followed will say, "If only we had another turn [at worldly life] so we could disassociate ourselves from them as they have disassociated themselves from us." Thus will Allah show them their deeds as regrets upon them. And they are never to emerge from the Fire.
ayahs.n: those who followed will say if only we had another turn at worldly life so we could disassociate ourselves from them as they have disassociated themselves from us thus will allah show them their deeds as regrets upon them and they are never to emerge from the fire

ayahs.t: O mankind, eat from whatever is on earth [that is] lawful and good and do not follow the footsteps of Satan. Indeed, he is to you a clear enemy.
ayahs.n: o mankind eat from whatever is on earth that is lawful and good and do not follow the footsteps of satan indeed he is to you a clear enemy

ayahs.t: He only orders you to evil and immorality and to say about Allah what you do not know.
ayahs.n: he only orders you to evil and immorality and to say about allah what you do not know

ayahs.t: And when it is said to them, "Follow what Allah has revealed," they say, "Rather, we will follow that which we found our fathers doing." Even though their fathers understood nothing, nor were they guided?
ayahs.n: and when it is said to them follow what allah has revealed they say rather we will follow that which we found our fathers doing even though their fathers understood nothing nor were they guided

ayahs.t: The example of those who disbelieve is like that of one who shouts at what hears nothing but calls and cries cattle or sheep - deaf, dumb and blind, so they do not understand.
ayahs.n: the example of those who disbelieve is like that of one who shouts at what hears nothing but calls and cries cattle or sheep deaf dumb and blind so they do not understand

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, eat from the good things which We have provided for you and be grateful to Allah if it is [indeed] Him that you worship.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed eat from the good things which we have provided for you and be grateful to allah if it is indeed him that you worship

ayahs.t: He has only forbidden to you dead animals, blood, the flesh of swine, and that which has been dedicated to other than Allah. But whoever is forced [by necessity], neither desiring [it] nor transgressing [its limit], there is no sin upon him. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: he has only forbidden to you dead animals blood the flesh of swine and that which has been dedicated to other than allah but whoever is forced by necessity neither desiring it nor transgressing its limit there is no sin upon him indeed allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: Indeed, they who conceal what Allah has sent down of the Book and exchange it for a small price - those consume not into their bellies except the Fire. And Allah will not speak to them on the Day of Resurrection, nor will He purify them. And they will have a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: indeed they who conceal what allah has sent down of the book and exchange it for a small price those consume not into their bellies except the fire and allah will not speak to them on the day of resurrection nor will he purify them and they will have a painful punishment

ayahs.t: Those are the ones who have exchanged guidance for error and forgiveness for punishment. How patient they are in pursuit of the Fire!
ayahs.n: those are the ones who have exchanged guidance for error and forgiveness for punishment how patient they are in pursuit of the fire

ayahs.t: That is [deserved by them] because Allah has sent down the Book in truth. And indeed, those who differ over the Book are in extreme dissension.
ayahs.n: that is deserved by them because allah has sent down the book in truth and indeed those who differ over the book are in extreme dissension

ayahs.t: Righteousness is not that you turn your faces toward the east or the west, but [true] righteousness is [in] one who believes in Allah, the Last Day, the angels, the Book, and the prophets and gives wealth, in spite of love for it, to relatives, orphans, the needy, the traveler, those who ask [for help], and for freeing slaves; [and who] establishes prayer and gives zakah; [those who] fulfill their promise when they promise; and [those who] are patient in poverty and hardship and during battle. Those are the ones who have been true, and it is those who are the righteous.
ayahs.n: righteousness is not that you turn your faces toward the east or the west but true righteousness is in one who believes in allah the last day the angels the book and the prophets and gives wealth in spite of love for it to relatives orphans the needy the traveler those who ask for help and for freeing slaves and who establishes prayer and gives zakah those who fulfill their promise when they promise and those who are patient in poverty and hardship and during battle those are the ones who have been true and it is those who are the righteous

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, prescribed for you is legal retribution for those murdered - the free for the free, the slave for the slave, and the female for the female. But whoever overlooks from his brother anything, then there should be a suitable follow-up and payment to him with good conduct. This is an alleviation from your Lord and a mercy. But whoever transgresses after that will have a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed prescribed for you is legal retribution for those murdered the free for the free the slave for the slave and the female for the female but whoever overlooks from his brother anything then there should be a suitable follow up and payment to him with good conduct this is an alleviation from your lord and a mercy but whoever transgresses after that will have a painful punishment

ayahs.t: And there is for you in legal retribution [saving of] life, O you [people] of understanding, that you may become righteous.
ayahs.n: and there is for you in legal retribution saving of life o you people of understanding that you may become righteous

ayahs.t: Prescribed for you when death approaches [any] one of you if he leaves wealth [is that he should make] a bequest for the parents and near relatives according to what is acceptable - a duty upon the righteous.
ayahs.n: prescribed for you when death approaches any one of you if he leaves wealth is that he should make a bequest for the parents and near relatives according to what is acceptable a duty upon the righteous

ayahs.t: Then whoever alters the bequest after he has heard it - the sin is only upon those who have altered it. Indeed, Allah is Hearing and Knowing.
ayahs.n: then whoever alters the bequest after he has heard it the sin is only upon those who have altered it indeed allah is hearing and knowing

ayahs.t: But if one fears from the bequeather [some] error or sin and corrects that which is between them, there is no sin upon him. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: but if one fears from the bequeather some error or sin and corrects that which is between them there is no sin upon him indeed allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, decreed upon you is fasting as it was decreed upon those before you that you may become righteous -
ayahs.n: o you who have believed decreed upon you is fasting as it was decreed upon those before you that you may become righteous

ayahs.t: [Fasting for] a limited number of days. So whoever among you is ill or on a journey [during them] - then an equal number of days [are to be made up]. And upon those who are able [to fast, but with hardship] - a ransom [as substitute] of feeding a poor person [each day]. And whoever volunteers excess - it is better for him. But to fast is best for you, if you only knew.
ayahs.n: fasting for a limited number of days so whoever among you is ill or on a journey during them then an equal number of days are to be made up and upon those who are able to fast but with hardship a ransom as substitute of feeding a poor person each day and whoever volunteers excess it is better for him but to fast is best for you if you only knew

ayahs.t: The month of Ramadhan [is that] in which was revealed the Qur'an, a guidance for the people and clear proofs of guidance and criterion. So whoever sights [the new moon of] the month, let him fast it; and whoever is ill or on a journey - then an equal number of other days. Allah intends for you ease and does not intend for you hardship and [wants] for you to complete the period and to glorify Allah for that [to] which He has guided you; and perhaps you will be grateful.
ayahs.n: the month of ramadhan is that in which was revealed the quran a guidance for the people and clear proofs of guidance and criterion so whoever sights the new moon of the month let him fast it and whoever is ill or on a journey then an equal number of other days allah intends for you ease and does not intend for you hardship and wants for you to complete the period and to glorify allah for that to which he has guided you and perhaps you will be grateful

ayahs.t: And when My servants ask you, [O Muhammad], concerning Me - indeed I am near. I respond to the invocation of the supplicant when he calls upon Me. So let them respond to Me [by obedience] and believe in Me that they may be [rightly] guided.
ayahs.n: and when my servants ask you o muhammad concerning me indeed i am near i respond to the invocation of the supplicant when he calls upon me so let them respond to me by obedience and believe in me that they may be rightly guided

ayahs.t: It has been made permissible for you the night preceding fasting to go to your wives [for sexual relations]. They are clothing for you and you are clothing for them. Allah knows that you used to deceive yourselves, so He accepted your repentance and forgave you. So now, have relations with them and seek that which Allah has decreed for you. And eat and drink until the white thread of dawn becomes distinct to you from the black thread [of night]. Then complete the fast until the sunset. And do not have relations with them as long as you are staying for worship in the mosques. These are the limits [set by] Allah, so do not approach them. Thus does Allah make clear His ordinances to the people that they may become righteous.
ayahs.n: it has been made permissible for you the night preceding fasting to go to your wives for sexual relations they are clothing for you and you are clothing for them allah knows that you used to deceive yourselves so he accepted your repentance and forgave you so now have relations with them and seek that which allah has decreed for you and eat and drink until the white thread of dawn becomes distinct to you from the black thread of night then complete the fast until the sunset and do not have relations with them as long as you are staying for worship in the mosques these are the limits set by allah so do not approach them thus does allah make clear his ordinances to the people that they may become righteous

ayahs.t: And do not consume one another's wealth unjustly or send it [in bribery] to the rulers in order that [they might aid] you [to] consume a portion of the wealth of the people in sin, while you know [it is unlawful].
ayahs.n: and do not consume one anothers wealth unjustly or send it in bribery to the rulers in order that they might aid you to consume a portion of the wealth of the people in sin while you know it is unlawful

ayahs.t: They ask you, [O Muhammad], about the new moons. Say, "They are measurements of time for the people and for Hajj."
