ayahs.t: [Mention] when Allah said, "O Jesus, indeed I will take you and raise you to Myself and purify you from those who disbelieve and make those who follow you [in submission to Allah alone] superior to those who disbelieve until the Day of Resurrection. Then to Me is your return, and I will judge between you concerning that in which you used to differ.
ayahs.n: mention when allah said o jesus indeed i will take you and raise you to myself and purify you from those who disbelieve and make those who follow you in submission to allah alone superior to those who disbelieve until the day of resurrection then to me is your return and i will judge between you concerning that in which you used to differ

ayahs.t: And as for those who disbelieved, I will punish them with a severe punishment in this world and the Hereafter, and they will have no helpers."
ayahs.n: and as for those who disbelieved i will punish them with a severe punishment in this world and the hereafter and they will have no helpers

ayahs.t: But as for those who believed and did righteous deeds, He will give them in full their rewards, and Allah does not like the wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: but as for those who believed and did righteous deeds he will give them in full their rewards and allah does not like the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: This is what We recite to you, [O Muhammad], of [Our] verses and the precise [and wise] message.
ayahs.n: this is what we recite to you o muhammad of our verses and the precise and wise message

ayahs.t: Indeed, the example of Jesus to Allah is like that of Adam. He created Him from dust; then He said to him, "Be," and he was.
ayahs.n: indeed the example of jesus to allah is like that of adam he created him from dust then he said to him be and he was

ayahs.t: The truth is from your Lord, so do not be among the doubters.
ayahs.n: the truth is from your lord so do not be among the doubters

ayahs.t: Then whoever argues with you about it after [this] knowledge has come to you - say, "Come, let us call our sons and your sons, our women and your women, ourselves and yourselves, then supplicate earnestly [together] and invoke the curse of Allah upon the liars [among us]."
ayahs.n: then whoever argues with you about it after this knowledge has come to you say come let us call our sons and your sons our women and your women ourselves and yourselves then supplicate earnestly together and invoke the curse of allah upon the liars among us

ayahs.t: Indeed, this is the true narration. And there is no deity except Allah. And indeed, Allah is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
ayahs.n: indeed this is the true narration and there is no deity except allah and indeed allah is the exalted in might the wise

ayahs.t: But if they turn away, then indeed - Allah is Knowing of the corrupters.
ayahs.n: but if they turn away then indeed allah is knowing of the corrupters

ayahs.t: Say, "O People of the Scripture, come to a word that is equitable between us and you - that we will not worship except Allah and not associate anything with Him and not take one another as lords instead of Allah." But if they turn away, then say, "Bear witness that we are Muslims [submitting to Him]."
ayahs.n: say o people of the scripture come to a word that is equitable between us and you that we will not worship except allah and not associate anything with him and not take one another as lords instead of allah but if they turn away then say bear witness that we are muslims submitting to him

ayahs.t: O People of the Scripture, why do you argue about Abraham while the Torah and the Gospel were not revealed until after him? Then will you not reason?
ayahs.n: o people of the scripture why do you argue about abraham while the torah and the gospel were not revealed until after him then will you not reason

ayahs.t: Here you are - those who have argued about that of which you have [some] knowledge, but why do you argue about that of which you have no knowledge? And Allah knows, while you know not.
ayahs.n: here you are those who have argued about that of which you have some knowledge but why do you argue about that of which you have no knowledge and allah knows while you know not

ayahs.t: Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian, but he was one inclining toward truth, a Muslim [submitting to Allah]. And he was not of the polytheists.
ayahs.n: abraham was neither a jew nor a christian but he was one inclining toward truth a muslim submitting to allah and he was not of the polytheists

ayahs.t: Indeed, the most worthy of Abraham among the people are those who followed him [in submission to Allah] and this prophet, and those who believe [in his message]. And Allah is the ally of the believers.
ayahs.n: indeed the most worthy of abraham among the people are those who followed him in submission to allah and this prophet and those who believe in his message and allah is the ally of the believers

ayahs.t: A faction of the people of the Scripture wish they could mislead you. But they do not mislead except themselves, and they perceive [it] not.
ayahs.n: a faction of the people of the scripture wish they could mislead you but they do not mislead except themselves and they perceive it not

ayahs.t: O People of the Scripture, why do you disbelieve in the verses of Allah while you witness [to their truth]?
ayahs.n: o people of the scripture why do you disbelieve in the verses of allah while you witness to their truth

ayahs.t: O People of the Scripture, why do you confuse the truth with falsehood and conceal the truth while you know [it]?
ayahs.n: o people of the scripture why do you confuse the truth with falsehood and conceal the truth while you know it

ayahs.t: And a faction of the People of the Scripture say [to each other], "Believe in that which was revealed to the believers at the beginning of the day and reject it at its end that perhaps they will abandon their religion,
ayahs.n: and a faction of the people of the scripture say to each other believe in that which was revealed to the believers at the beginning of the day and reject it at its end that perhaps they will abandon their religion

ayahs.t: And do not trust except those who follow your religion." Say, "Indeed, the [true] guidance is the guidance of Allah. [Do you fear] lest someone be given [knowledge] like you were given or that they would [thereby] argue with you before your Lord?" Say, "Indeed, [all] bounty is in the hand of Allah - He grants it to whom He wills. And Allah is all-Encompassing and Wise."
ayahs.n: and do not trust except those who follow your religion say indeed the true guidance is the guidance of allah do you fear lest someone be given knowledge like you were given or that they would thereby argue with you before your lord say indeed all bounty is in the hand of allah he grants it to whom he wills and allah is all encompassing and wise

ayahs.t: He selects for His mercy whom He wills. And Allah is the possessor of great bounty.
ayahs.n: he selects for his mercy whom he wills and allah is the possessor of great bounty

ayahs.t: And among the People of the Scripture is he who, if you entrust him with a great amount [of wealth], he will return it to you. And among them is he who, if you entrust him with a [single] silver coin, he will not return it to you unless you are constantly standing over him [demanding it]. That is because they say, "There is no blame upon us concerning the unlearned." And they speak untruth about Allah while they know [it].
ayahs.n: and among the people of the scripture is he who if you entrust him with a great amount of wealth he will return it to you and among them is he who if you entrust him with a single silver coin he will not return it to you unless you are constantly standing over him demanding it that is because they say there is no blame upon us concerning the unlearned and they speak untruth about allah while they know it

ayahs.t: But yes, whoever fulfills his commitment and fears Allah - then indeed, Allah loves those who fear Him.
ayahs.n: but yes whoever fulfills his commitment and fears allah then indeed allah loves those who fear him

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who exchange the covenant of Allah and their [own] oaths for a small price will have no share in the Hereafter, and Allah will not speak to them or look at them on the Day of Resurrection, nor will He purify them; and they will have a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: indeed those who exchange the covenant of allah and their own oaths for a small price will have no share in the hereafter and allah will not speak to them or look at them on the day of resurrection nor will he purify them and they will have a painful punishment

ayahs.t: And indeed, there is among them a party who alter the Scripture with their tongues so you may think it is from the Scripture, but it is not from the Scripture. And they say, "This is from Allah," but it is not from Allah. And they speak untruth about Allah while they know.
ayahs.n: and indeed there is among them a party who alter the scripture with their tongues so you may think it is from the scripture but it is not from the scripture and they say this is from allah but it is not from allah and they speak untruth about allah while they know

ayahs.t: It is not for a human [prophet] that Allah should give him the Scripture and authority and prophethood and then he would say to the people, "Be servants to me rather than Allah," but [instead, he would say], "Be pious scholars of the Lord because of what you have taught of the Scripture and because of what you have studied."
ayahs.n: it is not for a human prophet that allah should give him the scripture and authority and prophethood and then he would say to the people be servants to me rather than allah but instead he would say be pious scholars of the lord because of what you have taught of the scripture and because of what you have studied

ayahs.t: Nor could he order you to take the angels and prophets as lords. Would he order you to disbelief after you had been Muslims?
ayahs.n: nor could he order you to take the angels and prophets as lords would he order you to disbelief after you had been muslims

ayahs.t: And [recall, O People of the Scripture], when Allah took the covenant of the prophets, [saying], "Whatever I give you of the Scripture and wisdom and then there comes to you a messenger confirming what is with you, you [must] believe in him and support him." [Allah] said, "Have you acknowledged and taken upon that My commitment?" They said, "We have acknowledged it." He said, "Then bear witness, and I am with you among the witnesses."
ayahs.n: and recall o people of the scripture when allah took the covenant of the prophets saying whatever i give you of the scripture and wisdom and then there comes to you a messenger confirming what is with you you must believe in him and support him allah said have you acknowledged and taken upon that my commitment they said we have acknowledged it he said then bear witness and i am with you among the witnesses

ayahs.t: And whoever turned away after that - they were the defiantly disobedient.
ayahs.n: and whoever turned away after that they were the defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: So is it other than the religion of Allah they desire, while to Him have submitted [all] those within the heavens and earth, willingly or by compulsion, and to Him they will be returned?
ayahs.n: so is it other than the religion of allah they desire while to him have submitted all those within the heavens and earth willingly or by compulsion and to him they will be returned

ayahs.t: Say, "We have believed in Allah and in what was revealed to us and what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Descendants, and in what was given to Moses and Jesus and to the prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them, and we are Muslims [submitting] to Him."
ayahs.n: say we have believed in allah and in what was revealed to us and what was revealed to abraham ishmael isaac jacob and the descendants and in what was given to moses and jesus and to the prophets from their lord we make no distinction between any of them and we are muslims submitting to him

ayahs.t: And whoever desires other than Islam as religion - never will it be accepted from him, and he, in the Hereafter, will be among the losers.
ayahs.n: and whoever desires other than islam as religion never will it be accepted from him and he in the hereafter will be among the losers

ayahs.t: How shall Allah guide a people who disbelieved after their belief and had witnessed that the Messenger is true and clear signs had come to them? And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
ayahs.n: how shall allah guide a people who disbelieved after their belief and had witnessed that the messenger is true and clear signs had come to them and allah does not guide the wrongdoing people

ayahs.t: Those - their recompense will be that upon them is the curse of Allah and the angels and the people, all together,
ayahs.n: those their recompense will be that upon them is the curse of allah and the angels and the people all together

ayahs.t: Abiding eternally therein. The punishment will not be lightened for them, nor will they be reprieved.
ayahs.n: abiding eternally therein the punishment will not be lightened for them nor will they be reprieved

ayahs.t: Except for those who repent after that and correct themselves. For indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: except for those who repent after that and correct themselves for indeed allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who reject the message after their belief and then increase in disbelief - never will their [claimed] repentance be accepted, and they are the ones astray.
ayahs.n: indeed those who reject the message after their belief and then increase in disbelief never will their claimed repentance be accepted and they are the ones astray

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who disbelieve and die while they are disbelievers - never would the [whole] capacity of the earth in gold be accepted from one of them if he would [seek to] ransom himself with it. For those there will be a painful punishment, and they will have no helpers.
ayahs.n: indeed those who disbelieve and die while they are disbelievers never would the whole capacity of the earth in gold be accepted from one of them if he would seek to ransom himself with it for those there will be a painful punishment and they will have no helpers

ayahs.t: Never will you attain the good [reward] until you spend [in the way of Allah] from that which you love. And whatever you spend - indeed, Allah is Knowing of it.
ayahs.n: never will you attain the good reward until you spend in the way of allah from that which you love and whatever you spend indeed allah is knowing of it

ayahs.t: All food was lawful to the Children of Israel except what Israel had made unlawful to himself before the Torah was revealed. Say, [O Muhammad], "So bring the Torah and recite it, if you should be truthful."
ayahs.n: all food was lawful to the children of israel except what israel had made unlawful to himself before the torah was revealed say o muhammad so bring the torah and recite it if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: And whoever invents about Allah untruth after that - then those are [truly] the wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: and whoever invents about allah untruth after that then those are truly the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: Say, "Allah has told the truth. So follow the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth; and he was not of the polytheists."
ayahs.n: say allah has told the truth so follow the religion of abraham inclining toward truth and he was not of the polytheists

ayahs.t: Indeed, the first House [of worship] established for mankind was that at Makkah - blessed and a guidance for the worlds.
ayahs.n: indeed the first house of worship established for mankind was that at makkah blessed and a guidance for the worlds

ayahs.t: In it are clear signs [such as] the standing place of Abraham. And whoever enters it shall be safe. And [due] to Allah from the people is a pilgrimage to the House - for whoever is able to find thereto a way. But whoever disbelieves - then indeed, Allah is free from need of the worlds.
ayahs.n: in it are clear signs such as the standing place of abraham and whoever enters it shall be safe and due to allah from the people is a pilgrimage to the house for whoever is able to find thereto a way but whoever disbelieves then indeed allah is free from need of the worlds

ayahs.t: Say, "O People of the Scripture, why do you disbelieve in the verses of Allah while Allah is Witness over what you do?"
ayahs.n: say o people of the scripture why do you disbelieve in the verses of allah while allah is witness over what you do

ayahs.t: Say, "O People of the Scripture, why do you avert from the way of Allah those who believe, seeking to make it [seem] deviant, while you are witnesses [to the truth]? And Allah is not unaware of what you do."
ayahs.n: say o people of the scripture why do you avert from the way of allah those who believe seeking to make it seem deviant while you are witnesses to the truth and allah is not unaware of what you do

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, if you obey a party of those who were given the Scripture, they would turn you back, after your belief, [to being] unbelievers.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed if you obey a party of those who were given the scripture they would turn you back after your belief to being unbelievers

ayahs.t: And how could you disbelieve while to you are being recited the verses of Allah and among you is His Messenger? And whoever holds firmly to Allah has [indeed] been guided to a straight path.
ayahs.n: and how could you disbelieve while to you are being recited the verses of allah and among you is his messenger and whoever holds firmly to allah has indeed been guided to a straight path

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, fear Allah as He should be feared and do not die except as Muslims [in submission to Him].
ayahs.n: o you who have believed fear allah as he should be feared and do not die except as muslims in submission to him

ayahs.t: And hold firmly to the rope of Allah all together and do not become divided. And remember the favor of Allah upon you - when you were enemies and He brought your hearts together and you became, by His favor, brothers. And you were on the edge of a pit of the Fire, and He saved you from it. Thus does Allah make clear to you His verses that you may be guided.
ayahs.n: and hold firmly to the rope of allah all together and do not become divided and remember the favor of allah upon you when you were enemies and he brought your hearts together and you became by his favor brothers and you were on the edge of a pit of the fire and he saved you from it thus does allah make clear to you his verses that you may be guided

ayahs.t: And let there be [arising] from you a nation inviting to [all that is] good, enjoining what is right and forbidding what is wrong, and those will be the successful.
ayahs.n: and let there be arising from you a nation inviting to all that is good enjoining what is right and forbidding what is wrong and those will be the successful

ayahs.t: And do not be like the ones who became divided and differed after the clear proofs had come to them. And those will have a great punishment.
ayahs.n: and do not be like the ones who became divided and differed after the clear proofs had come to them and those will have a great punishment

ayahs.t: On the Day [some] faces will turn white and [some] faces will turn black. As for those whose faces turn black, [to them it will be said], "Did you disbelieve after your belief? Then taste the punishment for what you used to reject."
ayahs.n: on the day some faces will turn white and some faces will turn black as for those whose faces turn black to them it will be said did you disbelieve after your belief then taste the punishment for what you used to reject

ayahs.t: But as for those whose faces will turn white, [they will be] within the mercy of Allah. They will abide therein eternally.
ayahs.n: but as for those whose faces will turn white they will be within the mercy of allah they will abide therein eternally

ayahs.t: These are the verses of Allah. We recite them to you, [O Muhammad], in truth; and Allah wants no injustice to the worlds.
ayahs.n: these are the verses of allah we recite them to you o muhammad in truth and allah wants no injustice to the worlds

ayahs.t: To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And to Allah will [all] matters be returned.
ayahs.n: to allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth and to allah will all matters be returned

ayahs.t: You are the best nation produced [as an example] for mankind. You enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong and believe in Allah. If only the People of the Scripture had believed, it would have been better for them. Among them are believers, but most of them are defiantly disobedient.
ayahs.n: you are the best nation produced as an example for mankind you enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong and believe in allah if only the people of the scripture had believed it would have been better for them among them are believers but most of them are defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: They will not harm you except for [some] annoyance. And if they fight you, they will show you their backs; then they will not be aided.
ayahs.n: they will not harm you except for some annoyance and if they fight you they will show you their backs then they will not be aided

ayahs.t: They have been put under humiliation [by Allah] wherever they are overtaken, except for a covenant from Allah and a rope from the Muslims. And they have drawn upon themselves anger from Allah and have been put under destitution. That is because they disbelieved in the verses of Allah and killed the prophets without right. That is because they disobeyed and [habitually] transgressed.
ayahs.n: they have been put under humiliation by allah wherever they are overtaken except for a covenant from allah and a rope from the muslims and they have drawn upon themselves anger from allah and have been put under destitution that is because they disbelieved in the verses of allah and killed the prophets without right that is because they disobeyed and habitually transgressed

ayahs.t: They are not [all] the same; among the People of the Scripture is a community standing [in obedience], reciting the verses of Allah during periods of the night and prostrating [in prayer].
ayahs.n: they are not all the same among the people of the scripture is a community standing in obedience reciting the verses of allah during periods of the night and prostrating in prayer

ayahs.t: They believe in Allah and the Last Day, and they enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong and hasten to good deeds. And those are among the righteous.
ayahs.n: they believe in allah and the last day and they enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong and hasten to good deeds and those are among the righteous

ayahs.t: And whatever good they do - never will it be removed from them. And Allah is Knowing of the righteous.
ayahs.n: and whatever good they do never will it be removed from them and allah is knowing of the righteous

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who disbelieve - never will their wealth or their children avail them against Allah at all, and those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally.
ayahs.n: indeed those who disbelieve never will their wealth or their children avail them against allah at all and those are the companions of the fire they will abide therein eternally

ayahs.t: The example of what they spend in this worldly life is like that of a wind containing frost which strikes the harvest of a people who have wronged themselves and destroys it. And Allah has not wronged them, but they wrong themselves.
ayahs.n: the example of what they spend in this worldly life is like that of a wind containing frost which strikes the harvest of a people who have wronged themselves and destroys it and allah has not wronged them but they wrong themselves

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, do not take as intimates those other than yourselves, for they will not spare you [any] ruin. They wish you would have hardship. Hatred has already appeared from their mouths, and what their breasts conceal is greater. We have certainly made clear to you the signs, if you will use reason.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed do not take as intimates those other than yourselves for they will not spare you any ruin they wish you would have hardship hatred has already appeared from their mouths and what their breasts conceal is greater we have certainly made clear to you the signs if you will use reason

ayahs.t: Here you are loving them but they are not loving you, while you believe in the Scripture - all of it. And when they meet you, they say, "We believe." But when they are alone, they bite their fingertips at you in rage. Say, "Die in your rage. Indeed, Allah is Knowing of that within the breasts."
ayahs.n: here you are loving them but they are not loving you while you believe in the scripture all of it and when they meet you they say we believe but when they are alone they bite their fingertips at you in rage say die in your rage indeed allah is knowing of that within the breasts

ayahs.t: If good touches you, it distresses them; but if harm strikes you, they rejoice at it. And if you are patient and fear Allah, their plot will not harm you at all. Indeed, Allah is encompassing of what they do.
ayahs.n: if good touches you it distresses them but if harm strikes you they rejoice at it and if you are patient and fear allah their plot will not harm you at all indeed allah is encompassing of what they do

ayahs.t: And [remember] when you, [O Muhammad], left your family in the morning to post the believers at their stations for the battle [of Uhud] - and Allah is Hearing and Knowing -
ayahs.n: and remember when you o muhammad left your family in the morning to post the believers at their stations for the battle of uhud and allah is hearing and knowing

ayahs.t: When two parties among you were about to lose courage, but Allah was their ally; and upon Allah the believers should rely.
ayahs.n: when two parties among you were about to lose courage but allah was their ally and upon allah the believers should rely

ayahs.t: And already had Allah given you victory at [the battle of] Badr while you were few in number. Then fear Allah; perhaps you will be grateful.
ayahs.n: and already had allah given you victory at the battle of badr while you were few in number then fear allah perhaps you will be grateful

ayahs.t: [Remember] when you said to the believers, "Is it not sufficient for you that your Lord should reinforce you with three thousand angels sent down?
ayahs.n: remember when you said to the believers is it not sufficient for you that your lord should reinforce you with three thousand angels sent down

ayahs.t: Yes, if you remain patient and conscious of Allah and the enemy come upon you [attacking] in rage, your Lord will reinforce you with five thousand angels having marks [of distinction]
ayahs.n: yes if you remain patient and conscious of allah and the enemy come upon you attacking in rage your lord will reinforce you with five thousand angels having marks of distinction

ayahs.t: And Allah made it not except as [a sign of] good tidings for you and to reassure your hearts thereby. And victory is not except from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise -
ayahs.n: and allah made it not except as a sign of good tidings for you and to reassure your hearts thereby and victory is not except from allah the exalted in might the wise

ayahs.t: That He might cut down a section of the disbelievers or suppress them so that they turn back disappointed.
ayahs.n: that he might cut down a section of the disbelievers or suppress them so that they turn back disappointed

ayahs.t: Not for you, [O Muhammad, but for Allah], is the decision whether He should [cut them down] or forgive them or punish them, for indeed, they are wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: not for you o muhammad but for allah is the decision whether he should cut them down or forgive them or punish them for indeed they are wrongdoers

ayahs.t: And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. He forgives whom He wills and punishes whom He wills. And Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: and to allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth he forgives whom he wills and punishes whom he wills and allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, do not consume usury, doubled and multiplied, but fear Allah that you may be successful.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed do not consume usury doubled and multiplied but fear allah that you may be successful

ayahs.t: And fear the Fire, which has been prepared for the disbelievers.
ayahs.n: and fear the fire which has been prepared for the disbelievers

ayahs.t: And obey Allah and the Messenger that you may obtain mercy.
ayahs.n: and obey allah and the messenger that you may obtain mercy

ayahs.t: And hasten to forgiveness from your Lord and a garden as wide as the heavens and earth, prepared for the righteous
ayahs.n: and hasten to forgiveness from your lord and a garden as wide as the heavens and earth prepared for the righteous

ayahs.t: Who spend [in the cause of Allah] during ease and hardship and who restrain anger and who pardon the people - and Allah loves the doers of good;
ayahs.n: who spend in the cause of allah during ease and hardship and who restrain anger and who pardon the people and allah loves the doers of good

ayahs.t: And those who, when they commit an immorality or wrong themselves [by transgression], remember Allah and seek forgiveness for their sins - and who can forgive sins except Allah? - and [who] do not persist in what they have done while they know.
ayahs.n: and those who when they commit an immorality or wrong themselves by transgression remember allah and seek forgiveness for their sins and who can forgive sins except allah and who do not persist in what they have done while they know

ayahs.t: Those - their reward is forgiveness from their Lord and gardens beneath which rivers flow [in Paradise], wherein they will abide eternally; and excellent is the reward of the [righteous] workers.
ayahs.n: those their reward is forgiveness from their lord and gardens beneath which rivers flow in paradise wherein they will abide eternally and excellent is the reward of the righteous workers

ayahs.t: Similar situations [as yours] have passed on before you, so proceed throughout the earth and observe how was the end of those who denied.
ayahs.n: similar situations as yours have passed on before you so proceed throughout the earth and observe how was the end of those who denied

ayahs.t: This [Qur'an] is a clear statement to [all] the people and a guidance and instruction for those conscious of Allah.
ayahs.n: this quran is a clear statement to all the people and a guidance and instruction for those conscious of allah

ayahs.t: So do not weaken and do not grieve, and you will be superior if you are [true] believers.
ayahs.n: so do not weaken and do not grieve and you will be superior if you are true believers

ayahs.t: If a wound should touch you - there has already touched the [opposing] people a wound similar to it. And these days [of varying conditions] We alternate among the people so that Allah may make evident those who believe and [may] take to Himself from among you martyrs - and Allah does not like the wrongdoers -
ayahs.n: if a wound should touch you there has already touched the opposing people a wound similar to it and these days of varying conditions we alternate among the people so that allah may make evident those who believe and may take to himself from among you martyrs and allah does not like the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: And that Allah may purify the believers [through trials] and destroy the disbelievers.
ayahs.n: and that allah may purify the believers through trials and destroy the disbelievers

ayahs.t: Or do you think that you will enter Paradise while Allah has not yet made evident those of you who fight in His cause and made evident those who are steadfast?
ayahs.n: or do you think that you will enter paradise while allah has not yet made evident those of you who fight in his cause and made evident those who are steadfast

ayahs.t: And you had certainly wished for martyrdom before you encountered it, and you have [now] seen it [before you] while you were looking on.
ayahs.n: and you had certainly wished for martyrdom before you encountered it and you have now seen it before you while you were looking on

ayahs.t: Muhammad is not but a messenger. [Other] messengers have passed on before him. So if he was to die or be killed, would you turn back on your heels [to unbelief]? And he who turns back on his heels will never harm Allah at all; but Allah will reward the grateful.
ayahs.n: muhammad is not but a messenger other messengers have passed on before him so if he was to die or be killed would you turn back on your heels to unbelief and he who turns back on his heels will never harm allah at all but allah will reward the grateful

ayahs.t: And it is not [possible] for one to die except by permission of Allah at a decree determined. And whoever desires the reward of this world - We will give him thereof; and whoever desires the reward of the Hereafter - We will give him thereof. And we will reward the grateful.
ayahs.n: and it is not possible for one to die except by permission of allah at a decree determined and whoever desires the reward of this world we will give him thereof and whoever desires the reward of the hereafter we will give him thereof and we will reward the grateful

ayahs.t: And how many a prophet [fought and] with him fought many religious scholars. But they never lost assurance due to what afflicted them in the cause of Allah, nor did they weaken or submit. And Allah loves the steadfast.
ayahs.n: and how many a prophet fought and with him fought many religious scholars but they never lost assurance due to what afflicted them in the cause of allah nor did they weaken or submit and allah loves the steadfast

ayahs.t: And their words were not but that they said, "Our Lord, forgive us our sins and the excess [committed] in our affairs and plant firmly our feet and give us victory over the disbelieving people."
ayahs.n: and their words were not but that they said our lord forgive us our sins and the excess committed in our affairs and plant firmly our feet and give us victory over the disbelieving people

ayahs.t: So Allah gave them the reward of this world and the good reward of the Hereafter. And Allah loves the doers of good.
ayahs.n: so allah gave them the reward of this world and the good reward of the hereafter and allah loves the doers of good

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, if you obey those who disbelieve, they will turn you back on your heels, and you will [then] become losers.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed if you obey those who disbelieve they will turn you back on your heels and you will then become losers

ayahs.t: But Allah is your protector, and He is the best of helpers.
ayahs.n: but allah is your protector and he is the best of helpers

ayahs.t: We will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve for what they have associated with Allah of which He had not sent down [any] authority. And their refuge will be the Fire, and wretched is the residence of the wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: we will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve for what they have associated with allah of which he had not sent down any authority and their refuge will be the fire and wretched is the residence of the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: And Allah had certainly fulfilled His promise to you when you were killing the enemy by His permission until [the time] when you lost courage and fell to disputing about the order [given by the Prophet] and disobeyed after He had shown you that which you love. Among you are some who desire this world, and among you are some who desire the Hereafter. Then he turned you back from them [defeated] that He might test you. And He has already forgiven you, and Allah is the possessor of bounty for the believers.
ayahs.n: and allah had certainly fulfilled his promise to you when you were killing the enemy by his permission until the time when you lost courage and fell to disputing about the order given by the prophet and disobeyed after he had shown you that which you love among you are some who desire this world and among you are some who desire the hereafter then he turned you back from them defeated that he might test you and he has already forgiven you and allah is the possessor of bounty for the believers

ayahs.t: [Remember] when you [fled and] climbed [the mountain] without looking aside at anyone while the Messenger was calling you from behind. So Allah repaid you with distress upon distress so you would not grieve for that which had escaped you [of victory and spoils of war] or [for] that which had befallen you [of injury and death]. And Allah is [fully] Acquainted with what you do.
ayahs.n: remember when you fled and climbed the mountain without looking aside at anyone while the messenger was calling you from behind so allah repaid you with distress upon distress so you would not grieve for that which had escaped you of victory and spoils of war or for that which had befallen you of injury and death and allah is fully acquainted with what you do

ayahs.t: Then after distress, He sent down upon you security [in the form of] drowsiness, overcoming a faction of you, while another faction worried about themselves, thinking of Allah other than the truth - the thought of ignorance, saying, "Is there anything for us [to have done] in this matter?" Say, "Indeed, the matter belongs completely to Allah." They conceal within themselves what they will not reveal to you. They say, "If there was anything we could have done in the matter, some of us would not have been killed right here." Say, "Even if you had been inside your houses, those decreed to be killed would have come out to their death beds." [It was] so that Allah might test what is in your breasts and purify what is in your hearts. And Allah is Knowing of that within the breasts.
ayahs.n: then after distress he sent down upon you security in the form of drowsiness overcoming a faction of you while another faction worried about themselves thinking of allah other than the truth the thought of ignorance saying is there anything for us to have done in this matter say indeed the matter belongs completely to allah they conceal within themselves what they will not reveal to you they say if there was anything we could have done in the matter some of us would not have been killed right here say even if you had been inside your houses those decreed to be killed would have come out to their death beds it was so that allah might test what is in your breasts and purify what is in your hearts and allah is knowing of that within the breasts

ayahs.t: Indeed, those of you who turned back on the day the two armies met, it was Satan who caused them to slip because of some [blame] they had earned. But Allah has already forgiven them. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Forbearing.
ayahs.n: indeed those of you who turned back on the day the two armies met it was satan who caused them to slip because of some blame they had earned but allah has already forgiven them indeed allah is forgiving and forbearing

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, do not be like those who disbelieved and said about their brothers when they traveled through the land or went out to fight, "If they had been with us, they would not have died or have been killed," so Allah makes that [misconception] a regret within their hearts. And it is Allah who gives life and causes death, and Allah is Seeing of what you do.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed do not be like those who disbelieved and said about their brothers when they traveled through the land or went out to fight if they had been with us they would not have died or have been killed so allah makes that misconception a regret within their hearts and it is allah who gives life and causes death and allah is seeing of what you do

ayahs.t: And if you are killed in the cause of Allah or die - then forgiveness from Allah and mercy are better than whatever they accumulate [in this world].
ayahs.n: and if you are killed in the cause of allah or die then forgiveness from allah and mercy are better than whatever they accumulate in this world

ayahs.t: And whether you die or are killed, unto Allah you will be gathered.
ayahs.n: and whether you die or are killed unto allah you will be gathered

ayahs.t: So by mercy from Allah, [O Muhammad], you were lenient with them. And if you had been rude [in speech] and harsh in heart, they would have disbanded from about you. So pardon them and ask forgiveness for them and consult them in the matter. And when you have decided, then rely upon Allah. Indeed, Allah loves those who rely [upon Him].
ayahs.n: so by mercy from allah o muhammad you were lenient with them and if you had been rude in speech and harsh in heart they would have disbanded from about you so pardon them and ask forgiveness for them and consult them in the matter and when you have decided then rely upon allah indeed allah loves those who rely upon him

ayahs.t: If Allah should aid you, no one can overcome you; but if He should forsake you, who is there that can aid you after Him? And upon Allah let the believers rely.
ayahs.n: if allah should aid you no one can overcome you but if he should forsake you who is there that can aid you after him and upon allah let the believers rely

ayahs.t: It is not [attributable] to any prophet that he would act unfaithfully [in regard to war booty]. And whoever betrays, [taking unlawfully], will come with what he took on the Day of Resurrection. Then will every soul be [fully] compensated for what it earned, and they will not be wronged.
ayahs.n: it is not attributable to any prophet that he would act unfaithfully in regard to war booty and whoever betrays taking unlawfully will come with what he took on the day of resurrection then will every soul be fully compensated for what it earned and they will not be wronged

ayahs.t: So is one who pursues the pleasure of Allah like one who brings upon himself the anger of Allah and whose refuge is Hell? And wretched is the destination.
ayahs.n: so is one who pursues the pleasure of allah like one who brings upon himself the anger of allah and whose refuge is hell and wretched is the destination

ayahs.t: They are [varying] degrees in the sight of Allah, and Allah is Seeing of whatever they do.
ayahs.n: they are varying degrees in the sight of allah and allah is seeing of whatever they do

ayahs.t: Certainly did Allah confer [great] favor upon the believers when He sent among them a Messenger from themselves, reciting to them His verses and purifying them and teaching them the Book and wisdom, although they had been before in manifest error.
ayahs.n: certainly did allah confer great favor upon the believers when he sent among them a messenger from themselves reciting to them his verses and purifying them and teaching them the book and wisdom although they had been before in manifest error

ayahs.t: Why [is it that] when a [single] disaster struck you [on the day of Uhud], although you had struck [the enemy in the battle of Badr] with one twice as great, you said, "From where is this?" Say, "It is from yourselves." Indeed, Allah is over all things competent.
ayahs.n: why is it that when a single disaster struck you on the day of uhud although you had struck the enemy in the battle of badr with one twice as great you said from where is this say it is from yourselves indeed allah is over all things competent

ayahs.t: And what struck you on the day the two armies met was by permission of Allah that He might make evident the [true] believers.
ayahs.n: and what struck you on the day the two armies met was by permission of allah that he might make evident the true believers

ayahs.t: And that He might make evident those who are hypocrites. For it was said to them, "Come, fight in the way of Allah or [at least] defend." They said, "If we had known [there would be] fighting, we would have followed you." They were nearer to disbelief that day than to faith, saying with their mouths what was not in their hearts. And Allah is most Knowing of what they conceal -
ayahs.n: and that he might make evident those who are hypocrites for it was said to them come fight in the way of allah or at least defend they said if we had known there would be fighting we would have followed you they were nearer to disbelief that day than to faith saying with their mouths what was not in their hearts and allah is most knowing of what they conceal

ayahs.t: Those who said about their brothers while sitting [at home], "If they had obeyed us, they would not have been killed." Say, "Then prevent death from yourselves, if you should be truthful."
ayahs.n: those who said about their brothers while sitting at home if they had obeyed us they would not have been killed say then prevent death from yourselves if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: And never think of those who have been killed in the cause of Allah as dead. Rather, they are alive with their Lord, receiving provision,
ayahs.n: and never think of those who have been killed in the cause of allah as dead rather they are alive with their lord receiving provision

ayahs.t: Rejoicing in what Allah has bestowed upon them of His bounty, and they receive good tidings about those [to be martyred] after them who have not yet joined them - that there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve.
ayahs.n: rejoicing in what allah has bestowed upon them of his bounty and they receive good tidings about those to be martyred after them who have not yet joined them that there will be no fear concerning them nor will they grieve

ayahs.t: They receive good tidings of favor from Allah and bounty and [of the fact] that Allah does not allow the reward of believers to be lost -
ayahs.n: they receive good tidings of favor from allah and bounty and of the fact that allah does not allow the reward of believers to be lost

ayahs.t: Those [believers] who responded to Allah and the Messenger after injury had struck them. For those who did good among them and feared Allah is a great reward -
ayahs.n: those believers who responded to allah and the messenger after injury had struck them for those who did good among them and feared allah is a great reward

ayahs.t: Those to whom hypocrites said, "Indeed, the people have gathered against you, so fear them." But it [merely] increased them in faith, and they said, "Sufficient for us is Allah, and [He is] the best Disposer of affairs."
ayahs.n: those to whom hypocrites said indeed the people have gathered against you so fear them but it merely increased them in faith and they said sufficient for us is allah and he is the best disposer of affairs

ayahs.t: So they returned with favor from Allah and bounty, no harm having touched them. And they pursued the pleasure of Allah, and Allah is the possessor of great bounty.
ayahs.n: so they returned with favor from allah and bounty no harm having touched them and they pursued the pleasure of allah and allah is the possessor of great bounty

ayahs.t: That is only Satan who frightens [you] of his supporters. So fear them not, but fear Me, if you are [indeed] believers.
ayahs.n: that is only satan who frightens you of his supporters so fear them not but fear me if you are indeed believers

ayahs.t: And do not be grieved, [O Muhammad], by those who hasten into disbelief. Indeed, they will never harm Allah at all. Allah intends that He should give them no share in the Hereafter, and for them is a great punishment.
ayahs.n: and do not be grieved o muhammad by those who hasten into disbelief indeed they will never harm allah at all allah intends that he should give them no share in the hereafter and for them is a great punishment

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who purchase disbelief [in exchange] for faith - never will they harm Allah at all, and for them is a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: indeed those who purchase disbelief in exchange for faith never will they harm allah at all and for them is a painful punishment

ayahs.t: And let not those who disbelieve ever think that [because] We extend their time [of enjoyment] it is better for them. We only extend it for them so that they may increase in sin, and for them is a humiliating punishment.
ayahs.n: and let not those who disbelieve ever think that because we extend their time of enjoyment it is better for them we only extend it for them so that they may increase in sin and for them is a humiliating punishment

ayahs.t: Allah would not leave the believers in that [state] you are in [presently] until He separates the evil from the good. Nor would Allah reveal to you the unseen. But [instead], Allah chooses of His messengers whom He wills, so believe in Allah and His messengers. And if you believe and fear Him, then for you is a great reward.
ayahs.n: allah would not leave the believers in that state you are in presently until he separates the evil from the good nor would allah reveal to you the unseen but instead allah chooses of his messengers whom he wills so believe in allah and his messengers and if you believe and fear him then for you is a great reward

ayahs.t: And let not those who [greedily] withhold what Allah has given them of His bounty ever think that it is better for them. Rather, it is worse for them. Their necks will be encircled by what they withheld on the Day of Resurrection. And to Allah belongs the heritage of the heavens and the earth. And Allah, with what you do, is [fully] Acquainted.
ayahs.n: and let not those who greedily withhold what allah has given them of his bounty ever think that it is better for them rather it is worse for them their necks will be encircled by what they withheld on the day of resurrection and to allah belongs the heritage of the heavens and the earth and allah with what you do is fully acquainted

ayahs.t: Allah has certainly heard the statement of those [Jews] who said, "Indeed, Allah is poor, while we are rich." We will record what they said and their killing of the prophets without right and will say, "Taste the punishment of the Burning Fire.
ayahs.n: allah has certainly heard the statement of those jews who said indeed allah is poor while we are rich we will record what they said and their killing of the prophets without right and will say taste the punishment of the burning fire

ayahs.t: That is for what your hands have put forth and because Allah is not ever unjust to [His] servants."
ayahs.n: that is for what your hands have put forth and because allah is not ever unjust to his servants

ayahs.t: [They are] those who said, "Indeed, Allah has taken our promise not to believe any messenger until he brings us an offering which fire [from heaven] will consume." Say, "There have already come to you messengers before me with clear proofs and [even] that of which you speak. So why did you kill them, if you should be truthful?"
ayahs.n: they are those who said indeed allah has taken our promise not to believe any messenger until he brings us an offering which fire from heaven will consume say there have already come to you messengers before me with clear proofs and even that of which you speak so why did you kill them if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: Then if they deny you, [O Muhammad] - so were messengers denied before you, who brought clear proofs and written ordinances and the enlightening Scripture.
ayahs.n: then if they deny you o muhammad so were messengers denied before you who brought clear proofs and written ordinances and the enlightening scripture

ayahs.t: Every soul will taste death, and you will only be given your [full] compensation on the Day of Resurrection. So he who is drawn away from the Fire and admitted to Paradise has attained [his desire]. And what is the life of this world except the enjoyment of delusion.
ayahs.n: every soul will taste death and you will only be given your full compensation on the day of resurrection so he who is drawn away from the fire and admitted to paradise has attained his desire and what is the life of this world except the enjoyment of delusion

ayahs.t: You will surely be tested in your possessions and in yourselves. And you will surely hear from those who were given the Scripture before you and from those who associate others with Allah much abuse. But if you are patient and fear Allah - indeed, that is of the matters [worthy] of determination.
ayahs.n: you will surely be tested in your possessions and in yourselves and you will surely hear from those who were given the scripture before you and from those who associate others with allah much abuse but if you are patient and fear allah indeed that is of the matters worthy of determination

ayahs.t: And [mention, O Muhammad], when Allah took a covenant from those who were given the Scripture, [saying], "You must make it clear to the people and not conceal it." But they threw it away behind their backs and exchanged it for a small price. And wretched is that which they purchased.
ayahs.n: and mention o muhammad when allah took a covenant from those who were given the scripture saying you must make it clear to the people and not conceal it but they threw it away behind their backs and exchanged it for a small price and wretched is that which they purchased

ayahs.t: And never think that those who rejoice in what they have perpetrated and like to be praised for what they did not do - never think them [to be] in safety from the punishment, and for them is a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: and never think that those who rejoice in what they have perpetrated and like to be praised for what they did not do never think them to be in safety from the punishment and for them is a painful punishment

ayahs.t: And to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is over all things competent.
ayahs.n: and to allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and allah is over all things competent

ayahs.t: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of the night and the day are signs for those of understanding.
ayahs.n: indeed in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of the night and the day are signs for those of understanding

ayahs.t: Who remember Allah while standing or sitting or [lying] on their sides and give thought to the creation of the heavens and the earth, [saying], "Our Lord, You did not create this aimlessly; exalted are You [above such a thing]; then protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
ayahs.n: who remember allah while standing or sitting or lying on their sides and give thought to the creation of the heavens and the earth saying our lord you did not create this aimlessly exalted are you above such a thing then protect us from the punishment of the fire

ayahs.t: Our Lord, indeed whoever You admit to the Fire - You have disgraced him, and for the wrongdoers there are no helpers.
ayahs.n: our lord indeed whoever you admit to the fire you have disgraced him and for the wrongdoers there are no helpers

ayahs.t: Our Lord, indeed we have heard a caller calling to faith, [saying], 'Believe in your Lord,' and we have believed. Our Lord, so forgive us our sins and remove from us our misdeeds and cause us to die with the righteous.
ayahs.n: our lord indeed we have heard a caller calling to faith saying believe in your lord and we have believed our lord so forgive us our sins and remove from us our misdeeds and cause us to die with the righteous

ayahs.t: Our Lord, and grant us what You promised us through Your messengers and do not disgrace us on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed, You do not fail in [Your] promise."
ayahs.n: our lord and grant us what you promised us through your messengers and do not disgrace us on the day of resurrection indeed you do not fail in your promise

ayahs.t: And their Lord responded to them, "Never will I allow to be lost the work of [any] worker among you, whether male or female; you are of one another. So those who emigrated or were evicted from their homes or were harmed in My cause or fought or were killed - I will surely remove from them their misdeeds, and I will surely admit them to gardens beneath which rivers flow as reward from Allah, and Allah has with Him the best reward."
ayahs.n: and their lord responded to them never will i allow to be lost the work of any worker among you whether male or female you are of one another so those who emigrated or were evicted from their homes or were harmed in my cause or fought or were killed i will surely remove from them their misdeeds and i will surely admit them to gardens beneath which rivers flow as reward from allah and allah has with him the best reward

ayahs.t: Be not deceived by the [uninhibited] movement of the disbelievers throughout the land.
ayahs.n: be not deceived by the uninhibited movement of the disbelievers throughout the land

ayahs.t: [It is but] a small enjoyment; then their [final] refuge is Hell, and wretched is the resting place.
ayahs.n: it is but a small enjoyment then their final refuge is hell and wretched is the resting place

ayahs.t: But those who feared their Lord will have gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding eternally therein, as accommodation from Allah. And that which is with Allah is best for the righteous.
ayahs.n: but those who feared their lord will have gardens beneath which rivers flow abiding eternally therein as accommodation from allah and that which is with allah is best for the righteous

ayahs.t: And indeed, among the People of the Scripture are those who believe in Allah and what was revealed to you and what was revealed to them, [being] humbly submissive to Allah. They do not exchange the verses of Allah for a small price. Those will have their reward with their Lord. Indeed, Allah is swift in account.
ayahs.n: and indeed among the people of the scripture are those who believe in allah and what was revealed to you and what was revealed to them being humbly submissive to allah they do not exchange the verses of allah for a small price those will have their reward with their lord indeed allah is swift in account

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, persevere and endure and remain stationed and fear Allah that you may be successful.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed persevere and endure and remain stationed and fear allah that you may be successful

ayahs.t: O mankind, fear your Lord, who created you from one soul and created from it its mate and dispersed from both of them many men and women. And fear Allah, through whom you ask one another, and the wombs. Indeed Allah is ever, over you, an Observer.
ayahs.n: o mankind fear your lord who created you from one soul and created from it its mate and dispersed from both of them many men and women and fear allah through whom you ask one another and the wombs indeed allah is ever over you an observer

ayahs.t: And give to the orphans their properties and do not substitute the defective [of your own] for the good [of theirs]. And do not consume their properties into your own. Indeed, that is ever a great sin.
ayahs.n: and give to the orphans their properties and do not substitute the defective of your own for the good of theirs and do not consume their properties into your own indeed that is ever a great sin

ayahs.t: And if you fear that you will not deal justly with the orphan girls, then marry those that please you of [other] women, two or three or four. But if you fear that you will not be just, then [marry only] one or those your right hand possesses. That is more suitable that you may not incline [to injustice].
ayahs.n: and if you fear that you will not deal justly with the orphan girls then marry those that please you of other women two or three or four but if you fear that you will not be just then marry only one or those your right hand possesses that is more suitable that you may not incline to injustice

ayahs.t: And give the women [upon marriage] their [bridal] gifts graciously. But if they give up willingly to you anything of it, then take it in satisfaction and ease.
ayahs.n: and give the women upon marriage their bridal gifts graciously but if they give up willingly to you anything of it then take it in satisfaction and ease

ayahs.t: And do not give the weak-minded your property, which Allah has made a means of sustenance for you, but provide for them with it and clothe them and speak to them words of appropriate kindness.
ayahs.n: and do not give the weak minded your property which allah has made a means of sustenance for you but provide for them with it and clothe them and speak to them words of appropriate kindness

ayahs.t: And test the orphans [in their abilities] until they reach marriageable age. Then if you perceive in them sound judgement, release their property to them. And do not consume it excessively and quickly, [anticipating] that they will grow up. And whoever, [when acting as guardian], is self-sufficient should refrain [from taking a fee]; and whoever is poor - let him take according to what is acceptable. Then when you release their property to them, bring witnesses upon them. And sufficient is Allah as Accountant.
ayahs.n: and test the orphans in their abilities until they reach marriageable age then if you perceive in them sound judgement release their property to them and do not consume it excessively and quickly anticipating that they will grow up and whoever when acting as guardian is self sufficient should refrain from taking a fee and whoever is poor let him take according to what is acceptable then when you release their property to them bring witnesses upon them and sufficient is allah as accountant

ayahs.t: For men is a share of what the parents and close relatives leave, and for women is a share of what the parents and close relatives leave, be it little or much - an obligatory share.
ayahs.n: for men is a share of what the parents and close relatives leave and for women is a share of what the parents and close relatives leave be it little or much an obligatory share

ayahs.t: And when [other] relatives and orphans and the needy are present at the [time of] division, then provide for them [something] out of the estate and speak to them words of appropriate kindness.
ayahs.n: and when other relatives and orphans and the needy are present at the time of division then provide for them something out of the estate and speak to them words of appropriate kindness

ayahs.t: And let those [executors and guardians] fear [injustice] as if they [themselves] had left weak offspring behind and feared for them. So let them fear Allah and speak words of appropriate justice.
ayahs.n: and let those executors and guardians fear injustice as if they themselves had left weak offspring behind and feared for them so let them fear allah and speak words of appropriate justice

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who devour the property of orphans unjustly are only consuming into their bellies fire. And they will be burned in a Blaze.
ayahs.n: indeed those who devour the property of orphans unjustly are only consuming into their bellies fire and they will be burned in a blaze

ayahs.t: Allah instructs you concerning your children: for the male, what is equal to the share of two females. But if there are [only] daughters, two or more, for them is two thirds of one's estate. And if there is only one, for her is half. And for one's parents, to each one of them is a sixth of his estate if he left children. But if he had no children and the parents [alone] inherit from him, then for his mother is one third. And if he had brothers [or sisters], for his mother is a sixth, after any bequest he [may have] made or debt. Your parents or your children - you know not which of them are nearest to you in benefit. [These shares are] an obligation [imposed] by Allah. Indeed, Allah is ever Knowing and Wise.
ayahs.n: allah instructs you concerning your children for the male what is equal to the share of two females but if there are only daughters two or more for them is two thirds of ones estate and if there is only one for her is half and for ones parents to each one of them is a sixth of his estate if he left children but if he had no children and the parents alone inherit from him then for his mother is one third and if he had brothers or sisters for his mother is a sixth after any bequest he may have made or debt your parents or your children you know not which of them are nearest to you in benefit these shares are an obligation imposed by allah indeed allah is ever knowing and wise

ayahs.t: And for you is half of what your wives leave if they have no child. But if they have a child, for you is one fourth of what they leave, after any bequest they [may have] made or debt. And for the wives is one fourth if you leave no child. But if you leave a child, then for them is an eighth of what you leave, after any bequest you [may have] made or debt. And if a man or woman leaves neither ascendants nor descendants but has a brother or a sister, then for each one of them is a sixth. But if they are more than two, they share a third, after any bequest which was made or debt, as long as there is no detriment [caused]. [This is] an ordinance from Allah, and Allah is Knowing and Forbearing.
ayahs.n: and for you is half of what your wives leave if they have no child but if they have a child for you is one fourth of what they leave after any bequest they may have made or debt and for the wives is one fourth if you leave no child but if you leave a child then for them is an eighth of what you leave after any bequest you may have made or debt and if a man or woman leaves neither ascendants nor descendants but has a brother or a sister then for each one of them is a sixth but if they are more than two they share a third after any bequest which was made or debt as long as there is no detriment caused this is an ordinance from allah and allah is knowing and forbearing

ayahs.t: These are the limits [set by] Allah, and whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger will be admitted by Him to gardens [in Paradise] under which rivers flow, abiding eternally therein; and that is the great attainment.
ayahs.n: these are the limits set by allah and whoever obeys allah and his messenger will be admitted by him to gardens in paradise under which rivers flow abiding eternally therein and that is the great attainment

ayahs.t: And whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger and transgresses His limits - He will put him into the Fire to abide eternally therein, and he will have a humiliating punishment.
ayahs.n: and whoever disobeys allah and his messenger and transgresses his limits he will put him into the fire to abide eternally therein and he will have a humiliating punishment

ayahs.t: Those who commit unlawful sexual intercourse of your women - bring against them four [witnesses] from among you. And if they testify, confine the guilty women to houses until death takes them or Allah ordains for them [another] way.
ayahs.n: those who commit unlawful sexual intercourse of your women bring against them four witnesses from among you and if they testify confine the guilty women to houses until death takes them or allah ordains for them another way

ayahs.t: And the two who commit it among you, dishonor them both. But if they repent and correct themselves, leave them alone. Indeed, Allah is ever Accepting of repentance and Merciful.
ayahs.n: and the two who commit it among you dishonor them both but if they repent and correct themselves leave them alone indeed allah is ever accepting of repentance and merciful

ayahs.t: The repentance accepted by Allah is only for those who do wrong in ignorance [or carelessness] and then repent soon after. It is those to whom Allah will turn in forgiveness, and Allah is ever Knowing and Wise.
ayahs.n: the repentance accepted by allah is only for those who do wrong in ignorance or carelessness and then repent soon after it is those to whom allah will turn in forgiveness and allah is ever knowing and wise

ayahs.t: But repentance is not [accepted] of those who [continue to] do evil deeds up until, when death comes to one of them, he says, "Indeed, I have repented now," or of those who die while they are disbelievers. For them We have prepared a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: but repentance is not accepted of those who continue to do evil deeds up until when death comes to one of them he says indeed i have repented now or of those who die while they are disbelievers for them we have prepared a painful punishment

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, it is not lawful for you to inherit women by compulsion. And do not make difficulties for them in order to take [back] part of what you gave them unless they commit a clear immorality. And live with them in kindness. For if you dislike them - perhaps you dislike a thing and Allah makes therein much good.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed it is not lawful for you to inherit women by compulsion and do not make difficulties for them in order to take back part of what you gave them unless they commit a clear immorality and live with them in kindness for if you dislike them perhaps you dislike a thing and allah makes therein much good

ayahs.t: But if you want to replace one wife with another and you have given one of them a great amount [in gifts], do not take [back] from it anything. Would you take it in injustice and manifest sin?
ayahs.n: but if you want to replace one wife with another and you have given one of them a great amount in gifts do not take back from it anything would you take it in injustice and manifest sin

ayahs.t: And how could you take it while you have gone in unto each other and they have taken from you a solemn covenant?
ayahs.n: and how could you take it while you have gone in unto each other and they have taken from you a solemn covenant

ayahs.t: And do not marry those [women] whom your fathers married, except what has already occurred. Indeed, it was an immorality and hateful [to Allah] and was evil as a way.
ayahs.n: and do not marry those women whom your fathers married except what has already occurred indeed it was an immorality and hateful to allah and was evil as a way

ayahs.t: Prohibited to you [for marriage] are your mothers, your daughters, your sisters, your father's sisters, your mother's sisters, your brother's daughters, your sister's daughters, your [milk] mothers who nursed you, your sisters through nursing, your wives' mothers, and your step-daughters under your guardianship [born] of your wives unto whom you have gone in. But if you have not gone in unto them, there is no sin upon you. And [also prohibited are] the wives of your sons who are from your [own] loins, and that you take [in marriage] two sisters simultaneously, except for what has already occurred. Indeed, Allah is ever Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: prohibited to you for marriage are your mothers your daughters your sisters your fathers sisters your mothers sisters your brothers daughters your sisters daughters your milk mothers who nursed you your sisters through nursing your wives mothers and your step daughters under your guardianship born of your wives unto whom you have gone in but if you have not gone in unto them there is no sin upon you and also prohibited are the wives of your sons who are from your own loins and that you take in marriage two sisters simultaneously except for what has already occurred indeed allah is ever forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: And [also prohibited to you are all] married women except those your right hands possess. [This is] the decree of Allah upon you. And lawful to you are [all others] beyond these, [provided] that you seek them [in marriage] with [gifts from] your property, desiring chastity, not unlawful sexual intercourse. So for whatever you enjoy [of marriage] from them, give them their due compensation as an obligation. And there is no blame upon you for what you mutually agree to beyond the obligation. Indeed, Allah is ever Knowing and Wise.
ayahs.n: and also prohibited to you are all married women except those your right hands possess this is the decree of allah upon you and lawful to you are all others beyond these provided that you seek them in marriage with gifts from your property desiring chastity not unlawful sexual intercourse so for whatever you enjoy of marriage from them give them their due compensation as an obligation and there is no blame upon you for what you mutually agree to beyond the obligation indeed allah is ever knowing and wise

ayahs.t: And whoever among you cannot [find] the means to marry free, believing women, then [he may marry] from those whom your right hands possess of believing slave girls. And Allah is most knowing about your faith. You [believers] are of one another. So marry them with the permission of their people and give them their due compensation according to what is acceptable. [They should be] chaste, neither [of] those who commit unlawful intercourse randomly nor those who take [secret] lovers. But once they are sheltered in marriage, if they should commit adultery, then for them is half the punishment for free [unmarried] women. This [allowance] is for him among you who fears sin, but to be patient is better for you. And Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: and whoever among you cannot find the means to marry free believing women then he may marry from those whom your right hands possess of believing slave girls and allah is most knowing about your faith you believers are of one another so marry them with the permission of their people and give them their due compensation according to what is acceptable they should be chaste neither of those who commit unlawful intercourse randomly nor those who take secret lovers but once they are sheltered in marriage if they should commit adultery then for them is half the punishment for free unmarried women this allowance is for him among you who fears sin but to be patient is better for you and allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: Allah wants to make clear to you [the lawful from the unlawful] and guide you to the [good] practices of those before you and to accept your repentance. And Allah is Knowing and Wise.
ayahs.n: allah wants to make clear to you the lawful from the unlawful and guide you to the good practices of those before you and to accept your repentance and allah is knowing and wise

ayahs.t: Allah wants to accept your repentance, but those who follow [their] passions want you to digress [into] a great deviation.
ayahs.n: allah wants to accept your repentance but those who follow their passions want you to digress into a great deviation

ayahs.t: And Allah wants to lighten for you [your difficulties]; and mankind was created weak.
ayahs.n: and allah wants to lighten for you your difficulties and mankind was created weak

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, do not consume one another's wealth unjustly but only [in lawful] business by mutual consent. And do not kill yourselves [or one another]. Indeed, Allah is to you ever Merciful.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed do not consume one anothers wealth unjustly but only in lawful business by mutual consent and do not kill yourselves or one another indeed allah is to you ever merciful

ayahs.t: And whoever does that in aggression and injustice - then We will drive him into a Fire. And that, for Allah, is [always] easy.
ayahs.n: and whoever does that in aggression and injustice then we will drive him into a fire and that for allah is always easy

ayahs.t: If you avoid the major sins which you are forbidden, We will remove from you your lesser sins and admit you to a noble entrance [into Paradise].
ayahs.n: if you avoid the major sins which you are forbidden we will remove from you your lesser sins and admit you to a noble entrance into paradise

ayahs.t: And do not wish for that by which Allah has made some of you exceed others. For men is a share of what they have earned, and for women is a share of what they have earned. And ask Allah of his bounty. Indeed Allah is ever, of all things, Knowing.
ayahs.n: and do not wish for that by which allah has made some of you exceed others for men is a share of what they have earned and for women is a share of what they have earned and ask allah of his bounty indeed allah is ever of all things knowing

ayahs.t: And for all, We have made heirs to what is left by parents and relatives. And to those whom your oaths have bound [to you] - give them their share. Indeed Allah is ever, over all things, a Witness.
ayahs.n: and for all we have made heirs to what is left by parents and relatives and to those whom your oaths have bound to you give them their share indeed allah is ever over all things a witness

ayahs.t: Men are in charge of women by [right of] what Allah has given one over the other and what they spend [for maintenance] from their wealth. So righteous women are devoutly obedient, guarding in [the husband's] absence what Allah would have them guard. But those [wives] from whom you fear arrogance - [first] advise them; [then if they persist], forsake them in bed; and [finally], strike them. But if they obey you [once more], seek no means against them. Indeed, Allah is ever Exalted and Grand.
ayahs.n: men are in charge of women by right of what allah has given one over the other and what they spend for maintenance from their wealth so righteous women are devoutly obedient guarding in the husbands absence what allah would have them guard but those wives from whom you fear arrogance first advise them then if they persist forsake them in bed and finally strike them but if they obey you once more seek no means against them indeed allah is ever exalted and grand

ayahs.t: And if you fear dissension between the two, send an arbitrator from his people and an arbitrator from her people. If they both desire reconciliation, Allah will cause it between them. Indeed, Allah is ever Knowing and Acquainted [with all things].
ayahs.n: and if you fear dissension between the two send an arbitrator from his people and an arbitrator from her people if they both desire reconciliation allah will cause it between them indeed allah is ever knowing and acquainted with all things

ayahs.t: Worship Allah and associate nothing with Him, and to parents do good, and to relatives, orphans, the needy, the near neighbor, the neighbor farther away, the companion at your side, the traveler, and those whom your right hands possess. Indeed, Allah does not like those who are self-deluding and boastful.
ayahs.n: worship allah and associate nothing with him and to parents do good and to relatives orphans the needy the near neighbor the neighbor farther away the companion at your side the traveler and those whom your right hands possess indeed allah does not like those who are self deluding and boastful

ayahs.t: Who are stingy and enjoin upon [other] people stinginess and conceal what Allah has given them of His bounty - and We have prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment -
