ayahs.n: and when the punishment descended upon them they said o moses invoke for us your lord by what he has promised you if you can remove the punishment from us we will surely believe you and we will send with you the children of israel

ayahs.t: But when We removed the punishment from them until a term which they were to reach, then at once they broke their word.
ayahs.n: but when we removed the punishment from them until a term which they were to reach then at once they broke their word

ayahs.t: So We took retribution from them, and We drowned them in the sea because they denied Our signs and were heedless of them.
ayahs.n: so we took retribution from them and we drowned them in the sea because they denied our signs and were heedless of them

ayahs.t: And We caused the people who had been oppressed to inherit the eastern regions of the land and the western ones, which We had blessed. And the good word of your Lord was fulfilled for the Children of Israel because of what they had patiently endured. And We destroyed [all] that Pharaoh and his people were producing and what they had been building.
ayahs.n: and we caused the people who had been oppressed to inherit the eastern regions of the land and the western ones which we had blessed and the good word of your lord was fulfilled for the children of israel because of what they had patiently endured and we destroyed all that pharaoh and his people were producing and what they had been building

ayahs.t: And We took the Children of Israel across the sea; then they came upon a people intent in devotion to [some] idols of theirs. They said, "O Moses, make for us a god just as they have gods." He said, "Indeed, you are a people behaving ignorantly.
ayahs.n: and we took the children of israel across the sea then they came upon a people intent in devotion to some idols of theirs they said o moses make for us a god just as they have gods he said indeed you are a people behaving ignorantly

ayahs.t: Indeed, those [worshippers] - destroyed is that in which they are [engaged], and worthless is whatever they were doing."
ayahs.n: indeed those worshippers destroyed is that in which they are engaged and worthless is whatever they were doing

ayahs.t: He said, "Is it other than Allah I should desire for you as a god while He has preferred you over the worlds?"
ayahs.n: he said is it other than allah i should desire for you as a god while he has preferred you over the worlds

ayahs.t: And [recall, O Children of Israel], when We saved you from the people of Pharaoh, [who were] afflicting you with the worst torment - killing your sons and keeping your women alive. And in that was a great trial from your Lord.
ayahs.n: and recall o children of israel when we saved you from the people of pharaoh who were afflicting you with the worst torment killing your sons and keeping your women alive and in that was a great trial from your lord

ayahs.t: And We made an appointment with Moses for thirty nights and perfected them by [the addition of] ten; so the term of his Lord was completed as forty nights. And Moses said to his brother Aaron, "Take my place among my people, do right [by them], and do not follow the way of the corrupters."
ayahs.n: and we made an appointment with moses for thirty nights and perfected them by the addition of ten so the term of his lord was completed as forty nights and moses said to his brother aaron take my place among my people do right by them and do not follow the way of the corrupters

ayahs.t: And when Moses arrived at Our appointed time and his Lord spoke to him, he said, "My Lord, show me [Yourself] that I may look at You." [Allah] said, "You will not see Me, but look at the mountain; if it should remain in place, then you will see Me." But when his Lord appeared to the mountain, He rendered it level, and Moses fell unconscious. And when he awoke, he said, "Exalted are You! I have repented to You, and I am the first of the believers."
ayahs.n: and when moses arrived at our appointed time and his lord spoke to him he said my lord show me yourself that i may look at you allah said you will not see me but look at the mountain if it should remain in place then you will see me but when his lord appeared to the mountain he rendered it level and moses fell unconscious and when he awoke he said exalted are you i have repented to you and i am the first of the believers

ayahs.t: [Allah] said, "O Moses, I have chosen you over the people with My messages and My words [to you]. So take what I have given you and be among the grateful."
ayahs.n: allah said o moses i have chosen you over the people with my messages and my words to you so take what i have given you and be among the grateful

ayahs.t: And We wrote for him on the tablets [something] of all things - instruction and explanation for all things, [saying], "Take them with determination and order your people to take the best of it. I will show you the home of the defiantly disobedient."
ayahs.n: and we wrote for him on the tablets something of all things instruction and explanation for all things saying take them with determination and order your people to take the best of it i will show you the home of the defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: I will turn away from My signs those who are arrogant upon the earth without right; and if they should see every sign, they will not believe in it. And if they see the way of consciousness, they will not adopt it as a way; but if they see the way of error, they will adopt it as a way. That is because they have denied Our signs and they were heedless of them.
ayahs.n: i will turn away from my signs those who are arrogant upon the earth without right and if they should see every sign they will not believe in it and if they see the way of consciousness they will not adopt it as a way but if they see the way of error they will adopt it as a way that is because they have denied our signs and they were heedless of them

ayahs.t: Those who denied Our signs and the meeting of the Hereafter - their deeds have become worthless. Are they recompensed except for what they used to do?
ayahs.n: those who denied our signs and the meeting of the hereafter their deeds have become worthless are they recompensed except for what they used to do

ayahs.t: And the people of Moses made, after [his departure], from their ornaments a calf - an image having a lowing sound. Did they not see that it could neither speak to them nor guide them to a way? They took it [for worship], and they were wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: and the people of moses made after his departure from their ornaments a calf an image having a lowing sound did they not see that it could neither speak to them nor guide them to a way they took it for worship and they were wrongdoers

ayahs.t: And when regret overcame them and they saw that they had gone astray, they said, "If our Lord does not have mercy upon us and forgive us, we will surely be among the losers."
ayahs.n: and when regret overcame them and they saw that they had gone astray they said if our lord does not have mercy upon us and forgive us we will surely be among the losers

ayahs.t: And when Moses returned to his people, angry and grieved, he said, "How wretched is that by which you have replaced me after [my departure]. Were you impatient over the matter of your Lord?" And he threw down the tablets and seized his brother by [the hair of] his head, pulling him toward him. [Aaron] said, "O son of my mother, indeed the people oppressed me and were about to kill me, so let not the enemies rejoice over me and do not place me among the wrongdoing people."
ayahs.n: and when moses returned to his people angry and grieved he said how wretched is that by which you have replaced me after my departure were you impatient over the matter of your lord and he threw down the tablets and seized his brother by the hair of his head pulling him toward him aaron said o son of my mother indeed the people oppressed me and were about to kill me so let not the enemies rejoice over me and do not place me among the wrongdoing people

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "My Lord, forgive me and my brother and admit us into Your mercy, for You are the most merciful of the merciful."
ayahs.n: moses said my lord forgive me and my brother and admit us into your mercy for you are the most merciful of the merciful

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who took the calf [for worship] will obtain anger from their Lord and humiliation in the life of this world, and thus do We recompense the inventors [of falsehood].
ayahs.n: indeed those who took the calf for worship will obtain anger from their lord and humiliation in the life of this world and thus do we recompense the inventors of falsehood

ayahs.t: But those who committed misdeeds and then repented after them and believed - indeed your Lord, thereafter, is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: but those who committed misdeeds and then repented after them and believed indeed your lord thereafter is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: And when the anger subsided in Moses, he took up the tablets; and in their inscription was guidance and mercy for those who are fearful of their Lord.
ayahs.n: and when the anger subsided in moses he took up the tablets and in their inscription was guidance and mercy for those who are fearful of their lord

ayahs.t: And Moses chose from his people seventy men for Our appointment. And when the earthquake seized them, he said, "My Lord, if You had willed, You could have destroyed them before and me [as well]. Would You destroy us for what the foolish among us have done? This is not but Your trial by which You send astray whom You will and guide whom You will. You are our Protector, so forgive us and have mercy upon us; and You are the best of forgivers.
ayahs.n: and moses chose from his people seventy men for our appointment and when the earthquake seized them he said my lord if you had willed you could have destroyed them before and me as well would you destroy us for what the foolish among us have done this is not but your trial by which you send astray whom you will and guide whom you will you are our protector so forgive us and have mercy upon us and you are the best of forgivers

ayahs.t: And decree for us in this world [that which is] good and [also] in the Hereafter; indeed, we have turned back to You." [Allah] said, "My punishment - I afflict with it whom I will, but My mercy encompasses all things." So I will decree it [especially] for those who fear Me and give zakah and those who believe in Our verses -
ayahs.n: and decree for us in this world that which is good and also in the hereafter indeed we have turned back to you allah said my punishment i afflict with it whom i will but my mercy encompasses all things so i will decree it especially for those who fear me and give zakah and those who believe in our verses

ayahs.t: Those who follow the Messenger, the unlettered prophet, whom they find written in what they have of the Torah and the Gospel, who enjoins upon them what is right and forbids them what is wrong and makes lawful for them the good things and prohibits for them the evil and relieves them of their burden and the shackles which were upon them. So they who have believed in him, honored him, supported him and followed the light which was sent down with him - it is those who will be the successful.
ayahs.n: those who follow the messenger the unlettered prophet whom they find written in what they have of the torah and the gospel who enjoins upon them what is right and forbids them what is wrong and makes lawful for them the good things and prohibits for them the evil and relieves them of their burden and the shackles which were upon them so they who have believed in him honored him supported him and followed the light which was sent down with him it is those who will be the successful

ayahs.t: Say, [O Muhammad], "O mankind, indeed I am the Messenger of Allah to you all, [from Him] to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. There is no deity except Him; He gives life and causes death." So believe in Allah and His Messenger, the unlettered prophet, who believes in Allah and His words, and follow him that you may be guided.
ayahs.n: say o muhammad o mankind indeed i am the messenger of allah to you all from him to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth there is no deity except him he gives life and causes death so believe in allah and his messenger the unlettered prophet who believes in allah and his words and follow him that you may be guided

ayahs.t: And among the people of Moses is a community which guides by truth and by it establishes justice.
ayahs.n: and among the people of moses is a community which guides by truth and by it establishes justice

ayahs.t: And We divided them into twelve descendant tribes [as distinct] nations. And We inspired to Moses when his people implored him for water, "Strike with your staff the stone," and there gushed forth from it twelve springs. Every people knew its watering place. And We shaded them with clouds and sent down upon them manna and quails, [saying], "Eat from the good things with which We have provided you." And they wronged Us not, but they were [only] wronging themselves.
ayahs.n: and we divided them into twelve descendant tribes as distinct nations and we inspired to moses when his people implored him for water strike with your staff the stone and there gushed forth from it twelve springs every people knew its watering place and we shaded them with clouds and sent down upon them manna and quails saying eat from the good things with which we have provided you and they wronged us not but they were only wronging themselves

ayahs.t: And [mention, O Muhammad], when it was said to them, "Dwell in this city and eat from it wherever you will and say, 'Relieve us of our burdens,' and enter the gate bowing humbly; We will [then] forgive you your sins. We will increase the doers of good [in goodness and reward]."
ayahs.n: and mention o muhammad when it was said to them dwell in this city and eat from it wherever you will and say relieve us of our burdens and enter the gate bowing humbly we will then forgive you your sins we will increase the doers of good in goodness and reward

ayahs.t: But those who wronged among them changed [the words] to a statement other than that which had been said to them. So We sent upon them a punishment from the sky for the wrong that they were doing.
ayahs.n: but those who wronged among them changed the words to a statement other than that which had been said to them so we sent upon them a punishment from the sky for the wrong that they were doing

ayahs.t: And ask them about the town that was by the sea - when they transgressed in [the matter of] the sabbath - when their fish came to them openly on their sabbath day, and the day they had no sabbath they did not come to them. Thus did We give them trial because they were defiantly disobedient.
ayahs.n: and ask them about the town that was by the sea when they transgressed in the matter of the sabbath when their fish came to them openly on their sabbath day and the day they had no sabbath they did not come to them thus did we give them trial because they were defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: And when a community among them said, "Why do you advise [or warn] a people whom Allah is [about] to destroy or to punish with a severe punishment?" they [the advisors] said, "To be absolved before your Lord and perhaps they may fear Him."
ayahs.n: and when a community among them said why do you advise or warn a people whom allah is about to destroy or to punish with a severe punishment they the advisors said to be absolved before your lord and perhaps they may fear him

ayahs.t: And when they forgot that by which they had been reminded, We saved those who had forbidden evil and seized those who wronged, with a wretched punishment, because they were defiantly disobeying.
ayahs.n: and when they forgot that by which they had been reminded we saved those who had forbidden evil and seized those who wronged with a wretched punishment because they were defiantly disobeying

ayahs.t: So when they were insolent about that which they had been forbidden, We said to them, "Be apes, despised."
ayahs.n: so when they were insolent about that which they had been forbidden we said to them be apes despised

ayahs.t: And [mention] when your Lord declared that He would surely [continue to] send upon them until the Day of Resurrection those who would afflict them with the worst torment. Indeed, your Lord is swift in penalty; but indeed, He is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: and mention when your lord declared that he would surely continue to send upon them until the day of resurrection those who would afflict them with the worst torment indeed your lord is swift in penalty but indeed he is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: And We divided them throughout the earth into nations. Of them some were righteous, and of them some were otherwise. And We tested them with good [times] and bad that perhaps they would return [to obedience].
ayahs.n: and we divided them throughout the earth into nations of them some were righteous and of them some were otherwise and we tested them with good times and bad that perhaps they would return to obedience

ayahs.t: And there followed them successors who inherited the Scripture [while] taking the commodities of this lower life and saying, "It will be forgiven for us." And if an offer like it comes to them, they will [again] take it. Was not the covenant of the Scripture taken from them that they would not say about Allah except the truth, and they studied what was in it? And the home of the Hereafter is better for those who fear Allah, so will you not use reason?
ayahs.n: and there followed them successors who inherited the scripture while taking the commodities of this lower life and saying it will be forgiven for us and if an offer like it comes to them they will again take it was not the covenant of the scripture taken from them that they would not say about allah except the truth and they studied what was in it and the home of the hereafter is better for those who fear allah so will you not use reason

ayahs.t: But those who hold fast to the Book and establish prayer - indeed, We will not allow to be lost the reward of the reformers.
ayahs.n: but those who hold fast to the book and establish prayer indeed we will not allow to be lost the reward of the reformers

ayahs.t: And [mention] when We raised the mountain above them as if it was a dark cloud and they were certain that it would fall upon them, [and Allah said], "Take what We have given you with determination and remember what is in it that you might fear Allah."
ayahs.n: and mention when we raised the mountain above them as if it was a dark cloud and they were certain that it would fall upon them and allah said take what we have given you with determination and remember what is in it that you might fear allah

ayahs.t: And [mention] when your Lord took from the children of Adam - from their loins - their descendants and made them testify of themselves, [saying to them], "Am I not your Lord?" They said, "Yes, we have testified." [This] - lest you should say on the day of Resurrection, "Indeed, we were of this unaware."
ayahs.n: and mention when your lord took from the children of adam from their loins their descendants and made them testify of themselves saying to them am i not your lord they said yes we have testified this lest you should say on the day of resurrection indeed we were of this unaware

ayahs.t: Or [lest] you say, "It was only that our fathers associated [others in worship] with Allah before, and we were but descendants after them. Then would You destroy us for what the falsifiers have done?"
ayahs.n: or lest you say it was only that our fathers associated others in worship with allah before and we were but descendants after them then would you destroy us for what the falsifiers have done

ayahs.t: And thus do We [explain in] detail the verses, and perhaps they will return.
ayahs.n: and thus do we explain in detail the verses and perhaps they will return

ayahs.t: And recite to them, [O Muhammad], the news of him to whom we gave [knowledge of] Our signs, but he detached himself from them; so Satan pursued him, and he became of the deviators.
ayahs.n: and recite to them o muhammad the news of him to whom we gave knowledge of our signs but he detached himself from them so satan pursued him and he became of the deviators

ayahs.t: And if We had willed, we could have elevated him thereby, but he adhered [instead] to the earth and followed his own desire. So his example is like that of the dog: if you chase him, he pants, or if you leave him, he [still] pants. That is the example of the people who denied Our signs. So relate the stories that perhaps they will give thought.
ayahs.n: and if we had willed we could have elevated him thereby but he adhered instead to the earth and followed his own desire so his example is like that of the dog if you chase him he pants or if you leave him he still pants that is the example of the people who denied our signs so relate the stories that perhaps they will give thought

ayahs.t: How evil an example [is that of] the people who denied Our signs and used to wrong themselves.
ayahs.n: how evil an example is that of the people who denied our signs and used to wrong themselves

ayahs.t: Whoever Allah guides - he is the [rightly] guided; and whoever He sends astray - it is those who are the losers.
ayahs.n: whoever allah guides he is the rightly guided and whoever he sends astray it is those who are the losers

ayahs.t: And We have certainly created for Hell many of the jinn and mankind. They have hearts with which they do not understand, they have eyes with which they do not see, and they have ears with which they do not hear. Those are like livestock; rather, they are more astray. It is they who are the heedless.
ayahs.n: and we have certainly created for hell many of the jinn and mankind they have hearts with which they do not understand they have eyes with which they do not see and they have ears with which they do not hear those are like livestock rather they are more astray it is they who are the heedless

ayahs.t: And to Allah belong the best names, so invoke Him by them. And leave [the company of] those who practice deviation concerning His names. They will be recompensed for what they have been doing.
ayahs.n: and to allah belong the best names so invoke him by them and leave the company of those who practice deviation concerning his names they will be recompensed for what they have been doing

ayahs.t: And among those We created is a community which guides by truth and thereby establishes justice.
ayahs.n: and among those we created is a community which guides by truth and thereby establishes justice

ayahs.t: But those who deny Our signs - We will progressively lead them [to destruction] from where they do not know.
ayahs.n: but those who deny our signs we will progressively lead them to destruction from where they do not know

ayahs.t: And I will give them time. Indeed, my plan is firm.
ayahs.n: and i will give them time indeed my plan is firm

ayahs.t: Then do they not give thought? There is in their companion [Muhammad] no madness. He is not but a clear warner.
ayahs.n: then do they not give thought there is in their companion muhammad no madness he is not but a clear warner

ayahs.t: Do they not look into the realm of the heavens and the earth and everything that Allah has created and [think] that perhaps their appointed time has come near? So in what statement hereafter will they believe?
ayahs.n: do they not look into the realm of the heavens and the earth and everything that allah has created and think that perhaps their appointed time has come near so in what statement hereafter will they believe

ayahs.t: Whoever Allah sends astray - there is no guide for him. And He leaves them in their transgression, wandering blindly.
ayahs.n: whoever allah sends astray there is no guide for him and he leaves them in their transgression wandering blindly

ayahs.t: They ask you, [O Muhammad], about the Hour: when is its arrival? Say, "Its knowledge is only with my Lord. None will reveal its time except Him. It lays heavily upon the heavens and the earth. It will not come upon you except unexpectedly." They ask you as if you are familiar with it. Say, "Its knowledge is only with Allah, but most of the people do not know."
ayahs.n: they ask you o muhammad about the hour when is its arrival say its knowledge is only with my lord none will reveal its time except him it lays heavily upon the heavens and the earth it will not come upon you except unexpectedly they ask you as if you are familiar with it say its knowledge is only with allah but most of the people do not know

ayahs.t: Say, "I hold not for myself [the power of] benefit or harm, except what Allah has willed. And if I knew the unseen, I could have acquired much wealth, and no harm would have touched me. I am not except a warner and a bringer of good tidings to a people who believe."
ayahs.n: say i hold not for myself the power of benefit or harm except what allah has willed and if i knew the unseen i could have acquired much wealth and no harm would have touched me i am not except a warner and a bringer of good tidings to a people who believe

ayahs.t: It is He who created you from one soul and created from it its mate that he might dwell in security with her. And when he covers her, she carries a light burden and continues therein. And when it becomes heavy, they both invoke Allah, their Lord, "If You should give us a good [child], we will surely be among the grateful."
ayahs.n: it is he who created you from one soul and created from it its mate that he might dwell in security with her and when he covers her she carries a light burden and continues therein and when it becomes heavy they both invoke allah their lord if you should give us a good child we will surely be among the grateful

ayahs.t: But when He gives them a good [child], they ascribe partners to Him concerning that which He has given them. Exalted is Allah above what they associate with Him.
ayahs.n: but when he gives them a good child they ascribe partners to him concerning that which he has given them exalted is allah above what they associate with him

ayahs.t: Do they associate with Him those who create nothing and they are [themselves] created?
ayahs.n: do they associate with him those who create nothing and they are themselves created

ayahs.t: And the false deities are unable to [give] them help, nor can they help themselves.
ayahs.n: and the false deities are unable to give them help nor can they help themselves

ayahs.t: And if you [believers] invite them to guidance, they will not follow you. It is all the same for you whether you invite them or you are silent.
ayahs.n: and if you believers invite them to guidance they will not follow you it is all the same for you whether you invite them or you are silent

ayahs.t: Indeed, those you [polytheists] call upon besides Allah are servants like you. So call upon them and let them respond to you, if you should be truthful.
ayahs.n: indeed those you polytheists call upon besides allah are servants like you so call upon them and let them respond to you if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: Do they have feet by which they walk? Or do they have hands by which they strike? Or do they have eyes by which they see? Or do they have ears by which they hear? Say, [O Muhammad], "Call your 'partners' and then conspire against me and give me no respite.
ayahs.n: do they have feet by which they walk or do they have hands by which they strike or do they have eyes by which they see or do they have ears by which they hear say o muhammad call your partners and then conspire against me and give me no respite

ayahs.t: Indeed, my protector is Allah, who has sent down the Book; and He is an ally to the righteous.
ayahs.n: indeed my protector is allah who has sent down the book and he is an ally to the righteous

ayahs.t: And those you call upon besides Him are unable to help you, nor can they help themselves."
ayahs.n: and those you call upon besides him are unable to help you nor can they help themselves

ayahs.t: And if you invite them to guidance, they do not hear; and you see them looking at you while they do not see.
ayahs.n: and if you invite them to guidance they do not hear and you see them looking at you while they do not see

ayahs.t: Take what is given freely, enjoin what is good, and turn away from the ignorant.
ayahs.n: take what is given freely enjoin what is good and turn away from the ignorant

ayahs.t: And if an evil suggestion comes to you from Satan, then seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, He is Hearing and Knowing.
ayahs.n: and if an evil suggestion comes to you from satan then seek refuge in allah indeed he is hearing and knowing

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who fear Allah - when an impulse touches them from Satan, they remember [Him] and at once they have insight.
ayahs.n: indeed those who fear allah when an impulse touches them from satan they remember him and at once they have insight

ayahs.t: But their brothers - the devils increase them in error; then they do not stop short.
ayahs.n: but their brothers the devils increase them in error then they do not stop short

ayahs.t: And when you, [O Muhammad], do not bring them a sign, they say, "Why have you not contrived it?" Say, "I only follow what is revealed to me from my Lord. This [Qur'an] is enlightenment from your Lord and guidance and mercy for a people who believe."
ayahs.n: and when you o muhammad do not bring them a sign they say why have you not contrived it say i only follow what is revealed to me from my lord this quran is enlightenment from your lord and guidance and mercy for a people who believe

ayahs.t: So when the Qur'an is recited, then listen to it and pay attention that you may receive mercy.
ayahs.n: so when the quran is recited then listen to it and pay attention that you may receive mercy

ayahs.t: And remember your Lord within yourself in humility and in fear without being apparent in speech - in the mornings and the evenings. And do not be among the heedless.
ayahs.n: and remember your lord within yourself in humility and in fear without being apparent in speech in the mornings and the evenings and do not be among the heedless

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who are near your Lord are not prevented by arrogance from His worship, and they exalt Him, and to Him they prostrate.
ayahs.n: indeed those who are near your lord are not prevented by arrogance from his worship and they exalt him and to him they prostrate

ayahs.t: They ask you, [O Muhammad], about the bounties [of war]. Say, "The [decision concerning] bounties is for Allah and the Messenger." So fear Allah and amend that which is between you and obey Allah and His Messenger, if you should be believers.
ayahs.n: they ask you o muhammad about the bounties of war say the decision concerning bounties is for allah and the messenger so fear allah and amend that which is between you and obey allah and his messenger if you should be believers

ayahs.t: The believers are only those who, when Allah is mentioned, their hearts become fearful, and when His verses are recited to them, it increases them in faith; and upon their Lord they rely -
ayahs.n: the believers are only those who when allah is mentioned their hearts become fearful and when his verses are recited to them it increases them in faith and upon their lord they rely

ayahs.t: The ones who establish prayer, and from what We have provided them, they spend.
ayahs.n: the ones who establish prayer and from what we have provided them they spend

ayahs.t: Those are the believers, truly. For them are degrees [of high position] with their Lord and forgiveness and noble provision.
ayahs.n: those are the believers truly for them are degrees of high position with their lord and forgiveness and noble provision

ayahs.t: [It is] just as when your Lord brought you out of your home [for the battle of Badr] in truth, while indeed, a party among the believers were unwilling,
ayahs.n: it is just as when your lord brought you out of your home for the battle of badr in truth while indeed a party among the believers were unwilling

ayahs.t: Arguing with you concerning the truth after it had become clear, as if they were being driven toward death while they were looking on.
ayahs.n: arguing with you concerning the truth after it had become clear as if they were being driven toward death while they were looking on

ayahs.t: [Remember, O believers], when Allah promised you one of the two groups - that it would be yours - and you wished that the unarmed one would be yours. But Allah intended to establish the truth by His words and to eliminate the disbelievers
ayahs.n: remember o believers when allah promised you one of the two groups that it would be yours and you wished that the unarmed one would be yours but allah intended to establish the truth by his words and to eliminate the disbelievers

ayahs.t: That He should establish the truth and abolish falsehood, even if the criminals disliked it.
ayahs.n: that he should establish the truth and abolish falsehood even if the criminals disliked it

ayahs.t: [Remember] when you asked help of your Lord, and He answered you, "Indeed, I will reinforce you with a thousand from the angels, following one another."
ayahs.n: remember when you asked help of your lord and he answered you indeed i will reinforce you with a thousand from the angels following one another

ayahs.t: And Allah made it not but good tidings and so that your hearts would be assured thereby. And victory is not but from Allah. Indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might and Wise.
ayahs.n: and allah made it not but good tidings and so that your hearts would be assured thereby and victory is not but from allah indeed allah is exalted in might and wise

ayahs.t: [Remember] when He overwhelmed you with drowsiness [giving] security from Him and sent down upon you from the sky, rain by which to purify you and remove from you the evil [suggestions] of Satan and to make steadfast your hearts and plant firmly thereby your feet.
ayahs.n: remember when he overwhelmed you with drowsiness giving security from him and sent down upon you from the sky rain by which to purify you and remove from you the evil suggestions of satan and to make steadfast your hearts and plant firmly thereby your feet

ayahs.t: [Remember] when your Lord inspired to the angels, "I am with you, so strengthen those who have believed. I will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieved, so strike [them] upon the necks and strike from them every fingertip."
ayahs.n: remember when your lord inspired to the angels i am with you so strengthen those who have believed i will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieved so strike them upon the necks and strike from them every fingertip

ayahs.t: That is because they opposed Allah and His Messenger. And whoever opposes Allah and His Messenger - indeed, Allah is severe in penalty.
ayahs.n: that is because they opposed allah and his messenger and whoever opposes allah and his messenger indeed allah is severe in penalty

ayahs.t: "That [is yours], so taste it." And indeed for the disbelievers is the punishment of the Fire.
ayahs.n: that is yours so taste it and indeed for the disbelievers is the punishment of the fire

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, when you meet those who disbelieve advancing [for battle], do not turn to them your backs [in flight].
ayahs.n: o you who have believed when you meet those who disbelieve advancing for battle do not turn to them your backs in flight

ayahs.t: And whoever turns his back to them on such a day, unless swerving [as a strategy] for war or joining [another] company, has certainly returned with anger [upon him] from Allah, and his refuge is Hell - and wretched is the destination.
ayahs.n: and whoever turns his back to them on such a day unless swerving as a strategy for war or joining another company has certainly returned with anger upon him from allah and his refuge is hell and wretched is the destination

ayahs.t: And you did not kill them, but it was Allah who killed them. And you threw not, [O Muhammad], when you threw, but it was Allah who threw that He might test the believers with a good test. Indeed, Allah is Hearing and Knowing.
ayahs.n: and you did not kill them but it was allah who killed them and you threw not o muhammad when you threw but it was allah who threw that he might test the believers with a good test indeed allah is hearing and knowing

ayahs.t: That [is so], and [also] that Allah will weaken the plot of the disbelievers.
ayahs.n: that is so and also that allah will weaken the plot of the disbelievers

ayahs.t: If you [disbelievers] seek the victory - the defeat has come to you. And if you desist [from hostilities], it is best for you; but if you return [to war], We will return, and never will you be availed by your [large] company at all, even if it should increase; and [that is] because Allah is with the believers.
ayahs.n: if you disbelievers seek the victory the defeat has come to you and if you desist from hostilities it is best for you but if you return to war we will return and never will you be availed by your large company at all even if it should increase and that is because allah is with the believers

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, obey Allah and His Messenger and do not turn from him while you hear [his order].
ayahs.n: o you who have believed obey allah and his messenger and do not turn from him while you hear his order

ayahs.t: And do not be like those who say, "We have heard," while they do not hear.
ayahs.n: and do not be like those who say we have heard while they do not hear

ayahs.t: Indeed, the worst of living creatures in the sight of Allah are the deaf and dumb who do not use reason.
ayahs.n: indeed the worst of living creatures in the sight of allah are the deaf and dumb who do not use reason

ayahs.t: Had Allah known any good in them, He would have made them hear. And if He had made them hear, they would [still] have turned away, while they were refusing.
ayahs.n: had allah known any good in them he would have made them hear and if he had made them hear they would still have turned away while they were refusing

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, respond to Allah and to the Messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life. And know that Allah intervenes between a man and his heart and that to Him you will be gathered.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed respond to allah and to the messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life and know that allah intervenes between a man and his heart and that to him you will be gathered

ayahs.t: And fear a trial which will not strike those who have wronged among you exclusively, and know that Allah is severe in penalty.
ayahs.n: and fear a trial which will not strike those who have wronged among you exclusively and know that allah is severe in penalty

ayahs.t: And remember when you were few and oppressed in the land, fearing that people might abduct you, but He sheltered you, supported you with His victory, and provided you with good things - that you might be grateful.
ayahs.n: and remember when you were few and oppressed in the land fearing that people might abduct you but he sheltered you supported you with his victory and provided you with good things that you might be grateful

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, do not betray Allah and the Messenger or betray your trusts while you know [the consequence].
ayahs.n: o you who have believed do not betray allah and the messenger or betray your trusts while you know the consequence

ayahs.t: And know that your properties and your children are but a trial and that Allah has with Him a great reward.
ayahs.n: and know that your properties and your children are but a trial and that allah has with him a great reward

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, if you fear Allah, He will grant you a criterion and will remove from you your misdeeds and forgive you. And Allah is the possessor of great bounty.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed if you fear allah he will grant you a criterion and will remove from you your misdeeds and forgive you and allah is the possessor of great bounty

ayahs.t: And [remember, O Muhammad], when those who disbelieved plotted against you to restrain you or kill you or evict you [from Makkah]. But they plan, and Allah plans. And Allah is the best of planners.
ayahs.n: and remember o muhammad when those who disbelieved plotted against you to restrain you or kill you or evict you from makkah but they plan and allah plans and allah is the best of planners

ayahs.t: And when Our verses are recited to them, they say, "We have heard. If we willed, we could say [something] like this. This is not but legends of the former peoples."
ayahs.n: and when our verses are recited to them they say we have heard if we willed we could say something like this this is not but legends of the former peoples

ayahs.t: And [remember] when they said, "O Allah, if this should be the truth from You, then rain down upon us stones from the sky or bring us a painful punishment."
ayahs.n: and remember when they said o allah if this should be the truth from you then rain down upon us stones from the sky or bring us a painful punishment

ayahs.t: But Allah would not punish them while you, [O Muhammad], are among them, and Allah would not punish them while they seek forgiveness.
ayahs.n: but allah would not punish them while you o muhammad are among them and allah would not punish them while they seek forgiveness

ayahs.t: But why should Allah not punish them while they obstruct [people] from al-Masjid al- Haram and they were not [fit to be] its guardians? Its [true] guardians are not but the righteous, but most of them do not know.
ayahs.n: but why should allah not punish them while they obstruct people from al masjid al haram and they were not fit to be its guardians its true guardians are not but the righteous but most of them do not know

ayahs.t: And their prayer at the House was not except whistling and handclapping. So taste the punishment for what you disbelieved.
ayahs.n: and their prayer at the house was not except whistling and handclapping so taste the punishment for what you disbelieved

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who disbelieve spend their wealth to avert [people] from the way of Allah. So they will spend it; then it will be for them a [source of] regret; then they will be overcome. And those who have disbelieved - unto Hell they will be gathered.
ayahs.n: indeed those who disbelieve spend their wealth to avert people from the way of allah so they will spend it then it will be for them a source of regret then they will be overcome and those who have disbelieved unto hell they will be gathered

ayahs.t: [This is] so that Allah may distinguish the wicked from the good and place the wicked some of them upon others and heap them all together and put them into Hell. It is those who are the losers.
ayahs.n: this is so that allah may distinguish the wicked from the good and place the wicked some of them upon others and heap them all together and put them into hell it is those who are the losers

ayahs.t: Say to those who have disbelieved [that] if they cease, what has previously occurred will be forgiven for them. But if they return [to hostility] - then the precedent of the former [rebellious] peoples has already taken place.
ayahs.n: say to those who have disbelieved that if they cease what has previously occurred will be forgiven for them but if they return to hostility then the precedent of the former rebellious peoples has already taken place

ayahs.t: And fight them until there is no fitnah and [until] the religion, all of it, is for Allah. And if they cease - then indeed, Allah is Seeing of what they do.
ayahs.n: and fight them until there is no fitnah and until the religion all of it is for allah and if they cease then indeed allah is seeing of what they do

ayahs.t: But if they turn away - then know that Allah is your protector. Excellent is the protector, and Excellent is the helper.
ayahs.n: but if they turn away then know that allah is your protector excellent is the protector and excellent is the helper

ayahs.t: And know that anything you obtain of war booty - then indeed, for Allah is one fifth of it and for the Messenger and for [his] near relatives and the orphans, the needy, and the [stranded] traveler, if you have believed in Allah and in that which We sent down to Our Servant on the day of criterion - the day when the two armies met. And Allah, over all things, is competent.
ayahs.n: and know that anything you obtain of war booty then indeed for allah is one fifth of it and for the messenger and for his near relatives and the orphans the needy and the stranded traveler if you have believed in allah and in that which we sent down to our servant on the day of criterion the day when the two armies met and allah over all things is competent

ayahs.t: [Remember] when you were on the near side of the valley, and they were on the farther side, and the caravan was lower [in position] than you. If you had made an appointment [to meet], you would have missed the appointment. But [it was] so that Allah might accomplish a matter already destined - that those who perished [through disbelief] would perish upon evidence and those who lived [in faith] would live upon evidence; and indeed, Allah is Hearing and Knowing.
ayahs.n: remember when you were on the near side of the valley and they were on the farther side and the caravan was lower in position than you if you had made an appointment to meet you would have missed the appointment but it was so that allah might accomplish a matter already destined that those who perished through disbelief would perish upon evidence and those who lived in faith would live upon evidence and indeed allah is hearing and knowing

ayahs.t: [Remember, O Muhammad], when Allah showed them to you in your dream as few; and if He had shown them to you as many, you [believers] would have lost courage and would have disputed in the matter [of whether to fight], but Allah saved [you from that]. Indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts.
ayahs.n: remember o muhammad when allah showed them to you in your dream as few and if he had shown them to you as many you believers would have lost courage and would have disputed in the matter of whether to fight but allah saved you from that indeed he is knowing of that within the breasts

ayahs.t: And [remember] when He showed them to you, when you met, as few in your eyes, and He made you [appear] as few in their eyes so that Allah might accomplish a matter already destined. And to Allah are [all] matters returned.
ayahs.n: and remember when he showed them to you when you met as few in your eyes and he made you appear as few in their eyes so that allah might accomplish a matter already destined and to allah are all matters returned

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, when you encounter a company [from the enemy forces], stand firm and remember Allah much that you may be successful.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed when you encounter a company from the enemy forces stand firm and remember allah much that you may be successful

ayahs.t: And obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not dispute and [thus] lose courage and [then] your strength would depart; and be patient. Indeed, Allah is with the patient.
ayahs.n: and obey allah and his messenger and do not dispute and thus lose courage and then your strength would depart and be patient indeed allah is with the patient

ayahs.t: And do not be like those who came forth from their homes insolently and to be seen by people and avert [them] from the way of Allah. And Allah is encompassing of what they do.
ayahs.n: and do not be like those who came forth from their homes insolently and to be seen by people and avert them from the way of allah and allah is encompassing of what they do

ayahs.t: And [remember] when Satan made their deeds pleasing to them and said, "No one can overcome you today from among the people, and indeed, I am your protector." But when the two armies sighted each other, he turned on his heels and said, "Indeed, I am disassociated from you. Indeed, I see what you do not see; indeed I fear Allah. And Allah is severe in penalty."
ayahs.n: and remember when satan made their deeds pleasing to them and said no one can overcome you today from among the people and indeed i am your protector but when the two armies sighted each other he turned on his heels and said indeed i am disassociated from you indeed i see what you do not see indeed i fear allah and allah is severe in penalty

ayahs.t: [Remember] when the hypocrites and those in whose hearts was disease said, "Their religion has deluded those [Muslims]." But whoever relies upon Allah - then indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might and Wise.
ayahs.n: remember when the hypocrites and those in whose hearts was disease said their religion has deluded those muslims but whoever relies upon allah then indeed allah is exalted in might and wise

ayahs.t: And if you could but see when the angels take the souls of those who disbelieved... They are striking their faces and their backs and [saying], "Taste the punishment of the Burning Fire.
ayahs.n: and if you could but see when the angels take the souls of those who disbelieved they are striking their faces and their backs and saying taste the punishment of the burning fire

ayahs.t: That is for what your hands have put forth [of evil] and because Allah is not ever unjust to His servants."
ayahs.n: that is for what your hands have put forth of evil and because allah is not ever unjust to his servants

ayahs.t: [Theirs is] like the custom of the people of Pharaoh and of those before them. They disbelieved in the signs of Allah, so Allah seized them for their sins. Indeed, Allah is Powerful and severe in penalty.
ayahs.n: theirs is like the custom of the people of pharaoh and of those before them they disbelieved in the signs of allah so allah seized them for their sins indeed allah is powerful and severe in penalty

ayahs.t: That is because Allah would not change a favor which He had bestowed upon a people until they change what is within themselves. And indeed, Allah is Hearing and Knowing.
ayahs.n: that is because allah would not change a favor which he had bestowed upon a people until they change what is within themselves and indeed allah is hearing and knowing

ayahs.t: [Theirs is] like the custom of the people of Pharaoh and of those before them. They denied the signs of their Lord, so We destroyed them for their sins, and We drowned the people of Pharaoh. And all [of them] were wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: theirs is like the custom of the people of pharaoh and of those before them they denied the signs of their lord so we destroyed them for their sins and we drowned the people of pharaoh and all of them were wrongdoers

ayahs.t: Indeed, the worst of living creatures in the sight of Allah are those who have disbelieved, and they will not [ever] believe -
ayahs.n: indeed the worst of living creatures in the sight of allah are those who have disbelieved and they will not ever believe

ayahs.t: The ones with whom you made a treaty but then they break their pledge every time, and they do not fear Allah.
ayahs.n: the ones with whom you made a treaty but then they break their pledge every time and they do not fear allah

ayahs.t: So if you, [O Muhammad], gain dominance over them in war, disperse by [means of] them those behind them that perhaps they will be reminded.
ayahs.n: so if you o muhammad gain dominance over them in war disperse by means of them those behind them that perhaps they will be reminded

ayahs.t: If you [have reason to] fear from a people betrayal, throw [their treaty] back to them, [putting you] on equal terms. Indeed, Allah does not like traitors.
ayahs.n: if you have reason to fear from a people betrayal throw their treaty back to them putting you on equal terms indeed allah does not like traitors

ayahs.t: And let not those who disbelieve think they will escape. Indeed, they will not cause failure [to Allah].
ayahs.n: and let not those who disbelieve think they will escape indeed they will not cause failure to allah

ayahs.t: And prepare against them whatever you are able of power and of steeds of war by which you may terrify the enemy of Allah and your enemy and others besides them whom you do not know [but] whom Allah knows. And whatever you spend in the cause of Allah will be fully repaid to you, and you will not be wronged.
ayahs.n: and prepare against them whatever you are able of power and of steeds of war by which you may terrify the enemy of allah and your enemy and others besides them whom you do not know but whom allah knows and whatever you spend in the cause of allah will be fully repaid to you and you will not be wronged

ayahs.t: And if they incline to peace, then incline to it [also] and rely upon Allah. Indeed, it is He who is the Hearing, the Knowing.
ayahs.n: and if they incline to peace then incline to it also and rely upon allah indeed it is he who is the hearing the knowing

ayahs.t: But if they intend to deceive you - then sufficient for you is Allah. It is He who supported you with His help and with the believers
ayahs.n: but if they intend to deceive you then sufficient for you is allah it is he who supported you with his help and with the believers

ayahs.t: And brought together their hearts. If you had spent all that is in the earth, you could not have brought their hearts together; but Allah brought them together. Indeed, He is Exalted in Might and Wise.
ayahs.n: and brought together their hearts if you had spent all that is in the earth you could not have brought their hearts together but allah brought them together indeed he is exalted in might and wise

ayahs.t: O Prophet, sufficient for you is Allah and for whoever follows you of the believers.
ayahs.n: o prophet sufficient for you is allah and for whoever follows you of the believers

ayahs.t: O Prophet, urge the believers to battle. If there are among you twenty [who are] steadfast, they will overcome two hundred. And if there are among you one hundred [who are] steadfast, they will overcome a thousand of those who have disbelieved because they are a people who do not understand.
ayahs.n: o prophet urge the believers to battle if there are among you twenty who are steadfast they will overcome two hundred and if there are among you one hundred who are steadfast they will overcome a thousand of those who have disbelieved because they are a people who do not understand

ayahs.t: Now, Allah has lightened [the hardship] for you, and He knows that among you is weakness. So if there are from you one hundred [who are] steadfast, they will overcome two hundred. And if there are among you a thousand, they will overcome two thousand by permission of Allah. And Allah is with the steadfast.
ayahs.n: now allah has lightened the hardship for you and he knows that among you is weakness so if there are from you one hundred who are steadfast they will overcome two hundred and if there are among you a thousand they will overcome two thousand by permission of allah and allah is with the steadfast

ayahs.t: It is not for a prophet to have captives [of war] until he inflicts a massacre [upon Allah 's enemies] in the land. Some Muslims desire the commodities of this world, but Allah desires [for you] the Hereafter. And Allah is Exalted in Might and Wise.
ayahs.n: it is not for a prophet to have captives of war until he inflicts a massacre upon allah s enemies in the land some muslims desire the commodities of this world but allah desires for you the hereafter and allah is exalted in might and wise

ayahs.t: If not for a decree from Allah that preceded, you would have been touched for what you took by a great punishment.
ayahs.n: if not for a decree from allah that preceded you would have been touched for what you took by a great punishment

ayahs.t: So consume what you have taken of war booty [as being] lawful and good, and fear Allah. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: so consume what you have taken of war booty as being lawful and good and fear allah indeed allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: O Prophet, say to whoever is in your hands of the captives, "If Allah knows [any] good in your hearts, He will give you [something] better than what was taken from you, and He will forgive you; and Allah is Forgiving and Merciful."
ayahs.n: o prophet say to whoever is in your hands of the captives if allah knows any good in your hearts he will give you something better than what was taken from you and he will forgive you and allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: But if they intend to betray you - then they have already betrayed Allah before, and He empowered [you] over them. And Allah is Knowing and Wise.
ayahs.n: but if they intend to betray you then they have already betrayed allah before and he empowered you over them and allah is knowing and wise

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who have believed and emigrated and fought with their wealth and lives in the cause of Allah and those who gave shelter and aided - they are allies of one another. But those who believed and did not emigrate - for you there is no guardianship of them until they emigrate. And if they seek help of you for the religion, then you must help, except against a people between yourselves and whom is a treaty. And Allah is Seeing of what you do.
ayahs.n: indeed those who have believed and emigrated and fought with their wealth and lives in the cause of allah and those who gave shelter and aided they are allies of one another but those who believed and did not emigrate for you there is no guardianship of them until they emigrate and if they seek help of you for the religion then you must help except against a people between yourselves and whom is a treaty and allah is seeing of what you do

ayahs.t: And those who disbelieved are allies of one another. If you do not do so, there will be fitnah on earth and great corruption.
ayahs.n: and those who disbelieved are allies of one another if you do not do so there will be fitnah on earth and great corruption

ayahs.t: But those who have believed and emigrated and fought in the cause of Allah and those who gave shelter and aided - it is they who are the believers, truly. For them is forgiveness and noble provision.
ayahs.n: but those who have believed and emigrated and fought in the cause of allah and those who gave shelter and aided it is they who are the believers truly for them is forgiveness and noble provision

ayahs.t: And those who believed after [the initial emigration] and emigrated and fought with you - they are of you. But those of [blood] relationship are more entitled [to inheritance] in the decree of Allah. Indeed, Allah is Knowing of all things.
ayahs.n: and those who believed after the initial emigration and emigrated and fought with you they are of you but those of blood relationship are more entitled to inheritance in the decree of allah indeed allah is knowing of all things

ayahs.t: [This is a declaration of] disassociation, from Allah and His Messenger, to those with whom you had made a treaty among the polytheists.
ayahs.n: this is a declaration of disassociation from allah and his messenger to those with whom you had made a treaty among the polytheists

ayahs.t: So travel freely, [O disbelievers], throughout the land [during] four months but know that you cannot cause failure to Allah and that Allah will disgrace the disbelievers.
ayahs.n: so travel freely o disbelievers throughout the land during four months but know that you cannot cause failure to allah and that allah will disgrace the disbelievers

ayahs.t: And [it is] an announcement from Allah and His Messenger to the people on the day of the greater pilgrimage that Allah is disassociated from the disbelievers, and [so is] His Messenger. So if you repent, that is best for you; but if you turn away - then know that you will not cause failure to Allah. And give tidings to those who disbelieve of a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: and it is an announcement from allah and his messenger to the people on the day of the greater pilgrimage that allah is disassociated from the disbelievers and so is his messenger so if you repent that is best for you but if you turn away then know that you will not cause failure to allah and give tidings to those who disbelieve of a painful punishment

ayahs.t: Excepted are those with whom you made a treaty among the polytheists and then they have not been deficient toward you in anything or supported anyone against you; so complete for them their treaty until their term [has ended]. Indeed, Allah loves the righteous [who fear Him].
ayahs.n: excepted are those with whom you made a treaty among the polytheists and then they have not been deficient toward you in anything or supported anyone against you so complete for them their treaty until their term has ended indeed allah loves the righteous who fear him

ayahs.t: And when the sacred months have passed, then kill the polytheists wherever you find them and capture them and besiege them and sit in wait for them at every place of ambush. But if they should repent, establish prayer, and give zakah, let them [go] on their way. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: and when the sacred months have passed then kill the polytheists wherever you find them and capture them and besiege them and sit in wait for them at every place of ambush but if they should repent establish prayer and give zakah let them go on their way indeed allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: And if any one of the polytheists seeks your protection, then grant him protection so that he may hear the words of Allah. Then deliver him to his place of safety. That is because they are a people who do not know.
ayahs.n: and if any one of the polytheists seeks your protection then grant him protection so that he may hear the words of allah then deliver him to his place of safety that is because they are a people who do not know

ayahs.t: How can there be for the polytheists a treaty in the sight of Allah and with His Messenger, except for those with whom you made a treaty at al-Masjid al-Haram? So as long as they are upright toward you, be upright toward them. Indeed, Allah loves the righteous [who fear Him].
ayahs.n: how can there be for the polytheists a treaty in the sight of allah and with his messenger except for those with whom you made a treaty at al masjid al haram so as long as they are upright toward you be upright toward them indeed allah loves the righteous who fear him

ayahs.t: How [can there be a treaty] while, if they gain dominance over you, they do not observe concerning you any pact of kinship or covenant of protection? They satisfy you with their mouths, but their hearts refuse [compliance], and most of them are defiantly disobedient.
ayahs.n: how can there be a treaty while if they gain dominance over you they do not observe concerning you any pact of kinship or covenant of protection they satisfy you with their mouths but their hearts refuse compliance and most of them are defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: They have exchanged the signs of Allah for a small price and averted [people] from His way. Indeed, it was evil that they were doing.
ayahs.n: they have exchanged the signs of allah for a small price and averted people from his way indeed it was evil that they were doing

ayahs.t: They do not observe toward a believer any pact of kinship or covenant of protection. And it is they who are the transgressors.
ayahs.n: they do not observe toward a believer any pact of kinship or covenant of protection and it is they who are the transgressors

ayahs.t: But if they repent, establish prayer, and give zakah, then they are your brothers in religion; and We detail the verses for a people who know.
ayahs.n: but if they repent establish prayer and give zakah then they are your brothers in religion and we detail the verses for a people who know

ayahs.t: And if they break their oaths after their treaty and defame your religion, then fight the leaders of disbelief, for indeed, there are no oaths [sacred] to them; [fight them that] they might cease.
ayahs.n: and if they break their oaths after their treaty and defame your religion then fight the leaders of disbelief for indeed there are no oaths sacred to them fight them that they might cease

ayahs.t: Would you not fight a people who broke their oaths and determined to expel the Messenger, and they had begun [the attack upon] you the first time? Do you fear them? But Allah has more right that you should fear Him, if you are [truly] believers.
ayahs.n: would you not fight a people who broke their oaths and determined to expel the messenger and they had begun the attack upon you the first time do you fear them but allah has more right that you should fear him if you are truly believers

ayahs.t: Fight them; Allah will punish them by your hands and will disgrace them and give you victory over them and satisfy the breasts of a believing people
ayahs.n: fight them allah will punish them by your hands and will disgrace them and give you victory over them and satisfy the breasts of a believing people

ayahs.t: And remove the fury in the believers' hearts. And Allah turns in forgiveness to whom He wills; and Allah is Knowing and Wise.
ayahs.n: and remove the fury in the believers hearts and allah turns in forgiveness to whom he wills and allah is knowing and wise

ayahs.t: Do you think that you will be left [as you are] while Allah has not yet made evident those among you who strive [for His cause] and do not take other than Allah, His Messenger and the believers as intimates? And Allah is Acquainted with what you do.
ayahs.n: do you think that you will be left as you are while allah has not yet made evident those among you who strive for his cause and do not take other than allah his messenger and the believers as intimates and allah is acquainted with what you do

ayahs.t: It is not for the polytheists to maintain the mosques of Allah [while] witnessing against themselves with disbelief. [For] those, their deeds have become worthless, and in the Fire they will abide eternally.
ayahs.n: it is not for the polytheists to maintain the mosques of allah while witnessing against themselves with disbelief for those their deeds have become worthless and in the fire they will abide eternally

ayahs.t: The mosques of Allah are only to be maintained by those who believe in Allah and the Last Day and establish prayer and give zakah and do not fear except Allah, for it is expected that those will be of the [rightly] guided.
ayahs.n: the mosques of allah are only to be maintained by those who believe in allah and the last day and establish prayer and give zakah and do not fear except allah for it is expected that those will be of the rightly guided

ayahs.t: Have you made the providing of water for the pilgrim and the maintenance of al-Masjid al-Haram equal to [the deeds of] one who believes in Allah and the Last Day and strives in the cause of Allah? They are not equal in the sight of Allah. And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
ayahs.n: have you made the providing of water for the pilgrim and the maintenance of al masjid al haram equal to the deeds of one who believes in allah and the last day and strives in the cause of allah they are not equal in the sight of allah and allah does not guide the wrongdoing people

ayahs.t: The ones who have believed, emigrated and striven in the cause of Allah with their wealth and their lives are greater in rank in the sight of Allah. And it is those who are the attainers [of success].
ayahs.n: the ones who have believed emigrated and striven in the cause of allah with their wealth and their lives are greater in rank in the sight of allah and it is those who are the attainers of success

ayahs.t: Their Lord gives them good tidings of mercy from Him and approval and of gardens for them wherein is enduring pleasure.
ayahs.n: their lord gives them good tidings of mercy from him and approval and of gardens for them wherein is enduring pleasure

ayahs.t: [They will be] abiding therein forever. Indeed, Allah has with Him a great reward.
ayahs.n: they will be abiding therein forever indeed allah has with him a great reward

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, do not take your fathers or your brothers as allies if they have preferred disbelief over belief. And whoever does so among you - then it is those who are the wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed do not take your fathers or your brothers as allies if they have preferred disbelief over belief and whoever does so among you then it is those who are the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: Say, [O Muhammad], "If your fathers, your sons, your brothers, your wives, your relatives, wealth which you have obtained, commerce wherein you fear decline, and dwellings with which you are pleased are more beloved to you than Allah and His Messenger and jihad in His cause, then wait until Allah executes His command. And Allah does not guide the defiantly disobedient people."
ayahs.n: say o muhammad if your fathers your sons your brothers your wives your relatives wealth which you have obtained commerce wherein you fear decline and dwellings with which you are pleased are more beloved to you than allah and his messenger and jihad in his cause then wait until allah executes his command and allah does not guide the defiantly disobedient people

ayahs.t: Allah has already given you victory in many regions and [even] on the day of Hunayn, when your great number pleased you, but it did not avail you at all, and the earth was confining for you with its vastness; then you turned back, fleeing.
ayahs.n: allah has already given you victory in many regions and even on the day of hunayn when your great number pleased you but it did not avail you at all and the earth was confining for you with its vastness then you turned back fleeing

ayahs.t: Then Allah sent down His tranquillity upon His Messenger and upon the believers and sent down soldiers angels whom you did not see and punished those who disbelieved. And that is the recompense of the disbelievers.
ayahs.n: then allah sent down his tranquillity upon his messenger and upon the believers and sent down soldiers angels whom you did not see and punished those who disbelieved and that is the recompense of the disbelievers

ayahs.t: Then Allah will accept repentance after that for whom He wills; and Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: then allah will accept repentance after that for whom he wills and allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, indeed the polytheists are unclean, so let them not approach al-Masjid al-Haram after this, their [final] year. And if you fear privation, Allah will enrich you from His bounty if He wills. Indeed, Allah is Knowing and Wise.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed indeed the polytheists are unclean so let them not approach al masjid al haram after this their final year and if you fear privation allah will enrich you from his bounty if he wills indeed allah is knowing and wise

ayahs.t: Fight those who do not believe in Allah or in the Last Day and who do not consider unlawful what Allah and His Messenger have made unlawful and who do not adopt the religion of truth from those who were given the Scripture - [fight] until they give the jizyah willingly while they are humbled.
ayahs.n: fight those who do not believe in allah or in the last day and who do not consider unlawful what allah and his messenger have made unlawful and who do not adopt the religion of truth from those who were given the scripture fight until they give the jizyah willingly while they are humbled

ayahs.t: The Jews say, "Ezra is the son of Allah "; and the Christians say, "The Messiah is the son of Allah." That is their statement from their mouths; they imitate the saying of those who disbelieved [before them]. May Allah destroy them; how are they deluded?
ayahs.n: the jews say ezra is the son of allah and the christians say the messiah is the son of allah that is their statement from their mouths they imitate the saying of those who disbelieved before them may allah destroy them how are they deluded

ayahs.t: They have taken their scholars and monks as lords besides Allah, and [also] the Messiah, the son of Mary. And they were not commanded except to worship one God; there is no deity except Him. Exalted is He above whatever they associate with Him.
ayahs.n: they have taken their scholars and monks as lords besides allah and also the messiah the son of mary and they were not commanded except to worship one god there is no deity except him exalted is he above whatever they associate with him

ayahs.t: They want to extinguish the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah refuses except to perfect His light, although the disbelievers dislike it.
ayahs.n: they want to extinguish the light of allah with their mouths but allah refuses except to perfect his light although the disbelievers dislike it

ayahs.t: It is He who has sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth to manifest it over all religion, although they who associate others with Allah dislike it.
ayahs.n: it is he who has sent his messenger with guidance and the religion of truth to manifest it over all religion although they who associate others with allah dislike it

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, indeed many of the scholars and the monks devour the wealth of people unjustly and avert [them] from the way of Allah. And those who hoard gold and silver and spend it not in the way of Allah - give them tidings of a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed indeed many of the scholars and the monks devour the wealth of people unjustly and avert them from the way of allah and those who hoard gold and silver and spend it not in the way of allah give them tidings of a painful punishment

ayahs.t: The Day when it will be heated in the fire of Hell and seared therewith will be their foreheads, their flanks, and their backs, [it will be said], "This is what you hoarded for yourselves, so taste what you used to hoard."
ayahs.n: the day when it will be heated in the fire of hell and seared therewith will be their foreheads their flanks and their backs it will be said this is what you hoarded for yourselves so taste what you used to hoard

ayahs.t: Indeed, the number of months with Allah is twelve [lunar] months in the register of Allah [from] the day He created the heavens and the earth; of these, four are sacred. That is the correct religion, so do not wrong yourselves during them. And fight against the disbelievers collectively as they fight against you collectively. And know that Allah is with the righteous [who fear Him].
ayahs.n: indeed the number of months with allah is twelve lunar months in the register of allah from the day he created the heavens and the earth of these four are sacred that is the correct religion so do not wrong yourselves during them and fight against the disbelievers collectively as they fight against you collectively and know that allah is with the righteous who fear him

ayahs.t: Indeed, the postponing [of restriction within sacred months] is an increase in disbelief by which those who have disbelieved are led [further] astray. They make it lawful one year and unlawful another year to correspond to the number made unlawful by Allah and [thus] make lawful what Allah has made unlawful. Made pleasing to them is the evil of their deeds; and Allah does not guide the disbelieving people.
ayahs.n: indeed the postponing of restriction within sacred months is an increase in disbelief by which those who have disbelieved are led further astray they make it lawful one year and unlawful another year to correspond to the number made unlawful by allah and thus make lawful what allah has made unlawful made pleasing to them is the evil of their deeds and allah does not guide the disbelieving people

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, what is [the matter] with you that, when you are told to go forth in the cause of Allah, you adhere heavily to the earth? Are you satisfied with the life of this world rather than the Hereafter? But what is the enjoyment of worldly life compared to the Hereafter except a [very] little.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed what is the matter with you that when you are told to go forth in the cause of allah you adhere heavily to the earth are you satisfied with the life of this world rather than the hereafter but what is the enjoyment of worldly life compared to the hereafter except a very little

ayahs.t: If you do not go forth, He will punish you with a painful punishment and will replace you with another people, and you will not harm Him at all. And Allah is over all things competent.
ayahs.n: if you do not go forth he will punish you with a painful punishment and will replace you with another people and you will not harm him at all and allah is over all things competent

ayahs.t: If you do not aid the Prophet - Allah has already aided him when those who disbelieved had driven him out [of Makkah] as one of two, when they were in the cave and he said to his companion, "Do not grieve; indeed Allah is with us." And Allah sent down his tranquillity upon him and supported him with angels you did not see and made the word of those who disbelieved the lowest, while the word of Allah - that is the highest. And Allah is Exalted in Might and Wise.
ayahs.n: if you do not aid the prophet allah has already aided him when those who disbelieved had driven him out of makkah as one of two when they were in the cave and he said to his companion do not grieve indeed allah is with us and allah sent down his tranquillity upon him and supported him with angels you did not see and made the word of those who disbelieved the lowest while the word of allah that is the highest and allah is exalted in might and wise

ayahs.t: Go forth, whether light or heavy, and strive with your wealth and your lives in the cause of Allah. That is better for you, if you only knew.
ayahs.n: go forth whether light or heavy and strive with your wealth and your lives in the cause of allah that is better for you if you only knew

ayahs.t: Had it been an easy gain and a moderate trip, the hypocrites would have followed you, but distant to them was the journey. And they will swear by Allah, "If we were able, we would have gone forth with you," destroying themselves [through false oaths], and Allah knows that indeed they are liars.
ayahs.n: had it been an easy gain and a moderate trip the hypocrites would have followed you but distant to them was the journey and they will swear by allah if we were able we would have gone forth with you destroying themselves through false oaths and allah knows that indeed they are liars

ayahs.t: May Allah pardon you, [O Muhammad]; why did you give them permission [to remain behind]? [You should not have] until it was evident to you who were truthful and you knew [who were] the liars.
ayahs.n: may allah pardon you o muhammad why did you give them permission to remain behind you should not have until it was evident to you who were truthful and you knew who were the liars
