ayahs.t: Unquestionably, they the disbelievers turn away their breasts to hide themselves from Him. Unquestionably, [even] when they cover themselves in their clothing, Allah knows what they conceal and what they declare. Indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts.
ayahs.n: unquestionably they the disbelievers turn away their breasts to hide themselves from him unquestionably even when they cover themselves in their clothing allah knows what they conceal and what they declare indeed he is knowing of that within the breasts

ayahs.t: And there is no creature on earth but that upon Allah is its provision, and He knows its place of dwelling and place of storage. All is in a clear register.
ayahs.n: and there is no creature on earth but that upon allah is its provision and he knows its place of dwelling and place of storage all is in a clear register

ayahs.t: And it is He who created the heavens and the earth in six days - and His Throne had been upon water - that He might test you as to which of you is best in deed. But if you say, "Indeed, you are resurrected after death," those who disbelieve will surely say, "This is not but obvious magic."
ayahs.n: and it is he who created the heavens and the earth in six days and his throne had been upon water that he might test you as to which of you is best in deed but if you say indeed you are resurrected after death those who disbelieve will surely say this is not but obvious magic

ayahs.t: And if We hold back from them the punishment for a limited time, they will surely say, "What detains it?" Unquestionably, on the Day it comes to them, it will not be averted from them, and they will be enveloped by what they used to ridicule.
ayahs.n: and if we hold back from them the punishment for a limited time they will surely say what detains it unquestionably on the day it comes to them it will not be averted from them and they will be enveloped by what they used to ridicule

ayahs.t: And if We give man a taste of mercy from Us and then We withdraw it from him, indeed, he is despairing and ungrateful.
ayahs.n: and if we give man a taste of mercy from us and then we withdraw it from him indeed he is despairing and ungrateful

ayahs.t: But if We give him a taste of favor after hardship has touched him, he will surely say, "Bad times have left me." Indeed, he is exultant and boastful -
ayahs.n: but if we give him a taste of favor after hardship has touched him he will surely say bad times have left me indeed he is exultant and boastful

ayahs.t: Except for those who are patient and do righteous deeds; those will have forgiveness and great reward.
ayahs.n: except for those who are patient and do righteous deeds those will have forgiveness and great reward

ayahs.t: Then would you possibly leave [out] some of what is revealed to you, or is your breast constrained by it because they say, "Why has there not been sent down to him a treasure or come with him an angel?" But you are only a warner. And Allah is Disposer of all things.
ayahs.n: then would you possibly leave out some of what is revealed to you or is your breast constrained by it because they say why has there not been sent down to him a treasure or come with him an angel but you are only a warner and allah is disposer of all things

ayahs.t: Or do they say, "He invented it"? Say, "Then bring ten surahs like it that have been invented and call upon [for assistance] whomever you can besides Allah, if you should be truthful."
ayahs.n: or do they say he invented it say then bring ten surahs like it that have been invented and call upon for assistance whomever you can besides allah if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: And if they do not respond to you - then know that the Qur'an was revealed with the knowledge of Allah and that there is no deity except Him. Then, would you [not] be Muslims?
ayahs.n: and if they do not respond to you then know that the quran was revealed with the knowledge of allah and that there is no deity except him then would you not be muslims

ayahs.t: Whoever desires the life of this world and its adornments - We fully repay them for their deeds therein, and they therein will not be deprived.
ayahs.n: whoever desires the life of this world and its adornments we fully repay them for their deeds therein and they therein will not be deprived

ayahs.t: Those are the ones for whom there is not in the Hereafter but the Fire. And lost is what they did therein, and worthless is what they used to do.
ayahs.n: those are the ones for whom there is not in the hereafter but the fire and lost is what they did therein and worthless is what they used to do

ayahs.t: So is one who [stands] upon a clear evidence from his Lord [like the aforementioned]? And a witness from Him follows it, and before it was the Scripture of Moses to lead and as mercy. Those [believers in the former revelations] believe in the Qur'an. But whoever disbelieves in it from the [various] factions - the Fire is his promised destination. So be not in doubt about it. Indeed, it is the truth from your Lord, but most of the people do not believe.
ayahs.n: so is one who stands upon a clear evidence from his lord like the aforementioned and a witness from him follows it and before it was the scripture of moses to lead and as mercy those believers in the former revelations believe in the quran but whoever disbelieves in it from the various factions the fire is his promised destination so be not in doubt about it indeed it is the truth from your lord but most of the people do not believe

ayahs.t: And who is more unjust than he who invents a lie about Allah? Those will be presented before their Lord, and the witnesses will say, "These are the ones who lied against their Lord." Unquestionably, the curse of Allah is upon the wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: and who is more unjust than he who invents a lie about allah those will be presented before their lord and the witnesses will say these are the ones who lied against their lord unquestionably the curse of allah is upon the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: Who averted [people] from the way of Allah and sought to make it [seem] deviant while they, concerning the Hereafter, were disbelievers.
ayahs.n: who averted people from the way of allah and sought to make it seem deviant while they concerning the hereafter were disbelievers

ayahs.t: Those were not causing failure [to Allah] on earth, nor did they have besides Allah any protectors. For them the punishment will be multiplied. They were not able to hear, nor did they see.
ayahs.n: those were not causing failure to allah on earth nor did they have besides allah any protectors for them the punishment will be multiplied they were not able to hear nor did they see

ayahs.t: Those are the ones who will have lost themselves, and lost from them is what they used to invent.
ayahs.n: those are the ones who will have lost themselves and lost from them is what they used to invent

ayahs.t: Assuredly, it is they in the Hereafter who will be the greatest losers.
ayahs.n: assuredly it is they in the hereafter who will be the greatest losers

ayahs.t: Indeed, they who have believed and done righteous deeds and humbled themselves to their Lord - those are the companions of Paradise; they will abide eternally therein.
ayahs.n: indeed they who have believed and done righteous deeds and humbled themselves to their lord those are the companions of paradise they will abide eternally therein

ayahs.t: The example of the two parties is like the blind and deaf, and the seeing and hearing. Are they equal in comparison? Then, will you not remember?
ayahs.n: the example of the two parties is like the blind and deaf and the seeing and hearing are they equal in comparison then will you not remember

ayahs.t: And We had certainly sent Noah to his people, [saying], "Indeed, I am to you a clear warner
ayahs.n: and we had certainly sent noah to his people saying indeed i am to you a clear warner

ayahs.t: That you not worship except Allah. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a painful day."
ayahs.n: that you not worship except allah indeed i fear for you the punishment of a painful day

ayahs.t: So the eminent among those who disbelieved from his people said, "We do not see you but as a man like ourselves, and we do not see you followed except by those who are the lowest of us [and] at first suggestion. And we do not see in you over us any merit; rather, we think you are liars."
ayahs.n: so the eminent among those who disbelieved from his people said we do not see you but as a man like ourselves and we do not see you followed except by those who are the lowest of us and at first suggestion and we do not see in you over us any merit rather we think you are liars

ayahs.t: He said, "O my people have you considered: if I should be upon clear evidence from my Lord while He has given me mercy from Himself but it has been made unapparent to you, should we force it upon you while you are averse to it?
ayahs.n: he said o my people have you considered if i should be upon clear evidence from my lord while he has given me mercy from himself but it has been made unapparent to you should we force it upon you while you are averse to it

ayahs.t: And O my people, I ask not of you for it any wealth. My reward is not but from Allah. And I am not one to drive away those who have believed. Indeed, they will meet their Lord, but I see that you are a people behaving ignorantly.
ayahs.n: and o my people i ask not of you for it any wealth my reward is not but from allah and i am not one to drive away those who have believed indeed they will meet their lord but i see that you are a people behaving ignorantly

ayahs.t: And O my people, who would protect me from Allah if I drove them away? Then will you not be reminded?
ayahs.n: and o my people who would protect me from allah if i drove them away then will you not be reminded

ayahs.t: And I do not tell you that I have the depositories [containing the provision] of Allah or that I know the unseen, nor do I tell you that I am an angel, nor do I say of those upon whom your eyes look down that Allah will never grant them any good. Allah is most knowing of what is within their souls. Indeed, I would then be among the wrongdoers."
ayahs.n: and i do not tell you that i have the depositories containing the provision of allah or that i know the unseen nor do i tell you that i am an angel nor do i say of those upon whom your eyes look down that allah will never grant them any good allah is most knowing of what is within their souls indeed i would then be among the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: They said, "O Noah, you have disputed us and been frequent in dispute of us. So bring us what you threaten us, if you should be of the truthful."
ayahs.n: they said o noah you have disputed us and been frequent in dispute of us so bring us what you threaten us if you should be of the truthful

ayahs.t: He said, "Allah will only bring it to you if He wills, and you will not cause [Him] failure.
ayahs.n: he said allah will only bring it to you if he wills and you will not cause him failure

ayahs.t: And my advice will not benefit you - although I wished to advise you - If Allah should intend to put you in error. He is your Lord, and to Him you will be returned."
ayahs.n: and my advice will not benefit you although i wished to advise you if allah should intend to put you in error he is your lord and to him you will be returned

ayahs.t: Or do they say [about Prophet Muhammad], "He invented it"? Say, "If I have invented it, then upon me is [the consequence of] my crime; but I am innocent of what [crimes] you commit."
ayahs.n: or do they say about prophet muhammad he invented it say if i have invented it then upon me is the consequence of my crime but i am innocent of what crimes you commit

ayahs.t: And it was revealed to Noah that, "No one will believe from your people except those who have already believed, so do not be distressed by what they have been doing.
ayahs.n: and it was revealed to noah that no one will believe from your people except those who have already believed so do not be distressed by what they have been doing

ayahs.t: And construct the ship under Our observation and Our inspiration and do not address Me concerning those who have wronged; indeed, they are [to be] drowned."
ayahs.n: and construct the ship under our observation and our inspiration and do not address me concerning those who have wronged indeed they are to be drowned

ayahs.t: And he constructed the ship, and whenever an assembly of the eminent of his people passed by him, they ridiculed him. He said, "If you ridicule us, then we will ridicule you just as you ridicule.
ayahs.n: and he constructed the ship and whenever an assembly of the eminent of his people passed by him they ridiculed him he said if you ridicule us then we will ridicule you just as you ridicule

ayahs.t: And you are going to know who will get a punishment that will disgrace him [on earth] and upon whom will descend an enduring punishment [in the Hereafter]."
ayahs.n: and you are going to know who will get a punishment that will disgrace him on earth and upon whom will descend an enduring punishment in the hereafter

ayahs.t: [So it was], until when Our command came and the oven overflowed, We said, "Load upon the ship of each [creature] two mates and your family, except those about whom the word has preceded, and [include] whoever has believed." But none had believed with him, except a few.
ayahs.n: so it was until when our command came and the oven overflowed we said load upon the ship of each creature two mates and your family except those about whom the word has preceded and include whoever has believed but none had believed with him except a few

ayahs.t: And [Noah] said, "Embark therein; in the name of Allah is its course and its anchorage. Indeed, my Lord is Forgiving and Merciful."
ayahs.n: and noah said embark therein in the name of allah is its course and its anchorage indeed my lord is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: And it sailed with them through waves like mountains, and Noah called to his son who was apart [from them], "O my son, come aboard with us and be not with the disbelievers."
ayahs.n: and it sailed with them through waves like mountains and noah called to his son who was apart from them o my son come aboard with us and be not with the disbelievers

ayahs.t: [But] he said, "I will take refuge on a mountain to protect me from the water." [Noah] said, "There is no protector today from the decree of Allah, except for whom He gives mercy." And the waves came between them, and he was among the drowned.
ayahs.n: but he said i will take refuge on a mountain to protect me from the water noah said there is no protector today from the decree of allah except for whom he gives mercy and the waves came between them and he was among the drowned

ayahs.t: And it was said, "O earth, swallow your water, and O sky, withhold [your rain]." And the water subsided, and the matter was accomplished, and the ship came to rest on the [mountain of] Judiyy. And it was said, "Away with the wrongdoing people."
ayahs.n: and it was said o earth swallow your water and o sky withhold your rain and the water subsided and the matter was accomplished and the ship came to rest on the mountain of judiyy and it was said away with the wrongdoing people

ayahs.t: And Noah called to his Lord and said, "My Lord, indeed my son is of my family; and indeed, Your promise is true; and You are the most just of judges!"
ayahs.n: and noah called to his lord and said my lord indeed my son is of my family and indeed your promise is true and you are the most just of judges

ayahs.t: He said, "O Noah, indeed he is not of your family; indeed, he is [one whose] work was other than righteous, so ask Me not for that about which you have no knowledge. Indeed, I advise you, lest you be among the ignorant."
ayahs.n: he said o noah indeed he is not of your family indeed he is one whose work was other than righteous so ask me not for that about which you have no knowledge indeed i advise you lest you be among the ignorant

ayahs.t: [Noah] said, "My Lord, I seek refuge in You from asking that of which I have no knowledge. And unless You forgive me and have mercy upon me, I will be among the losers."
ayahs.n: noah said my lord i seek refuge in you from asking that of which i have no knowledge and unless you forgive me and have mercy upon me i will be among the losers

ayahs.t: It was said, "O Noah, disembark in security from Us and blessings upon you and upon nations [descending] from those with you. But other nations [of them] We will grant enjoyment; then there will touch them from Us a painful punishment."
ayahs.n: it was said o noah disembark in security from us and blessings upon you and upon nations descending from those with you but other nations of them we will grant enjoyment then there will touch them from us a painful punishment

ayahs.t: That is from the news of the unseen which We reveal to you, [O Muhammad]. You knew it not, neither you nor your people, before this. So be patient; indeed, the [best] outcome is for the righteous.
ayahs.n: that is from the news of the unseen which we reveal to you o muhammad you knew it not neither you nor your people before this so be patient indeed the best outcome is for the righteous

ayahs.t: And to 'Aad [We sent] their brother Hud. He said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. You are not but inventors [of falsehood].
ayahs.n: and to aad we sent their brother hud he said o my people worship allah you have no deity other than him you are not but inventors of falsehood

ayahs.t: O my people, I do not ask you for it any reward. My reward is only from the one who created me. Then will you not reason?
ayahs.n: o my people i do not ask you for it any reward my reward is only from the one who created me then will you not reason

ayahs.t: And O my people, ask forgiveness of your Lord and then repent to Him. He will send [rain from] the sky upon you in showers and increase you in strength [added] to your strength. And do not turn away, [being] criminals."
ayahs.n: and o my people ask forgiveness of your lord and then repent to him he will send rain from the sky upon you in showers and increase you in strength added to your strength and do not turn away being criminals

ayahs.t: They said, "O Hud, you have not brought us clear evidence, and we are not ones to leave our gods on your say-so. Nor are we believers in you.
ayahs.n: they said o hud you have not brought us clear evidence and we are not ones to leave our gods on your say so nor are we believers in you

ayahs.t: We only say that some of our gods have possessed you with evil." He said, "Indeed, I call Allah to witness, and witness [yourselves] that I am free from whatever you associate with Allah
ayahs.n: we only say that some of our gods have possessed you with evil he said indeed i call allah to witness and witness yourselves that i am free from whatever you associate with allah

ayahs.t: Other than Him. So plot against me all together; then do not give me respite.
ayahs.n: other than him so plot against me all together then do not give me respite

ayahs.t: Indeed, I have relied upon Allah, my Lord and your Lord. There is no creature but that He holds its forelock. Indeed, my Lord is on a path [that is] straight."
ayahs.n: indeed i have relied upon allah my lord and your lord there is no creature but that he holds its forelock indeed my lord is on a path that is straight

ayahs.t: But if they turn away, [say], "I have already conveyed that with which I was sent to you. My Lord will give succession to a people other than you, and you will not harm Him at all. Indeed my Lord is, over all things, Guardian."
ayahs.n: but if they turn away say i have already conveyed that with which i was sent to you my lord will give succession to a people other than you and you will not harm him at all indeed my lord is over all things guardian

ayahs.t: And when Our command came, We saved Hud and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us; and We saved them from a harsh punishment.
ayahs.n: and when our command came we saved hud and those who believed with him by mercy from us and we saved them from a harsh punishment

ayahs.t: And that was 'Aad, who rejected the signs of their Lord and disobeyed His messengers and followed the order of every obstinate tyrant.
ayahs.n: and that was aad who rejected the signs of their lord and disobeyed his messengers and followed the order of every obstinate tyrant

ayahs.t: And they were [therefore] followed in this world with a curse and [as well] on the Day of Resurrection. Unquestionably, 'Aad denied their Lord; then away with 'Aad, the people of Hud.
ayahs.n: and they were therefore followed in this world with a curse and as well on the day of resurrection unquestionably aad denied their lord then away with aad the people of hud

ayahs.t: And to Thamud [We sent] their brother Salih. He said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. He has produced you from the earth and settled you in it, so ask forgiveness of Him and then repent to Him. Indeed, my Lord is near and responsive."
ayahs.n: and to thamud we sent their brother salih he said o my people worship allah you have no deity other than him he has produced you from the earth and settled you in it so ask forgiveness of him and then repent to him indeed my lord is near and responsive

ayahs.t: They said, "O Salih, you were among us a man of promise before this. Do you forbid us to worship what our fathers worshipped? And indeed we are, about that to which you invite us, in disquieting doubt."
ayahs.n: they said o salih you were among us a man of promise before this do you forbid us to worship what our fathers worshipped and indeed we are about that to which you invite us in disquieting doubt

ayahs.t: He said, "O my people, have you considered: if I should be upon clear evidence from my Lord and He has given me mercy from Himself, who would protect me from Allah if I disobeyed Him? So you would not increase me except in loss.
ayahs.n: he said o my people have you considered if i should be upon clear evidence from my lord and he has given me mercy from himself who would protect me from allah if i disobeyed him so you would not increase me except in loss

ayahs.t: And O my people, this is the she-camel of Allah - [she is] to you a sign. So let her feed upon Allah 's earth and do not touch her with harm, or you will be taken by an impending punishment."
ayahs.n: and o my people this is the she camel of allah she is to you a sign so let her feed upon allah s earth and do not touch her with harm or you will be taken by an impending punishment

ayahs.t: But they hamstrung her, so he said, "Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days. That is a promise not to be denied."
ayahs.n: but they hamstrung her so he said enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days that is a promise not to be denied

ayahs.t: So when Our command came, We saved Salih and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us, and [saved them] from the disgrace of that day. Indeed, it is your Lord who is the Powerful, the Exalted in Might.
ayahs.n: so when our command came we saved salih and those who believed with him by mercy from us and saved them from the disgrace of that day indeed it is your lord who is the powerful the exalted in might

ayahs.t: And the shriek seized those who had wronged, and they became within their homes [corpses] fallen prone
ayahs.n: and the shriek seized those who had wronged and they became within their homes corpses fallen prone

ayahs.t: As if they had never prospered therein. Unquestionably, Thamud denied their Lord; then, away with Thamud.
ayahs.n: as if they had never prospered therein unquestionably thamud denied their lord then away with thamud

ayahs.t: And certainly did Our messengers come to Abraham with good tidings; they said, "Peace." He said, "Peace," and did not delay in bringing [them] a roasted calf.
ayahs.n: and certainly did our messengers come to abraham with good tidings they said peace he said peace and did not delay in bringing them a roasted calf

ayahs.t: But when he saw their hands not reaching for it, he distrusted them and felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not. We have been sent to the people of Lot."
ayahs.n: but when he saw their hands not reaching for it he distrusted them and felt from them apprehension they said fear not we have been sent to the people of lot

ayahs.t: And his Wife was standing, and she smiled. Then We gave her good tidings of Isaac and after Isaac, Jacob.
ayahs.n: and his wife was standing and she smiled then we gave her good tidings of isaac and after isaac jacob

ayahs.t: She said, "Woe to me! Shall I give birth while I am an old woman and this, my husband, is an old man? Indeed, this is an amazing thing!"
ayahs.n: she said woe to me shall i give birth while i am an old woman and this my husband is an old man indeed this is an amazing thing

ayahs.t: They said, "Are you amazed at the decree of Allah? May the mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, people of the house. Indeed, He is Praiseworthy and Honorable."
ayahs.n: they said are you amazed at the decree of allah may the mercy of allah and his blessings be upon you people of the house indeed he is praiseworthy and honorable

ayahs.t: And when the fright had left Abraham and the good tidings had reached him, he began to argue with Us concerning the people of Lot.
ayahs.n: and when the fright had left abraham and the good tidings had reached him he began to argue with us concerning the people of lot

ayahs.t: Indeed, Abraham was forbearing, grieving and [frequently] returning [to Allah].
ayahs.n: indeed abraham was forbearing grieving and frequently returning to allah

ayahs.t: [The angels said], "O Abraham, give up this [plea]. Indeed, the command of your Lord has come, and indeed, there will reach them a punishment that cannot be repelled."
ayahs.n: the angels said o abraham give up this plea indeed the command of your lord has come and indeed there will reach them a punishment that cannot be repelled

ayahs.t: And when Our messengers, [the angels], came to Lot, he was anguished for them and felt for them great discomfort and said, "This is a trying day."
ayahs.n: and when our messengers the angels came to lot he was anguished for them and felt for them great discomfort and said this is a trying day

ayahs.t: And his people came hastening to him, and before [this] they had been doing evil deeds. He said, "O my people, these are my daughters; they are purer for you. So fear Allah and do not disgrace me concerning my guests. Is there not among you a man of reason?"
ayahs.n: and his people came hastening to him and before this they had been doing evil deeds he said o my people these are my daughters they are purer for you so fear allah and do not disgrace me concerning my guests is there not among you a man of reason

ayahs.t: They said, "You have already known that we have not concerning your daughters any claim, and indeed, you know what we want."
ayahs.n: they said you have already known that we have not concerning your daughters any claim and indeed you know what we want

ayahs.t: He said, "If only I had against you some power or could take refuge in a strong support."
ayahs.n: he said if only i had against you some power or could take refuge in a strong support

ayahs.t: The angels said, "O Lot, indeed we are messengers of your Lord; [therefore], they will never reach you. So set out with your family during a portion of the night and let not any among you look back - except your wife; indeed, she will be struck by that which strikes them. Indeed, their appointment is [for] the morning. Is not the morning near?"
ayahs.n: the angels said o lot indeed we are messengers of your lord therefore they will never reach you so set out with your family during a portion of the night and let not any among you look back except your wife indeed she will be struck by that which strikes them indeed their appointment is for the morning is not the morning near

ayahs.t: So when Our command came, We made the highest part [of the city] its lowest and rained upon them stones of layered hard clay, [which were]
ayahs.n: so when our command came we made the highest part of the city its lowest and rained upon them stones of layered hard clay which were

ayahs.t: Marked from your Lord. And Allah 's punishment is not from the wrongdoers [very] far.
ayahs.n: marked from your lord and allah s punishment is not from the wrongdoers very far

ayahs.t: And to Madyan [We sent] their brother Shu'ayb. He said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. And do not decrease from the measure and the scale. Indeed, I see you in prosperity, but indeed, I fear for you the punishment of an all-encompassing Day.
ayahs.n: and to madyan we sent their brother shuayb he said o my people worship allah you have no deity other than him and do not decrease from the measure and the scale indeed i see you in prosperity but indeed i fear for you the punishment of an all encompassing day

ayahs.t: And O my people, give full measure and weight in justice and do not deprive the people of their due and do not commit abuse on the earth, spreading corruption.
ayahs.n: and o my people give full measure and weight in justice and do not deprive the people of their due and do not commit abuse on the earth spreading corruption

ayahs.t: What remains [lawful] from Allah is best for you, if you would be believers. But I am not a guardian over you."
ayahs.n: what remains lawful from allah is best for you if you would be believers but i am not a guardian over you

ayahs.t: They said, "O Shu'ayb, does your prayer command you that we should leave what our fathers worship or not do with our wealth what we please? Indeed, you are the forbearing, the discerning!"
ayahs.n: they said o shuayb does your prayer command you that we should leave what our fathers worship or not do with our wealth what we please indeed you are the forbearing the discerning

ayahs.t: He said, "O my people, have you considered: if I am upon clear evidence from my Lord and He has provided me with a good provision from Him...? And I do not intend to differ from you in that which I have forbidden you; I only intend reform as much as I am able. And my success is not but through Allah. Upon him I have relied, and to Him I return.
ayahs.n: he said o my people have you considered if i am upon clear evidence from my lord and he has provided me with a good provision from him and i do not intend to differ from you in that which i have forbidden you i only intend reform as much as i am able and my success is not but through allah upon him i have relied and to him i return

ayahs.t: And O my people, let not [your] dissension from me cause you to be struck by that similar to what struck the people of Noah or the people of Hud or the people of Salih. And the people of Lot are not from you far away.
ayahs.n: and o my people let not your dissension from me cause you to be struck by that similar to what struck the people of noah or the people of hud or the people of salih and the people of lot are not from you far away

ayahs.t: And ask forgiveness of your Lord and then repent to Him. Indeed, my Lord is Merciful and Affectionate."
ayahs.n: and ask forgiveness of your lord and then repent to him indeed my lord is merciful and affectionate

ayahs.t: They said, "O Shu'ayb, we do not understand much of what you say, and indeed, we consider you among us as weak. And if not for your family, we would have stoned you [to death]; and you are not to us one respected."
ayahs.n: they said o shuayb we do not understand much of what you say and indeed we consider you among us as weak and if not for your family we would have stoned you to death and you are not to us one respected

ayahs.t: He said, "O my people, is my family more respected for power by you than Allah? But you put Him behind your backs [in neglect]. Indeed, my Lord is encompassing of what you do.
ayahs.n: he said o my people is my family more respected for power by you than allah but you put him behind your backs in neglect indeed my lord is encompassing of what you do

ayahs.t: And O my people, work according to your position; indeed, I am working. You are going to know to whom will come a punishment that will disgrace him and who is a liar. So watch; indeed, I am with you a watcher, [awaiting the outcome]."
ayahs.n: and o my people work according to your position indeed i am working you are going to know to whom will come a punishment that will disgrace him and who is a liar so watch indeed i am with you a watcher awaiting the outcome

ayahs.t: And when Our command came, We saved Shu'ayb and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us. And the shriek seized those who had wronged, and they became within their homes [corpses] fallen prone
ayahs.n: and when our command came we saved shuayb and those who believed with him by mercy from us and the shriek seized those who had wronged and they became within their homes corpses fallen prone

ayahs.t: As if they had never prospered therein. Then, away with Madyan as Thamud was taken away.
ayahs.n: as if they had never prospered therein then away with madyan as thamud was taken away

ayahs.t: And We did certainly send Moses with Our signs and a clear authority
ayahs.n: and we did certainly send moses with our signs and a clear authority

ayahs.t: To Pharaoh and his establishment, but they followed the command of Pharaoh, and the command of Pharaoh was not [at all] discerning.
ayahs.n: to pharaoh and his establishment but they followed the command of pharaoh and the command of pharaoh was not at all discerning

ayahs.t: He will precede his people on the Day of Resurrection and lead them into the Fire; and wretched is the place to which they are led.
ayahs.n: he will precede his people on the day of resurrection and lead them into the fire and wretched is the place to which they are led

ayahs.t: And they were followed in this [world] with a curse and on the Day of Resurrection. And wretched is the gift which is given.
ayahs.n: and they were followed in this world with a curse and on the day of resurrection and wretched is the gift which is given

ayahs.t: That is from the news of the cities, which We relate to you; of them, some are [still] standing and some are [as] a harvest [mowed down].
ayahs.n: that is from the news of the cities which we relate to you of them some are still standing and some are as a harvest mowed down

ayahs.t: And We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves. And they were not availed at all by their gods which they invoked other than Allah when there came the command of your Lord. And they did not increase them in other than ruin.
ayahs.n: and we did not wrong them but they wronged themselves and they were not availed at all by their gods which they invoked other than allah when there came the command of your lord and they did not increase them in other than ruin

ayahs.t: And thus is the seizure of your Lord when He seizes the cities while they are committing wrong. Indeed, His seizure is painful and severe.
ayahs.n: and thus is the seizure of your lord when he seizes the cities while they are committing wrong indeed his seizure is painful and severe

ayahs.t: Indeed in that is a sign for those who fear the punishment of the Hereafter. That is a Day for which the people will be collected, and that is a Day [which will be] witnessed.
ayahs.n: indeed in that is a sign for those who fear the punishment of the hereafter that is a day for which the people will be collected and that is a day which will be witnessed

ayahs.t: And We do not delay it except for a limited term.
ayahs.n: and we do not delay it except for a limited term

ayahs.t: The Day it comes no soul will speak except by His permission. And among them will be the wretched and the prosperous.
ayahs.n: the day it comes no soul will speak except by his permission and among them will be the wretched and the prosperous

ayahs.t: As for those who were [destined to be] wretched, they will be in the Fire. For them therein is [violent] exhaling and inhaling.
ayahs.n: as for those who were destined to be wretched they will be in the fire for them therein is violent exhaling and inhaling

ayahs.t: [They will be] abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord should will. Indeed, your Lord is an effecter of what He intends.
ayahs.n: they will be abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure except what your lord should will indeed your lord is an effecter of what he intends

ayahs.t: And as for those who were [destined to be] prosperous, they will be in Paradise, abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord should will - a bestowal uninterrupted.
ayahs.n: and as for those who were destined to be prosperous they will be in paradise abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure except what your lord should will a bestowal uninterrupted

ayahs.t: So do not be in doubt, [O Muhammad], as to what these [polytheists] are worshipping. They worship not except as their fathers worshipped before. And indeed, We will give them their share undiminished.
ayahs.n: so do not be in doubt o muhammad as to what these polytheists are worshipping they worship not except as their fathers worshipped before and indeed we will give them their share undiminished

ayahs.t: And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture, but it came under disagreement. And if not for a word that preceded from your Lord, it would have been judged between them. And indeed they are, concerning the Qur'an, in disquieting doubt.
ayahs.n: and we had certainly given moses the scripture but it came under disagreement and if not for a word that preceded from your lord it would have been judged between them and indeed they are concerning the quran in disquieting doubt

ayahs.t: And indeed, each [of the believers and disbelievers] - your Lord will fully compensate them for their deeds. Indeed, He is Acquainted with what they do.
ayahs.n: and indeed each of the believers and disbelievers your lord will fully compensate them for their deeds indeed he is acquainted with what they do

ayahs.t: So remain on a right course as you have been commanded, [you] and those who have turned back with you [to Allah], and do not transgress. Indeed, He is Seeing of what you do.
ayahs.n: so remain on a right course as you have been commanded you and those who have turned back with you to allah and do not transgress indeed he is seeing of what you do

ayahs.t: And do not incline toward those who do wrong, lest you be touched by the Fire, and you would not have other than Allah any protectors; then you would not be helped.
ayahs.n: and do not incline toward those who do wrong lest you be touched by the fire and you would not have other than allah any protectors then you would not be helped

ayahs.t: And establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night. Indeed, good deeds do away with misdeeds. That is a reminder for those who remember.
ayahs.n: and establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night indeed good deeds do away with misdeeds that is a reminder for those who remember

ayahs.t: And be patient, for indeed, Allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good.
ayahs.n: and be patient for indeed allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good

ayahs.t: So why were there not among the generations before you those of enduring discrimination forbidding corruption on earth - except a few of those We saved from among them? But those who wronged pursued what luxury they were given therein, and they were criminals.
ayahs.n: so why were there not among the generations before you those of enduring discrimination forbidding corruption on earth except a few of those we saved from among them but those who wronged pursued what luxury they were given therein and they were criminals

ayahs.t: And your Lord would not have destroyed the cities unjustly while their people were reformers.
ayahs.n: and your lord would not have destroyed the cities unjustly while their people were reformers

ayahs.t: And if your Lord had willed, He could have made mankind one community; but they will not cease to differ.
ayahs.n: and if your lord had willed he could have made mankind one community but they will not cease to differ

ayahs.t: Except whom your Lord has given mercy, and for that He created them. But the word of your Lord is to be fulfilled that, "I will surely fill Hell with jinn and men all together."
ayahs.n: except whom your lord has given mercy and for that he created them but the word of your lord is to be fulfilled that i will surely fill hell with jinn and men all together

ayahs.t: And each [story] We relate to you from the news of the messengers is that by which We make firm your heart. And there has come to you, in this, the truth and an instruction and a reminder for the believers.
ayahs.n: and each story we relate to you from the news of the messengers is that by which we make firm your heart and there has come to you in this the truth and an instruction and a reminder for the believers

ayahs.t: And say to those who do not believe, "Work according to your position; indeed, we are working.
ayahs.n: and say to those who do not believe work according to your position indeed we are working

ayahs.t: And wait, indeed, we are waiting."
ayahs.n: and wait indeed we are waiting

ayahs.t: And to Allah belong the unseen [aspects] of the heavens and the earth and to Him will be returned the matter, all of it, so worship Him and rely upon Him. And your Lord is not unaware of that which you do.
ayahs.n: and to allah belong the unseen aspects of the heavens and the earth and to him will be returned the matter all of it so worship him and rely upon him and your lord is not unaware of that which you do

ayahs.t: Alif, Lam, Ra. These are the verses of the clear Book.
ayahs.n: alif lam ra these are the verses of the clear book

ayahs.t: Indeed, We have sent it down as an Arabic Qur'an that you might understand.
ayahs.n: indeed we have sent it down as an arabic quran that you might understand

ayahs.t: We relate to you, [O Muhammad], the best of stories in what We have revealed to you of this Qur'an although you were, before it, among the unaware.
ayahs.n: we relate to you o muhammad the best of stories in what we have revealed to you of this quran although you were before it among the unaware

ayahs.t: [Of these stories mention] when Joseph said to his father, "O my father, indeed I have seen [in a dream] eleven stars and the sun and the moon; I saw them prostrating to me."
ayahs.n: of these stories mention when joseph said to his father o my father indeed i have seen in a dream eleven stars and the sun and the moon i saw them prostrating to me

ayahs.t: He said, "O my son, do not relate your vision to your brothers or they will contrive against you a plan. Indeed Satan, to man, is a manifest enemy.
ayahs.n: he said o my son do not relate your vision to your brothers or they will contrive against you a plan indeed satan to man is a manifest enemy

ayahs.t: And thus will your Lord choose you and teach you the interpretation of narratives and complete His favor upon you and upon the family of Jacob, as He completed it upon your fathers before, Abraham and Isaac. Indeed, your Lord is Knowing and Wise."
ayahs.n: and thus will your lord choose you and teach you the interpretation of narratives and complete his favor upon you and upon the family of jacob as he completed it upon your fathers before abraham and isaac indeed your lord is knowing and wise

ayahs.t: Certainly were there in Joseph and his brothers signs for those who ask,
ayahs.n: certainly were there in joseph and his brothers signs for those who ask

ayahs.t: When they said, "Joseph and his brother are more beloved to our father than we, while we are a clan. Indeed, our father is in clear error.
ayahs.n: when they said joseph and his brother are more beloved to our father than we while we are a clan indeed our father is in clear error

ayahs.t: Kill Joseph or cast him out to [another] land; the countenance of your father will [then] be only for you, and you will be after that a righteous people."
ayahs.n: kill joseph or cast him out to another land the countenance of your father will then be only for you and you will be after that a righteous people

ayahs.t: Said a speaker among them, "Do not kill Joseph but throw him into the bottom of the well; some travelers will pick him up - if you would do [something]."
ayahs.n: said a speaker among them do not kill joseph but throw him into the bottom of the well some travelers will pick him up if you would do something

ayahs.t: They said, "O our father, why do you not entrust us with Joseph while indeed, we are to him sincere counselors?
ayahs.n: they said o our father why do you not entrust us with joseph while indeed we are to him sincere counselors

ayahs.t: Send him with us tomorrow that he may eat well and play. And indeed, we will be his guardians.
ayahs.n: send him with us tomorrow that he may eat well and play and indeed we will be his guardians

ayahs.t: [Jacob] said, "Indeed, it saddens me that you should take him, and I fear that a wolf would eat him while you are of him unaware."
ayahs.n: jacob said indeed it saddens me that you should take him and i fear that a wolf would eat him while you are of him unaware

ayahs.t: They said, "If a wolf should eat him while we are a [strong] clan, indeed, we would then be losers."
ayahs.n: they said if a wolf should eat him while we are a strong clan indeed we would then be losers

ayahs.t: So when they took him [out] and agreed to put him into the bottom of the well... But We inspired to him, "You will surely inform them [someday] about this affair of theirs while they do not perceive [your identity]."
ayahs.n: so when they took him out and agreed to put him into the bottom of the well but we inspired to him you will surely inform them someday about this affair of theirs while they do not perceive your identity

ayahs.t: And they came to their father at night, weeping.
ayahs.n: and they came to their father at night weeping

ayahs.t: They said, "O our father, indeed we went racing each other and left Joseph with our possessions, and a wolf ate him. But you would not believe us, even if we were truthful."
ayahs.n: they said o our father indeed we went racing each other and left joseph with our possessions and a wolf ate him but you would not believe us even if we were truthful

ayahs.t: And they brought upon his shirt false blood. [Jacob] said, "Rather, your souls have enticed you to something, so patience is most fitting. And Allah is the one sought for help against that which you describe."
ayahs.n: and they brought upon his shirt false blood jacob said rather your souls have enticed you to something so patience is most fitting and allah is the one sought for help against that which you describe

ayahs.t: And there came a company of travelers; then they sent their water drawer, and he let down his bucket. He said, "Good news! Here is a boy." And they concealed him, [taking him] as merchandise; and Allah was knowing of what they did.
ayahs.n: and there came a company of travelers then they sent their water drawer and he let down his bucket he said good news here is a boy and they concealed him taking him as merchandise and allah was knowing of what they did

ayahs.t: And they sold him for a reduced price - a few dirhams - and they were, concerning him, of those content with little.
ayahs.n: and they sold him for a reduced price a few dirhams and they were concerning him of those content with little

ayahs.t: And the one from Egypt who bought him said to his wife, "Make his residence comfortable. Perhaps he will benefit us, or we will adopt him as a son." And thus, We established Joseph in the land that We might teach him the interpretation of events. And Allah is predominant over His affair, but most of the people do not know.
ayahs.n: and the one from egypt who bought him said to his wife make his residence comfortable perhaps he will benefit us or we will adopt him as a son and thus we established joseph in the land that we might teach him the interpretation of events and allah is predominant over his affair but most of the people do not know

ayahs.t: And when Joseph reached maturity, We gave him judgment and knowledge. And thus We reward the doers of good.
ayahs.n: and when joseph reached maturity we gave him judgment and knowledge and thus we reward the doers of good

ayahs.t: And she, in whose house he was, sought to seduce him. She closed the doors and said, "Come, you." He said, "[I seek] the refuge of Allah. Indeed, he is my master, who has made good my residence. Indeed, wrongdoers will not succeed."
ayahs.n: and she in whose house he was sought to seduce him she closed the doors and said come you he said i seek the refuge of allah indeed he is my master who has made good my residence indeed wrongdoers will not succeed

ayahs.t: And she certainly determined [to seduce] him, and he would have inclined to her had he not seen the proof of his Lord. And thus [it was] that We should avert from him evil and immorality. Indeed, he was of Our chosen servants.
ayahs.n: and she certainly determined to seduce him and he would have inclined to her had he not seen the proof of his lord and thus it was that we should avert from him evil and immorality indeed he was of our chosen servants

ayahs.t: And they both raced to the door, and she tore his shirt from the back, and they found her husband at the door. She said, "What is the recompense of one who intended evil for your wife but that he be imprisoned or a painful punishment?"
ayahs.n: and they both raced to the door and she tore his shirt from the back and they found her husband at the door she said what is the recompense of one who intended evil for your wife but that he be imprisoned or a painful punishment

ayahs.t: [Joseph] said, "It was she who sought to seduce me." And a witness from her family testified. "If his shirt is torn from the front, then she has told the truth, and he is of the liars.
ayahs.n: joseph said it was she who sought to seduce me and a witness from her family testified if his shirt is torn from the front then she has told the truth and he is of the liars

ayahs.t: But if his shirt is torn from the back, then she has lied, and he is of the truthful."
ayahs.n: but if his shirt is torn from the back then she has lied and he is of the truthful

ayahs.t: So when her husband saw his shirt torn from the back, he said, "Indeed, it is of the women's plan. Indeed, your plan is great.
ayahs.n: so when her husband saw his shirt torn from the back he said indeed it is of the womens plan indeed your plan is great

ayahs.t: Joseph, ignore this. And, [my wife], ask forgiveness for your sin. Indeed, you were of the sinful."
ayahs.n: joseph ignore this and my wife ask forgiveness for your sin indeed you were of the sinful

ayahs.t: And women in the city said, "The wife of al-'Azeez is seeking to seduce her slave boy; he has impassioned her with love. Indeed, we see her [to be] in clear error."
ayahs.n: and women in the city said the wife of al azeez is seeking to seduce her slave boy he has impassioned her with love indeed we see her to be in clear error

ayahs.t: So when she heard of their scheming, she sent for them and prepared for them a banquet and gave each one of them a knife and said [to Joseph], "Come out before them." And when they saw him, they greatly admired him and cut their hands and said, "Perfect is Allah! This is not a man; this is none but a noble angel."
ayahs.n: so when she heard of their scheming she sent for them and prepared for them a banquet and gave each one of them a knife and said to joseph come out before them and when they saw him they greatly admired him and cut their hands and said perfect is allah this is not a man this is none but a noble angel

ayahs.t: She said, "That is the one about whom you blamed me. And I certainly sought to seduce him, but he firmly refused; and if he will not do what I order him, he will surely be imprisoned and will be of those debased."
ayahs.n: she said that is the one about whom you blamed me and i certainly sought to seduce him but he firmly refused and if he will not do what i order him he will surely be imprisoned and will be of those debased

ayahs.t: He said, "My Lord, prison is more to my liking than that to which they invite me. And if You do not avert from me their plan, I might incline toward them and [thus] be of the ignorant."
ayahs.n: he said my lord prison is more to my liking than that to which they invite me and if you do not avert from me their plan i might incline toward them and thus be of the ignorant

ayahs.t: So his Lord responded to him and averted from him their plan. Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
ayahs.n: so his lord responded to him and averted from him their plan indeed he is the hearing the knowing

ayahs.t: Then it appeared to them after they had seen the signs that al-'Azeez should surely imprison him for a time.
ayahs.n: then it appeared to them after they had seen the signs that al azeez should surely imprison him for a time

ayahs.t: And there entered the prison with him two young men. One of them said, "Indeed, I have seen myself [in a dream] pressing wine." The other said, "Indeed, I have seen myself carrying upon my head [some] bread, from which the birds were eating. Inform us of its interpretation; indeed, we see you to be of those who do good."
ayahs.n: and there entered the prison with him two young men one of them said indeed i have seen myself in a dream pressing wine the other said indeed i have seen myself carrying upon my head some bread from which the birds were eating inform us of its interpretation indeed we see you to be of those who do good

ayahs.t: He said, "You will not receive food that is provided to you except that I will inform you of its interpretation before it comes to you. That is from what my Lord has taught me. Indeed, I have left the religion of a people who do not believe in Allah, and they, in the Hereafter, are disbelievers.
ayahs.n: he said you will not receive food that is provided to you except that i will inform you of its interpretation before it comes to you that is from what my lord has taught me indeed i have left the religion of a people who do not believe in allah and they in the hereafter are disbelievers

ayahs.t: And I have followed the religion of my fathers, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob. And it was not for us to associate anything with Allah. That is from the favor of Allah upon us and upon the people, but most of the people are not grateful.
ayahs.n: and i have followed the religion of my fathers abraham isaac and jacob and it was not for us to associate anything with allah that is from the favor of allah upon us and upon the people but most of the people are not grateful

ayahs.t: O [my] two companions of prison, are separate lords better or Allah, the One, the Prevailing?
ayahs.n: o my two companions of prison are separate lords better or allah the one the prevailing

ayahs.t: You worship not besides Him except [mere] names you have named them, you and your fathers, for which Allah has sent down no authority. Legislation is not but for Allah. He has commanded that you worship not except Him. That is the correct religion, but most of the people do not know.
ayahs.n: you worship not besides him except mere names you have named them you and your fathers for which allah has sent down no authority legislation is not but for allah he has commanded that you worship not except him that is the correct religion but most of the people do not know

ayahs.t: O two companions of prison, as for one of you, he will give drink to his master of wine; but as for the other, he will be crucified, and the birds will eat from his head. The matter has been decreed about which you both inquire."
ayahs.n: o two companions of prison as for one of you he will give drink to his master of wine but as for the other he will be crucified and the birds will eat from his head the matter has been decreed about which you both inquire

ayahs.t: And he said to the one whom he knew would go free, "Mention me before your master." But Satan made him forget the mention [to] his master, and Joseph remained in prison several years.
ayahs.n: and he said to the one whom he knew would go free mention me before your master but satan made him forget the mention to his master and joseph remained in prison several years

ayahs.t: And [subsequently] the king said, "Indeed, I have seen [in a dream] seven fat cows being eaten by seven [that were] lean, and seven green spikes [of grain] and others [that were] dry. O eminent ones, explain to me my vision, if you should interpret visions."
ayahs.n: and subsequently the king said indeed i have seen in a dream seven fat cows being eaten by seven that were lean and seven green spikes of grain and others that were dry o eminent ones explain to me my vision if you should interpret visions

ayahs.t: They said, "[It is but] a mixture of false dreams, and we are not learned in the interpretation of dreams."
ayahs.n: they said it is but a mixture of false dreams and we are not learned in the interpretation of dreams

ayahs.t: But the one who was freed and remembered after a time said, "I will inform you of its interpretation, so send me forth."
ayahs.n: but the one who was freed and remembered after a time said i will inform you of its interpretation so send me forth

ayahs.t: [He said], "Joseph, O man of truth, explain to us about seven fat cows eaten by seven [that were] lean, and seven green spikes [of grain] and others [that were] dry - that I may return to the people; perhaps they will know [about you]."
ayahs.n: he said joseph o man of truth explain to us about seven fat cows eaten by seven that were lean and seven green spikes of grain and others that were dry that i may return to the people perhaps they will know about you

ayahs.t: [Joseph] said, "You will plant for seven years consecutively; and what you harvest leave in its spikes, except a little from which you will eat.
ayahs.n: joseph said you will plant for seven years consecutively and what you harvest leave in its spikes except a little from which you will eat

ayahs.t: Then will come after that seven difficult [years] which will consume what you saved for them, except a little from which you will store.
ayahs.n: then will come after that seven difficult years which will consume what you saved for them except a little from which you will store

ayahs.t: Then will come after that a year in which the people will be given rain and in which they will press [olives and grapes]."
ayahs.n: then will come after that a year in which the people will be given rain and in which they will press olives and grapes

ayahs.t: And the king said, "Bring him to me." But when the messenger came to him, [Joseph] said, "Return to your master and ask him what is the case of the women who cut their hands. Indeed, my Lord is Knowing of their plan."
ayahs.n: and the king said bring him to me but when the messenger came to him joseph said return to your master and ask him what is the case of the women who cut their hands indeed my lord is knowing of their plan

ayahs.t: Said [the king to the women], "What was your condition when you sought to seduce Joseph?" They said, "Perfect is Allah! We know about him no evil." The wife of al-'Azeez said, "Now the truth has become evident. It was I who sought to seduce him, and indeed, he is of the truthful.
ayahs.n: said the king to the women what was your condition when you sought to seduce joseph they said perfect is allah we know about him no evil the wife of al azeez said now the truth has become evident it was i who sought to seduce him and indeed he is of the truthful

ayahs.t: That is so al-'Azeez will know that I did not betray him in [his] absence and that Allah does not guide the plan of betrayers.
ayahs.n: that is so al azeez will know that i did not betray him in his absence and that allah does not guide the plan of betrayers

ayahs.t: And I do not acquit myself. Indeed, the soul is a persistent enjoiner of evil, except those upon which my Lord has mercy. Indeed, my Lord is Forgiving and Merciful."
ayahs.n: and i do not acquit myself indeed the soul is a persistent enjoiner of evil except those upon which my lord has mercy indeed my lord is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: And the king said, "Bring him to me; I will appoint him exclusively for myself." And when he spoke to him, he said, "Indeed, you are today established [in position] and trusted."
ayahs.n: and the king said bring him to me i will appoint him exclusively for myself and when he spoke to him he said indeed you are today established in position and trusted

ayahs.t: [Joseph] said, "Appoint me over the storehouses of the land. Indeed, I will be a knowing guardian."
ayahs.n: joseph said appoint me over the storehouses of the land indeed i will be a knowing guardian

ayahs.t: And thus We established Joseph in the land to settle therein wherever he willed. We touch with Our mercy whom We will, and We do not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good.
ayahs.n: and thus we established joseph in the land to settle therein wherever he willed we touch with our mercy whom we will and we do not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good

ayahs.t: And the reward of the Hereafter is better for those who believed and were fearing Allah.
ayahs.n: and the reward of the hereafter is better for those who believed and were fearing allah

ayahs.t: And the brothers of Joseph came [seeking food], and they entered upon him; and he recognized them, but he was to them unknown.
ayahs.n: and the brothers of joseph came seeking food and they entered upon him and he recognized them but he was to them unknown

ayahs.t: And when he had furnished them with their supplies, he said, "Bring me a brother of yours from your father. Do not you see that I give full measure and that I am the best of accommodators?
ayahs.n: and when he had furnished them with their supplies he said bring me a brother of yours from your father do not you see that i give full measure and that i am the best of accommodators

ayahs.t: But if you do not bring him to me, no measure will there be [hereafter] for you from me, nor will you approach me."
ayahs.n: but if you do not bring him to me no measure will there be hereafter for you from me nor will you approach me

ayahs.t: They said, "We will attempt to dissuade his father from [keeping] him, and indeed, we will do [it]."
ayahs.n: they said we will attempt to dissuade his father from keeping him and indeed we will do it

ayahs.t: And [Joseph] said to his servants, "Put their merchandise into their saddlebags so they might recognize it when they have gone back to their people that perhaps they will [again] return."
ayahs.n: and joseph said to his servants put their merchandise into their saddlebags so they might recognize it when they have gone back to their people that perhaps they will again return

ayahs.t: So when they returned to their father, they said, "O our father, [further] measure has been denied to us, so send with us our brother [that] we will be given measure. And indeed, we will be his guardians."
ayahs.n: so when they returned to their father they said o our father further measure has been denied to us so send with us our brother that we will be given measure and indeed we will be his guardians

ayahs.t: He said, "Should I entrust you with him except [under coercion] as I entrusted you with his brother before? But Allah is the best guardian, and He is the most merciful of the merciful."
ayahs.n: he said should i entrust you with him except under coercion as i entrusted you with his brother before but allah is the best guardian and he is the most merciful of the merciful

ayahs.t: And when they opened their baggage, they found their merchandise returned to them. They said, "O our father, what [more] could we desire? This is our merchandise returned to us. And we will obtain supplies for our family and protect our brother and obtain an increase of a camel's load; that is an easy measurement."
ayahs.n: and when they opened their baggage they found their merchandise returned to them they said o our father what more could we desire this is our merchandise returned to us and we will obtain supplies for our family and protect our brother and obtain an increase of a camels load that is an easy measurement

ayahs.t: [Jacob] said, "Never will I send him with you until you give me a promise by Allah that you will bring him [back] to me, unless you should be surrounded by enemies." And when they had given their promise, he said, "Allah, over what we say, is Witness."
ayahs.n: jacob said never will i send him with you until you give me a promise by allah that you will bring him back to me unless you should be surrounded by enemies and when they had given their promise he said allah over what we say is witness

ayahs.t: And he said, "O my sons, do not enter from one gate but enter from different gates; and I cannot avail you against [the decree of] Allah at all. The decision is only for Allah; upon Him I have relied, and upon Him let those who would rely [indeed] rely."
ayahs.n: and he said o my sons do not enter from one gate but enter from different gates and i cannot avail you against the decree of allah at all the decision is only for allah upon him i have relied and upon him let those who would rely indeed rely

ayahs.t: And when they entered from where their father had ordered them, it did not avail them against Allah at all except [it was] a need within the soul of Jacob, which he satisfied. And indeed, he was a possessor of knowledge because of what We had taught him, but most of the people do not know.
ayahs.n: and when they entered from where their father had ordered them it did not avail them against allah at all except it was a need within the soul of jacob which he satisfied and indeed he was a possessor of knowledge because of what we had taught him but most of the people do not know

ayahs.t: And when they entered upon Joseph, he took his brother to himself; he said, "Indeed, I am your brother, so do not despair over what they used to do [to me]."
ayahs.n: and when they entered upon joseph he took his brother to himself he said indeed i am your brother so do not despair over what they used to do to me

ayahs.t: So when he had furnished them with their supplies, he put the [gold measuring] bowl into the bag of his brother. Then an announcer called out, "O caravan, indeed you are thieves."
ayahs.n: so when he had furnished them with their supplies he put the gold measuring bowl into the bag of his brother then an announcer called out o caravan indeed you are thieves

ayahs.t: They said while approaching them, "What is it you are missing?"
ayahs.n: they said while approaching them what is it you are missing

ayahs.t: They said, "We are missing the measure of the king. And for he who produces it is [the reward of] a camel's load, and I am responsible for it."
ayahs.n: they said we are missing the measure of the king and for he who produces it is the reward of a camels load and i am responsible for it

ayahs.t: They said, "By Allah, you have certainly known that we did not come to cause corruption in the land, and we have not been thieves."
ayahs.n: they said by allah you have certainly known that we did not come to cause corruption in the land and we have not been thieves

ayahs.t: The accusers said, "Then what would be its recompense if you should be liars?"
ayahs.n: the accusers said then what would be its recompense if you should be liars

ayahs.t: [The brothers] said, "Its recompense is that he in whose bag it is found - he [himself] will be its recompense. Thus do we recompense the wrongdoers."
ayahs.n: the brothers said its recompense is that he in whose bag it is found he himself will be its recompense thus do we recompense the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: So he began [the search] with their bags before the bag of his brother; then he extracted it from the bag of his brother. Thus did We plan for Joseph. He could not have taken his brother within the religion of the king except that Allah willed. We raise in degrees whom We will, but over every possessor of knowledge is one [more] knowing.
ayahs.n: so he began the search with their bags before the bag of his brother then he extracted it from the bag of his brother thus did we plan for joseph he could not have taken his brother within the religion of the king except that allah willed we raise in degrees whom we will but over every possessor of knowledge is one more knowing

ayahs.t: They said, "If he steals - a brother of his has stolen before." But Joseph kept it within himself and did not reveal it to them. He said, "You are worse in position, and Allah is most knowing of what you describe."
ayahs.n: they said if he steals a brother of his has stolen before but joseph kept it within himself and did not reveal it to them he said you are worse in position and allah is most knowing of what you describe

ayahs.t: They said, "O 'Azeez, indeed he has a father [who is] an old man, so take one of us in place of him. Indeed, we see you as a doer of good."
ayahs.n: they said o azeez indeed he has a father who is an old man so take one of us in place of him indeed we see you as a doer of good

ayahs.t: He said, "[I seek] the refuge of Allah [to prevent] that we take except him with whom we found our possession. Indeed, we would then be unjust."
ayahs.n: he said i seek the refuge of allah to prevent that we take except him with whom we found our possession indeed we would then be unjust

ayahs.t: So when they had despaired of him, they secluded themselves in private consultation. The eldest of them said, "Do you not know that your father has taken upon you an oath by Allah and [that] before you failed in [your duty to] Joseph? So I will never leave [this] land until my father permits me or Allah decides for me, and He is the best of judges.
ayahs.n: so when they had despaired of him they secluded themselves in private consultation the eldest of them said do you not know that your father has taken upon you an oath by allah and that before you failed in your duty to joseph so i will never leave this land until my father permits me or allah decides for me and he is the best of judges

ayahs.t: Return to your father and say, "O our father, indeed your son has stolen, and we did not testify except to what we knew. And we were not witnesses of the unseen,
ayahs.n: return to your father and say o our father indeed your son has stolen and we did not testify except to what we knew and we were not witnesses of the unseen

ayahs.t: And ask the city in which we were and the caravan in which we came - and indeed, we are truthful,"
ayahs.n: and ask the city in which we were and the caravan in which we came and indeed we are truthful

ayahs.t: [Jacob] said, "Rather, your souls have enticed you to something, so patience is most fitting. Perhaps Allah will bring them to me all together. Indeed it is He who is the Knowing, the Wise."
ayahs.n: jacob said rather your souls have enticed you to something so patience is most fitting perhaps allah will bring them to me all together indeed it is he who is the knowing the wise

ayahs.t: And he turned away from them and said, "Oh, my sorrow over Joseph," and his eyes became white from grief, for he was [of that] a suppressor.
ayahs.n: and he turned away from them and said oh my sorrow over joseph and his eyes became white from grief for he was of that a suppressor

ayahs.t: They said, "By Allah, you will not cease remembering Joseph until you become fatally ill or become of those who perish."
ayahs.n: they said by allah you will not cease remembering joseph until you become fatally ill or become of those who perish

ayahs.t: He said, "I only complain of my suffering and my grief to Allah, and I know from Allah that which you do not know.
ayahs.n: he said i only complain of my suffering and my grief to allah and i know from allah that which you do not know

ayahs.t: O my sons, go and find out about Joseph and his brother and despair not of relief from Allah. Indeed, no one despairs of relief from Allah except the disbelieving people."
ayahs.n: o my sons go and find out about joseph and his brother and despair not of relief from allah indeed no one despairs of relief from allah except the disbelieving people

ayahs.t: So when they entered upon Joseph, they said, "O 'Azeez, adversity has touched us and our family, and we have come with goods poor in quality, but give us full measure and be charitable to us. Indeed, Allah rewards the charitable."
ayahs.n: so when they entered upon joseph they said o azeez adversity has touched us and our family and we have come with goods poor in quality but give us full measure and be charitable to us indeed allah rewards the charitable

ayahs.t: He said, "Do you know what you did with Joseph and his brother when you were ignorant?"
ayahs.n: he said do you know what you did with joseph and his brother when you were ignorant

ayahs.t: They said, "Are you indeed Joseph?" He said "I am Joseph, and this is my brother. Allah has certainly favored us. Indeed, he who fears Allah and is patient, then indeed, Allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good."
ayahs.n: they said are you indeed joseph he said i am joseph and this is my brother allah has certainly favored us indeed he who fears allah and is patient then indeed allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good

ayahs.t: They said, "By Allah, certainly has Allah preferred you over us, and indeed, we have been sinners."
ayahs.n: they said by allah certainly has allah preferred you over us and indeed we have been sinners

ayahs.t: He said, "No blame will there be upon you today. Allah will forgive you; and He is the most merciful of the merciful."
ayahs.n: he said no blame will there be upon you today allah will forgive you and he is the most merciful of the merciful

ayahs.t: Take this, my shirt, and cast it over the face of my father; he will become seeing. And bring me your family, all together."
ayahs.n: take this my shirt and cast it over the face of my father he will become seeing and bring me your family all together

ayahs.t: And when the caravan departed [from Egypt], their father said, "Indeed, I find the smell of Joseph [and would say that he was alive] if you did not think me weakened in mind."
ayahs.n: and when the caravan departed from egypt their father said indeed i find the smell of joseph and would say that he was alive if you did not think me weakened in mind

ayahs.t: They said, "By Allah, indeed you are in your [same] old error."
ayahs.n: they said by allah indeed you are in your same old error

ayahs.t: And when the bearer of good tidings arrived, he cast it over his face, and he returned [once again] seeing. He said, "Did I not tell you that I know from Allah that which you do not know?"
ayahs.n: and when the bearer of good tidings arrived he cast it over his face and he returned once again seeing he said did i not tell you that i know from allah that which you do not know

ayahs.t: They said, "O our father, ask for us forgiveness of our sins; indeed, we have been sinners."
ayahs.n: they said o our father ask for us forgiveness of our sins indeed we have been sinners

ayahs.t: He said, "I will ask forgiveness for you from my Lord. Indeed, it is He who is the Forgiving, the Merciful."
ayahs.n: he said i will ask forgiveness for you from my lord indeed it is he who is the forgiving the merciful

ayahs.t: And when they entered upon Joseph, he took his parents to himself and said, "Enter Egypt, Allah willing, safe [and secure]."
ayahs.n: and when they entered upon joseph he took his parents to himself and said enter egypt allah willing safe and secure

ayahs.t: And he raised his parents upon the throne, and they bowed to him in prostration. And he said, "O my father, this is the explanation of my vision of before. My Lord has made it reality. And He was certainly good to me when He took me out of prison and brought you [here] from bedouin life after Satan had induced [estrangement] between me and my brothers. Indeed, my Lord is Subtle in what He wills. Indeed, it is He who is the Knowing, the Wise.
ayahs.n: and he raised his parents upon the throne and they bowed to him in prostration and he said o my father this is the explanation of my vision of before my lord has made it reality and he was certainly good to me when he took me out of prison and brought you here from bedouin life after satan had induced estrangement between me and my brothers indeed my lord is subtle in what he wills indeed it is he who is the knowing the wise

ayahs.t: My Lord, You have given me [something] of sovereignty and taught me of the interpretation of dreams. Creator of the heavens and earth, You are my protector in this world and in the Hereafter. Cause me to die a Muslim and join me with the righteous."
ayahs.n: my lord you have given me something of sovereignty and taught me of the interpretation of dreams creator of the heavens and earth you are my protector in this world and in the hereafter cause me to die a muslim and join me with the righteous

ayahs.t: That is from the news of the unseen which We reveal, [O Muhammad], to you. And you were not with them when they put together their plan while they conspired.
ayahs.n: that is from the news of the unseen which we reveal o muhammad to you and you were not with them when they put together their plan while they conspired

ayahs.t: And most of the people, although you strive [for it], are not believers.
ayahs.n: and most of the people although you strive for it are not believers

ayahs.t: And you do not ask of them for it any payment. It is not except a reminder to the worlds.
ayahs.n: and you do not ask of them for it any payment it is not except a reminder to the worlds

ayahs.t: And how many a sign within the heavens and earth do they pass over while they, therefrom, are turning away.
ayahs.n: and how many a sign within the heavens and earth do they pass over while they therefrom are turning away

ayahs.t: And most of them believe not in Allah except while they associate others with Him.
ayahs.n: and most of them believe not in allah except while they associate others with him

ayahs.t: Then do they feel secure that there will not come to them an overwhelming [aspect] of the punishment of Allah or that the Hour will not come upon them suddenly while they do not perceive?
ayahs.n: then do they feel secure that there will not come to them an overwhelming aspect of the punishment of allah or that the hour will not come upon them suddenly while they do not perceive

ayahs.t: Say, "This is my way; I invite to Allah with insight, I and those who follow me. And exalted is Allah; and I am not of those who associate others with Him."
ayahs.n: say this is my way i invite to allah with insight i and those who follow me and exalted is allah and i am not of those who associate others with him

ayahs.t: And We sent not before you [as messengers] except men to whom We revealed from among the people of cities.
