ayahs.n: and allah presents an example of two men one of them dumb and unable to do a thing while he is a burden to his guardian wherever he directs him he brings no good is he equal to one who commands justice while he is on a straight path

ayahs.t: And to Allah belongs the unseen [aspects] of the heavens and the earth. And the command for the Hour is not but as a glance of the eye or even nearer. Indeed, Allah is over all things competent.
ayahs.n: and to allah belongs the unseen aspects of the heavens and the earth and the command for the hour is not but as a glance of the eye or even nearer indeed allah is over all things competent

ayahs.t: And Allah has extracted you from the wombs of your mothers not knowing a thing, and He made for you hearing and vision and intellect that perhaps you would be grateful.
ayahs.n: and allah has extracted you from the wombs of your mothers not knowing a thing and he made for you hearing and vision and intellect that perhaps you would be grateful

ayahs.t: Do they not see the birds controlled in the atmosphere of the sky? None holds them up except Allah. Indeed in that are signs for a people who believe.
ayahs.n: do they not see the birds controlled in the atmosphere of the sky none holds them up except allah indeed in that are signs for a people who believe

ayahs.t: And Allah has made for you from your homes a place of rest and made for you from the hides of the animals tents which you find light on your day of travel and your day of encampment; and from their wool, fur and hair is furnishing and enjoyment for a time.
ayahs.n: and allah has made for you from your homes a place of rest and made for you from the hides of the animals tents which you find light on your day of travel and your day of encampment and from their wool fur and hair is furnishing and enjoyment for a time

ayahs.t: And Allah has made for you, from that which He has created, shadows and has made for you from the mountains, shelters and has made for you garments which protect you from the heat and garments which protect you from your [enemy in] battle. Thus does He complete His favor upon you that you might submit [to Him].
ayahs.n: and allah has made for you from that which he has created shadows and has made for you from the mountains shelters and has made for you garments which protect you from the heat and garments which protect you from your enemy in battle thus does he complete his favor upon you that you might submit to him

ayahs.t: But if they turn away, [O Muhammad] - then only upon you is [responsibility for] clear notification.
ayahs.n: but if they turn away o muhammad then only upon you is responsibility for clear notification

ayahs.t: They recognize the favor of Allah; then they deny it. And most of them are disbelievers.
ayahs.n: they recognize the favor of allah then they deny it and most of them are disbelievers

ayahs.t: And [mention] the Day when We will resurrect from every nation a witness. Then it will not be permitted to the disbelievers [to apologize or make excuses], nor will they be asked to appease [Allah].
ayahs.n: and mention the day when we will resurrect from every nation a witness then it will not be permitted to the disbelievers to apologize or make excuses nor will they be asked to appease allah

ayahs.t: And when those who wronged see the punishment, it will not be lightened for them, nor will they be reprieved.
ayahs.n: and when those who wronged see the punishment it will not be lightened for them nor will they be reprieved

ayahs.t: And when those who associated others with Allah see their "partners," they will say," Our Lord, these are our partners [to You] whom we used to invoke besides You." But they will throw at them the statement, "Indeed, you are liars."
ayahs.n: and when those who associated others with allah see their partners they will say our lord these are our partners to you whom we used to invoke besides you but they will throw at them the statement indeed you are liars

ayahs.t: And they will impart to Allah that Day [their] submission, and lost from them is what they used to invent.
ayahs.n: and they will impart to allah that day their submission and lost from them is what they used to invent

ayahs.t: Those who disbelieved and averted [others] from the way of Allah - We will increase them in punishment over [their] punishment for what corruption they were causing.
ayahs.n: those who disbelieved and averted others from the way of allah we will increase them in punishment over their punishment for what corruption they were causing

ayahs.t: And [mention] the Day when We will resurrect among every nation a witness over them from themselves. And We will bring you, [O Muhammad], as a witness over your nation. And We have sent down to you the Book as clarification for all things and as guidance and mercy and good tidings for the Muslims.
ayahs.n: and mention the day when we will resurrect among every nation a witness over them from themselves and we will bring you o muhammad as a witness over your nation and we have sent down to you the book as clarification for all things and as guidance and mercy and good tidings for the muslims

ayahs.t: Indeed, Allah orders justice and good conduct and giving to relatives and forbids immorality and bad conduct and oppression. He admonishes you that perhaps you will be reminded.
ayahs.n: indeed allah orders justice and good conduct and giving to relatives and forbids immorality and bad conduct and oppression he admonishes you that perhaps you will be reminded

ayahs.t: And fulfill the covenant of Allah when you have taken it, [O believers], and do not break oaths after their confirmation while you have made Allah, over you, a witness. Indeed, Allah knows what you do.
ayahs.n: and fulfill the covenant of allah when you have taken it o believers and do not break oaths after their confirmation while you have made allah over you a witness indeed allah knows what you do

ayahs.t: And do not be like she who untwisted her spun thread after it was strong [by] taking your oaths as [means of] deceit between you because one community is more plentiful [in number or wealth] than another community. Allah only tries you thereby. And He will surely make clear to you on the Day of Resurrection that over which you used to differ.
ayahs.n: and do not be like she who untwisted her spun thread after it was strong by taking your oaths as means of deceit between you because one community is more plentiful in number or wealth than another community allah only tries you thereby and he will surely make clear to you on the day of resurrection that over which you used to differ

ayahs.t: And if Allah had willed, He could have made you [of] one religion, but He causes to stray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. And you will surely be questioned about what you used to do.
ayahs.n: and if allah had willed he could have made you of one religion but he causes to stray whom he wills and guides whom he wills and you will surely be questioned about what you used to do

ayahs.t: And do not take your oaths as [means of] deceit between you, lest a foot slip after it was [once] firm, and you would taste evil [in this world] for what [people] you diverted from the way of Allah, and you would have [in the Hereafter] a great punishment.
ayahs.n: and do not take your oaths as means of deceit between you lest a foot slip after it was once firm and you would taste evil in this world for what people you diverted from the way of allah and you would have in the hereafter a great punishment

ayahs.t: And do not exchange the covenant of Allah for a small price. Indeed, what is with Allah is best for you, if only you could know.
ayahs.n: and do not exchange the covenant of allah for a small price indeed what is with allah is best for you if only you could know

ayahs.t: Whatever you have will end, but what Allah has is lasting. And We will surely give those who were patient their reward according to the best of what they used to do.
ayahs.n: whatever you have will end but what allah has is lasting and we will surely give those who were patient their reward according to the best of what they used to do

ayahs.t: Whoever does righteousness, whether male or female, while he is a believer - We will surely cause him to live a good life, and We will surely give them their reward [in the Hereafter] according to the best of what they used to do.
ayahs.n: whoever does righteousness whether male or female while he is a believer we will surely cause him to live a good life and we will surely give them their reward in the hereafter according to the best of what they used to do

ayahs.t: So when you recite the Qur'an, [first] seek refuge in Allah from Satan, the expelled [from His mercy].
ayahs.n: so when you recite the quran first seek refuge in allah from satan the expelled from his mercy

ayahs.t: Indeed, there is for him no authority over those who have believed and rely upon their Lord.
ayahs.n: indeed there is for him no authority over those who have believed and rely upon their lord

ayahs.t: His authority is only over those who take him as an ally and those who through him associate others with Allah.
ayahs.n: his authority is only over those who take him as an ally and those who through him associate others with allah

ayahs.t: And when We substitute a verse in place of a verse - and Allah is most knowing of what He sends down - they say, "You, [O Muhammad], are but an inventor [of lies]." But most of them do not know.
ayahs.n: and when we substitute a verse in place of a verse and allah is most knowing of what he sends down they say you o muhammad are but an inventor of lies but most of them do not know

ayahs.t: Say, [O Muhammad], "The Pure Spirit has brought it down from your Lord in truth to make firm those who believe and as guidance and good tidings to the Muslims."
ayahs.n: say o muhammad the pure spirit has brought it down from your lord in truth to make firm those who believe and as guidance and good tidings to the muslims

ayahs.t: And We certainly know that they say, "It is only a human being who teaches the Prophet." The tongue of the one they refer to is foreign, and this Qur'an is [in] a clear Arabic language.
ayahs.n: and we certainly know that they say it is only a human being who teaches the prophet the tongue of the one they refer to is foreign and this quran is in a clear arabic language

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who do not believe in the verses of Allah - Allah will not guide them, and for them is a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: indeed those who do not believe in the verses of allah allah will not guide them and for them is a painful punishment

ayahs.t: They only invent falsehood who do not believe in the verses of Allah, and it is those who are the liars.
ayahs.n: they only invent falsehood who do not believe in the verses of allah and it is those who are the liars

ayahs.t: Whoever disbelieves in Allah after his belief... except for one who is forced [to renounce his religion] while his heart is secure in faith. But those who [willingly] open their breasts to disbelief, upon them is wrath from Allah, and for them is a great punishment;
ayahs.n: whoever disbelieves in allah after his belief except for one who is forced to renounce his religion while his heart is secure in faith but those who willingly open their breasts to disbelief upon them is wrath from allah and for them is a great punishment

ayahs.t: That is because they preferred the worldly life over the Hereafter and that Allah does not guide the disbelieving people.
ayahs.n: that is because they preferred the worldly life over the hereafter and that allah does not guide the disbelieving people

ayahs.t: Those are the ones over whose hearts and hearing and vision Allah has sealed, and it is those who are the heedless.
ayahs.n: those are the ones over whose hearts and hearing and vision allah has sealed and it is those who are the heedless

ayahs.t: Assuredly, it is they, in the Hereafter, who will be the losers.
ayahs.n: assuredly it is they in the hereafter who will be the losers

ayahs.t: Then, indeed your Lord, to those who emigrated after they had been compelled [to renounce their religion] and thereafter fought [for the cause of Allah] and were patient - indeed, your Lord, after that, is Forgiving and Merciful
ayahs.n: then indeed your lord to those who emigrated after they had been compelled to renounce their religion and thereafter fought for the cause of allah and were patient indeed your lord after that is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: On the Day when every soul will come disputing for itself, and every soul will be fully compensated for what it did, and they will not be wronged.
ayahs.n: on the day when every soul will come disputing for itself and every soul will be fully compensated for what it did and they will not be wronged

ayahs.t: And Allah presents an example: a city which was safe and secure, its provision coming to it in abundance from every location, but it denied the favors of Allah. So Allah made it taste the envelopment of hunger and fear for what they had been doing.
ayahs.n: and allah presents an example a city which was safe and secure its provision coming to it in abundance from every location but it denied the favors of allah so allah made it taste the envelopment of hunger and fear for what they had been doing

ayahs.t: And there had certainly come to them a Messenger from among themselves, but they denied him; so punishment overtook them while they were wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: and there had certainly come to them a messenger from among themselves but they denied him so punishment overtook them while they were wrongdoers

ayahs.t: Then eat of what Allah has provided for you [which is] lawful and good. And be grateful for the favor of Allah, if it is [indeed] Him that you worship.
ayahs.n: then eat of what allah has provided for you which is lawful and good and be grateful for the favor of allah if it is indeed him that you worship

ayahs.t: He has only forbidden to you dead animals, blood, the flesh of swine, and that which has been dedicated to other than Allah. But whoever is forced [by necessity], neither desiring [it] nor transgressing [its limit] - then indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: he has only forbidden to you dead animals blood the flesh of swine and that which has been dedicated to other than allah but whoever is forced by necessity neither desiring it nor transgressing its limit then indeed allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: And do not say about what your tongues assert of untruth, "This is lawful and this is unlawful," to invent falsehood about Allah. Indeed, those who invent falsehood about Allah will not succeed.
ayahs.n: and do not say about what your tongues assert of untruth this is lawful and this is unlawful to invent falsehood about allah indeed those who invent falsehood about allah will not succeed

ayahs.t: [It is but] a brief enjoyment, and they will have a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: it is but a brief enjoyment and they will have a painful punishment

ayahs.t: And to those who are Jews We have prohibited that which We related to you before. And We did not wrong them [thereby], but they were wronging themselves.
ayahs.n: and to those who are jews we have prohibited that which we related to you before and we did not wrong them thereby but they were wronging themselves

ayahs.t: Then, indeed your Lord, to those who have done wrong out of ignorance and then repent after that and correct themselves - indeed, your Lord, thereafter, is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: then indeed your lord to those who have done wrong out of ignorance and then repent after that and correct themselves indeed your lord thereafter is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: Indeed, Abraham was a [comprehensive] leader, devoutly obedient to Allah, inclining toward truth, and he was not of those who associate others with Allah.
ayahs.n: indeed abraham was a comprehensive leader devoutly obedient to allah inclining toward truth and he was not of those who associate others with allah

ayahs.t: [He was] grateful for His favors. Allah chose him and guided him to a straight path.
ayahs.n: he was grateful for his favors allah chose him and guided him to a straight path

ayahs.t: And We gave him good in this world, and indeed, in the Hereafter he will be among the righteous.
ayahs.n: and we gave him good in this world and indeed in the hereafter he will be among the righteous

ayahs.t: Then We revealed to you, [O Muhammad], to follow the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth; and he was not of those who associate with Allah.
ayahs.n: then we revealed to you o muhammad to follow the religion of abraham inclining toward truth and he was not of those who associate with allah

ayahs.t: The sabbath was only appointed for those who differed over it. And indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ.
ayahs.n: the sabbath was only appointed for those who differed over it and indeed your lord will judge between them on the day of resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ

ayahs.t: Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good instruction, and argue with them in a way that is best. Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has strayed from His way, and He is most knowing of who is [rightly] guided.
ayahs.n: invite to the way of your lord with wisdom and good instruction and argue with them in a way that is best indeed your lord is most knowing of who has strayed from his way and he is most knowing of who is rightly guided

ayahs.t: And if you punish [an enemy, O believers], punish with an equivalent of that with which you were harmed. But if you are patient - it is better for those who are patient.
ayahs.n: and if you punish an enemy o believers punish with an equivalent of that with which you were harmed but if you are patient it is better for those who are patient

ayahs.t: And be patient, [O Muhammad], and your patience is not but through Allah. And do not grieve over them and do not be in distress over what they conspire.
ayahs.n: and be patient o muhammad and your patience is not but through allah and do not grieve over them and do not be in distress over what they conspire

ayahs.t: Indeed, Allah is with those who fear Him and those who are doers of good.
ayahs.n: indeed allah is with those who fear him and those who are doers of good

ayahs.t: Exalted is He who took His Servant by night from al-Masjid al-Haram to al-Masjid al-Aqsa, whose surroundings We have blessed, to show him of Our signs. Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Seeing.
ayahs.n: exalted is he who took his servant by night from al masjid al haram to al masjid al aqsa whose surroundings we have blessed to show him of our signs indeed he is the hearing the seeing

ayahs.t: And We gave Moses the Scripture and made it a guidance for the Children of Israel that you not take other than Me as Disposer of affairs,
ayahs.n: and we gave moses the scripture and made it a guidance for the children of israel that you not take other than me as disposer of affairs

ayahs.t: O descendants of those We carried [in the ship] with Noah. Indeed, he was a grateful servant.
ayahs.n: o descendants of those we carried in the ship with noah indeed he was a grateful servant

ayahs.t: And We conveyed to the Children of Israel in the Scripture that, "You will surely cause corruption on the earth twice, and you will surely reach [a degree of] great haughtiness.
ayahs.n: and we conveyed to the children of israel in the scripture that you will surely cause corruption on the earth twice and you will surely reach a degree of great haughtiness

ayahs.t: So when the [time of] promise came for the first of them, We sent against you servants of Ours - those of great military might, and they probed [even] into the homes, and it was a promise fulfilled.
ayahs.n: so when the time of promise came for the first of them we sent against you servants of ours those of great military might and they probed even into the homes and it was a promise fulfilled

ayahs.t: Then We gave back to you a return victory over them. And We reinforced you with wealth and sons and made you more numerous in manpower
ayahs.n: then we gave back to you a return victory over them and we reinforced you with wealth and sons and made you more numerous in manpower

ayahs.t: [And said], "If you do good, you do good for yourselves; and if you do evil, [you do it] to yourselves." Then when the final promise came, [We sent your enemies] to sadden your faces and to enter the temple in Jerusalem, as they entered it the first time, and to destroy what they had taken over with [total] destruction.
ayahs.n: and said if you do good you do good for yourselves and if you do evil you do it to yourselves then when the final promise came we sent your enemies to sadden your faces and to enter the temple in jerusalem as they entered it the first time and to destroy what they had taken over with total destruction

ayahs.t: [Then Allah said], "It is expected, [if you repent], that your Lord will have mercy upon you. But if you return [to sin], We will return [to punishment]. And We have made Hell, for the disbelievers, a prison-bed."
ayahs.n: then allah said it is expected if you repent that your lord will have mercy upon you but if you return to sin we will return to punishment and we have made hell for the disbelievers a prison bed

ayahs.t: Indeed, this Qur'an guides to that which is most suitable and gives good tidings to the believers who do righteous deeds that they will have a great reward.
ayahs.n: indeed this quran guides to that which is most suitable and gives good tidings to the believers who do righteous deeds that they will have a great reward

ayahs.t: And that those who do not believe in the Hereafter - We have prepared for them a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: and that those who do not believe in the hereafter we have prepared for them a painful punishment

ayahs.t: And man supplicates for evil as he supplicates for good, and man is ever hasty.
ayahs.n: and man supplicates for evil as he supplicates for good and man is ever hasty

ayahs.t: And We have made the night and day two signs, and We erased the sign of the night and made the sign of the day visible that you may seek bounty from your Lord and may know the number of years and the account [of time]. And everything We have set out in detail.
ayahs.n: and we have made the night and day two signs and we erased the sign of the night and made the sign of the day visible that you may seek bounty from your lord and may know the number of years and the account of time and everything we have set out in detail

ayahs.t: And [for] every person We have imposed his fate upon his neck, and We will produce for him on the Day of Resurrection a record which he will encounter spread open.
ayahs.n: and for every person we have imposed his fate upon his neck and we will produce for him on the day of resurrection a record which he will encounter spread open

ayahs.t: [It will be said], "Read your record. Sufficient is yourself against you this Day as accountant."
ayahs.n: it will be said read your record sufficient is yourself against you this day as accountant

ayahs.t: Whoever is guided is only guided for [the benefit of] his soul. And whoever errs only errs against it. And no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another. And never would We punish until We sent a messenger.
ayahs.n: whoever is guided is only guided for the benefit of his soul and whoever errs only errs against it and no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another and never would we punish until we sent a messenger

ayahs.t: And when We intend to destroy a city, We command its affluent but they defiantly disobey therein; so the word comes into effect upon it, and We destroy it with [complete] destruction.
ayahs.n: and when we intend to destroy a city we command its affluent but they defiantly disobey therein so the word comes into effect upon it and we destroy it with complete destruction

ayahs.t: And how many have We destroyed from the generations after Noah. And sufficient is your Lord, concerning the sins of His servants, as Acquainted and Seeing.
ayahs.n: and how many have we destroyed from the generations after noah and sufficient is your lord concerning the sins of his servants as acquainted and seeing

ayahs.t: Whoever should desire the immediate - We hasten for him from it what We will to whom We intend. Then We have made for him Hell, which he will [enter to] burn, censured and banished.
ayahs.n: whoever should desire the immediate we hasten for him from it what we will to whom we intend then we have made for him hell which he will enter to burn censured and banished

ayahs.t: But whoever desires the Hereafter and exerts the effort due to it while he is a believer - it is those whose effort is ever appreciated [by Allah].
ayahs.n: but whoever desires the hereafter and exerts the effort due to it while he is a believer it is those whose effort is ever appreciated by allah

ayahs.t: To each [category] We extend - to these and to those - from the gift of your Lord. And never has the gift of your Lord been restricted.
ayahs.n: to each category we extend to these and to those from the gift of your lord and never has the gift of your lord been restricted

ayahs.t: Look how We have favored [in provision] some of them over others. But the Hereafter is greater in degrees [of difference] and greater in distinction.
ayahs.n: look how we have favored in provision some of them over others but the hereafter is greater in degrees of difference and greater in distinction

ayahs.t: Do not make [as equal] with Allah another deity and [thereby] become censured and forsaken.
ayahs.n: do not make as equal with allah another deity and thereby become censured and forsaken

ayahs.t: And your Lord has decreed that you not worship except Him, and to parents, good treatment. Whether one or both of them reach old age [while] with you, say not to them [so much as], "uff," and do not repel them but speak to them a noble word.
ayahs.n: and your lord has decreed that you not worship except him and to parents good treatment whether one or both of them reach old age while with you say not to them so much as uff and do not repel them but speak to them a noble word

ayahs.t: And lower to them the wing of humility out of mercy and say, "My Lord, have mercy upon them as they brought me up [when I was] small."
ayahs.n: and lower to them the wing of humility out of mercy and say my lord have mercy upon them as they brought me up when i was small

ayahs.t: Your Lord is most knowing of what is within yourselves. If you should be righteous [in intention] - then indeed He is ever, to the often returning [to Him], Forgiving.
ayahs.n: your lord is most knowing of what is within yourselves if you should be righteous in intention then indeed he is ever to the often returning to him forgiving

ayahs.t: And give the relative his right, and [also] the poor and the traveler, and do not spend wastefully.
ayahs.n: and give the relative his right and also the poor and the traveler and do not spend wastefully

ayahs.t: Indeed, the wasteful are brothers of the devils, and ever has Satan been to his Lord ungrateful.
ayahs.n: indeed the wasteful are brothers of the devils and ever has satan been to his lord ungrateful

ayahs.t: And if you [must] turn away from the needy awaiting mercy from your Lord which you expect, then speak to them a gentle word.
ayahs.n: and if you must turn away from the needy awaiting mercy from your lord which you expect then speak to them a gentle word

ayahs.t: And do not make your hand [as] chained to your neck or extend it completely and [thereby] become blamed and insolvent.
ayahs.n: and do not make your hand as chained to your neck or extend it completely and thereby become blamed and insolvent

ayahs.t: Indeed, your Lord extends provision for whom He wills and restricts [it]. Indeed He is ever, concerning His servants, Acquainted and Seeing.
ayahs.n: indeed your lord extends provision for whom he wills and restricts it indeed he is ever concerning his servants acquainted and seeing

ayahs.t: And do not kill your children for fear of poverty. We provide for them and for you. Indeed, their killing is ever a great sin.
ayahs.n: and do not kill your children for fear of poverty we provide for them and for you indeed their killing is ever a great sin

ayahs.t: And do not approach unlawful sexual intercourse. Indeed, it is ever an immorality and is evil as a way.
ayahs.n: and do not approach unlawful sexual intercourse indeed it is ever an immorality and is evil as a way

ayahs.t: And do not kill the soul which Allah has forbidden, except by right. And whoever is killed unjustly - We have given his heir authority, but let him not exceed limits in [the matter of] taking life. Indeed, he has been supported [by the law].
ayahs.n: and do not kill the soul which allah has forbidden except by right and whoever is killed unjustly we have given his heir authority but let him not exceed limits in the matter of taking life indeed he has been supported by the law

ayahs.t: And do not approach the property of an orphan, except in the way that is best, until he reaches maturity. And fulfill [every] commitment. Indeed, the commitment is ever [that about which one will be] questioned.
ayahs.n: and do not approach the property of an orphan except in the way that is best until he reaches maturity and fulfill every commitment indeed the commitment is ever that about which one will be questioned

ayahs.t: And give full measure when you measure, and weigh with an even balance. That is the best [way] and best in result.
ayahs.n: and give full measure when you measure and weigh with an even balance that is the best way and best in result

ayahs.t: And do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge. Indeed, the hearing, the sight and the heart - about all those [one] will be questioned.
ayahs.n: and do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge indeed the hearing the sight and the heart about all those one will be questioned

ayahs.t: And do not walk upon the earth exultantly. Indeed, you will never tear the earth [apart], and you will never reach the mountains in height.
ayahs.n: and do not walk upon the earth exultantly indeed you will never tear the earth apart and you will never reach the mountains in height

ayahs.t: All that - its evil is ever, in the sight of your Lord, detested.
ayahs.n: all that its evil is ever in the sight of your lord detested

ayahs.t: That is from what your Lord has revealed to you, [O Muhammad], of wisdom. And, [O mankind], do not make [as equal] with Allah another deity, lest you be thrown into Hell, blamed and banished.
ayahs.n: that is from what your lord has revealed to you o muhammad of wisdom and o mankind do not make as equal with allah another deity lest you be thrown into hell blamed and banished

ayahs.t: Then, has your Lord chosen you for [having] sons and taken from among the angels daughters? Indeed, you say a grave saying.
ayahs.n: then has your lord chosen you for having sons and taken from among the angels daughters indeed you say a grave saying

ayahs.t: And We have certainly diversified [the contents] in this Qur'an that mankind may be reminded, but it does not increase the disbelievers except in aversion.
ayahs.n: and we have certainly diversified the contents in this quran that mankind may be reminded but it does not increase the disbelievers except in aversion

ayahs.t: Say, [O Muhammad], "If there had been with Him [other] gods, as they say, then they [each] would have sought to the Owner of the Throne a way."
ayahs.n: say o muhammad if there had been with him other gods as they say then they each would have sought to the owner of the throne a way

ayahs.t: Exalted is He and high above what they say by great sublimity.
ayahs.n: exalted is he and high above what they say by great sublimity

ayahs.t: The seven heavens and the earth and whatever is in them exalt Him. And there is not a thing except that it exalts [Allah] by His praise, but you do not understand their [way of] exalting. Indeed, He is ever Forbearing and Forgiving.
ayahs.n: the seven heavens and the earth and whatever is in them exalt him and there is not a thing except that it exalts allah by his praise but you do not understand their way of exalting indeed he is ever forbearing and forgiving

ayahs.t: And when you recite the Qur'an, We put between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter a concealed partition.
ayahs.n: and when you recite the quran we put between you and those who do not believe in the hereafter a concealed partition

ayahs.t: And We have placed over their hearts coverings, lest they understand it, and in their ears deafness. And when you mention your Lord alone in the Qur'an, they turn back in aversion.
ayahs.n: and we have placed over their hearts coverings lest they understand it and in their ears deafness and when you mention your lord alone in the quran they turn back in aversion

ayahs.t: We are most knowing of how they listen to it when they listen to you and [of] when they are in private conversation, when the wrongdoers say, "You follow not but a man affected by magic."
ayahs.n: we are most knowing of how they listen to it when they listen to you and of when they are in private conversation when the wrongdoers say you follow not but a man affected by magic

ayahs.t: Look how they strike for you comparisons; but they have strayed, so they cannot [find] a way.
ayahs.n: look how they strike for you comparisons but they have strayed so they cannot find a way

ayahs.t: And they say, "When we are bones and crumbled particles, will we [truly] be resurrected as a new creation?"
ayahs.n: and they say when we are bones and crumbled particles will we truly be resurrected as a new creation

ayahs.t: Say, "Be you stones or iron
ayahs.n: say be you stones or iron

ayahs.t: Or [any] creation of that which is great within your breasts." And they will say, "Who will restore us?" Say, "He who brought you forth the first time." Then they will nod their heads toward you and say, "When is that?" Say, "Perhaps it will be soon -
ayahs.n: or any creation of that which is great within your breasts and they will say who will restore us say he who brought you forth the first time then they will nod their heads toward you and say when is that say perhaps it will be soon

ayahs.t: On the Day He will call you and you will respond with praise of Him and think that you had not remained [in the world] except for a little."
ayahs.n: on the day he will call you and you will respond with praise of him and think that you had not remained in the world except for a little

ayahs.t: And tell My servants to say that which is best. Indeed, Satan induces [dissension] among them. Indeed Satan is ever, to mankind, a clear enemy.
ayahs.n: and tell my servants to say that which is best indeed satan induces dissension among them indeed satan is ever to mankind a clear enemy

ayahs.t: Your Lord is most knowing of you. If He wills, He will have mercy upon you; or if He wills, He will punish you. And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], over them as a manager.
ayahs.n: your lord is most knowing of you if he wills he will have mercy upon you or if he wills he will punish you and we have not sent you o muhammad over them as a manager

ayahs.t: And your Lord is most knowing of whoever is in the heavens and the earth. And We have made some of the prophets exceed others [in various ways], and to David We gave the book [of Psalms].
ayahs.n: and your lord is most knowing of whoever is in the heavens and the earth and we have made some of the prophets exceed others in various ways and to david we gave the book of psalms

ayahs.t: Say, "Invoke those you have claimed [as gods] besides Him, for they do not possess the [ability for] removal of adversity from you or [for its] transfer [to someone else]."
ayahs.n: say invoke those you have claimed as gods besides him for they do not possess the ability for removal of adversity from you or for its transfer to someone else

ayahs.t: Those whom they invoke seek means of access to their Lord, [striving as to] which of them would be nearest, and they hope for His mercy and fear His punishment. Indeed, the punishment of your Lord is ever feared.
ayahs.n: those whom they invoke seek means of access to their lord striving as to which of them would be nearest and they hope for his mercy and fear his punishment indeed the punishment of your lord is ever feared

ayahs.t: And there is no city but that We will destroy it before the Day of Resurrection or punish it with a severe punishment. That has ever been in the Register inscribed.
ayahs.n: and there is no city but that we will destroy it before the day of resurrection or punish it with a severe punishment that has ever been in the register inscribed

ayahs.t: And nothing has prevented Us from sending signs except that the former peoples denied them. And We gave Thamud the she-camel as a visible sign, but they wronged her. And We send not the signs except as a warning.
ayahs.n: and nothing has prevented us from sending signs except that the former peoples denied them and we gave thamud the she camel as a visible sign but they wronged her and we send not the signs except as a warning

ayahs.t: And [remember, O Muhammad], when We told you, "Indeed, your Lord has encompassed the people." And We did not make the sight which We showed you except as a trial for the people, as was the accursed tree [mentioned] in the Qur'an. And We threaten them, but it increases them not except in great transgression.
ayahs.n: and remember o muhammad when we told you indeed your lord has encompassed the people and we did not make the sight which we showed you except as a trial for the people as was the accursed tree mentioned in the quran and we threaten them but it increases them not except in great transgression

ayahs.t: And [mention] when We said to the angles, "Prostrate to Adam," and they prostrated, except for Iblees. He said, "Should I prostrate to one You created from clay?"
ayahs.n: and mention when we said to the angles prostrate to adam and they prostrated except for iblees he said should i prostrate to one you created from clay

ayahs.t: [Iblees] said, "Do You see this one whom You have honored above me? If You delay me until the Day of Resurrection, I will surely destroy his descendants, except for a few."
ayahs.n: iblees said do you see this one whom you have honored above me if you delay me until the day of resurrection i will surely destroy his descendants except for a few

ayahs.t: [Allah] said, "Go, for whoever of them follows you, indeed Hell will be the recompense of you - an ample recompense.
ayahs.n: allah said go for whoever of them follows you indeed hell will be the recompense of you an ample recompense

ayahs.t: And incite [to senselessness] whoever you can among them with your voice and assault them with your horses and foot soldiers and become a partner in their wealth and their children and promise them." But Satan does not promise them except delusion.
ayahs.n: and incite to senselessness whoever you can among them with your voice and assault them with your horses and foot soldiers and become a partner in their wealth and their children and promise them but satan does not promise them except delusion

ayahs.t: Indeed, over My [believing] servants there is for you no authority. And sufficient is your Lord as Disposer of affairs.
ayahs.n: indeed over my believing servants there is for you no authority and sufficient is your lord as disposer of affairs

ayahs.t: It is your Lord who drives the ship for you through the sea that you may seek of His bounty. Indeed, He is ever, to you, Merciful.
ayahs.n: it is your lord who drives the ship for you through the sea that you may seek of his bounty indeed he is ever to you merciful

ayahs.t: And when adversity touches you at sea, lost are [all] those you invoke except for Him. But when He delivers you to the land, you turn away [from Him]. And ever is man ungrateful.
ayahs.n: and when adversity touches you at sea lost are all those you invoke except for him but when he delivers you to the land you turn away from him and ever is man ungrateful

ayahs.t: Then do you feel secure that [instead] He will not cause a part of the land to swallow you or send against you a storm of stones? Then you would not find for yourselves an advocate.
ayahs.n: then do you feel secure that instead he will not cause a part of the land to swallow you or send against you a storm of stones then you would not find for yourselves an advocate

ayahs.t: Or do you feel secure that He will not send you back into the sea another time and send upon you a hurricane of wind and drown you for what you denied? Then you would not find for yourselves against Us an avenger.
ayahs.n: or do you feel secure that he will not send you back into the sea another time and send upon you a hurricane of wind and drown you for what you denied then you would not find for yourselves against us an avenger

ayahs.t: And We have certainly honored the children of Adam and carried them on the land and sea and provided for them of the good things and preferred them over much of what We have created, with [definite] preference.
ayahs.n: and we have certainly honored the children of adam and carried them on the land and sea and provided for them of the good things and preferred them over much of what we have created with definite preference

ayahs.t: [Mention, O Muhammad], the Day We will call forth every people with their record [of deeds]. Then whoever is given his record in his right hand - those will read their records, and injustice will not be done to them, [even] as much as a thread [inside the date seed].
ayahs.n: mention o muhammad the day we will call forth every people with their record of deeds then whoever is given his record in his right hand those will read their records and injustice will not be done to them even as much as a thread inside the date seed

ayahs.t: And whoever is blind in this [life] will be blind in the Hereafter and more astray in way.
ayahs.n: and whoever is blind in this life will be blind in the hereafter and more astray in way

ayahs.t: And indeed, they were about to tempt you away from that which We revealed to you in order to [make] you invent about Us something else; and then they would have taken you as a friend.
ayahs.n: and indeed they were about to tempt you away from that which we revealed to you in order to make you invent about us something else and then they would have taken you as a friend

ayahs.t: And if We had not strengthened you, you would have almost inclined to them a little.
ayahs.n: and if we had not strengthened you you would have almost inclined to them a little

ayahs.t: Then [if you had], We would have made you taste double [punishment in] life and double [after] death. Then you would not find for yourself against Us a helper.
ayahs.n: then if you had we would have made you taste double punishment in life and double after death then you would not find for yourself against us a helper

ayahs.t: And indeed, they were about to drive you from the land to evict you therefrom. And then [when they do], they will not remain [there] after you, except for a little.
ayahs.n: and indeed they were about to drive you from the land to evict you therefrom and then when they do they will not remain there after you except for a little

ayahs.t: [That is Our] established way for those We had sent before you of Our messengers; and you will not find in Our way any alteration.
ayahs.n: that is our established way for those we had sent before you of our messengers and you will not find in our way any alteration

ayahs.t: Establish prayer at the decline of the sun [from its meridian] until the darkness of the night and [also] the Qur'an of dawn. Indeed, the recitation of dawn is ever witnessed.
ayahs.n: establish prayer at the decline of the sun from its meridian until the darkness of the night and also the quran of dawn indeed the recitation of dawn is ever witnessed

ayahs.t: And from [part of] the night, pray with it as additional [worship] for you; it is expected that your Lord will resurrect you to a praised station.
ayahs.n: and from part of the night pray with it as additional worship for you it is expected that your lord will resurrect you to a praised station

ayahs.t: And say, "My Lord, cause me to enter a sound entrance and to exit a sound exit and grant me from Yourself a supporting authority."
ayahs.n: and say my lord cause me to enter a sound entrance and to exit a sound exit and grant me from yourself a supporting authority

ayahs.t: And say, "Truth has come, and falsehood has departed. Indeed is falsehood, [by nature], ever bound to depart."
ayahs.n: and say truth has come and falsehood has departed indeed is falsehood by nature ever bound to depart

ayahs.t: And We send down of the Qur'an that which is healing and mercy for the believers, but it does not increase the wrongdoers except in loss.
ayahs.n: and we send down of the quran that which is healing and mercy for the believers but it does not increase the wrongdoers except in loss

ayahs.t: And when We bestow favor upon the disbeliever, he turns away and distances himself; and when evil touches him, he is ever despairing.
ayahs.n: and when we bestow favor upon the disbeliever he turns away and distances himself and when evil touches him he is ever despairing

ayahs.t: Say, "Each works according to his manner, but your Lord is most knowing of who is best guided in way."
ayahs.n: say each works according to his manner but your lord is most knowing of who is best guided in way

ayahs.t: And they ask you, [O Muhammad], about the soul. Say, "The soul is of the affair of my Lord. And mankind have not been given of knowledge except a little."
ayahs.n: and they ask you o muhammad about the soul say the soul is of the affair of my lord and mankind have not been given of knowledge except a little

ayahs.t: And if We willed, We could surely do away with that which We revealed to you. Then you would not find for yourself concerning it an advocate against Us.
ayahs.n: and if we willed we could surely do away with that which we revealed to you then you would not find for yourself concerning it an advocate against us

ayahs.t: Except [We have left it with you] as a mercy from your Lord. Indeed, His favor upon you has ever been great.
ayahs.n: except we have left it with you as a mercy from your lord indeed his favor upon you has ever been great

ayahs.t: Say, "If mankind and the jinn gathered in order to produce the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce the like of it, even if they were to each other assistants."
ayahs.n: say if mankind and the jinn gathered in order to produce the like of this quran they could not produce the like of it even if they were to each other assistants

ayahs.t: And We have certainly diversified for the people in this Qur'an from every [kind] of example, but most of the people refused [anything] except disbelief.
ayahs.n: and we have certainly diversified for the people in this quran from every kind of example but most of the people refused anything except disbelief

ayahs.t: And they say, "We will not believe you until you break open for us from the ground a spring.
ayahs.n: and they say we will not believe you until you break open for us from the ground a spring

ayahs.t: Or [until] you have a garden of palm tress and grapes and make rivers gush forth within them in force [and abundance]
ayahs.n: or until you have a garden of palm tress and grapes and make rivers gush forth within them in force and abundance

ayahs.t: Or you make the heaven fall upon us in fragments as you have claimed or you bring Allah and the angels before [us]
ayahs.n: or you make the heaven fall upon us in fragments as you have claimed or you bring allah and the angels before us

ayahs.t: Or you have a house of gold or you ascend into the sky. And [even then], we will not believe in your ascension until you bring down to us a book we may read." Say, "Exalted is my Lord! Was I ever but a human messenger?"
ayahs.n: or you have a house of gold or you ascend into the sky and even then we will not believe in your ascension until you bring down to us a book we may read say exalted is my lord was i ever but a human messenger

ayahs.t: And what prevented the people from believing when guidance came to them except that they said, "Has Allah sent a human messenger?"
ayahs.n: and what prevented the people from believing when guidance came to them except that they said has allah sent a human messenger

ayahs.t: Say, "If there were upon the earth angels walking securely, We would have sent down to them from the heaven an angel [as a] messenger."
ayahs.n: say if there were upon the earth angels walking securely we would have sent down to them from the heaven an angel as a messenger

ayahs.t: Say, "Sufficient is Allah as Witness between me and you. Indeed he is ever, concerning His servants, Acquainted and Seeing."
ayahs.n: say sufficient is allah as witness between me and you indeed he is ever concerning his servants acquainted and seeing

ayahs.t: And whoever Allah guides - he is the [rightly] guided; and whoever He sends astray - you will never find for them protectors besides Him, and We will gather them on the Day of Resurrection [fallen] on their faces - blind, dumb and deaf. Their refuge is Hell; every time it subsides We increase them in blazing fire.
ayahs.n: and whoever allah guides he is the rightly guided and whoever he sends astray you will never find for them protectors besides him and we will gather them on the day of resurrection fallen on their faces blind dumb and deaf their refuge is hell every time it subsides we increase them in blazing fire

ayahs.t: That is their recompense because they disbelieved in Our verses and said, "When we are bones and crumbled particles, will we [truly] be resurrected [in] a new creation?"
ayahs.n: that is their recompense because they disbelieved in our verses and said when we are bones and crumbled particles will we truly be resurrected in a new creation

ayahs.t: Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and earth, is [the one] Able to create the likes of them? And He has appointed for them a term, about which there is no doubt. But the wrongdoers refuse [anything] except disbelief.
ayahs.n: do they not see that allah who created the heavens and earth is the one able to create the likes of them and he has appointed for them a term about which there is no doubt but the wrongdoers refuse anything except disbelief

ayahs.t: Say [to them], "If you possessed the depositories of the mercy of my Lord, then you would withhold out of fear of spending." And ever has man been stingy.
ayahs.n: say to them if you possessed the depositories of the mercy of my lord then you would withhold out of fear of spending and ever has man been stingy

ayahs.t: And We had certainly given Moses nine evident signs, so ask the Children of Israel [about] when he came to them and Pharaoh said to him, "Indeed I think, O Moses, that you are affected by magic."
ayahs.n: and we had certainly given moses nine evident signs so ask the children of israel about when he came to them and pharaoh said to him indeed i think o moses that you are affected by magic

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "You have already known that none has sent down these [signs] except the Lord of the heavens and the earth as evidence, and indeed I think, O Pharaoh, that you are destroyed."
ayahs.n: moses said you have already known that none has sent down these signs except the lord of the heavens and the earth as evidence and indeed i think o pharaoh that you are destroyed

ayahs.t: So he intended to drive them from the land, but We drowned him and those with him all together.
ayahs.n: so he intended to drive them from the land but we drowned him and those with him all together

ayahs.t: And We said after Pharaoh to the Children of Israel, "Dwell in the land, and when there comes the promise of the Hereafter, We will bring you forth in [one] gathering."
ayahs.n: and we said after pharaoh to the children of israel dwell in the land and when there comes the promise of the hereafter we will bring you forth in one gathering

ayahs.t: And with the truth We have sent the Qur'an down, and with the truth it has descended. And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a bringer of good tidings and a warner.
ayahs.n: and with the truth we have sent the quran down and with the truth it has descended and we have not sent you o muhammad except as a bringer of good tidings and a warner

ayahs.t: And [it is] a Qur'an which We have separated [by intervals] that you might recite it to the people over a prolonged period. And We have sent it down progressively.
ayahs.n: and it is a quran which we have separated by intervals that you might recite it to the people over a prolonged period and we have sent it down progressively

ayahs.t: Say, "Believe in it or do not believe. Indeed, those who were given knowledge before it - when it is recited to them, they fall upon their faces in prostration,
ayahs.n: say believe in it or do not believe indeed those who were given knowledge before it when it is recited to them they fall upon their faces in prostration

ayahs.t: And they say, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, the promise of our Lord has been fulfilled."
ayahs.n: and they say exalted is our lord indeed the promise of our lord has been fulfilled

ayahs.t: And they fall upon their faces weeping, and the Qur'an increases them in humble submission.
ayahs.n: and they fall upon their faces weeping and the quran increases them in humble submission

ayahs.t: Say, "Call upon Allah or call upon the Most Merciful. Whichever [name] you call - to Him belong the best names." And do not recite [too] loudly in your prayer or [too] quietly but seek between that an [intermediate] way.
ayahs.n: say call upon allah or call upon the most merciful whichever name you call to him belong the best names and do not recite too loudly in your prayer or too quietly but seek between that an intermediate way

ayahs.t: And say, "Praise to Allah, who has not taken a son and has had no partner in [His] dominion and has no [need of a] protector out of weakness; and glorify Him with [great] glorification."
ayahs.n: and say praise to allah who has not taken a son and has had no partner in his dominion and has no need of a protector out of weakness and glorify him with great glorification

ayahs.t: [All] praise is [due] to Allah, who has sent down upon His Servant the Book and has not made therein any deviance.
ayahs.n: all praise is due to allah who has sent down upon his servant the book and has not made therein any deviance

ayahs.t: [He has made it] straight, to warn of severe punishment from Him and to give good tidings to the believers who do righteous deeds that they will have a good reward
ayahs.n: he has made it straight to warn of severe punishment from him and to give good tidings to the believers who do righteous deeds that they will have a good reward

ayahs.t: In which they will remain forever
ayahs.n: in which they will remain forever

ayahs.t: And to warn those who say, "Allah has taken a son."
ayahs.n: and to warn those who say allah has taken a son

ayahs.t: They have no knowledge of it, nor had their fathers. Grave is the word that comes out of their mouths; they speak not except a lie.
ayahs.n: they have no knowledge of it nor had their fathers grave is the word that comes out of their mouths they speak not except a lie

ayahs.t: Then perhaps you would kill yourself through grief over them, [O Muhammad], if they do not believe in this message, [and] out of sorrow.
ayahs.n: then perhaps you would kill yourself through grief over them o muhammad if they do not believe in this message and out of sorrow

ayahs.t: Indeed, We have made that which is on the earth adornment for it that We may test them [as to] which of them is best in deed.
ayahs.n: indeed we have made that which is on the earth adornment for it that we may test them as to which of them is best in deed

ayahs.t: And indeed, We will make that which is upon it [into] a barren ground.
ayahs.n: and indeed we will make that which is upon it into a barren ground

ayahs.t: Or have you thought that the companions of the cave and the inscription were, among Our signs, a wonder?
ayahs.n: or have you thought that the companions of the cave and the inscription were among our signs a wonder

ayahs.t: [Mention] when the youths retreated to the cave and said, "Our Lord, grant us from Yourself mercy and prepare for us from our affair right guidance."
ayahs.n: mention when the youths retreated to the cave and said our lord grant us from yourself mercy and prepare for us from our affair right guidance

ayahs.t: So We cast [a cover of sleep] over their ears within the cave for a number of years.
ayahs.n: so we cast a cover of sleep over their ears within the cave for a number of years

ayahs.t: Then We awakened them that We might show which of the two factions was most precise in calculating what [extent] they had remained in time.
ayahs.n: then we awakened them that we might show which of the two factions was most precise in calculating what extent they had remained in time

ayahs.t: It is We who relate to you, [O Muhammad], their story in truth. Indeed, they were youths who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.
ayahs.n: it is we who relate to you o muhammad their story in truth indeed they were youths who believed in their lord and we increased them in guidance

ayahs.t: And We made firm their hearts when they stood up and said, "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. Never will we invoke besides Him any deity. We would have certainly spoken, then, an excessive transgression.
ayahs.n: and we made firm their hearts when they stood up and said our lord is the lord of the heavens and the earth never will we invoke besides him any deity we would have certainly spoken then an excessive transgression

ayahs.t: These, our people, have taken besides Him deities. Why do they not bring for [worship of] them a clear authority? And who is more unjust than one who invents about Allah a lie?"
ayahs.n: these our people have taken besides him deities why do they not bring for worship of them a clear authority and who is more unjust than one who invents about allah a lie

ayahs.t: [The youths said to one another], "And when you have withdrawn from them and that which they worship other than Allah, retreat to the cave. Your Lord will spread out for you of His mercy and will prepare for you from your affair facility."
ayahs.n: the youths said to one another and when you have withdrawn from them and that which they worship other than allah retreat to the cave your lord will spread out for you of his mercy and will prepare for you from your affair facility

ayahs.t: And [had you been present], you would see the sun when it rose, inclining away from their cave on the right, and when it set, passing away from them on the left, while they were [laying] within an open space thereof. That was from the signs of Allah. He whom Allah guides is the [rightly] guided, but he whom He leaves astray - never will you find for him a protecting guide.
ayahs.n: and had you been present you would see the sun when it rose inclining away from their cave on the right and when it set passing away from them on the left while they were laying within an open space thereof that was from the signs of allah he whom allah guides is the rightly guided but he whom he leaves astray never will you find for him a protecting guide

ayahs.t: And you would think them awake, while they were asleep. And We turned them to the right and to the left, while their dog stretched his forelegs at the entrance. If you had looked at them, you would have turned from them in flight and been filled by them with terror.
ayahs.n: and you would think them awake while they were asleep and we turned them to the right and to the left while their dog stretched his forelegs at the entrance if you had looked at them you would have turned from them in flight and been filled by them with terror

ayahs.t: And similarly, We awakened them that they might question one another. Said a speaker from among them, "How long have you remained [here]?" They said, "We have remained a day or part of a day." They said, "Your Lord is most knowing of how long you remained. So send one of you with this silver coin of yours to the city and let him look to which is the best of food and bring you provision from it and let him be cautious. And let no one be aware of you.
ayahs.n: and similarly we awakened them that they might question one another said a speaker from among them how long have you remained here they said we have remained a day or part of a day they said your lord is most knowing of how long you remained so send one of you with this silver coin of yours to the city and let him look to which is the best of food and bring you provision from it and let him be cautious and let no one be aware of you

ayahs.t: Indeed, if they come to know of you, they will stone you or return you to their religion. And never would you succeed, then - ever."
ayahs.n: indeed if they come to know of you they will stone you or return you to their religion and never would you succeed then ever

ayahs.t: And similarly, We caused them to be found that they [who found them] would know that the promise of Allah is truth and that of the Hour there is no doubt. [That was] when they disputed among themselves about their affair and [then] said, "Construct over them a structure. Their Lord is most knowing about them." Said those who prevailed in the matter, "We will surely take [for ourselves] over them a masjid."
ayahs.n: and similarly we caused them to be found that they who found them would know that the promise of allah is truth and that of the hour there is no doubt that was when they disputed among themselves about their affair and then said construct over them a structure their lord is most knowing about them said those who prevailed in the matter we will surely take for ourselves over them a masjid

ayahs.t: They will say there were three, the fourth of them being their dog; and they will say there were five, the sixth of them being their dog - guessing at the unseen; and they will say there were seven, and the eighth of them was their dog. Say, [O Muhammad], "My Lord is most knowing of their number. None knows them except a few. So do not argue about them except with an obvious argument and do not inquire about them among [the speculators] from anyone."
ayahs.n: they will say there were three the fourth of them being their dog and they will say there were five the sixth of them being their dog guessing at the unseen and they will say there were seven and the eighth of them was their dog say o muhammad my lord is most knowing of their number none knows them except a few so do not argue about them except with an obvious argument and do not inquire about them among the speculators from anyone

ayahs.t: And never say of anything, "Indeed, I will do that tomorrow,"
ayahs.n: and never say of anything indeed i will do that tomorrow

ayahs.t: Except [when adding], "If Allah wills." And remember your Lord when you forget [it] and say, "Perhaps my Lord will guide me to what is nearer than this to right conduct."
ayahs.n: except when adding if allah wills and remember your lord when you forget it and say perhaps my lord will guide me to what is nearer than this to right conduct

ayahs.t: And they remained in their cave for three hundred years and exceeded by nine.
ayahs.n: and they remained in their cave for three hundred years and exceeded by nine

ayahs.t: Say, "Allah is most knowing of how long they remained. He has [knowledge of] the unseen [aspects] of the heavens and the earth. How Seeing is He and how Hearing! They have not besides Him any protector, and He shares not His legislation with anyone."
ayahs.n: say allah is most knowing of how long they remained he has knowledge of the unseen aspects of the heavens and the earth how seeing is he and how hearing they have not besides him any protector and he shares not his legislation with anyone

ayahs.t: And recite, [O Muhammad], what has been revealed to you of the Book of your Lord. There is no changer of His words, and never will you find in other than Him a refuge.
ayahs.n: and recite o muhammad what has been revealed to you of the book of your lord there is no changer of his words and never will you find in other than him a refuge

ayahs.t: And keep yourself patient [by being] with those who call upon their Lord in the morning and the evening, seeking His countenance. And let not your eyes pass beyond them, desiring adornments of the worldly life, and do not obey one whose heart We have made heedless of Our remembrance and who follows his desire and whose affair is ever [in] neglect.
ayahs.n: and keep yourself patient by being with those who call upon their lord in the morning and the evening seeking his countenance and let not your eyes pass beyond them desiring adornments of the worldly life and do not obey one whose heart we have made heedless of our remembrance and who follows his desire and whose affair is ever in neglect

ayahs.t: And say, "The truth is from your Lord, so whoever wills - let him believe; and whoever wills - let him disbelieve." Indeed, We have prepared for the wrongdoers a fire whose walls will surround them. And if they call for relief, they will be relieved with water like murky oil, which scalds [their] faces. Wretched is the drink, and evil is the resting place.
ayahs.n: and say the truth is from your lord so whoever wills let him believe and whoever wills let him disbelieve indeed we have prepared for the wrongdoers a fire whose walls will surround them and if they call for relief they will be relieved with water like murky oil which scalds their faces wretched is the drink and evil is the resting place

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds - indeed, We will not allow to be lost the reward of any who did well in deeds.
ayahs.n: indeed those who have believed and done righteous deeds indeed we will not allow to be lost the reward of any who did well in deeds

ayahs.t: Those will have gardens of perpetual residence; beneath them rivers will flow. They will be adorned therein with bracelets of gold and will wear green garments of fine silk and brocade, reclining therein on adorned couches. Excellent is the reward, and good is the resting place.
ayahs.n: those will have gardens of perpetual residence beneath them rivers will flow they will be adorned therein with bracelets of gold and will wear green garments of fine silk and brocade reclining therein on adorned couches excellent is the reward and good is the resting place

ayahs.t: And present to them an example of two men: We granted to one of them two gardens of grapevines, and We bordered them with palm trees and placed between them [fields of] crops.
ayahs.n: and present to them an example of two men we granted to one of them two gardens of grapevines and we bordered them with palm trees and placed between them fields of crops

ayahs.t: Each of the two gardens produced its fruit and did not fall short thereof in anything. And We caused to gush forth within them a river.
ayahs.n: each of the two gardens produced its fruit and did not fall short thereof in anything and we caused to gush forth within them a river

ayahs.t: And he had fruit, so he said to his companion while he was conversing with him, "I am greater than you in wealth and mightier in [numbers of] men."
ayahs.n: and he had fruit so he said to his companion while he was conversing with him i am greater than you in wealth and mightier in numbers of men

ayahs.t: And he entered his garden while he was unjust to himself. He said, "I do not think that this will perish - ever.
ayahs.n: and he entered his garden while he was unjust to himself he said i do not think that this will perish ever

ayahs.t: And I do not think the Hour will occur. And even if I should be brought back to my Lord, I will surely find better than this as a return."
ayahs.n: and i do not think the hour will occur and even if i should be brought back to my lord i will surely find better than this as a return

ayahs.t: His companion said to him while he was conversing with him, "Have you disbelieved in He who created you from dust and then from a sperm-drop and then proportioned you [as] a man?
ayahs.n: his companion said to him while he was conversing with him have you disbelieved in he who created you from dust and then from a sperm drop and then proportioned you as a man

ayahs.t: But as for me, He is Allah, my Lord, and I do not associate with my Lord anyone.
ayahs.n: but as for me he is allah my lord and i do not associate with my lord anyone

ayahs.t: And why did you, when you entered your garden, not say, 'What Allah willed [has occurred]; there is no power except in Allah '? Although you see me less than you in wealth and children,
ayahs.n: and why did you when you entered your garden not say what allah willed has occurred there is no power except in allah although you see me less than you in wealth and children

ayahs.t: It may be that my Lord will give me [something] better than your garden and will send upon it a calamity from the sky, and it will become a smooth, dusty ground,
ayahs.n: it may be that my lord will give me something better than your garden and will send upon it a calamity from the sky and it will become a smooth dusty ground

ayahs.t: Or its water will become sunken [into the earth], so you would never be able to seek it."
ayahs.n: or its water will become sunken into the earth so you would never be able to seek it

ayahs.t: And his fruits were encompassed [by ruin], so he began to turn his hands about [in dismay] over what he had spent on it, while it had collapsed upon its trellises, and said, "Oh, I wish I had not associated with my Lord anyone."
ayahs.n: and his fruits were encompassed by ruin so he began to turn his hands about in dismay over what he had spent on it while it had collapsed upon its trellises and said oh i wish i had not associated with my lord anyone

ayahs.t: And there was for him no company to aid him other than Allah, nor could he defend himself.
ayahs.n: and there was for him no company to aid him other than allah nor could he defend himself

ayahs.t: There the authority is [completely] for Allah, the Truth. He is best in reward and best in outcome.
ayahs.n: there the authority is completely for allah the truth he is best in reward and best in outcome

ayahs.t: And present to them the example of the life of this world, [its being] like rain which We send down from the sky, and the vegetation of the earth mingles with it and [then] it becomes dry remnants, scattered by the winds. And Allah is ever, over all things, Perfect in Ability.
ayahs.n: and present to them the example of the life of this world its being like rain which we send down from the sky and the vegetation of the earth mingles with it and then it becomes dry remnants scattered by the winds and allah is ever over all things perfect in ability

ayahs.t: Wealth and children are [but] adornment of the worldly life. But the enduring good deeds are better to your Lord for reward and better for [one's] hope.
ayahs.n: wealth and children are but adornment of the worldly life but the enduring good deeds are better to your lord for reward and better for ones hope

ayahs.t: And [warn of] the Day when We will remove the mountains and you will see the earth prominent, and We will gather them and not leave behind from them anyone.
ayahs.n: and warn of the day when we will remove the mountains and you will see the earth prominent and we will gather them and not leave behind from them anyone

ayahs.t: And they will be presented before your Lord in rows, [and He will say], "You have certainly come to Us just as We created you the first time. But you claimed that We would never make for you an appointment."
ayahs.n: and they will be presented before your lord in rows and he will say you have certainly come to us just as we created you the first time but you claimed that we would never make for you an appointment

ayahs.t: And the record [of deeds] will be placed [open], and you will see the criminals fearful of that within it, and they will say, "Oh, woe to us! What is this book that leaves nothing small or great except that it has enumerated it?" And they will find what they did present [before them]. And your Lord does injustice to no one.
ayahs.n: and the record of deeds will be placed open and you will see the criminals fearful of that within it and they will say oh woe to us what is this book that leaves nothing small or great except that it has enumerated it and they will find what they did present before them and your lord does injustice to no one

ayahs.t: And [mention] when We said to the angels, "Prostrate to Adam," and they prostrated, except for Iblees. He was of the jinn and departed from the command of his Lord. Then will you take him and his descendants as allies other than Me while they are enemies to you? Wretched it is for the wrongdoers as an exchange.
ayahs.n: and mention when we said to the angels prostrate to adam and they prostrated except for iblees he was of the jinn and departed from the command of his lord then will you take him and his descendants as allies other than me while they are enemies to you wretched it is for the wrongdoers as an exchange

ayahs.t: I did not make them witness to the creation of the heavens and the earth or to the creation of themselves, and I would not have taken the misguiders as assistants.
ayahs.n: i did not make them witness to the creation of the heavens and the earth or to the creation of themselves and i would not have taken the misguiders as assistants

ayahs.t: And [warn of] the Day when He will say, "Call 'My partners' whom you claimed," and they will invoke them, but they will not respond to them. And We will put between them [a valley of] destruction.
ayahs.n: and warn of the day when he will say call my partners whom you claimed and they will invoke them but they will not respond to them and we will put between them a valley of destruction

ayahs.t: And the criminals will see the Fire and will be certain that they are to fall therein. And they will not find from it a way elsewhere.
ayahs.n: and the criminals will see the fire and will be certain that they are to fall therein and they will not find from it a way elsewhere

ayahs.t: And We have certainly diversified in this Qur'an for the people from every [kind of] example; but man has ever been, most of anything, [prone to] dispute.
ayahs.n: and we have certainly diversified in this quran for the people from every kind of example but man has ever been most of anything prone to dispute

ayahs.t: And nothing has prevented the people from believing when guidance came to them and from asking forgiveness of their Lord except that there [must] befall them the [accustomed] precedent of the former peoples or that the punishment should come [directly] before them.
ayahs.n: and nothing has prevented the people from believing when guidance came to them and from asking forgiveness of their lord except that there must befall them the accustomed precedent of the former peoples or that the punishment should come directly before them

ayahs.t: And We send not the messengers except as bringers of good tidings and warners. And those who disbelieve dispute by [using] falsehood to [attempt to] invalidate thereby the truth and have taken My verses, and that of which they are warned, in ridicule.
ayahs.n: and we send not the messengers except as bringers of good tidings and warners and those who disbelieve dispute by using falsehood to attempt to invalidate thereby the truth and have taken my verses and that of which they are warned in ridicule

ayahs.t: And who is more unjust than one who is reminded of the verses of his Lord but turns away from them and forgets what his hands have put forth? Indeed, We have placed over their hearts coverings, lest they understand it, and in their ears deafness. And if you invite them to guidance - they will never be guided, then - ever.
ayahs.n: and who is more unjust than one who is reminded of the verses of his lord but turns away from them and forgets what his hands have put forth indeed we have placed over their hearts coverings lest they understand it and in their ears deafness and if you invite them to guidance they will never be guided then ever

ayahs.t: And your Lord is the Forgiving, full of mercy. If He were to impose blame upon them for what they earned, He would have hastened for them the punishment. Rather, for them is an appointment from which they will never find an escape.
ayahs.n: and your lord is the forgiving full of mercy if he were to impose blame upon them for what they earned he would have hastened for them the punishment rather for them is an appointment from which they will never find an escape

ayahs.t: And those cities - We destroyed them when they wronged, and We made for their destruction an appointed time.
ayahs.n: and those cities we destroyed them when they wronged and we made for their destruction an appointed time

ayahs.t: And [mention] when Moses said to his servant, "I will not cease [traveling] until I reach the junction of the two seas or continue for a long period."
ayahs.n: and mention when moses said to his servant i will not cease traveling until i reach the junction of the two seas or continue for a long period

ayahs.t: But when they reached the junction between them, they forgot their fish, and it took its course into the sea, slipping away.
ayahs.n: but when they reached the junction between them they forgot their fish and it took its course into the sea slipping away

ayahs.t: So when they had passed beyond it, [Moses] said to his boy, "Bring us our morning meal. We have certainly suffered in this, our journey, [much] fatigue."
ayahs.n: so when they had passed beyond it moses said to his boy bring us our morning meal we have certainly suffered in this our journey much fatigue

ayahs.t: He said, "Did you see when we retired to the rock? Indeed, I forgot [there] the fish. And none made me forget it except Satan - that I should mention it. And it took its course into the sea amazingly".
ayahs.n: he said did you see when we retired to the rock indeed i forgot there the fish and none made me forget it except satan that i should mention it and it took its course into the sea amazingly

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "That is what we were seeking." So they returned, following their footprints.
ayahs.n: moses said that is what we were seeking so they returned following their footprints

ayahs.t: And they found a servant from among Our servants to whom we had given mercy from us and had taught him from Us a [certain] knowledge.
ayahs.n: and they found a servant from among our servants to whom we had given mercy from us and had taught him from us a certain knowledge

ayahs.t: Moses said to him, "May I follow you on [the condition] that you teach me from what you have been taught of sound judgement?"
ayahs.n: moses said to him may i follow you on the condition that you teach me from what you have been taught of sound judgement
