ayahs.t: He said, "Indeed, with me you will never be able to have patience.
ayahs.n: he said indeed with me you will never be able to have patience

ayahs.t: And how can you have patience for what you do not encompass in knowledge?"
ayahs.n: and how can you have patience for what you do not encompass in knowledge

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "You will find me, if Allah wills, patient, and I will not disobey you in [any] order."
ayahs.n: moses said you will find me if allah wills patient and i will not disobey you in any order

ayahs.t: He said, "Then if you follow me, do not ask me about anything until I make to you about it mention."
ayahs.n: he said then if you follow me do not ask me about anything until i make to you about it mention

ayahs.t: So they set out, until when they had embarked on the ship, al-Khidh r tore it open. [Moses] said, "Have you torn it open to drown its people? You have certainly done a grave thing."
ayahs.n: so they set out until when they had embarked on the ship al khidh r tore it open moses said have you torn it open to drown its people you have certainly done a grave thing

ayahs.t: [Al-Khidh r] said, "Did I not say that with me you would never be able to have patience?"
ayahs.n: al khidh r said did i not say that with me you would never be able to have patience

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "Do not blame me for what I forgot and do not cover me in my matter with difficulty."
ayahs.n: moses said do not blame me for what i forgot and do not cover me in my matter with difficulty

ayahs.t: So they set out, until when they met a boy, al-Khidh r killed him. [Moses] said, "Have you killed a pure soul for other than [having killed] a soul? You have certainly done a deplorable thing."
ayahs.n: so they set out until when they met a boy al khidh r killed him moses said have you killed a pure soul for other than having killed a soul you have certainly done a deplorable thing

ayahs.t: [Al-Khidh r] said, "Did I not tell you that with me you would never be able to have patience?"
ayahs.n: al khidh r said did i not tell you that with me you would never be able to have patience

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "If I should ask you about anything after this, then do not keep me as a companion. You have obtained from me an excuse."
ayahs.n: moses said if i should ask you about anything after this then do not keep me as a companion you have obtained from me an excuse

ayahs.t: So they set out, until when they came to the people of a town, they asked its people for food, but they refused to offer them hospitality. And they found therein a wall about to collapse, so al-Khidh r restored it. [Moses] said, "If you wished, you could have taken for it a payment."
ayahs.n: so they set out until when they came to the people of a town they asked its people for food but they refused to offer them hospitality and they found therein a wall about to collapse so al khidh r restored it moses said if you wished you could have taken for it a payment

ayahs.t: [Al-Khidh r] said, "This is parting between me and you. I will inform you of the interpretation of that about which you could not have patience.
ayahs.n: al khidh r said this is parting between me and you i will inform you of the interpretation of that about which you could not have patience

ayahs.t: As for the ship, it belonged to poor people working at sea. So I intended to cause defect in it as there was after them a king who seized every [good] ship by force.
ayahs.n: as for the ship it belonged to poor people working at sea so i intended to cause defect in it as there was after them a king who seized every good ship by force

ayahs.t: And as for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared that he would overburden them by transgression and disbelief.
ayahs.n: and as for the boy his parents were believers and we feared that he would overburden them by transgression and disbelief

ayahs.t: So we intended that their Lord should substitute for them one better than him in purity and nearer to mercy.
ayahs.n: so we intended that their lord should substitute for them one better than him in purity and nearer to mercy

ayahs.t: And as for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the city, and there was beneath it a treasure for them, and their father had been righteous. So your Lord intended that they reach maturity and extract their treasure, as a mercy from your Lord. And I did it not of my own accord. That is the interpretation of that about which you could not have patience."
ayahs.n: and as for the wall it belonged to two orphan boys in the city and there was beneath it a treasure for them and their father had been righteous so your lord intended that they reach maturity and extract their treasure as a mercy from your lord and i did it not of my own accord that is the interpretation of that about which you could not have patience

ayahs.t: And they ask you, [O Muhammad], about Dhul-Qarnayn. Say, "I will recite to you about him a report."
ayahs.n: and they ask you o muhammad about dhul qarnayn say i will recite to you about him a report

ayahs.t: Indeed We established him upon the earth, and We gave him to everything a way.
ayahs.n: indeed we established him upon the earth and we gave him to everything a way

ayahs.t: So he followed a way
ayahs.n: so he followed a way

ayahs.t: Until, when he reached the setting of the sun, he found it [as if] setting in a spring of dark mud, and he found near it a people. Allah said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, either you punish [them] or else adopt among them [a way of] goodness."
ayahs.n: until when he reached the setting of the sun he found it as if setting in a spring of dark mud and he found near it a people allah said o dhul qarnayn either you punish them or else adopt among them a way of goodness

ayahs.t: He said, "As for one who wrongs, we will punish him. Then he will be returned to his Lord, and He will punish him with a terrible punishment.
ayahs.n: he said as for one who wrongs we will punish him then he will be returned to his lord and he will punish him with a terrible punishment

ayahs.t: But as for one who believes and does righteousness, he will have a reward of Paradise, and we will speak to him from our command with ease."
ayahs.n: but as for one who believes and does righteousness he will have a reward of paradise and we will speak to him from our command with ease

ayahs.t: Then he followed a way
ayahs.n: then he followed a way

ayahs.t: Until, when he came to the rising of the sun, he found it rising on a people for whom We had not made against it any shield.
ayahs.n: until when he came to the rising of the sun he found it rising on a people for whom we had not made against it any shield

ayahs.t: Thus. And We had encompassed [all] that he had in knowledge.
ayahs.n: thus and we had encompassed all that he had in knowledge

ayahs.t: Then he followed a way
ayahs.n: then he followed a way

ayahs.t: Until, when he reached [a pass] between two mountains, he found beside them a people who could hardly understand [his] speech.
ayahs.n: until when he reached a pass between two mountains he found beside them a people who could hardly understand his speech

ayahs.t: They said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, indeed Gog and Magog are [great] corrupters in the land. So may we assign for you an expenditure that you might make between us and them a barrier?"
ayahs.n: they said o dhul qarnayn indeed gog and magog are great corrupters in the land so may we assign for you an expenditure that you might make between us and them a barrier

ayahs.t: He said, "That in which my Lord has established me is better [than what you offer], but assist me with strength; I will make between you and them a dam.
ayahs.n: he said that in which my lord has established me is better than what you offer but assist me with strength i will make between you and them a dam

ayahs.t: Bring me sheets of iron" - until, when he had leveled [them] between the two mountain walls, he said, "Blow [with bellows]," until when he had made it [like] fire, he said, "Bring me, that I may pour over it molten copper."
ayahs.n: bring me sheets of iron until when he had leveled them between the two mountain walls he said blow with bellows until when he had made it like fire he said bring me that i may pour over it molten copper

ayahs.t: So Gog and Magog were unable to pass over it, nor were they able [to effect] in it any penetration.
ayahs.n: so gog and magog were unable to pass over it nor were they able to effect in it any penetration

ayahs.t: [Dhul-Qarnayn] said, "This is a mercy from my Lord; but when the promise of my Lord comes, He will make it level, and ever is the promise of my Lord true."
ayahs.n: dhul qarnayn said this is a mercy from my lord but when the promise of my lord comes he will make it level and ever is the promise of my lord true

ayahs.t: And We will leave them that day surging over each other, and [then] the Horn will be blown, and We will assemble them in [one] assembly.
ayahs.n: and we will leave them that day surging over each other and then the horn will be blown and we will assemble them in one assembly

ayahs.t: And We will present Hell that Day to the Disbelievers, on display -
ayahs.n: and we will present hell that day to the disbelievers on display

ayahs.t: Those whose eyes had been within a cover [removed] from My remembrance, and they were not able to hear.
ayahs.n: those whose eyes had been within a cover removed from my remembrance and they were not able to hear

ayahs.t: Then do those who disbelieve think that they can take My servants instead of Me as allies? Indeed, We have prepared Hell for the disbelievers as a lodging.
ayahs.n: then do those who disbelieve think that they can take my servants instead of me as allies indeed we have prepared hell for the disbelievers as a lodging

ayahs.t: Say, [O Muhammad], "Shall we [believers] inform you of the greatest losers as to [their] deeds?
ayahs.n: say o muhammad shall we believers inform you of the greatest losers as to their deeds

ayahs.t: [They are] those whose effort is lost in worldly life, while they think that they are doing well in work."
ayahs.n: they are those whose effort is lost in worldly life while they think that they are doing well in work

ayahs.t: Those are the ones who disbelieve in the verses of their Lord and in [their] meeting Him, so their deeds have become worthless; and We will not assign to them on the Day of Resurrection any importance.
ayahs.n: those are the ones who disbelieve in the verses of their lord and in their meeting him so their deeds have become worthless and we will not assign to them on the day of resurrection any importance

ayahs.t: That is their recompense - Hell - for what they denied and [because] they took My signs and My messengers in ridicule.
ayahs.n: that is their recompense hell for what they denied and because they took my signs and my messengers in ridicule

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds - they will have the Gardens of Paradise as a lodging,
ayahs.n: indeed those who have believed and done righteous deeds they will have the gardens of paradise as a lodging

ayahs.t: Wherein they abide eternally. They will not desire from it any transfer.
ayahs.n: wherein they abide eternally they will not desire from it any transfer

ayahs.t: Say, "If the sea were ink for [writing] the words of my Lord, the sea would be exhausted before the words of my Lord were exhausted, even if We brought the like of it as a supplement."
ayahs.n: say if the sea were ink for writing the words of my lord the sea would be exhausted before the words of my lord were exhausted even if we brought the like of it as a supplement

ayahs.t: Say, "I am only a man like you, to whom has been revealed that your god is one God. So whoever would hope for the meeting with his Lord - let him do righteous work and not associate in the worship of his Lord anyone."
ayahs.n: say i am only a man like you to whom has been revealed that your god is one god so whoever would hope for the meeting with his lord let him do righteous work and not associate in the worship of his lord anyone

ayahs.t: Kaf, Ha, Ya, 'Ayn, Sad.
ayahs.n: kaf ha ya ayn sad

ayahs.t: [This is] a mention of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zechariah
ayahs.n: this is a mention of the mercy of your lord to his servant zechariah

ayahs.t: When he called to his Lord a private supplication.
ayahs.n: when he called to his lord a private supplication

ayahs.t: He said, "My Lord, indeed my bones have weakened, and my head has filled with white, and never have I been in my supplication to You, my Lord, unhappy.
ayahs.n: he said my lord indeed my bones have weakened and my head has filled with white and never have i been in my supplication to you my lord unhappy

ayahs.t: And indeed, I fear the successors after me, and my wife has been barren, so give me from Yourself an heir
ayahs.n: and indeed i fear the successors after me and my wife has been barren so give me from yourself an heir

ayahs.t: Who will inherit me and inherit from the family of Jacob. And make him, my Lord, pleasing [to You]."
ayahs.n: who will inherit me and inherit from the family of jacob and make him my lord pleasing to you

ayahs.t: [He was told], "O Zechariah, indeed We give you good tidings of a boy whose name will be John. We have not assigned to any before [this] name."
ayahs.n: he was told o zechariah indeed we give you good tidings of a boy whose name will be john we have not assigned to any before this name

ayahs.t: He said, "My Lord, how will I have a boy when my wife has been barren and I have reached extreme old age?"
ayahs.n: he said my lord how will i have a boy when my wife has been barren and i have reached extreme old age

ayahs.t: [An angel] said, "Thus [it will be]; your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me, for I created you before, while you were nothing.' "
ayahs.n: an angel said thus it will be your lord says it is easy for me for i created you before while you were nothing

ayahs.t: [Zechariah] said, "My Lord, make for me a sign." He said, "Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three nights, [being] sound."
ayahs.n: zechariah said my lord make for me a sign he said your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three nights being sound

ayahs.t: So he came out to his people from the prayer chamber and signaled to them to exalt [Allah] in the morning and afternoon.
ayahs.n: so he came out to his people from the prayer chamber and signaled to them to exalt allah in the morning and afternoon

ayahs.t: [Allah] said, "O John, take the Scripture with determination." And We gave him judgement [while yet] a boy
ayahs.n: allah said o john take the scripture with determination and we gave him judgement while yet a boy

ayahs.t: And affection from Us and purity, and he was fearing of Allah
ayahs.n: and affection from us and purity and he was fearing of allah

ayahs.t: And dutiful to his parents, and he was not a disobedient tyrant.
ayahs.n: and dutiful to his parents and he was not a disobedient tyrant

ayahs.t: And peace be upon him the day he was born and the day he dies and the day he is raised alive.
ayahs.n: and peace be upon him the day he was born and the day he dies and the day he is raised alive

ayahs.t: And mention, [O Muhammad], in the Book [the story of] Mary, when she withdrew from her family to a place toward the east.
ayahs.n: and mention o muhammad in the book the story of mary when she withdrew from her family to a place toward the east

ayahs.t: And she took, in seclusion from them, a screen. Then We sent to her Our Angel, and he represented himself to her as a well-proportioned man.
ayahs.n: and she took in seclusion from them a screen then we sent to her our angel and he represented himself to her as a well proportioned man

ayahs.t: She said, "Indeed, I seek refuge in the Most Merciful from you, [so leave me], if you should be fearing of Allah."
ayahs.n: she said indeed i seek refuge in the most merciful from you so leave me if you should be fearing of allah

ayahs.t: He said, "I am only the messenger of your Lord to give you [news of] a pure boy."
ayahs.n: he said i am only the messenger of your lord to give you news of a pure boy

ayahs.t: She said, "How can I have a boy while no man has touched me and I have not been unchaste?"
ayahs.n: she said how can i have a boy while no man has touched me and i have not been unchaste

ayahs.t: He said, "Thus [it will be]; your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me, and We will make him a sign to the people and a mercy from Us. And it is a matter [already] decreed.' "
ayahs.n: he said thus it will be your lord says it is easy for me and we will make him a sign to the people and a mercy from us and it is a matter already decreed

ayahs.t: So she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a remote place.
ayahs.n: so she conceived him and she withdrew with him to a remote place

ayahs.t: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She said, "Oh, I wish I had died before this and was in oblivion, forgotten."
ayahs.n: and the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree she said oh i wish i had died before this and was in oblivion forgotten

ayahs.t: But he called her from below her, "Do not grieve; your Lord has provided beneath you a stream.
ayahs.n: but he called her from below her do not grieve your lord has provided beneath you a stream

ayahs.t: And shake toward you the trunk of the palm tree; it will drop upon you ripe, fresh dates.
ayahs.n: and shake toward you the trunk of the palm tree it will drop upon you ripe fresh dates

ayahs.t: So eat and drink and be contented. And if you see from among humanity anyone, say, 'Indeed, I have vowed to the Most Merciful abstention, so I will not speak today to [any] man.' "
ayahs.n: so eat and drink and be contented and if you see from among humanity anyone say indeed i have vowed to the most merciful abstention so i will not speak today to any man

ayahs.t: Then she brought him to her people, carrying him. They said, "O Mary, you have certainly done a thing unprecedented.
ayahs.n: then she brought him to her people carrying him they said o mary you have certainly done a thing unprecedented

ayahs.t: O sister of Aaron, your father was not a man of evil, nor was your mother unchaste."
ayahs.n: o sister of aaron your father was not a man of evil nor was your mother unchaste

ayahs.t: So she pointed to him. They said, "How can we speak to one who is in the cradle a child?"
ayahs.n: so she pointed to him they said how can we speak to one who is in the cradle a child

ayahs.t: [Jesus] said, "Indeed, I am the servant of Allah. He has given me the Scripture and made me a prophet.
ayahs.n: jesus said indeed i am the servant of allah he has given me the scripture and made me a prophet

ayahs.t: And He has made me blessed wherever I am and has enjoined upon me prayer and zakah as long as I remain alive
ayahs.n: and he has made me blessed wherever i am and has enjoined upon me prayer and zakah as long as i remain alive

ayahs.t: And [made me] dutiful to my mother, and He has not made me a wretched tyrant.
ayahs.n: and made me dutiful to my mother and he has not made me a wretched tyrant

ayahs.t: And peace is on me the day I was born and the day I will die and the day I am raised alive."
ayahs.n: and peace is on me the day i was born and the day i will die and the day i am raised alive

ayahs.t: That is Jesus, the son of Mary - the word of truth about which they are in dispute.
ayahs.n: that is jesus the son of mary the word of truth about which they are in dispute

ayahs.t: It is not [befitting] for Allah to take a son; exalted is He! When He decrees an affair, He only says to it, "Be," and it is.
ayahs.n: it is not befitting for allah to take a son exalted is he when he decrees an affair he only says to it be and it is

ayahs.t: [Jesus said], "And indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. That is a straight path."
ayahs.n: jesus said and indeed allah is my lord and your lord so worship him that is a straight path

ayahs.t: Then the factions differed [concerning Jesus] from among them, so woe to those who disbelieved - from the scene of a tremendous Day.
ayahs.n: then the factions differed concerning jesus from among them so woe to those who disbelieved from the scene of a tremendous day

ayahs.t: How [clearly] they will hear and see the Day they come to Us, but the wrongdoers today are in clear error.
ayahs.n: how clearly they will hear and see the day they come to us but the wrongdoers today are in clear error

ayahs.t: And warn them, [O Muhammad], of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be concluded; and [yet], they are in [a state of] heedlessness, and they do not believe.
ayahs.n: and warn them o muhammad of the day of regret when the matter will be concluded and yet they are in a state of heedlessness and they do not believe

ayahs.t: Indeed, it is We who will inherit the earth and whoever is on it, and to Us they will be returned.
ayahs.n: indeed it is we who will inherit the earth and whoever is on it and to us they will be returned

ayahs.t: And mention in the Book [the story of] Abraham. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet.
ayahs.n: and mention in the book the story of abraham indeed he was a man of truth and a prophet

ayahs.t: [Mention] when he said to his father, "O my father, why do you worship that which does not hear and does not see and will not benefit you at all?
ayahs.n: mention when he said to his father o my father why do you worship that which does not hear and does not see and will not benefit you at all

ayahs.t: O my father, indeed there has come to me of knowledge that which has not come to you, so follow me; I will guide you to an even path.
ayahs.n: o my father indeed there has come to me of knowledge that which has not come to you so follow me i will guide you to an even path

ayahs.t: O my father, do not worship Satan. Indeed Satan has ever been, to the Most Merciful, disobedient.
ayahs.n: o my father do not worship satan indeed satan has ever been to the most merciful disobedient

ayahs.t: O my father, indeed I fear that there will touch you a punishment from the Most Merciful so you would be to Satan a companion [in Hellfire]."
ayahs.n: o my father indeed i fear that there will touch you a punishment from the most merciful so you would be to satan a companion in hellfire

ayahs.t: [His father] said, "Have you no desire for my gods, O Abraham? If you do not desist, I will surely stone you, so avoid me a prolonged time."
ayahs.n: his father said have you no desire for my gods o abraham if you do not desist i will surely stone you so avoid me a prolonged time

ayahs.t: [Abraham] said, "Peace will be upon you. I will ask forgiveness for you of my Lord. Indeed, He is ever gracious to me.
ayahs.n: abraham said peace will be upon you i will ask forgiveness for you of my lord indeed he is ever gracious to me

ayahs.t: And I will leave you and those you invoke other than Allah and will invoke my Lord. I expect that I will not be in invocation to my Lord unhappy."
ayahs.n: and i will leave you and those you invoke other than allah and will invoke my lord i expect that i will not be in invocation to my lord unhappy

ayahs.n: so when he had left them and those they worshipped other than allah we gave him isaac and jacob and each of them we made a prophet

ayahs.t: And We gave them of Our mercy, and we made for them a reputation of high honor.
ayahs.n: and we gave them of our mercy and we made for them a reputation of high honor

ayahs.t: And mention in the Book, Moses. Indeed, he was chosen, and he was a messenger and a prophet.
ayahs.n: and mention in the book moses indeed he was chosen and he was a messenger and a prophet

ayahs.t: And We called him from the side of the mount at [his] right and brought him near, confiding [to him].
ayahs.n: and we called him from the side of the mount at his right and brought him near confiding to him

ayahs.t: And We gave him out of Our mercy his brother Aaron as a prophet.
ayahs.n: and we gave him out of our mercy his brother aaron as a prophet

ayahs.t: And mention in the Book, Ishmael. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and he was a messenger and a prophet.
ayahs.n: and mention in the book ishmael indeed he was true to his promise and he was a messenger and a prophet

ayahs.t: And he used to enjoin on his people prayer and zakah and was to his Lord pleasing.
ayahs.n: and he used to enjoin on his people prayer and zakah and was to his lord pleasing

ayahs.t: And mention in the Book, Idrees. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet.
ayahs.n: and mention in the book idrees indeed he was a man of truth and a prophet

ayahs.t: And We raised him to a high station.
ayahs.n: and we raised him to a high station

ayahs.t: Those were the ones upon whom Allah bestowed favor from among the prophets of the descendants of Adam and of those We carried [in the ship] with Noah, and of the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and of those whom We guided and chose. When the verses of the Most Merciful were recited to them, they fell in prostration and weeping.
ayahs.n: those were the ones upon whom allah bestowed favor from among the prophets of the descendants of adam and of those we carried in the ship with noah and of the descendants of abraham and israel and of those whom we guided and chose when the verses of the most merciful were recited to them they fell in prostration and weeping

ayahs.t: But there came after them successors who neglected prayer and pursued desires; so they are going to meet evil -
ayahs.n: but there came after them successors who neglected prayer and pursued desires so they are going to meet evil

ayahs.t: Except those who repent, believe and do righteousness; for those will enter Paradise and will not be wronged at all.
ayahs.n: except those who repent believe and do righteousness for those will enter paradise and will not be wronged at all

ayahs.t: [Therein are] gardens of perpetual residence which the Most Merciful has promised His servants in the unseen. Indeed, His promise has ever been coming.
ayahs.n: therein are gardens of perpetual residence which the most merciful has promised his servants in the unseen indeed his promise has ever been coming

ayahs.t: They will not hear therein any ill speech - only [greetings of] peace - and they will have their provision therein, morning and afternoon.
ayahs.n: they will not hear therein any ill speech only greetings of peace and they will have their provision therein morning and afternoon

ayahs.t: That is Paradise, which We give as inheritance to those of Our servants who were fearing of Allah.
ayahs.n: that is paradise which we give as inheritance to those of our servants who were fearing of allah

ayahs.t: [Gabriel said], "And we [angels] descend not except by the order of your Lord. To Him belongs that before us and that behind us and what is in between. And never is your Lord forgetful -
ayahs.n: gabriel said and we angels descend not except by the order of your lord to him belongs that before us and that behind us and what is in between and never is your lord forgetful

ayahs.t: Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them - so worship Him and have patience for His worship. Do you know of any similarity to Him?"
ayahs.n: lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them so worship him and have patience for his worship do you know of any similarity to him

ayahs.t: And the disbeliever says, "When I have died, am I going to be brought forth alive?"
ayahs.n: and the disbeliever says when i have died am i going to be brought forth alive

ayahs.t: Does man not remember that We created him before, while he was nothing?
ayahs.n: does man not remember that we created him before while he was nothing

ayahs.t: So by your Lord, We will surely gather them and the devils; then We will bring them to be present around Hell upon their knees.
ayahs.n: so by your lord we will surely gather them and the devils then we will bring them to be present around hell upon their knees

ayahs.t: Then We will surely extract from every sect those of them who were worst against the Most Merciful in insolence.
ayahs.n: then we will surely extract from every sect those of them who were worst against the most merciful in insolence

ayahs.t: Then, surely it is We who are most knowing of those most worthy of burning therein.
ayahs.n: then surely it is we who are most knowing of those most worthy of burning therein

ayahs.t: And there is none of you except he will come to it. This is upon your Lord an inevitability decreed.
ayahs.n: and there is none of you except he will come to it this is upon your lord an inevitability decreed

ayahs.t: Then We will save those who feared Allah and leave the wrongdoers within it, on their knees.
ayahs.n: then we will save those who feared allah and leave the wrongdoers within it on their knees

ayahs.t: And when Our verses are recited to them as clear evidences, those who disbelieve say to those who believe, "Which of [our] two parties is best in position and best in association?"
ayahs.n: and when our verses are recited to them as clear evidences those who disbelieve say to those who believe which of our two parties is best in position and best in association

ayahs.t: And how many a generation have We destroyed before them who were better in possessions and [outward] appearance?
ayahs.n: and how many a generation have we destroyed before them who were better in possessions and outward appearance

ayahs.t: Say, "Whoever is in error - let the Most Merciful extend for him an extension [in wealth and time] until, when they see that which they were promised - either punishment [in this world] or the Hour [of resurrection] - they will come to know who is worst in position and weaker in soldiers."
ayahs.n: say whoever is in error let the most merciful extend for him an extension in wealth and time until when they see that which they were promised either punishment in this world or the hour of resurrection they will come to know who is worst in position and weaker in soldiers

ayahs.t: And Allah increases those who were guided, in guidance, and the enduring good deeds are better to your Lord for reward and better for recourse.
ayahs.n: and allah increases those who were guided in guidance and the enduring good deeds are better to your lord for reward and better for recourse

ayahs.t: Then, have you seen he who disbelieved in Our verses and said, "I will surely be given wealth and children [in the next life]?"
ayahs.n: then have you seen he who disbelieved in our verses and said i will surely be given wealth and children in the next life

ayahs.t: Has he looked into the unseen, or has he taken from the Most Merciful a promise?
ayahs.n: has he looked into the unseen or has he taken from the most merciful a promise

ayahs.t: No! We will record what he says and extend for him from the punishment extensively.
ayahs.n: no we will record what he says and extend for him from the punishment extensively

ayahs.t: And We will inherit him [in] what he mentions, and he will come to Us alone.
ayahs.n: and we will inherit him in what he mentions and he will come to us alone

ayahs.t: And they have taken besides Allah [false] deities that they would be for them [a source of] honor.
ayahs.n: and they have taken besides allah false deities that they would be for them a source of honor

ayahs.t: No! Those "gods" will deny their worship of them and will be against them opponents [on the Day of Judgement].
ayahs.n: no those gods will deny their worship of them and will be against them opponents on the day of judgement

ayahs.t: Do you not see that We have sent the devils upon the disbelievers, inciting them to [evil] with [constant] incitement?
ayahs.n: do you not see that we have sent the devils upon the disbelievers inciting them to evil with constant incitement

ayahs.t: So be not impatient over them. We only count out to them a [limited] number.
ayahs.n: so be not impatient over them we only count out to them a limited number

ayahs.t: On the Day We will gather the righteous to the Most Merciful as a delegation
ayahs.n: on the day we will gather the righteous to the most merciful as a delegation

ayahs.t: And will drive the criminals to Hell in thirst
ayahs.n: and will drive the criminals to hell in thirst

ayahs.n: none will have power of intercession except he who had taken from the most merciful a covenant

ayahs.n: and they say the most merciful has taken for himself a son

ayahs.t: You have done an atrocious thing.
ayahs.n: you have done an atrocious thing

ayahs.t: The heavens almost rupture therefrom and the earth splits open and the mountains collapse in devastation
ayahs.n: the heavens almost rupture therefrom and the earth splits open and the mountains collapse in devastation

ayahs.t: That they attribute to the Most Merciful a son.
ayahs.n: that they attribute to the most merciful a son

ayahs.t: And it is not appropriate for the Most Merciful that He should take a son.
ayahs.n: and it is not appropriate for the most merciful that he should take a son

ayahs.n: there is no one in the heavens and earth but that he comes to the most merciful as a servant

ayahs.t: He has enumerated them and counted them a [full] counting.
ayahs.n: he has enumerated them and counted them a full counting

ayahs.t: And all of them are coming to Him on the Day of Resurrection alone.
ayahs.n: and all of them are coming to him on the day of resurrection alone

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds - the Most Merciful will appoint for them affection.
ayahs.n: indeed those who have believed and done righteous deeds the most merciful will appoint for them affection

ayahs.n: so o muhammad we have only made quran easy in the arabic language that you may give good tidings thereby to the righteous and warn thereby a hostile people

ayahs.n: and how many have we destroyed before them of generations do you perceive of them anyone or hear from them a sound

ayahs.t: Ta, Ha.

ayahs.n: we have not sent down to you the quran that you be distressed

ayahs.n: but only as a reminder for those who fear allah

ayahs.n: and if you speak aloud then indeed he knows the secret and what is even more hidden

ayahs.n: and has the story of moses reached you

ayahs.n: indeed the hour is coming i almost conceal it so that every soul may be recompensed according to that for which it strives

ayahs.n: go to pharaoh indeed he has transgressed

ayahs.n: and untie the knot from my tongue

ayahs.t: [And We favored you] when your sister went and said, 'Shall I direct you to someone who will be responsible for him?' So We restored you to your mother that she might be content and not grieve. And you killed someone, but We saved you from retaliation and tried you with a [severe] trial. And you remained [some] years among the people of Madyan. Then you came [here] at the decreed time, O Moses.
ayahs.n: and we favored you when your sister went and said shall i direct you to someone who will be responsible for him so we restored you to your mother that she might be content and not grieve and you killed someone but we saved you from retaliation and tried you with a severe trial and you remained some years among the people of madyan then you came here at the decreed time o moses

ayahs.n: go you and your brother with my signs and do not slacken in my remembrance

ayahs.n: and speak to him with gentle speech that perhaps he may be reminded or fear allah

ayahs.n: indeed it has been revealed to us that the punishment will be upon whoever denies and turns away

ayahs.n: pharaoh said so who is the lord of you two o moses

ayahs.n: he said our lord is he who gave each thing its form and then guided it

ayahs.n: pharaoh said then what is the case of the former generations

ayahs.n: moses said the knowledge thereof is with my lord in a record my lord neither errs nor forgets

ayahs.n: from the earth we created you and into it we will return you and from it we will extract you another time

ayahs.n: and we certainly showed pharaoh our signs all of them but he denied and refused

ayahs.n: then we will surely bring you magic like it so make between us and you an appointment which we will not fail to keep and neither will you in a place assigned

ayahs.n: moses said your appointment is on the day of the festival when the people assemble at mid morning

ayahs.n: moses said to the magicians summoned by pharaoh woe to you do not invent a lie against allah or he will exterminate you with a punishment and he has failed who invents such falsehood

ayahs.n: so they disputed over their affair among themselves and concealed their private conversation

ayahs.n: they said indeed these are two magicians who want to drive you out of your land with their magic and do away with your most exemplary way

ayahs.n: pharaoh said you believed him before i gave you permission indeed he is your leader who has taught you magic so i will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides and i will crucify you on the trunks of palm trees and you will surely know which of us is more severe in giving punishment and more enduring

ayahs.n: but indeed i am the perpetual forgiver of whoever repents and believes and does righteousness and then continues in guidance

ayahs.t: [Allah] said, "And what made you hasten from your people, O Moses?"
ayahs.n: allah said and what made you hasten from your people o moses

ayahs.t: He said, "They are close upon my tracks, and I hastened to You, my Lord, that You be pleased."
ayahs.n: he said they are close upon my tracks and i hastened to you my lord that you be pleased

ayahs.t: [Allah] said, "But indeed, We have tried your people after you [departed], and the Samiri has led them astray."
ayahs.n: allah said but indeed we have tried your people after you departed and the samiri has led them astray

ayahs.t: So Moses returned to his people, angry and grieved. He said, "O my people, did your Lord not make you a good promise? Then, was the time [of its fulfillment] too long for you, or did you wish that wrath from your Lord descend upon you, so you broke your promise [of obedience] to me?"
ayahs.n: so moses returned to his people angry and grieved he said o my people did your lord not make you a good promise then was the time of its fulfillment too long for you or did you wish that wrath from your lord descend upon you so you broke your promise of obedience to me

ayahs.t: They said, "We did not break our promise to you by our will, but we were made to carry burdens from the ornaments of the people [of Pharaoh], so we threw them [into the fire], and thus did the Samiri throw."
ayahs.n: they said we did not break our promise to you by our will but we were made to carry burdens from the ornaments of the people of pharaoh so we threw them into the fire and thus did the samiri throw

ayahs.t: And he extracted for them [the statue of] a calf which had a lowing sound, and they said, "This is your god and the god of Moses, but he forgot."
ayahs.n: and he extracted for them the statue of a calf which had a lowing sound and they said this is your god and the god of moses but he forgot

ayahs.t: Did they not see that it could not return to them any speech and that it did not possess for them any harm or benefit?
ayahs.n: did they not see that it could not return to them any speech and that it did not possess for them any harm or benefit

ayahs.t: And Aaron had already told them before [the return of Moses], "O my people, you are only being tested by it, and indeed, your Lord is the Most Merciful, so follow me and obey my order."
ayahs.n: and aaron had already told them before the return of moses o my people you are only being tested by it and indeed your lord is the most merciful so follow me and obey my order

ayahs.t: They said, "We will never cease being devoted to the calf until Moses returns to us."
ayahs.n: they said we will never cease being devoted to the calf until moses returns to us

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "O Aaron, what prevented you, when you saw them going astray,
ayahs.n: moses said o aaron what prevented you when you saw them going astray

ayahs.t: From following me? Then have you disobeyed my order?"
ayahs.n: from following me then have you disobeyed my order

ayahs.t: [Aaron] said, "O son of my mother, do not seize [me] by my beard or by my head. Indeed, I feared that you would say, 'You caused division among the Children of Israel, and you did not observe [or await] my word.' "
ayahs.n: aaron said o son of my mother do not seize me by my beard or by my head indeed i feared that you would say you caused division among the children of israel and you did not observe or await my word

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "And what is your case, O Samiri?"
ayahs.n: moses said and what is your case o samiri

ayahs.t: He said, "I saw what they did not see, so I took a handful [of dust] from the track of the messenger and threw it, and thus did my soul entice me."
ayahs.n: he said i saw what they did not see so i took a handful of dust from the track of the messenger and threw it and thus did my soul entice me

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "Then go. And indeed, it is [decreed] for you in [this] life to say, 'No contact.' And indeed, you have an appointment [in the Hereafter] you will not fail to keep. And look at your 'god' to which you remained devoted. We will surely burn it and blow it into the sea with a blast.
ayahs.n: moses said then go and indeed it is decreed for you in this life to say no contact and indeed you have an appointment in the hereafter you will not fail to keep and look at your god to which you remained devoted we will surely burn it and blow it into the sea with a blast

ayahs.t: Your god is only Allah, except for whom there is no deity. He has encompassed all things in knowledge."
ayahs.n: your god is only allah except for whom there is no deity he has encompassed all things in knowledge

ayahs.t: Thus, [O Muhammad], We relate to you from the news of what has preceded. And We have certainly given you from Us the Qur'an.
ayahs.n: thus o muhammad we relate to you from the news of what has preceded and we have certainly given you from us the quran

ayahs.t: Whoever turns away from it - then indeed, he will bear on the Day of Resurrection a burden,
ayahs.n: whoever turns away from it then indeed he will bear on the day of resurrection a burden

ayahs.t: [Abiding] eternally therein, and evil it is for them on the Day of Resurrection as a load -
ayahs.n: abiding eternally therein and evil it is for them on the day of resurrection as a load

ayahs.t: The Day the Horn will be blown. And We will gather the criminals, that Day, blue-eyed.
ayahs.n: the day the horn will be blown and we will gather the criminals that day blue eyed

ayahs.t: They will murmur among themselves, "You remained not but ten [days in the world]."
ayahs.n: they will murmur among themselves you remained not but ten days in the world

ayahs.t: We are most knowing of what they say when the best of them in manner will say, "You remained not but one day."
ayahs.n: we are most knowing of what they say when the best of them in manner will say you remained not but one day

ayahs.t: And they ask you about the mountains, so say, "My Lord will blow them away with a blast.
ayahs.n: and they ask you about the mountains so say my lord will blow them away with a blast

ayahs.t: And He will leave the earth a level plain;
ayahs.n: and he will leave the earth a level plain

ayahs.t: You will not see therein a depression or an elevation."
ayahs.n: you will not see therein a depression or an elevation

ayahs.t: That Day, everyone will follow [the call of] the Caller [with] no deviation therefrom, and [all] voices will be stilled before the Most Merciful, so you will not hear except a whisper [of footsteps].
ayahs.n: that day everyone will follow the call of the caller with no deviation therefrom and all voices will be stilled before the most merciful so you will not hear except a whisper of footsteps

ayahs.t: That Day, no intercession will benefit except [that of] one to whom the Most Merciful has given permission and has accepted his word.
ayahs.n: that day no intercession will benefit except that of one to whom the most merciful has given permission and has accepted his word

ayahs.t: Allah knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, but they do not encompass it in knowledge.
ayahs.n: allah knows what is presently before them and what will be after them but they do not encompass it in knowledge

ayahs.t: And [all] faces will be humbled before the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of existence. And he will have failed who carries injustice.
ayahs.n: and all faces will be humbled before the ever living the sustainer of existence and he will have failed who carries injustice

ayahs.t: But he who does of righteous deeds while he is a believer - he will neither fear injustice nor deprivation.
ayahs.n: but he who does of righteous deeds while he is a believer he will neither fear injustice nor deprivation

ayahs.t: And thus We have sent it down as an Arabic Qur'an and have diversified therein the warnings that perhaps they will avoid [sin] or it would cause them remembrance.
ayahs.n: and thus we have sent it down as an arabic quran and have diversified therein the warnings that perhaps they will avoid sin or it would cause them remembrance

ayahs.t: So high [above all] is Allah, the Sovereign, the Truth. And, [O Muhammad], do not hasten with [recitation of] the Qur'an before its revelation is completed to you, and say, "My Lord, increase me in knowledge."
ayahs.n: so high above all is allah the sovereign the truth and o muhammad do not hasten with recitation of the quran before its revelation is completed to you and say my lord increase me in knowledge

ayahs.t: And We had already taken a promise from Adam before, but he forgot; and We found not in him determination.
ayahs.n: and we had already taken a promise from adam before but he forgot and we found not in him determination

ayahs.t: And [mention] when We said to the angels, "Prostrate to Adam," and they prostrated, except Iblees; he refused.
ayahs.n: and mention when we said to the angels prostrate to adam and they prostrated except iblees he refused

ayahs.t: So We said, "O Adam, indeed this is an enemy to you and to your wife. Then let him not remove you from Paradise so you would suffer.
ayahs.n: so we said o adam indeed this is an enemy to you and to your wife then let him not remove you from paradise so you would suffer

ayahs.t: Indeed, it is [promised] for you not to be hungry therein or be unclothed.
ayahs.n: indeed it is promised for you not to be hungry therein or be unclothed

ayahs.t: And indeed, you will not be thirsty therein or be hot from the sun."
ayahs.n: and indeed you will not be thirsty therein or be hot from the sun

ayahs.t: Then Satan whispered to him; he said, "O Adam, shall I direct you to the tree of eternity and possession that will not deteriorate?"
ayahs.n: then satan whispered to him he said o adam shall i direct you to the tree of eternity and possession that will not deteriorate

ayahs.t: And Adam and his wife ate of it, and their private parts became apparent to them, and they began to fasten over themselves from the leaves of Paradise. And Adam disobeyed his Lord and erred.
ayahs.n: and adam and his wife ate of it and their private parts became apparent to them and they began to fasten over themselves from the leaves of paradise and adam disobeyed his lord and erred

ayahs.t: Then his Lord chose him and turned to him in forgiveness and guided [him].
ayahs.n: then his lord chose him and turned to him in forgiveness and guided him

ayahs.t: [Allah] said, "Descend from Paradise - all, [your descendants] being enemies to one another. And if there should come to you guidance from Me - then whoever follows My guidance will neither go astray [in the world] nor suffer [in the Hereafter].
ayahs.n: allah said descend from paradise all your descendants being enemies to one another and if there should come to you guidance from me then whoever follows my guidance will neither go astray in the world nor suffer in the hereafter

ayahs.t: And whoever turns away from My remembrance - indeed, he will have a depressed life, and We will gather him on the Day of Resurrection blind."
ayahs.n: and whoever turns away from my remembrance indeed he will have a depressed life and we will gather him on the day of resurrection blind

ayahs.t: He will say, "My Lord, why have you raised me blind while I was [once] seeing?"
ayahs.n: he will say my lord why have you raised me blind while i was once seeing

ayahs.t: [Allah] will say, "Thus did Our signs come to you, and you forgot them; and thus will you this Day be forgotten."
ayahs.n: allah will say thus did our signs come to you and you forgot them and thus will you this day be forgotten

ayahs.t: And thus do We recompense he who transgressed and did not believe in the signs of his Lord. And the punishment of the Hereafter is more severe and more enduring.
ayahs.n: and thus do we recompense he who transgressed and did not believe in the signs of his lord and the punishment of the hereafter is more severe and more enduring

ayahs.t: Then, has it not become clear to them how many generations We destroyed before them as they walk among their dwellings? Indeed in that are signs for those of intelligence.
ayahs.n: then has it not become clear to them how many generations we destroyed before them as they walk among their dwellings indeed in that are signs for those of intelligence

ayahs.t: And if not for a word that preceded from your Lord, punishment would have been an obligation [due immediately], and [if not for] a specified term [decreed].
ayahs.n: and if not for a word that preceded from your lord punishment would have been an obligation due immediately and if not for a specified term decreed

ayahs.t: So be patient over what they say and exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting; and during periods of the night [exalt Him] and at the ends of the day, that you may be satisfied.
ayahs.n: so be patient over what they say and exalt allah with praise of your lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting and during periods of the night exalt him and at the ends of the day that you may be satisfied

ayahs.t: And do not extend your eyes toward that by which We have given enjoyment to [some] categories of them, [its being but] the splendor of worldly life by which We test them. And the provision of your Lord is better and more enduring.
ayahs.n: and do not extend your eyes toward that by which we have given enjoyment to some categories of them its being but the splendor of worldly life by which we test them and the provision of your lord is better and more enduring

ayahs.t: And enjoin prayer upon your family [and people] and be steadfast therein. We ask you not for provision; We provide for you, and the [best] outcome is for [those of] righteousness.
ayahs.n: and enjoin prayer upon your family and people and be steadfast therein we ask you not for provision we provide for you and the best outcome is for those of righteousness

ayahs.t: And they say, "Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?" Has there not come to them evidence of what was in the former scriptures?
ayahs.n: and they say why does he not bring us a sign from his lord has there not come to them evidence of what was in the former scriptures

ayahs.t: And if We had destroyed them with a punishment before him, they would have said, "Our Lord, why did You not send to us a messenger so we could have followed Your verses before we were humiliated and disgraced?"
ayahs.n: and if we had destroyed them with a punishment before him they would have said our lord why did you not send to us a messenger so we could have followed your verses before we were humiliated and disgraced

ayahs.t: Say, "Each [of us] is waiting; so wait. For you will know who are the companions of the sound path and who is guided."
ayahs.n: say each of us is waiting so wait for you will know who are the companions of the sound path and who is guided

ayahs.t: [The time of] their account has approached for the people, while they are in heedlessness turning away.
ayahs.n: the time of their account has approached for the people while they are in heedlessness turning away

ayahs.t: No mention comes to them anew from their Lord except that they listen to it while they are at play
ayahs.n: no mention comes to them anew from their lord except that they listen to it while they are at play

ayahs.t: With their hearts distracted. And those who do wrong conceal their private conversation, [saying], "Is this [Prophet] except a human being like you? So would you approach magic while you are aware [of it]?"
ayahs.n: with their hearts distracted and those who do wrong conceal their private conversation saying is this prophet except a human being like you so would you approach magic while you are aware of it

ayahs.t: The Prophet said, "My Lord knows whatever is said throughout the heaven and earth, and He is the Hearing, the Knowing."
ayahs.n: the prophet said my lord knows whatever is said throughout the heaven and earth and he is the hearing the knowing

ayahs.t: But they say, "[The revelation is but] a mixture of false dreams; rather, he has invented it; rather, he is a poet. So let him bring us a sign just as the previous [messengers] were sent [with miracles]."
ayahs.n: but they say the revelation is but a mixture of false dreams rather he has invented it rather he is a poet so let him bring us a sign just as the previous messengers were sent with miracles

ayahs.t: Not a [single] city which We destroyed believed before them, so will they believe?
ayahs.n: not a single city which we destroyed believed before them so will they believe

ayahs.t: And We sent not before you, [O Muhammad], except men to whom We revealed [the message], so ask the people of the message if you do not know.
ayahs.n: and we sent not before you o muhammad except men to whom we revealed the message so ask the people of the message if you do not know

ayahs.t: And We did not make the prophets forms not eating food, nor were they immortal [on earth].
ayahs.n: and we did not make the prophets forms not eating food nor were they immortal on earth

ayahs.t: Then We fulfilled for them the promise, and We saved them and whom We willed and destroyed the transgressors.
ayahs.n: then we fulfilled for them the promise and we saved them and whom we willed and destroyed the transgressors

ayahs.t: We have certainly sent down to you a Book in which is your mention. Then will you not reason?
ayahs.n: we have certainly sent down to you a book in which is your mention then will you not reason

ayahs.t: And how many a city which was unjust have We shattered and produced after it another people.
ayahs.n: and how many a city which was unjust have we shattered and produced after it another people

ayahs.t: And when its inhabitants perceived Our punishment, at once they fled from it.
ayahs.n: and when its inhabitants perceived our punishment at once they fled from it

ayahs.t: [Some angels said], "Do not flee but return to where you were given luxury and to your homes - perhaps you will be questioned."
ayahs.n: some angels said do not flee but return to where you were given luxury and to your homes perhaps you will be questioned

ayahs.t: They said, "O woe to us! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."
ayahs.n: they said o woe to us indeed we were wrongdoers

ayahs.t: And that declaration of theirs did not cease until We made them [as] a harvest [mowed down], extinguished [like a fire].
ayahs.n: and that declaration of theirs did not cease until we made them as a harvest mowed down extinguished like a fire

ayahs.t: And We did not create the heaven and earth and that between them in play.
ayahs.n: and we did not create the heaven and earth and that between them in play

ayahs.t: Had We intended to take a diversion, We could have taken it from [what is] with Us - if [indeed] We were to do so.
ayahs.n: had we intended to take a diversion we could have taken it from what is with us if indeed we were to do so

ayahs.t: Rather, We dash the truth upon falsehood, and it destroys it, and thereupon it departs. And for you is destruction from that which you describe.
ayahs.n: rather we dash the truth upon falsehood and it destroys it and thereupon it departs and for you is destruction from that which you describe

ayahs.t: To Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. And those near Him are not prevented by arrogance from His worship, nor do they tire.
ayahs.n: to him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth and those near him are not prevented by arrogance from his worship nor do they tire

ayahs.t: They exalt [Him] night and day [and] do not slacken.
ayahs.n: they exalt him night and day and do not slacken

ayahs.t: Or have men taken for themselves gods from the earth who resurrect [the dead]?
ayahs.n: or have men taken for themselves gods from the earth who resurrect the dead

ayahs.t: Had there been within the heavens and earth gods besides Allah, they both would have been ruined. So exalted is Allah, Lord of the Throne, above what they describe.
ayahs.n: had there been within the heavens and earth gods besides allah they both would have been ruined so exalted is allah lord of the throne above what they describe

ayahs.t: He is not questioned about what He does, but they will be questioned.
ayahs.n: he is not questioned about what he does but they will be questioned

ayahs.t: Or have they taken gods besides Him? Say, [O Muhammad], "Produce your proof. This [Qur'an] is the message for those with me and the message of those before me." But most of them do not know the truth, so they are turning away.
ayahs.n: or have they taken gods besides him say o muhammad produce your proof this quran is the message for those with me and the message of those before me but most of them do not know the truth so they are turning away

ayahs.t: And We sent not before you any messenger except that We revealed to him that, "There is no deity except Me, so worship Me."
ayahs.n: and we sent not before you any messenger except that we revealed to him that there is no deity except me so worship me

ayahs.t: And they say, "The Most Merciful has taken a son." Exalted is He! Rather, they are [but] honored servants.
ayahs.n: and they say the most merciful has taken a son exalted is he rather they are but honored servants

ayahs.t: They cannot precede Him in word, and they act by His command.
ayahs.n: they cannot precede him in word and they act by his command

ayahs.t: He knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, and they cannot intercede except on behalf of one whom He approves. And they, from fear of Him, are apprehensive.
ayahs.n: he knows what is presently before them and what will be after them and they cannot intercede except on behalf of one whom he approves and they from fear of him are apprehensive

ayahs.t: And whoever of them should say, "Indeed, I am a god besides Him"- that one We would recompense with Hell. Thus do We recompense the wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: and whoever of them should say indeed i am a god besides him that one we would recompense with hell thus do we recompense the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: Have those who disbelieved not considered that the heavens and the earth were a joined entity, and We separated them and made from water every living thing? Then will they not believe?
ayahs.n: have those who disbelieved not considered that the heavens and the earth were a joined entity and we separated them and made from water every living thing then will they not believe

ayahs.t: And We placed within the earth firmly set mountains, lest it should shift with them, and We made therein [mountain] passes [as] roads that they might be guided.
ayahs.n: and we placed within the earth firmly set mountains lest it should shift with them and we made therein mountain passes as roads that they might be guided

ayahs.t: And We made the sky a protected ceiling, but they, from its signs, are turning away.
ayahs.n: and we made the sky a protected ceiling but they from its signs are turning away

ayahs.t: And it is He who created the night and the day and the sun and the moon; all [heavenly bodies] in an orbit are swimming.
ayahs.n: and it is he who created the night and the day and the sun and the moon all heavenly bodies in an orbit are swimming

ayahs.t: And We did not grant to any man before you eternity [on earth]; so if you die - would they be eternal?
ayahs.n: and we did not grant to any man before you eternity on earth so if you die would they be eternal

ayahs.t: Every soul will taste death. And We test you with evil and with good as trial; and to Us you will be returned.
ayahs.n: every soul will taste death and we test you with evil and with good as trial and to us you will be returned

ayahs.t: And when those who disbelieve see you, [O Muhammad], they take you not except in ridicule, [saying], "Is this the one who insults your gods?" And they are, at the mention of the Most Merciful, disbelievers.
ayahs.n: and when those who disbelieve see you o muhammad they take you not except in ridicule saying is this the one who insults your gods and they are at the mention of the most merciful disbelievers

ayahs.t: Man was created of haste. I will show you My signs, so do not impatiently urge Me.
ayahs.n: man was created of haste i will show you my signs so do not impatiently urge me

ayahs.t: And they say, "When is this promise, if you should be truthful?"
ayahs.n: and they say when is this promise if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: If those who disbelieved but knew the time when they will not avert the Fire from their faces or from their backs and they will not be aided...
ayahs.n: if those who disbelieved but knew the time when they will not avert the fire from their faces or from their backs and they will not be aided

ayahs.t: Rather, it will come to them unexpectedly and bewilder them, and they will not be able to repel it, nor will they be reprieved.
ayahs.n: rather it will come to them unexpectedly and bewilder them and they will not be able to repel it nor will they be reprieved

ayahs.t: And already were messengers ridiculed before you, but those who mocked them were enveloped by what they used to ridicule.
ayahs.n: and already were messengers ridiculed before you but those who mocked them were enveloped by what they used to ridicule

ayahs.t: Say, "Who can protect you at night or by day from the Most Merciful?" But they are, from the remembrance of their Lord, turning away.
ayahs.n: say who can protect you at night or by day from the most merciful but they are from the remembrance of their lord turning away

ayahs.t: Or do they have gods to defend them other than Us? They are unable [even] to help themselves, nor can they be protected from Us.
ayahs.n: or do they have gods to defend them other than us they are unable even to help themselves nor can they be protected from us

ayahs.t: But, [on the contrary], We have provided good things for these [disbelievers] and their fathers until life was prolonged for them. Then do they not see that We set upon the land, reducing it from its borders? So it is they who will overcome?
ayahs.n: but on the contrary we have provided good things for these disbelievers and their fathers until life was prolonged for them then do they not see that we set upon the land reducing it from its borders so it is they who will overcome

ayahs.t: Say, "I only warn you by revelation." But the deaf do not hear the call when they are warned.
ayahs.n: say i only warn you by revelation but the deaf do not hear the call when they are warned

ayahs.t: And if [as much as] a whiff of the punishment of your Lord should touch them, they would surely say, "O woe to us! Indeed, we have been wrongdoers."
ayahs.n: and if as much as a whiff of the punishment of your lord should touch them they would surely say o woe to us indeed we have been wrongdoers

ayahs.t: And We place the scales of justice for the Day of Resurrection, so no soul will be treated unjustly at all. And if there is [even] the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it forth. And sufficient are We as accountant.
ayahs.n: and we place the scales of justice for the day of resurrection so no soul will be treated unjustly at all and if there is even the weight of a mustard seed we will bring it forth and sufficient are we as accountant

ayahs.t: And We had already given Moses and Aaron the criterion and a light and a reminder for the righteous
ayahs.n: and we had already given moses and aaron the criterion and a light and a reminder for the righteous

ayahs.t: Who fear their Lord unseen, while they are of the Hour apprehensive.
ayahs.n: who fear their lord unseen while they are of the hour apprehensive

ayahs.t: And this [Qur'an] is a blessed message which We have sent down. Then are you with it unacquainted?
ayahs.n: and this quran is a blessed message which we have sent down then are you with it unacquainted

ayahs.t: And We had certainly given Abraham his sound judgement before, and We were of him well-Knowing
ayahs.n: and we had certainly given abraham his sound judgement before and we were of him well knowing

ayahs.t: When he said to his father and his people, "What are these statues to which you are devoted?"
ayahs.n: when he said to his father and his people what are these statues to which you are devoted

ayahs.t: They said, "We found our fathers worshippers of them."
ayahs.n: they said we found our fathers worshippers of them

ayahs.t: He said, "You were certainly, you and your fathers, in manifest error."
ayahs.n: he said you were certainly you and your fathers in manifest error

ayahs.t: They said, "Have you come to us with truth, or are you of those who jest?"
ayahs.n: they said have you come to us with truth or are you of those who jest

ayahs.t: He said, "[No], rather, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth who created them, and I, to that, am of those who testify.
ayahs.n: he said no rather your lord is the lord of the heavens and the earth who created them and i to that am of those who testify

ayahs.t: And [I swear] by Allah, I will surely plan against your idols after you have turned and gone away."
ayahs.n: and i swear by allah i will surely plan against your idols after you have turned and gone away

ayahs.t: So he made them into fragments, except a large one among them, that they might return to it [and question].
ayahs.n: so he made them into fragments except a large one among them that they might return to it and question

ayahs.t: They said, "Who has done this to our gods? Indeed, he is of the wrongdoers."
ayahs.n: they said who has done this to our gods indeed he is of the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: They said, "We heard a young man mention them who is called Abraham."
ayahs.n: they said we heard a young man mention them who is called abraham

ayahs.t: They said, "Then bring him before the eyes of the people that they may testify."
ayahs.n: they said then bring him before the eyes of the people that they may testify

ayahs.t: They said, "Have you done this to our gods, O Abraham?"
ayahs.n: they said have you done this to our gods o abraham

ayahs.t: He said, "Rather, this - the largest of them - did it, so ask them, if they should [be able to] speak."
ayahs.n: he said rather this the largest of them did it so ask them if they should be able to speak

ayahs.t: So they returned to [blaming] themselves and said [to each other], "Indeed, you are the wrongdoers."
ayahs.n: so they returned to blaming themselves and said to each other indeed you are the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: Then they reversed themselves, [saying], "You have already known that these do not speak!"
ayahs.n: then they reversed themselves saying you have already known that these do not speak

ayahs.t: He said, "Then do you worship instead of Allah that which does not benefit you at all or harm you?
ayahs.n: he said then do you worship instead of allah that which does not benefit you at all or harm you

ayahs.t: Uff to you and to what you worship instead of Allah. Then will you not use reason?"
ayahs.n: uff to you and to what you worship instead of allah then will you not use reason

ayahs.t: They said, "Burn him and support your gods - if you are to act."
ayahs.n: they said burn him and support your gods if you are to act

ayahs.t: Allah said, "O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham."
ayahs.n: allah said o fire be coolness and safety upon abraham

ayahs.t: And they intended for him harm, but We made them the greatest losers.
ayahs.n: and they intended for him harm but we made them the greatest losers

ayahs.t: And We delivered him and Lot to the land which We had blessed for the worlds.
ayahs.n: and we delivered him and lot to the land which we had blessed for the worlds

ayahs.t: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in addition, and all [of them] We made righteous.
ayahs.n: and we gave him isaac and jacob in addition and all of them we made righteous

ayahs.t: And We made them leaders guiding by Our command. And We inspired to them the doing of good deeds, establishment of prayer, and giving of zakah; and they were worshippers of Us.
ayahs.n: and we made them leaders guiding by our command and we inspired to them the doing of good deeds establishment of prayer and giving of zakah and they were worshippers of us

ayahs.t: And to Lot We gave judgement and knowledge, and We saved him from the city that was committing wicked deeds. Indeed, they were a people of evil, defiantly disobedient.
ayahs.n: and to lot we gave judgement and knowledge and we saved him from the city that was committing wicked deeds indeed they were a people of evil defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: And We admitted him into Our mercy. Indeed, he was of the righteous.
ayahs.n: and we admitted him into our mercy indeed he was of the righteous

ayahs.t: And [mention] Noah, when he called [to Allah] before [that time], so We responded to him and saved him and his family from the great flood.
ayahs.n: and mention noah when he called to allah before that time so we responded to him and saved him and his family from the great flood

ayahs.t: And We saved him from the people who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a people of evil, so We drowned them, all together.
ayahs.n: and we saved him from the people who denied our signs indeed they were a people of evil so we drowned them all together

ayahs.t: And [mention] David and Solomon, when they judged concerning the field - when the sheep of a people overran it [at night], and We were witness to their judgement.
ayahs.n: and mention david and solomon when they judged concerning the field when the sheep of a people overran it at night and we were witness to their judgement

ayahs.t: And We gave understanding of the case to Solomon, and to each [of them] We gave judgement and knowledge. And We subjected the mountains to exalt [Us], along with David and [also] the birds. And We were doing [that].
ayahs.n: and we gave understanding of the case to solomon and to each of them we gave judgement and knowledge and we subjected the mountains to exalt us along with david and also the birds and we were doing that

ayahs.t: And We taught him the fashioning of coats of armor to protect you from your [enemy in] battle. So will you then be grateful?
ayahs.n: and we taught him the fashioning of coats of armor to protect you from your enemy in battle so will you then be grateful

ayahs.t: And to Solomon [We subjected] the wind, blowing forcefully, proceeding by his command toward the land which We had blessed. And We are ever, of all things, Knowing.
ayahs.n: and to solomon we subjected the wind blowing forcefully proceeding by his command toward the land which we had blessed and we are ever of all things knowing

ayahs.t: And of the devils were those who dived for him and did work other than that. And We were of them a guardian.
ayahs.n: and of the devils were those who dived for him and did work other than that and we were of them a guardian

ayahs.t: And [mention] Job, when he called to his Lord, "Indeed, adversity has touched me, and you are the Most Merciful of the merciful."
ayahs.n: and mention job when he called to his lord indeed adversity has touched me and you are the most merciful of the merciful

ayahs.t: So We responded to him and removed what afflicted him of adversity. And We gave him [back] his family and the like thereof with them as mercy from Us and a reminder for the worshippers [of Allah].
ayahs.n: so we responded to him and removed what afflicted him of adversity and we gave him back his family and the like thereof with them as mercy from us and a reminder for the worshippers of allah

ayahs.t: And [mention] Ishmael and Idrees and Dhul-Kifl; all were of the patient.
ayahs.n: and mention ishmael and idrees and dhul kifl all were of the patient

ayahs.t: And We admitted them into Our mercy. Indeed, they were of the righteous.
ayahs.n: and we admitted them into our mercy indeed they were of the righteous

ayahs.t: And [mention] the man of the fish, when he went off in anger and thought that We would not decree [anything] upon him. And he called out within the darknesses, "There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers."
ayahs.n: and mention the man of the fish when he went off in anger and thought that we would not decree anything upon him and he called out within the darknesses there is no deity except you exalted are you indeed i have been of the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: So We responded to him and saved him from the distress. And thus do We save the believers.
ayahs.n: so we responded to him and saved him from the distress and thus do we save the believers

ayahs.t: And [mention] Zechariah, when he called to his Lord, "My Lord, do not leave me alone [with no heir], while you are the best of inheritors."
ayahs.n: and mention zechariah when he called to his lord my lord do not leave me alone with no heir while you are the best of inheritors

ayahs.t: So We responded to him, and We gave to him John, and amended for him his wife. Indeed, they used to hasten to good deeds and supplicate Us in hope and fear, and they were to Us humbly submissive.
ayahs.n: so we responded to him and we gave to him john and amended for him his wife indeed they used to hasten to good deeds and supplicate us in hope and fear and they were to us humbly submissive

ayahs.t: And [mention] the one who guarded her chastity, so We blew into her [garment] through Our angel [Gabriel], and We made her and her son a sign for the worlds.
ayahs.n: and mention the one who guarded her chastity so we blew into her garment through our angel gabriel and we made her and her son a sign for the worlds

ayahs.t: Indeed this, your religion, is one religion, and I am your Lord, so worship Me.
ayahs.n: indeed this your religion is one religion and i am your lord so worship me

ayahs.t: And [yet] they divided their affair among themselves, [but] all to Us will return.
ayahs.n: and yet they divided their affair among themselves but all to us will return

ayahs.t: So whoever does righteous deeds while he is a believer - no denial will there be for his effort, and indeed We, of it, are recorders.
ayahs.n: so whoever does righteous deeds while he is a believer no denial will there be for his effort and indeed we of it are recorders

ayahs.t: And there is prohibition upon [the people of] a city which We have destroyed that they will [ever] return
ayahs.n: and there is prohibition upon the people of a city which we have destroyed that they will ever return

ayahs.t: Until when [the dam of] Gog and Magog has been opened and they, from every elevation, descend
ayahs.n: until when the dam of gog and magog has been opened and they from every elevation descend

ayahs.t: And [when] the true promise has approached; then suddenly the eyes of those who disbelieved will be staring [in horror, while they say], "O woe to us; we had been unmindful of this; rather, we were wrongdoers."
ayahs.n: and when the true promise has approached then suddenly the eyes of those who disbelieved will be staring in horror while they say o woe to us we had been unmindful of this rather we were wrongdoers

ayahs.t: Indeed, you [disbelievers] and what you worship other than Allah are the firewood of Hell. You will be coming to [enter] it.
ayahs.n: indeed you disbelievers and what you worship other than allah are the firewood of hell you will be coming to enter it

ayahs.t: Had these [false deities] been [actual] gods, they would not have come to it, but all are eternal therein.
ayahs.n: had these false deities been actual gods they would not have come to it but all are eternal therein

ayahs.t: For them therein is heavy sighing, and they therein will not hear.
ayahs.n: for them therein is heavy sighing and they therein will not hear

ayahs.t: Indeed, those for whom the best [reward] has preceded from Us - they are from it far removed.
ayahs.n: indeed those for whom the best reward has preceded from us they are from it far removed

ayahs.t: They will not hear its sound, while they are, in that which their souls desire, abiding eternally.
ayahs.n: they will not hear its sound while they are in that which their souls desire abiding eternally

ayahs.t: They will not be grieved by the greatest terror, and the angels will meet them, [saying], "This is your Day which you have been promised" -
ayahs.n: they will not be grieved by the greatest terror and the angels will meet them saying this is your day which you have been promised

ayahs.t: The Day when We will fold the heaven like the folding of a [written] sheet for the records. As We began the first creation, We will repeat it. [That is] a promise binding upon Us. Indeed, We will do it.
ayahs.n: the day when we will fold the heaven like the folding of a written sheet for the records as we began the first creation we will repeat it that is a promise binding upon us indeed we will do it

ayahs.t: And We have already written in the book [of Psalms] after the [previous] mention that the land [of Paradise] is inherited by My righteous servants.
ayahs.n: and we have already written in the book of psalms after the previous mention that the land of paradise is inherited by my righteous servants

ayahs.t: Indeed, in this [Qur'an] is notification for a worshipping people.
ayahs.n: indeed in this quran is notification for a worshipping people

ayahs.t: And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a mercy to the worlds.
ayahs.n: and we have not sent you o muhammad except as a mercy to the worlds

ayahs.t: Say, "It is only revealed to me that your god is but one God; so will you be Muslims [in submission to Him]?"
ayahs.n: say it is only revealed to me that your god is but one god so will you be muslims in submission to him

ayahs.t: But if they turn away, then say, "I have announced to [all of] you equally. And I know not whether near or far is that which you are promised.
ayahs.n: but if they turn away then say i have announced to all of you equally and i know not whether near or far is that which you are promised

ayahs.t: Indeed, He knows what is declared of speech, and He knows what you conceal.
ayahs.n: indeed he knows what is declared of speech and he knows what you conceal

ayahs.t: And I know not; perhaps it is a trial for you and enjoyment for a time."
ayahs.n: and i know not perhaps it is a trial for you and enjoyment for a time
