ayahs.t: There is not upon the blind [any] constraint nor upon the lame constraint nor upon the ill constraint nor upon yourselves when you eat from your [own] houses or the houses of your fathers or the houses of your mothers or the houses of your brothers or the houses of your sisters or the houses of your father's brothers or the houses of your father's sisters or the houses of your mother's brothers or the houses of your mother's sisters or [from houses] whose keys you possess or [from the house] of your friend. There is no blame upon you whether you eat together or separately. But when you enter houses, give greetings of peace upon each other - a greeting from Allah, blessed and good. Thus does Allah make clear to you the verses [of ordinance] that you may understand.
ayahs.n: there is not upon the blind any constraint nor upon the lame constraint nor upon the ill constraint nor upon yourselves when you eat from your own houses or the houses of your fathers or the houses of your mothers or the houses of your brothers or the houses of your sisters or the houses of your fathers brothers or the houses of your fathers sisters or the houses of your mothers brothers or the houses of your mothers sisters or from houses whose keys you possess or from the house of your friend there is no blame upon you whether you eat together or separately but when you enter houses give greetings of peace upon each other a greeting from allah blessed and good thus does allah make clear to you the verses of ordinance that you may understand

ayahs.t: The believers are only those who believe in Allah and His Messenger and, when they are [meeting] with him for a matter of common interest, do not depart until they have asked his permission. Indeed, those who ask your permission, [O Muhammad] - those are the ones who believe in Allah and His Messenger. So when they ask your permission for something of their affairs, then give permission to whom you will among them and ask forgiveness for them of Allah. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: the believers are only those who believe in allah and his messenger and when they are meeting with him for a matter of common interest do not depart until they have asked his permission indeed those who ask your permission o muhammad those are the ones who believe in allah and his messenger so when they ask your permission for something of their affairs then give permission to whom you will among them and ask forgiveness for them of allah indeed allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: Do not make [your] calling of the Messenger among yourselves as the call of one of you to another. Already Allah knows those of you who slip away, concealed by others. So let those beware who dissent from the Prophet's order, lest fitnah strike them or a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: do not make your calling of the messenger among yourselves as the call of one of you to another already allah knows those of you who slip away concealed by others so let those beware who dissent from the prophets order lest fitnah strike them or a painful punishment

ayahs.t: Unquestionably, to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and earth. Already He knows that upon which you [stand] and [knows] the Day when they will be returned to Him and He will inform them of what they have done. And Allah is Knowing of all things.
ayahs.n: unquestionably to allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and earth already he knows that upon which you stand and knows the day when they will be returned to him and he will inform them of what they have done and allah is knowing of all things

ayahs.t: Blessed is He who sent down the Criterion upon His Servant that he may be to the worlds a warner -
ayahs.n: blessed is he who sent down the criterion upon his servant that he may be to the worlds a warner

ayahs.t: He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and who has not taken a son and has not had a partner in dominion and has created each thing and determined it with [precise] determination.
ayahs.n: he to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and who has not taken a son and has not had a partner in dominion and has created each thing and determined it with precise determination

ayahs.t: But they have taken besides Him gods which create nothing, while they are created, and possess not for themselves any harm or benefit and possess not [power to cause] death or life or resurrection.
ayahs.n: but they have taken besides him gods which create nothing while they are created and possess not for themselves any harm or benefit and possess not power to cause death or life or resurrection

ayahs.t: And those who disbelieve say, "This [Qur'an] is not except a falsehood he invented, and another people assisted him in it." But they have committed an injustice and a lie.
ayahs.n: and those who disbelieve say this quran is not except a falsehood he invented and another people assisted him in it but they have committed an injustice and a lie

ayahs.t: And they say, "Legends of the former peoples which he has written down, and they are dictated to him morning and afternoon."
ayahs.n: and they say legends of the former peoples which he has written down and they are dictated to him morning and afternoon

ayahs.t: Say, [O Muhammad], "It has been revealed by He who knows [every] secret within the heavens and the earth. Indeed, He is ever Forgiving and Merciful."
ayahs.n: say o muhammad it has been revealed by he who knows every secret within the heavens and the earth indeed he is ever forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: And they say, "What is this messenger that eats food and walks in the markets? Why was there not sent down to him an angel so he would be with him a warner?
ayahs.n: and they say what is this messenger that eats food and walks in the markets why was there not sent down to him an angel so he would be with him a warner

ayahs.t: Or [why is not] a treasure presented to him [from heaven], or does he [not] have a garden from which he eats?" And the wrongdoers say, "You follow not but a man affected by magic."
ayahs.n: or why is not a treasure presented to him from heaven or does he not have a garden from which he eats and the wrongdoers say you follow not but a man affected by magic

ayahs.t: Look how they strike for you comparisons; but they have strayed, so they cannot [find] a way.
ayahs.n: look how they strike for you comparisons but they have strayed so they cannot find a way

ayahs.t: Blessed is He who, if He willed, could have made for you [something] better than that - gardens beneath which rivers flow - and could make for you palaces.
ayahs.n: blessed is he who if he willed could have made for you something better than that gardens beneath which rivers flow and could make for you palaces

ayahs.t: But they have denied the Hour, and We have prepared for those who deny the Hour a Blaze.
ayahs.n: but they have denied the hour and we have prepared for those who deny the hour a blaze

ayahs.t: When the Hellfire sees them from a distant place, they will hear its fury and roaring.
ayahs.n: when the hellfire sees them from a distant place they will hear its fury and roaring

ayahs.t: And when they are thrown into a narrow place therein bound in chains, they will cry out thereupon for destruction.
ayahs.n: and when they are thrown into a narrow place therein bound in chains they will cry out thereupon for destruction

ayahs.t: [They will be told], "Do not cry this Day for one destruction but cry for much destruction."
ayahs.n: they will be told do not cry this day for one destruction but cry for much destruction

ayahs.t: Say, "Is that better or the Garden of Eternity which is promised to the righteous? It will be for them a reward and destination.
ayahs.n: say is that better or the garden of eternity which is promised to the righteous it will be for them a reward and destination

ayahs.t: For them therein is whatever they wish, [while] abiding eternally. It is ever upon your Lord a promise [worthy to be] requested.
ayahs.n: for them therein is whatever they wish while abiding eternally it is ever upon your lord a promise worthy to be requested

ayahs.t: And [mention] the Day He will gather them and that which they worship besides Allah and will say, "Did you mislead these, My servants, or did they [themselves] stray from the way?"
ayahs.n: and mention the day he will gather them and that which they worship besides allah and will say did you mislead these my servants or did they themselves stray from the way

ayahs.t: They will say, "Exalted are You! It was not for us to take besides You any allies. But You provided comforts for them and their fathers until they forgot the message and became a people ruined."
ayahs.n: they will say exalted are you it was not for us to take besides you any allies but you provided comforts for them and their fathers until they forgot the message and became a people ruined

ayahs.t: So they will deny you, [disbelievers], in what you say, and you cannot avert [punishment] or [find] help. And whoever commits injustice among you - We will make him taste a great punishment.
ayahs.n: so they will deny you disbelievers in what you say and you cannot avert punishment or find help and whoever commits injustice among you we will make him taste a great punishment

ayahs.t: And We did not send before you, [O Muhammad], any of the messengers except that they ate food and walked in the markets. And We have made some of you [people] as trial for others - will you have patience? And ever is your Lord, Seeing.
ayahs.n: and we did not send before you o muhammad any of the messengers except that they ate food and walked in the markets and we have made some of you people as trial for others will you have patience and ever is your lord seeing

ayahs.t: And those who do not expect the meeting with Us say, "Why were not angels sent down to us, or [why] do we [not] see our Lord?" They have certainly become arrogant within themselves and [become] insolent with great insolence.
ayahs.n: and those who do not expect the meeting with us say why were not angels sent down to us or why do we not see our lord they have certainly become arrogant within themselves and become insolent with great insolence

ayahs.t: The day they see the angels - no good tidings will there be that day for the criminals, and [the angels] will say, "Prevented and inaccessible."
ayahs.n: the day they see the angels no good tidings will there be that day for the criminals and the angels will say prevented and inaccessible

ayahs.t: And We will regard what they have done of deeds and make them as dust dispersed.
ayahs.n: and we will regard what they have done of deeds and make them as dust dispersed

ayahs.t: The companions of Paradise, that Day, are [in] a better settlement and better resting place.
ayahs.n: the companions of paradise that day are in a better settlement and better resting place

ayahs.t: And [mention] the Day when the heaven will split open with [emerging] clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive descent.
ayahs.n: and mention the day when the heaven will split open with emerging clouds and the angels will be sent down in successive descent

ayahs.t: True sovereignty, that Day, is for the Most Merciful. And it will be upon the disbelievers a difficult Day.
ayahs.n: true sovereignty that day is for the most merciful and it will be upon the disbelievers a difficult day

ayahs.t: And the Day the wrongdoer will bite on his hands [in regret] he will say, "Oh, I wish I had taken with the Messenger a way.
ayahs.n: and the day the wrongdoer will bite on his hands in regret he will say oh i wish i had taken with the messenger a way

ayahs.t: Oh, woe to me! I wish I had not taken that one as a friend.
ayahs.n: oh woe to me i wish i had not taken that one as a friend

ayahs.t: He led me away from the remembrance after it had come to me. And ever is Satan, to man, a deserter."
ayahs.n: he led me away from the remembrance after it had come to me and ever is satan to man a deserter

ayahs.t: And the Messenger has said, "O my Lord, indeed my people have taken this Qur'an as [a thing] abandoned."
ayahs.n: and the messenger has said o my lord indeed my people have taken this quran as a thing abandoned

ayahs.t: And thus have We made for every prophet an enemy from among the criminals. But sufficient is your Lord as a guide and a helper.
ayahs.n: and thus have we made for every prophet an enemy from among the criminals but sufficient is your lord as a guide and a helper

ayahs.t: And those who disbelieve say, "Why was the Qur'an not revealed to him all at once?" Thus [it is] that We may strengthen thereby your heart. And We have spaced it distinctly.
ayahs.n: and those who disbelieve say why was the quran not revealed to him all at once thus it is that we may strengthen thereby your heart and we have spaced it distinctly

ayahs.t: And they do not come to you with an argument except that We bring you the truth and the best explanation.
ayahs.n: and they do not come to you with an argument except that we bring you the truth and the best explanation

ayahs.t: The ones who are gathered on their faces to Hell - those are the worst in position and farthest astray in [their] way.
ayahs.n: the ones who are gathered on their faces to hell those are the worst in position and farthest astray in their way

ayahs.t: And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture and appointed with him his brother Aaron as an assistant.
ayahs.n: and we had certainly given moses the scripture and appointed with him his brother aaron as an assistant

ayahs.t: And We said, "Go both of you to the people who have denied Our signs." Then We destroyed them with [complete] destruction.
ayahs.n: and we said go both of you to the people who have denied our signs then we destroyed them with complete destruction

ayahs.t: And the people of Noah - when they denied the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them for mankind a sign. And We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: and the people of noah when they denied the messengers we drowned them and we made them for mankind a sign and we have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment

ayahs.t: And [We destroyed] 'Aad and Thamud and the companions of the well and many generations between them.
ayahs.n: and we destroyed aad and thamud and the companions of the well and many generations between them

ayahs.t: And for each We presented examples [as warnings], and each We destroyed with [total] destruction.
ayahs.n: and for each we presented examples as warnings and each we destroyed with total destruction

ayahs.t: And they have already come upon the town which was showered with a rain of evil. So have they not seen it? But they are not expecting resurrection.
ayahs.n: and they have already come upon the town which was showered with a rain of evil so have they not seen it but they are not expecting resurrection

ayahs.t: And when they see you, [O Muhammad], they take you not except in ridicule, [saying], "Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a messenger?
ayahs.n: and when they see you o muhammad they take you not except in ridicule saying is this the one whom allah has sent as a messenger

ayahs.t: He almost would have misled us from our gods had we not been steadfast in [worship of] them." But they are going to know, when they see the punishment, who is farthest astray in [his] way.
ayahs.n: he almost would have misled us from our gods had we not been steadfast in worship of them but they are going to know when they see the punishment who is farthest astray in his way

ayahs.t: Have you seen the one who takes as his god his own desire? Then would you be responsible for him?
ayahs.n: have you seen the one who takes as his god his own desire then would you be responsible for him

ayahs.t: Or do you think that most of them hear or reason? They are not except like livestock. Rather, they are [even] more astray in [their] way.
ayahs.n: or do you think that most of them hear or reason they are not except like livestock rather they are even more astray in their way

ayahs.t: Have you not considered your Lord - how He extends the shadow, and if He willed, He could have made it stationary? Then We made the sun for it an indication.
ayahs.n: have you not considered your lord how he extends the shadow and if he willed he could have made it stationary then we made the sun for it an indication

ayahs.t: Then We hold it in hand for a brief grasp.
ayahs.n: then we hold it in hand for a brief grasp

ayahs.t: And it is He who has made the night for you as clothing and sleep [a means for] rest and has made the day a resurrection.
ayahs.n: and it is he who has made the night for you as clothing and sleep a means for rest and has made the day a resurrection

ayahs.t: And it is He who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy, and We send down from the sky pure water
ayahs.n: and it is he who sends the winds as good tidings before his mercy and we send down from the sky pure water

ayahs.t: That We may bring to life thereby a dead land and give it as drink to those We created of numerous livestock and men.
ayahs.n: that we may bring to life thereby a dead land and give it as drink to those we created of numerous livestock and men

ayahs.t: And We have certainly distributed it among them that they might be reminded, but most of the people refuse except disbelief.
ayahs.n: and we have certainly distributed it among them that they might be reminded but most of the people refuse except disbelief

ayahs.t: And if We had willed, We could have sent into every city a warner.
ayahs.n: and if we had willed we could have sent into every city a warner

ayahs.t: So do not obey the disbelievers, and strive against them with the Qur'an a great striving.
ayahs.n: so do not obey the disbelievers and strive against them with the quran a great striving

ayahs.t: And it is He who has released [simultaneously] the two seas, one fresh and sweet and one salty and bitter, and He placed between them a barrier and prohibiting partition.
ayahs.n: and it is he who has released simultaneously the two seas one fresh and sweet and one salty and bitter and he placed between them a barrier and prohibiting partition

ayahs.t: And it is He who has created from water a human being and made him [a relative by] lineage and marriage. And ever is your Lord competent [concerning creation].
ayahs.n: and it is he who has created from water a human being and made him a relative by lineage and marriage and ever is your lord competent concerning creation

ayahs.t: But they worship rather than Allah that which does not benefit them or harm them, and the disbeliever is ever, against his Lord, an assistant [to Satan].
ayahs.n: but they worship rather than allah that which does not benefit them or harm them and the disbeliever is ever against his lord an assistant to satan

ayahs.t: And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a bringer of good tidings and a warner.
ayahs.n: and we have not sent you o muhammad except as a bringer of good tidings and a warner

ayahs.t: Say, "I do not ask of you for it any payment - only that whoever wills might take to his Lord a way."
ayahs.n: say i do not ask of you for it any payment only that whoever wills might take to his lord a way

ayahs.t: And rely upon the Ever-Living who does not die, and exalt [Allah] with His praise. And sufficient is He to be, with the sins of His servants, Acquainted -
ayahs.n: and rely upon the ever living who does not die and exalt allah with his praise and sufficient is he to be with the sins of his servants acquainted

ayahs.t: He who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days and then established Himself above the Throne - the Most Merciful, so ask about Him one well informed.
ayahs.n: he who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days and then established himself above the throne the most merciful so ask about him one well informed

ayahs.t: And when it is said to them, "Prostrate to the Most Merciful," they say, "And what is the Most Merciful? Should we prostrate to that which you order us?" And it increases them in aversion.
ayahs.n: and when it is said to them prostrate to the most merciful they say and what is the most merciful should we prostrate to that which you order us and it increases them in aversion

ayahs.t: Blessed is He who has placed in the sky great stars and placed therein a [burning] lamp and luminous moon.
ayahs.n: blessed is he who has placed in the sky great stars and placed therein a burning lamp and luminous moon

ayahs.t: And it is He who has made the night and the day in succession for whoever desires to remember or desires gratitude.
ayahs.n: and it is he who has made the night and the day in succession for whoever desires to remember or desires gratitude

ayahs.t: And the servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk upon the earth easily, and when the ignorant address them [harshly], they say [words of] peace,
ayahs.n: and the servants of the most merciful are those who walk upon the earth easily and when the ignorant address them harshly they say words of peace

ayahs.t: And those who spend [part of] the night to their Lord prostrating and standing [in prayer]
ayahs.n: and those who spend part of the night to their lord prostrating and standing in prayer

ayahs.t: And those who say, "Our Lord, avert from us the punishment of Hell. Indeed, its punishment is ever adhering;
ayahs.n: and those who say our lord avert from us the punishment of hell indeed its punishment is ever adhering

ayahs.t: Indeed, it is evil as a settlement and residence."
ayahs.n: indeed it is evil as a settlement and residence

ayahs.t: And [they are] those who, when they spend, do so not excessively or sparingly but are ever, between that, [justly] moderate
ayahs.n: and they are those who when they spend do so not excessively or sparingly but are ever between that justly moderate

ayahs.t: And those who do not invoke with Allah another deity or kill the soul which Allah has forbidden [to be killed], except by right, and do not commit unlawful sexual intercourse. And whoever should do that will meet a penalty.
ayahs.n: and those who do not invoke with allah another deity or kill the soul which allah has forbidden to be killed except by right and do not commit unlawful sexual intercourse and whoever should do that will meet a penalty

ayahs.t: Multiplied for him is the punishment on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein humiliated -
ayahs.n: multiplied for him is the punishment on the day of resurrection and he will abide therein humiliated

ayahs.t: Except for those who repent, believe and do righteous work. For them Allah will replace their evil deeds with good. And ever is Allah Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: except for those who repent believe and do righteous work for them allah will replace their evil deeds with good and ever is allah forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: And he who repents and does righteousness does indeed turn to Allah with [accepted] repentance.
ayahs.n: and he who repents and does righteousness does indeed turn to allah with accepted repentance

ayahs.t: And [they are] those who do not testify to falsehood, and when they pass near ill speech, they pass by with dignity.
ayahs.n: and they are those who do not testify to falsehood and when they pass near ill speech they pass by with dignity

ayahs.t: And those who, when reminded of the verses of their Lord, do not fall upon them deaf and blind.
ayahs.n: and those who when reminded of the verses of their lord do not fall upon them deaf and blind

ayahs.t: And those who say, "Our Lord, grant us from among our wives and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us an example for the righteous."
ayahs.n: and those who say our lord grant us from among our wives and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us an example for the righteous

ayahs.t: Those will be awarded the Chamber for what they patiently endured, and they will be received therein with greetings and [words of] peace.
ayahs.n: those will be awarded the chamber for what they patiently endured and they will be received therein with greetings and words of peace

ayahs.t: Abiding eternally therein. Good is the settlement and residence.
ayahs.n: abiding eternally therein good is the settlement and residence

ayahs.t: Say, "What would my Lord care for you if not for your supplication?" For you [disbelievers] have denied, so your denial is going to be adherent.
ayahs.n: say what would my lord care for you if not for your supplication for you disbelievers have denied so your denial is going to be adherent

ayahs.t: Ta, Seen, Meem.
ayahs.n: ta seen meem

ayahs.t: These are the verses of the clear Book.
ayahs.n: these are the verses of the clear book

ayahs.t: Perhaps, [O Muhammad], you would kill yourself with grief that they will not be believers.
ayahs.n: perhaps o muhammad you would kill yourself with grief that they will not be believers

ayahs.t: If We willed, We could send down to them from the sky a sign for which their necks would remain humbled.
ayahs.n: if we willed we could send down to them from the sky a sign for which their necks would remain humbled

ayahs.t: And no revelation comes to them anew from the Most Merciful except that they turn away from it.
ayahs.n: and no revelation comes to them anew from the most merciful except that they turn away from it

ayahs.t: For they have already denied, but there will come to them the news of that which they used to ridicule.
ayahs.n: for they have already denied but there will come to them the news of that which they used to ridicule

ayahs.t: Did they not look at the earth - how much We have produced therein from every noble kind?
ayahs.n: did they not look at the earth how much we have produced therein from every noble kind

ayahs.t: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
ayahs.n: indeed in that is a sign but most of them were not to be believers

ayahs.t: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
ayahs.n: and indeed your lord he is the exalted in might the merciful

ayahs.t: And [mention] when your Lord called Moses, [saying], "Go to the wrongdoing people -
ayahs.n: and mention when your lord called moses saying go to the wrongdoing people

ayahs.t: The people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear Allah?"
ayahs.n: the people of pharaoh will they not fear allah

ayahs.t: He said, "My Lord, indeed I fear that they will deny me
ayahs.n: he said my lord indeed i fear that they will deny me

ayahs.t: And that my breast will tighten and my tongue will not be fluent, so send for Aaron.
ayahs.n: and that my breast will tighten and my tongue will not be fluent so send for aaron

ayahs.t: And they have upon me a [claim due to] sin, so I fear that they will kill me."
ayahs.n: and they have upon me a claim due to sin so i fear that they will kill me

ayahs.t: [Allah] said, "No. Go both of you with Our signs; indeed, We are with you, listening.
ayahs.n: allah said no go both of you with our signs indeed we are with you listening

ayahs.t: Go to Pharaoh and say, 'We are the messengers of the Lord of the worlds,
ayahs.n: go to pharaoh and say we are the messengers of the lord of the worlds

ayahs.t: [Commanded to say], "Send with us the Children of Israel."'"
ayahs.n: commanded to say send with us the children of israel

ayahs.t: [Pharaoh] said, "Did we not raise you among us as a child, and you remained among us for years of your life?
ayahs.n: pharaoh said did we not raise you among us as a child and you remained among us for years of your life

ayahs.t: And [then] you did your deed which you did, and you were of the ungrateful."
ayahs.n: and then you did your deed which you did and you were of the ungrateful

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "I did it, then, while I was of those astray.
ayahs.n: moses said i did it then while i was of those astray

ayahs.t: So I fled from you when I feared you. Then my Lord granted me wisdom and prophethood and appointed me [as one] of the messengers.
ayahs.n: so i fled from you when i feared you then my lord granted me wisdom and prophethood and appointed me as one of the messengers

ayahs.t: And is this a favor of which you remind me - that you have enslaved the Children of Israel?"
ayahs.n: and is this a favor of which you remind me that you have enslaved the children of israel

ayahs.t: Said Pharaoh, "And what is the Lord of the worlds?"
ayahs.n: said pharaoh and what is the lord of the worlds

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "The Lord of the heavens and earth and that between them, if you should be convinced."
ayahs.n: moses said the lord of the heavens and earth and that between them if you should be convinced

ayahs.t: [Pharaoh] said to those around him, "Do you not hear?"
ayahs.n: pharaoh said to those around him do you not hear

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "Your Lord and the Lord of your first forefathers."
ayahs.n: moses said your lord and the lord of your first forefathers

ayahs.t: [Pharaoh] said, "Indeed, your 'messenger' who has been sent to you is mad."
ayahs.n: pharaoh said indeed your messenger who has been sent to you is mad

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "Lord of the east and the west and that between them, if you were to reason."
ayahs.n: moses said lord of the east and the west and that between them if you were to reason

ayahs.t: [Pharaoh] said, "If you take a god other than me, I will surely place you among those imprisoned."
ayahs.n: pharaoh said if you take a god other than me i will surely place you among those imprisoned

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "Even if I brought you proof manifest?"
ayahs.n: moses said even if i brought you proof manifest

ayahs.t: [Pharaoh] said, "Then bring it, if you should be of the truthful."
ayahs.n: pharaoh said then bring it if you should be of the truthful

ayahs.t: So [Moses] threw his staff, and suddenly it was a serpent manifest.
ayahs.n: so moses threw his staff and suddenly it was a serpent manifest

ayahs.t: And he drew out his hand; thereupon it was white for the observers.
ayahs.n: and he drew out his hand thereupon it was white for the observers

ayahs.t: [Pharaoh] said to the eminent ones around him, "Indeed, this is a learned magician.
ayahs.n: pharaoh said to the eminent ones around him indeed this is a learned magician

ayahs.t: He wants to drive you out of your land by his magic, so what do you advise?"
ayahs.n: he wants to drive you out of your land by his magic so what do you advise

ayahs.t: They said, "Postpone [the matter of] him and his brother and send among the cities gatherers
ayahs.n: they said postpone the matter of him and his brother and send among the cities gatherers

ayahs.t: Who will bring you every learned, skilled magician."
ayahs.n: who will bring you every learned skilled magician

ayahs.t: So the magicians were assembled for the appointment of a well-known day.
ayahs.n: so the magicians were assembled for the appointment of a well known day

ayahs.t: And it was said to the people, "Will you congregate
ayahs.n: and it was said to the people will you congregate

ayahs.t: That we might follow the magicians if they are the predominant?"
ayahs.n: that we might follow the magicians if they are the predominant

ayahs.t: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, "Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?"
ayahs.n: and when the magicians arrived they said to pharaoh is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant

ayahs.t: He said, "Yes, and indeed, you will then be of those near [to me]."
ayahs.n: he said yes and indeed you will then be of those near to me

ayahs.t: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."

ayahs.n: and indeed o muhammad you receive the quran from one wise and knowing

ayahs.t: [Mention] when Moses said to his family, "Indeed, I have perceived a fire. I will bring you from there information or will bring you a burning torch that you may warm yourselves."
ayahs.n: mention when moses said to his family indeed i have perceived a fire i will bring you from there information or will bring you a burning torch that you may warm yourselves

ayahs.t: But when he came to it, he was called, "Blessed is whoever is at the fire and whoever is around it. And exalted is Allah, Lord of the worlds.
ayahs.n: but when he came to it he was called blessed is whoever is at the fire and whoever is around it and exalted is allah lord of the worlds

ayahs.t: O Moses, indeed it is I - Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise."
ayahs.n: o moses indeed it is i allah the exalted in might the wise

ayahs.t: And [he was told], "Throw down your staff." But when he saw it writhing as if it were a snake, he turned in flight and did not return. [Allah said], "O Moses, fear not. Indeed, in My presence the messengers do not fear.
ayahs.n: and he was told throw down your staff but when he saw it writhing as if it were a snake he turned in flight and did not return allah said o moses fear not indeed in my presence the messengers do not fear

ayahs.t: Otherwise, he who wrongs, then substitutes good after evil - indeed, I am Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: otherwise he who wrongs then substitutes good after evil indeed i am forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: And put your hand into the opening of your garment [at the breast]; it will come out white without disease. [These are] among the nine signs [you will take] to Pharaoh and his people. Indeed, they have been a people defiantly disobedient."
ayahs.n: and put your hand into the opening of your garment at the breast it will come out white without disease these are among the nine signs you will take to pharaoh and his people indeed they have been a people defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: But when there came to them Our visible signs, they said, "This is obvious magic."
ayahs.n: but when there came to them our visible signs they said this is obvious magic

ayahs.t: And they rejected them, while their [inner] selves were convinced thereof, out of injustice and haughtiness. So see how was the end of the corrupters.
ayahs.n: and they rejected them while their inner selves were convinced thereof out of injustice and haughtiness so see how was the end of the corrupters

ayahs.t: And We had certainly given to David and Solomon knowledge, and they said, "Praise [is due] to Allah, who has favored us over many of His believing servants."
ayahs.n: and we had certainly given to david and solomon knowledge and they said praise is due to allah who has favored us over many of his believing servants

ayahs.t: And Solomon inherited David. He said, "O people, we have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given from all things. Indeed, this is evident bounty."
ayahs.n: and solomon inherited david he said o people we have been taught the language of birds and we have been given from all things indeed this is evident bounty

ayahs.t: And gathered for Solomon were his soldiers of the jinn and men and birds, and they were [marching] in rows.
ayahs.n: and gathered for solomon were his soldiers of the jinn and men and birds and they were marching in rows

ayahs.t: Until, when they came upon the valley of the ants, an ant said, "O ants, enter your dwellings that you not be crushed by Solomon and his soldiers while they perceive not."
ayahs.n: until when they came upon the valley of the ants an ant said o ants enter your dwellings that you not be crushed by solomon and his soldiers while they perceive not

ayahs.t: So [Solomon] smiled, amused at her speech, and said, "My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to do righteousness of which You approve. And admit me by Your mercy into [the ranks of] Your righteous servants."
ayahs.n: so solomon smiled amused at her speech and said my lord enable me to be grateful for your favor which you have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to do righteousness of which you approve and admit me by your mercy into the ranks of your righteous servants

ayahs.t: And he took attendance of the birds and said, "Why do I not see the hoopoe - or is he among the absent?
ayahs.n: and he took attendance of the birds and said why do i not see the hoopoe or is he among the absent

ayahs.t: I will surely punish him with a severe punishment or slaughter him unless he brings me clear authorization."
ayahs.n: i will surely punish him with a severe punishment or slaughter him unless he brings me clear authorization

ayahs.t: But the hoopoe stayed not long and said, "I have encompassed [in knowledge] that which you have not encompassed, and I have come to you from Sheba with certain news.
ayahs.n: but the hoopoe stayed not long and said i have encompassed in knowledge that which you have not encompassed and i have come to you from sheba with certain news

ayahs.t: Indeed, I found [there] a woman ruling them, and she has been given of all things, and she has a great throne.
ayahs.n: indeed i found there a woman ruling them and she has been given of all things and she has a great throne

ayahs.t: I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allah, and Satan has made their deeds pleasing to them and averted them from [His] way, so they are not guided,
ayahs.n: i found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of allah and satan has made their deeds pleasing to them and averted them from his way so they are not guided

ayahs.t: [And] so they do not prostrate to Allah, who brings forth what is hidden within the heavens and the earth and knows what you conceal and what you declare -
ayahs.n: and so they do not prostrate to allah who brings forth what is hidden within the heavens and the earth and knows what you conceal and what you declare

ayahs.t: Allah - there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Great Throne."
ayahs.n: allah there is no deity except him lord of the great throne

ayahs.t: [Solomon] said, "We will see whether you were truthful or were of the liars.
ayahs.n: solomon said we will see whether you were truthful or were of the liars

ayahs.t: Take this letter of mine and deliver it to them. Then leave them and see what [answer] they will return."
ayahs.n: take this letter of mine and deliver it to them then leave them and see what answer they will return

ayahs.t: She said, "O eminent ones, indeed, to me has been delivered a noble letter.
ayahs.n: she said o eminent ones indeed to me has been delivered a noble letter

ayahs.t: Indeed, it is from Solomon, and indeed, it reads: 'In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful,
ayahs.n: indeed it is from solomon and indeed it reads in the name of allah the entirely merciful the especially merciful

ayahs.t: Be not haughty with me but come to me in submission [as Muslims].' "
ayahs.n: be not haughty with me but come to me in submission as muslims

ayahs.t: She said, "O eminent ones, advise me in my affair. I would not decide a matter until you witness [for] me."
ayahs.n: she said o eminent ones advise me in my affair i would not decide a matter until you witness for me

ayahs.t: They said, "We are men of strength and of great military might, but the command is yours, so see what you will command."
ayahs.n: they said we are men of strength and of great military might but the command is yours so see what you will command

ayahs.t: She said, "Indeed kings - when they enter a city, they ruin it and render the honored of its people humbled. And thus do they do.
ayahs.n: she said indeed kings when they enter a city they ruin it and render the honored of its people humbled and thus do they do

ayahs.t: But indeed, I will send to them a gift and see with what [reply] the messengers will return."
ayahs.n: but indeed i will send to them a gift and see with what reply the messengers will return

ayahs.t: So when they came to Solomon, he said, "Do you provide me with wealth? But what Allah has given me is better than what He has given you. Rather, it is you who rejoice in your gift.
ayahs.n: so when they came to solomon he said do you provide me with wealth but what allah has given me is better than what he has given you rather it is you who rejoice in your gift

ayahs.t: Return to them, for we will surely come to them with soldiers that they will be powerless to encounter, and we will surely expel them therefrom in humiliation, and they will be debased."
ayahs.n: return to them for we will surely come to them with soldiers that they will be powerless to encounter and we will surely expel them therefrom in humiliation and they will be debased

ayahs.t: [Solomon] said, "O assembly [of jinn], which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?"
ayahs.n: solomon said o assembly of jinn which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission

ayahs.t: A powerful one from among the jinn said, "I will bring it to you before you rise from your place, and indeed, I am for this [task] strong and trustworthy."
ayahs.n: a powerful one from among the jinn said i will bring it to you before you rise from your place and indeed i am for this task strong and trustworthy

ayahs.t: Said one who had knowledge from the Scripture, "I will bring it to you before your glance returns to you." And when [Solomon] saw it placed before him, he said, "This is from the favor of my Lord to test me whether I will be grateful or ungrateful. And whoever is grateful - his gratitude is only for [the benefit of] himself. And whoever is ungrateful - then indeed, my Lord is Free of need and Generous."
ayahs.n: said one who had knowledge from the scripture i will bring it to you before your glance returns to you and when solomon saw it placed before him he said this is from the favor of my lord to test me whether i will be grateful or ungrateful and whoever is grateful his gratitude is only for the benefit of himself and whoever is ungrateful then indeed my lord is free of need and generous

ayahs.t: He said, "Disguise for her her throne; we will see whether she will be guided [to truth] or will be of those who is not guided."
ayahs.n: he said disguise for her her throne we will see whether she will be guided to truth or will be of those who is not guided

ayahs.t: So when she arrived, it was said [to her], "Is your throne like this?" She said, "[It is] as though it was it." [Solomon said], "And we were given knowledge before her, and we have been Muslims [in submission to Allah].
ayahs.n: so when she arrived it was said to her is your throne like this she said it is as though it was it solomon said and we were given knowledge before her and we have been muslims in submission to allah

ayahs.t: And that which she was worshipping other than Allah had averted her [from submission to Him]. Indeed, she was from a disbelieving people."
ayahs.n: and that which she was worshipping other than allah had averted her from submission to him indeed she was from a disbelieving people

ayahs.t: She was told, "Enter the palace." But when she saw it, she thought it was a body of water and uncovered her shins [to wade through]. He said, "Indeed, it is a palace [whose floor is] made smooth with glass." She said, "My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself, and I submit with Solomon to Allah, Lord of the worlds."
ayahs.n: she was told enter the palace but when she saw it she thought it was a body of water and uncovered her shins to wade through he said indeed it is a palace whose floor is made smooth with glass she said my lord indeed i have wronged myself and i submit with solomon to allah lord of the worlds

ayahs.t: And We had certainly sent to Thamud their brother Salih, [saying], "Worship Allah," and at once they were two parties conflicting.
ayahs.n: and we had certainly sent to thamud their brother salih saying worship allah and at once they were two parties conflicting

ayahs.t: He said, "O my people, why are you impatient for evil instead of good? Why do you not seek forgiveness of Allah that you may receive mercy?"
ayahs.n: he said o my people why are you impatient for evil instead of good why do you not seek forgiveness of allah that you may receive mercy

ayahs.t: They said, "We consider you a bad omen, you and those with you." He said, "Your omen is with Allah. Rather, you are a people being tested."
ayahs.n: they said we consider you a bad omen you and those with you he said your omen is with allah rather you are a people being tested

ayahs.t: And there were in the city nine family heads causing corruption in the land and not amending [its affairs].
ayahs.n: and there were in the city nine family heads causing corruption in the land and not amending its affairs

ayahs.t: They said, "Take a mutual oath by Allah that we will kill him by night, he and his family. Then we will say to his executor, 'We did not witness the destruction of his family, and indeed, we are truthful.' "
ayahs.n: they said take a mutual oath by allah that we will kill him by night he and his family then we will say to his executor we did not witness the destruction of his family and indeed we are truthful

ayahs.t: And they planned a plan, and We planned a plan, while they perceived not.
ayahs.n: and they planned a plan and we planned a plan while they perceived not

ayahs.t: Then look how was the outcome of their plan - that We destroyed them and their people, all.
ayahs.n: then look how was the outcome of their plan that we destroyed them and their people all

ayahs.t: So those are their houses, desolate because of the wrong they had done. Indeed in that is a sign for people who know.
ayahs.n: so those are their houses desolate because of the wrong they had done indeed in that is a sign for people who know

ayahs.t: And We saved those who believed and used to fear Allah.
ayahs.n: and we saved those who believed and used to fear allah

ayahs.t: And [mention] Lot, when he said to his people, "Do you commit immorality while you are seeing?
ayahs.n: and mention lot when he said to his people do you commit immorality while you are seeing

ayahs.t: Do you indeed approach men with desire instead of women? Rather, you are a people behaving ignorantly."
ayahs.n: do you indeed approach men with desire instead of women rather you are a people behaving ignorantly

ayahs.t: But the answer of his people was not except that they said, "Expel the family of Lot from your city. Indeed, they are people who keep themselves pure."
ayahs.n: but the answer of his people was not except that they said expel the family of lot from your city indeed they are people who keep themselves pure

ayahs.t: So We saved him and his family, except for his wife; We destined her to be of those who remained behind.
ayahs.n: so we saved him and his family except for his wife we destined her to be of those who remained behind

ayahs.t: And We rained upon them a rain [of stones], and evil was the rain of those who were warned.
ayahs.n: and we rained upon them a rain of stones and evil was the rain of those who were warned

ayahs.t: Say, [O Muhammad], "Praise be to Allah, and peace upon His servants whom He has chosen. Is Allah better or what they associate with Him?"
ayahs.n: say o muhammad praise be to allah and peace upon his servants whom he has chosen is allah better or what they associate with him

ayahs.t: [More precisely], is He [not best] who created the heavens and the earth and sent down for you rain from the sky, causing to grow thereby gardens of joyful beauty which you could not [otherwise] have grown the trees thereof? Is there a deity with Allah? [No], but they are a people who ascribe equals [to Him].
ayahs.n: more precisely is he not best who created the heavens and the earth and sent down for you rain from the sky causing to grow thereby gardens of joyful beauty which you could not otherwise have grown the trees thereof is there a deity with allah no but they are a people who ascribe equals to him

ayahs.t: Is He [not best] who made the earth a stable ground and placed within it rivers and made for it firmly set mountains and placed between the two seas a barrier? Is there a deity with Allah? [No], but most of them do not know.
ayahs.n: is he not best who made the earth a stable ground and placed within it rivers and made for it firmly set mountains and placed between the two seas a barrier is there a deity with allah no but most of them do not know

ayahs.t: Is He [not best] who responds to the desperate one when he calls upon Him and removes evil and makes you inheritors of the earth? Is there a deity with Allah? Little do you remember.
ayahs.n: is he not best who responds to the desperate one when he calls upon him and removes evil and makes you inheritors of the earth is there a deity with allah little do you remember

ayahs.t: Is He [not best] who guides you through the darknesses of the land and sea and who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy? Is there a deity with Allah? High is Allah above whatever they associate with Him.
ayahs.n: is he not best who guides you through the darknesses of the land and sea and who sends the winds as good tidings before his mercy is there a deity with allah high is allah above whatever they associate with him

ayahs.t: Is He [not best] who begins creation and then repeats it and who provides for you from the heaven and earth? Is there a deity with Allah? Say, "Produce your proof, if you should be truthful."
ayahs.n: is he not best who begins creation and then repeats it and who provides for you from the heaven and earth is there a deity with allah say produce your proof if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: Say, "None in the heavens and earth knows the unseen except Allah, and they do not perceive when they will be resurrected."
ayahs.n: say none in the heavens and earth knows the unseen except allah and they do not perceive when they will be resurrected

ayahs.t: Rather, their knowledge is arrested concerning the Hereafter. Rather, they are in doubt about it. Rather, they are, concerning it, blind.
ayahs.n: rather their knowledge is arrested concerning the hereafter rather they are in doubt about it rather they are concerning it blind

ayahs.t: And those who disbelieve say, "When we have become dust as well as our forefathers, will we indeed be brought out [of the graves]?
ayahs.n: and those who disbelieve say when we have become dust as well as our forefathers will we indeed be brought out of the graves

ayahs.t: We have been promised this, we and our forefathers, before. This is not but legends of the former peoples."
ayahs.n: we have been promised this we and our forefathers before this is not but legends of the former peoples

ayahs.t: Say, [O Muhammad], "Travel through the land and observe how was the end of the criminals."
ayahs.n: say o muhammad travel through the land and observe how was the end of the criminals

ayahs.t: And grieve not over them or be in distress from what they conspire.
ayahs.n: and grieve not over them or be in distress from what they conspire

ayahs.t: And they say, "When is [the fulfillment of] this promise, if you should be truthful?"
ayahs.n: and they say when is the fulfillment of this promise if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: Say, "Perhaps it is close behind you - some of that for which you are impatient.
ayahs.n: say perhaps it is close behind you some of that for which you are impatient

ayahs.t: And indeed, your Lord is full of bounty for the people, but most of them do not show gratitude."
ayahs.n: and indeed your lord is full of bounty for the people but most of them do not show gratitude

ayahs.t: And indeed, your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they declare.
ayahs.n: and indeed your lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they declare

ayahs.t: And there is nothing concealed within the heaven and the earth except that it is in a clear Register.
ayahs.n: and there is nothing concealed within the heaven and the earth except that it is in a clear register

ayahs.t: Indeed, this Qur'an relates to the Children of Israel most of that over which they disagree.
ayahs.n: indeed this quran relates to the children of israel most of that over which they disagree

ayahs.t: And indeed, it is guidance and mercy for the believers.
ayahs.n: and indeed it is guidance and mercy for the believers

ayahs.t: Indeed, your Lord will judge between them by His [wise] judgement. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.
ayahs.n: indeed your lord will judge between them by his wise judgement and he is the exalted in might the knowing

ayahs.t: So rely upon Allah; indeed, you are upon the clear truth.
ayahs.n: so rely upon allah indeed you are upon the clear truth

ayahs.t: Indeed, you will not make the dead hear, nor will you make the deaf hear the call when they have turned their backs retreating.
ayahs.n: indeed you will not make the dead hear nor will you make the deaf hear the call when they have turned their backs retreating

ayahs.t: And you cannot guide the blind away from their error. You will only make hear those who believe in Our verses so they are Muslims [submitting to Allah].
ayahs.n: and you cannot guide the blind away from their error you will only make hear those who believe in our verses so they are muslims submitting to allah

ayahs.t: And when the word befalls them, We will bring forth for them a creature from the earth speaking to them, [saying] that the people were, of Our verses, not certain [in faith].
ayahs.n: and when the word befalls them we will bring forth for them a creature from the earth speaking to them saying that the people were of our verses not certain in faith

ayahs.t: And [warn of] the Day when We will gather from every nation a company of those who deny Our signs, and they will be [driven] in rows
ayahs.n: and warn of the day when we will gather from every nation a company of those who deny our signs and they will be driven in rows

ayahs.t: Until, when they arrive [at the place of Judgement], He will say, "Did you deny My signs while you encompassed them not in knowledge, or what [was it that] you were doing?"
ayahs.n: until when they arrive at the place of judgement he will say did you deny my signs while you encompassed them not in knowledge or what was it that you were doing

ayahs.t: And the decree will befall them for the wrong they did, and they will not [be able to] speak.
ayahs.n: and the decree will befall them for the wrong they did and they will not be able to speak

ayahs.t: Do they not see that We made the night that they may rest therein and the day giving sight? Indeed in that are signs for a people who believe.
ayahs.n: do they not see that we made the night that they may rest therein and the day giving sight indeed in that are signs for a people who believe

ayahs.t: And [warn of] the Day the Horn will be blown, and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified except whom Allah wills. And all will come to Him humbled.
ayahs.n: and warn of the day the horn will be blown and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified except whom allah wills and all will come to him humbled

ayahs.t: And you see the mountains, thinking them rigid, while they will pass as the passing of clouds. [It is] the work of Allah, who perfected all things. Indeed, He is Acquainted with that which you do.
ayahs.n: and you see the mountains thinking them rigid while they will pass as the passing of clouds it is the work of allah who perfected all things indeed he is acquainted with that which you do

ayahs.t: Whoever comes [at Judgement] with a good deed will have better than it, and they, from the terror of that Day, will be safe.
ayahs.n: whoever comes at judgement with a good deed will have better than it and they from the terror of that day will be safe

ayahs.t: And whoever comes with an evil deed - their faces will be overturned into the Fire, [and it will be said], "Are you recompensed except for what you used to do?"
ayahs.n: and whoever comes with an evil deed their faces will be overturned into the fire and it will be said are you recompensed except for what you used to do

ayahs.t: [Say, O Muhammad], "I have only been commanded to worship the Lord of this city, who made it sacred and to whom [belongs] all things. And I am commanded to be of the Muslims [those who submit to Allah]
ayahs.n: say o muhammad i have only been commanded to worship the lord of this city who made it sacred and to whom belongs all things and i am commanded to be of the muslims those who submit to allah

ayahs.t: And to recite the Qur'an." And whoever is guided is only guided for [the benefit of] himself; and whoever strays - say, "I am only [one] of the warners."
ayahs.n: and to recite the quran and whoever is guided is only guided for the benefit of himself and whoever strays say i am only one of the warners

ayahs.t: And say, "[All] praise is [due] to Allah. He will show you His signs, and you will recognize them. And your Lord is not unaware of what you do."
ayahs.n: and say all praise is due to allah he will show you his signs and you will recognize them and your lord is not unaware of what you do

ayahs.t: Ta, Seen, Meem.
ayahs.n: ta seen meem

ayahs.t: These are the verses of the clear Book.
ayahs.n: these are the verses of the clear book

ayahs.t: We recite to you from the news of Moses and Pharaoh in truth for a people who believe.
ayahs.n: we recite to you from the news of moses and pharaoh in truth for a people who believe

ayahs.t: Indeed, Pharaoh exalted himself in the land and made its people into factions, oppressing a sector among them, slaughtering their [newborn] sons and keeping their females alive. Indeed, he was of the corrupters.
ayahs.n: indeed pharaoh exalted himself in the land and made its people into factions oppressing a sector among them slaughtering their newborn sons and keeping their females alive indeed he was of the corrupters

ayahs.t: And We wanted to confer favor upon those who were oppressed in the land and make them leaders and make them inheritors
ayahs.n: and we wanted to confer favor upon those who were oppressed in the land and make them leaders and make them inheritors

ayahs.t: And establish them in the land and show Pharaoh and [his minister] Haman and their soldiers through them that which they had feared.
ayahs.n: and establish them in the land and show pharaoh and his minister haman and their soldiers through them that which they had feared

ayahs.t: And We inspired to the mother of Moses, "Suckle him; but when you fear for him, cast him into the river and do not fear and do not grieve. Indeed, We will return him to you and will make him [one] of the messengers."
ayahs.n: and we inspired to the mother of moses suckle him but when you fear for him cast him into the river and do not fear and do not grieve indeed we will return him to you and will make him one of the messengers

ayahs.t: And the family of Pharaoh picked him up [out of the river] so that he would become to them an enemy and a [cause of] grief. Indeed, Pharaoh and Haman and their soldiers were deliberate sinners.
ayahs.n: and the family of pharaoh picked him up out of the river so that he would become to them an enemy and a cause of grief indeed pharaoh and haman and their soldiers were deliberate sinners

ayahs.t: And the wife of Pharaoh said, "[He will be] a comfort of the eye for me and for you. Do not kill him; perhaps he may benefit us, or we may adopt him as a son." And they perceived not.
ayahs.n: and the wife of pharaoh said he will be a comfort of the eye for me and for you do not kill him perhaps he may benefit us or we may adopt him as a son and they perceived not

ayahs.t: And the heart of Moses' mother became empty [of all else]. She was about to disclose [the matter concerning] him had We not bound fast her heart that she would be of the believers.
ayahs.n: and the heart of moses mother became empty of all else she was about to disclose the matter concerning him had we not bound fast her heart that she would be of the believers

ayahs.t: And she said to his sister, "Follow him"; so she watched him from a distance while they perceived not.
ayahs.n: and she said to his sister follow him so she watched him from a distance while they perceived not

ayahs.t: And We had prevented from him [all] wet nurses before, so she said, "Shall I direct you to a household that will be responsible for him for you while they are to him [for his upbringing] sincere?"
ayahs.n: and we had prevented from him all wet nurses before so she said shall i direct you to a household that will be responsible for him for you while they are to him for his upbringing sincere

ayahs.t: So We restored him to his mother that she might be content and not grieve and that she would know that the promise of Allah is true. But most of the people do not know.
ayahs.n: so we restored him to his mother that she might be content and not grieve and that she would know that the promise of allah is true but most of the people do not know

ayahs.t: And when he attained his full strength and was [mentally] mature, We bestowed upon him judgement and knowledge. And thus do We reward the doers of good.
ayahs.n: and when he attained his full strength and was mentally mature we bestowed upon him judgement and knowledge and thus do we reward the doers of good

ayahs.t: And he entered the city at a time of inattention by its people and found therein two men fighting: one from his faction and one from among his enemy. And the one from his faction called for help to him against the one from his enemy, so Moses struck him and [unintentionally] killed him. [Moses] said, "This is from the work of Satan. Indeed, he is a manifest, misleading enemy."
ayahs.n: and he entered the city at a time of inattention by its people and found therein two men fighting one from his faction and one from among his enemy and the one from his faction called for help to him against the one from his enemy so moses struck him and unintentionally killed him moses said this is from the work of satan indeed he is a manifest misleading enemy

ayahs.t: He said, "My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself, so forgive me," and He forgave him. Indeed, He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.
ayahs.n: he said my lord indeed i have wronged myself so forgive me and he forgave him indeed he is the forgiving the merciful

ayahs.t: He said, "My Lord, for the favor You bestowed upon me, I will never be an assistant to the criminals."
ayahs.n: he said my lord for the favor you bestowed upon me i will never be an assistant to the criminals

ayahs.t: And he became inside the city fearful and anticipating [exposure], when suddenly the one who sought his help the previous day cried out to him [once again]. Moses said to him, "Indeed, you are an evident, [persistent] deviator."
ayahs.n: and he became inside the city fearful and anticipating exposure when suddenly the one who sought his help the previous day cried out to him once again moses said to him indeed you are an evident persistent deviator

ayahs.t: And when he wanted to strike the one who was an enemy to both of them, he said, "O Moses, do you intend to kill me as you killed someone yesterday? You only want to be a tyrant in the land and do not want to be of the amenders."
ayahs.n: and when he wanted to strike the one who was an enemy to both of them he said o moses do you intend to kill me as you killed someone yesterday you only want to be a tyrant in the land and do not want to be of the amenders

ayahs.t: And a man came from the farthest end of the city, running. He said, "O Moses, indeed the eminent ones are conferring over you [intending] to kill you, so leave [the city]; indeed, I am to you of the sincere advisors."
ayahs.n: and a man came from the farthest end of the city running he said o moses indeed the eminent ones are conferring over you intending to kill you so leave the city indeed i am to you of the sincere advisors

ayahs.t: So he left it, fearful and anticipating [apprehension]. He said, "My Lord, save me from the wrongdoing people."
ayahs.n: so he left it fearful and anticipating apprehension he said my lord save me from the wrongdoing people

ayahs.t: And when he directed himself toward Madyan, he said, "Perhaps my Lord will guide me to the sound way."
ayahs.n: and when he directed himself toward madyan he said perhaps my lord will guide me to the sound way

ayahs.t: And when he came to the well of Madyan, he found there a crowd of people watering [their flocks], and he found aside from them two women driving back [their flocks]. He said, "What is your circumstance?" They said, "We do not water until the shepherds dispatch [their flocks]; and our father is an old man."
ayahs.n: and when he came to the well of madyan he found there a crowd of people watering their flocks and he found aside from them two women driving back their flocks he said what is your circumstance they said we do not water until the shepherds dispatch their flocks and our father is an old man

ayahs.t: So he watered [their flocks] for them; then he went back to the shade and said, "My Lord, indeed I am, for whatever good You would send down to me, in need."
ayahs.n: so he watered their flocks for them then he went back to the shade and said my lord indeed i am for whatever good you would send down to me in need

ayahs.t: Then one of the two women came to him walking with shyness. She said, "Indeed, my father invites you that he may reward you for having watered for us." So when he came to him and related to him the story, he said, "Fear not. You have escaped from the wrongdoing people."
ayahs.n: then one of the two women came to him walking with shyness she said indeed my father invites you that he may reward you for having watered for us so when he came to him and related to him the story he said fear not you have escaped from the wrongdoing people

ayahs.t: One of the women said, "O my father, hire him. Indeed, the best one you can hire is the strong and the trustworthy."
ayahs.n: one of the women said o my father hire him indeed the best one you can hire is the strong and the trustworthy

ayahs.t: He said, "Indeed, I wish to wed you one of these, my two daughters, on [the condition] that you serve me for eight years; but if you complete ten, it will be [as a favor] from you. And I do not wish to put you in difficulty. You will find me, if Allah wills, from among the righteous."
ayahs.n: he said indeed i wish to wed you one of these my two daughters on the condition that you serve me for eight years but if you complete ten it will be as a favor from you and i do not wish to put you in difficulty you will find me if allah wills from among the righteous

ayahs.t: [Moses] said, "That is [established] between me and you. Whichever of the two terms I complete - there is no injustice to me, and Allah, over what we say, is Witness."
ayahs.n: moses said that is established between me and you whichever of the two terms i complete there is no injustice to me and allah over what we say is witness

ayahs.t: And when Moses had completed the term and was traveling with his family, he perceived from the direction of the mount a fire. He said to his family, "Stay here; indeed, I have perceived a fire. Perhaps I will bring you from there [some] information or burning wood from the fire that you may warm yourselves."
ayahs.n: and when moses had completed the term and was traveling with his family he perceived from the direction of the mount a fire he said to his family stay here indeed i have perceived a fire perhaps i will bring you from there some information or burning wood from the fire that you may warm yourselves

ayahs.t: But when he came to it, he was called from the right side of the valley in a blessed spot - from the tree, "O Moses, indeed I am Allah, Lord of the worlds."
ayahs.n: but when he came to it he was called from the right side of the valley in a blessed spot from the tree o moses indeed i am allah lord of the worlds

ayahs.t: And [he was told], "Throw down your staff." But when he saw it writhing as if it was a snake, he turned in flight and did not return. [Allah said], "O Moses, approach and fear not. Indeed, you are of the secure.
ayahs.n: and he was told throw down your staff but when he saw it writhing as if it was a snake he turned in flight and did not return allah said o moses approach and fear not indeed you are of the secure

ayahs.t: Insert your hand into the opening of your garment; it will come out white, without disease. And draw in your arm close to you [as prevention] from fear, for those are two proofs from your Lord to Pharaoh and his establishment. Indeed, they have been a people defiantly disobedient."
ayahs.n: insert your hand into the opening of your garment it will come out white without disease and draw in your arm close to you as prevention from fear for those are two proofs from your lord to pharaoh and his establishment indeed they have been a people defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: He said, "My Lord, indeed, I killed from among them someone, and I fear they will kill me.
ayahs.n: he said my lord indeed i killed from among them someone and i fear they will kill me

ayahs.t: And my brother Aaron is more fluent than me in tongue, so send him with me as support, verifying me. Indeed, I fear that they will deny me."
ayahs.n: and my brother aaron is more fluent than me in tongue so send him with me as support verifying me indeed i fear that they will deny me

ayahs.t: [Allah] said, "We will strengthen your arm through your brother and grant you both supremacy so they will not reach you. [It will be] through Our signs; you and those who follow you will be the predominant."
ayahs.n: allah said we will strengthen your arm through your brother and grant you both supremacy so they will not reach you it will be through our signs you and those who follow you will be the predominant

ayahs.t: But when Moses came to them with Our signs as clear evidences, they said, "This is not except invented magic, and we have not heard of this [religion] among our forefathers."
ayahs.n: but when moses came to them with our signs as clear evidences they said this is not except invented magic and we have not heard of this religion among our forefathers

ayahs.t: And Moses said, "My Lord is more knowing [than we or you] of who has come with guidance from Him and to whom will be succession in the home. Indeed, wrongdoers do not succeed."
ayahs.n: and moses said my lord is more knowing than we or you of who has come with guidance from him and to whom will be succession in the home indeed wrongdoers do not succeed

ayahs.t: And Pharaoh said, "O eminent ones, I have not known you to have a god other than me. Then ignite for me, O Haman, [a fire] upon the clay and make for me a tower that I may look at the God of Moses. And indeed, I do think he is among the liars."
ayahs.n: and pharaoh said o eminent ones i have not known you to have a god other than me then ignite for me o haman a fire upon the clay and make for me a tower that i may look at the god of moses and indeed i do think he is among the liars

ayahs.t: And he was arrogant, he and his soldiers, in the land, without right, and they thought that they would not be returned to Us.
ayahs.n: and he was arrogant he and his soldiers in the land without right and they thought that they would not be returned to us

ayahs.t: So We took him and his soldiers and threw them into the sea. So see how was the end of the wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: so we took him and his soldiers and threw them into the sea so see how was the end of the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: And We made them leaders inviting to the Fire, and on the Day of Resurrection they will not be helped.
ayahs.n: and we made them leaders inviting to the fire and on the day of resurrection they will not be helped

ayahs.t: And We caused to overtake them in this world a curse, and on the Day of Resurrection they will be of the despised.
ayahs.n: and we caused to overtake them in this world a curse and on the day of resurrection they will be of the despised

ayahs.t: And We gave Moses the Scripture, after We had destroyed the former generations, as enlightenment for the people and guidance and mercy that they might be reminded.
ayahs.n: and we gave moses the scripture after we had destroyed the former generations as enlightenment for the people and guidance and mercy that they might be reminded

ayahs.t: And you, [O Muhammad], were not on the western side [of the mount] when We revealed to Moses the command, and you were not among the witnesses [to that].
ayahs.n: and you o muhammad were not on the western side of the mount when we revealed to moses the command and you were not among the witnesses to that

ayahs.t: But We produced [many] generations [after Moses], and prolonged was their duration. And you were not a resident among the people of Madyan, reciting to them Our verses, but We were senders [of this message].
ayahs.n: but we produced many generations after moses and prolonged was their duration and you were not a resident among the people of madyan reciting to them our verses but we were senders of this message

ayahs.t: And you were not at the side of the mount when We called [Moses] but [were sent] as a mercy from your Lord to warn a people to whom no warner had come before you that they might be reminded.
ayahs.n: and you were not at the side of the mount when we called moses but were sent as a mercy from your lord to warn a people to whom no warner had come before you that they might be reminded

ayahs.t: And if not that a disaster should strike them for what their hands put forth [of sins] and they would say, "Our Lord, why did You not send us a messenger so we could have followed Your verses and been among the believers?"...
ayahs.n: and if not that a disaster should strike them for what their hands put forth of sins and they would say our lord why did you not send us a messenger so we could have followed your verses and been among the believers

ayahs.t: But when the truth came to them from Us, they said, "Why was he not given like that which was given to Moses?" Did they not disbelieve in that which was given to Moses before? They said, "[They are but] two works of magic supporting each other, and indeed we are, in both, disbelievers."
ayahs.n: but when the truth came to them from us they said why was he not given like that which was given to moses did they not disbelieve in that which was given to moses before they said they are but two works of magic supporting each other and indeed we are in both disbelievers

ayahs.t: Say, "Then bring a scripture from Allah which is more guiding than either of them that I may follow it, if you should be truthful."
ayahs.n: say then bring a scripture from allah which is more guiding than either of them that i may follow it if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: But if they do not respond to you - then know that they only follow their [own] desires. And who is more astray than one who follows his desire without guidance from Allah? Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
ayahs.n: but if they do not respond to you then know that they only follow their own desires and who is more astray than one who follows his desire without guidance from allah indeed allah does not guide the wrongdoing people

ayahs.t: And We have [repeatedly] conveyed to them the Qur'an that they might be reminded.
ayahs.n: and we have repeatedly conveyed to them the quran that they might be reminded

ayahs.t: Those to whom We gave the Scripture before it - they are believers in it.
ayahs.n: those to whom we gave the scripture before it they are believers in it

ayahs.t: And when it is recited to them, they say, "We have believed in it; indeed, it is the truth from our Lord. Indeed we were, [even] before it, Muslims [submitting to Allah]."
ayahs.n: and when it is recited to them they say we have believed in it indeed it is the truth from our lord indeed we were even before it muslims submitting to allah

ayahs.t: Those will be given their reward twice for what they patiently endured and [because] they avert evil through good, and from what We have provided them they spend.
ayahs.n: those will be given their reward twice for what they patiently endured and because they avert evil through good and from what we have provided them they spend

ayahs.t: And when they hear ill speech, they turn away from it and say, "For us are our deeds, and for you are your deeds. Peace will be upon you; we seek not the ignorant."
ayahs.n: and when they hear ill speech they turn away from it and say for us are our deeds and for you are your deeds peace will be upon you we seek not the ignorant

ayahs.t: Indeed, [O Muhammad], you do not guide whom you like, but Allah guides whom He wills. And He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.
ayahs.n: indeed o muhammad you do not guide whom you like but allah guides whom he wills and he is most knowing of the rightly guided

ayahs.t: And they say, "If we were to follow the guidance with you, we would be swept from our land." Have we not established for them a safe sanctuary to which are brought the fruits of all things as provision from Us? But most of them do not know.
ayahs.n: and they say if we were to follow the guidance with you we would be swept from our land have we not established for them a safe sanctuary to which are brought the fruits of all things as provision from us but most of them do not know

ayahs.t: And how many a city have We destroyed that was insolent in its [way of] living, and those are their dwellings which have not been inhabited after them except briefly. And it is We who were the inheritors.
ayahs.n: and how many a city have we destroyed that was insolent in its way of living and those are their dwellings which have not been inhabited after them except briefly and it is we who were the inheritors

ayahs.t: And never would your Lord have destroyed the cities until He had sent to their mother a messenger reciting to them Our verses. And We would not destroy the cities except while their people were wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: and never would your lord have destroyed the cities until he had sent to their mother a messenger reciting to them our verses and we would not destroy the cities except while their people were wrongdoers

ayahs.t: And whatever thing you [people] have been given - it is [only for] the enjoyment of worldly life and its adornment. And what is with Allah is better and more lasting; so will you not use reason?
ayahs.n: and whatever thing you people have been given it is only for the enjoyment of worldly life and its adornment and what is with allah is better and more lasting so will you not use reason

ayahs.t: Then is he whom We have promised a good promise which he will obtain like he for whom We provided enjoyment of worldly life [but] then he is, on the Day of Resurrection, among those presented [for punishment in Hell]?
ayahs.n: then is he whom we have promised a good promise which he will obtain like he for whom we provided enjoyment of worldly life but then he is on the day of resurrection among those presented for punishment in hell

ayahs.t: And [warn of] the Day He will call them and say, "Where are My 'partners' which you used to claim?"
ayahs.n: and warn of the day he will call them and say where are my partners which you used to claim

ayahs.t: Those upon whom the word will have come into effect will say, "Our Lord, these are the ones we led to error. We led them to error just as we were in error. We declare our disassociation [from them] to You. They did not used to worship us."
