ayahs.t: And when Our verses are recited to them as clear evidences, those who disbelieve say of the truth when it has come to them, "This is obvious magic."
ayahs.n: and when our verses are recited to them as clear evidences those who disbelieve say of the truth when it has come to them this is obvious magic

ayahs.t: Or do they say, "He has invented it?" Say, "If I have invented it, you will not possess for me [the power of protection] from Allah at all. He is most knowing of that in which you are involved. Sufficient is He as Witness between me and you, and He is the Forgiving the Merciful."
ayahs.n: or do they say he has invented it say if i have invented it you will not possess for me the power of protection from allah at all he is most knowing of that in which you are involved sufficient is he as witness between me and you and he is the forgiving the merciful

ayahs.t: Say, "I am not something original among the messengers, nor do I know what will be done with me or with you. I only follow that which is revealed to me, and I am not but a clear warner."
ayahs.n: say i am not something original among the messengers nor do i know what will be done with me or with you i only follow that which is revealed to me and i am not but a clear warner

ayahs.t: Say, "Have you considered: if the Qur'an was from Allah, and you disbelieved in it while a witness from the Children of Israel has testified to something similar and believed while you were arrogant...?" Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
ayahs.n: say have you considered if the quran was from allah and you disbelieved in it while a witness from the children of israel has testified to something similar and believed while you were arrogant indeed allah does not guide the wrongdoing people

ayahs.t: And those who disbelieve say of those who believe, "If it had [truly] been good, they would not have preceded us to it." And when they are not guided by it, they will say, "This is an ancient falsehood."
ayahs.n: and those who disbelieve say of those who believe if it had truly been good they would not have preceded us to it and when they are not guided by it they will say this is an ancient falsehood

ayahs.t: And before it was the scripture of Moses to lead and as a mercy. And this is a confirming Book in an Arabic tongue to warn those who have wronged and as good tidings to the doers of good.
ayahs.n: and before it was the scripture of moses to lead and as a mercy and this is a confirming book in an arabic tongue to warn those who have wronged and as good tidings to the doers of good

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who have said, "Our Lord is Allah," and then remained on a right course - there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve.
ayahs.n: indeed those who have said our lord is allah and then remained on a right course there will be no fear concerning them nor will they grieve

ayahs.t: Those are the companions of Paradise, abiding eternally therein as reward for what they used to do.
ayahs.n: those are the companions of paradise abiding eternally therein as reward for what they used to do

ayahs.t: And We have enjoined upon man, to his parents, good treatment. His mother carried him with hardship and gave birth to him with hardship, and his gestation and weaning [period] is thirty months. [He grows] until, when he reaches maturity and reaches [the age of] forty years, he says, "My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to work righteousness of which You will approve and make righteous for me my offspring. Indeed, I have repented to You, and indeed, I am of the Muslims."
ayahs.n: and we have enjoined upon man to his parents good treatment his mother carried him with hardship and gave birth to him with hardship and his gestation and weaning period is thirty months he grows until when he reaches maturity and reaches the age of forty years he says my lord enable me to be grateful for your favor which you have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to work righteousness of which you will approve and make righteous for me my offspring indeed i have repented to you and indeed i am of the muslims

ayahs.t: Those are the ones from whom We will accept the best of what they did and overlook their misdeeds, [their being] among the companions of Paradise. [That is] the promise of truth which they had been promised.
ayahs.n: those are the ones from whom we will accept the best of what they did and overlook their misdeeds their being among the companions of paradise that is the promise of truth which they had been promised

ayahs.t: But one who says to his parents, "Uff to you; do you promise me that I will be brought forth [from the earth] when generations before me have already passed on [into oblivion]?" while they call to Allah for help [and to their son], "Woe to you! Believe! Indeed, the promise of Allah is truth." But he says, "This is not but legends of the former people" -
ayahs.n: but one who says to his parents uff to you do you promise me that i will be brought forth from the earth when generations before me have already passed on into oblivion while they call to allah for help and to their son woe to you believe indeed the promise of allah is truth but he says this is not but legends of the former people

ayahs.t: Those are the ones upon whom the word has come into effect, [who will be] among nations which had passed on before them of jinn and men. Indeed, they [all] were losers.
ayahs.n: those are the ones upon whom the word has come into effect who will be among nations which had passed on before them of jinn and men indeed they all were losers

ayahs.t: And for all there are degrees [of reward and punishment] for what they have done, and [it is] so that He may fully compensate them for their deeds, and they will not be wronged.
ayahs.n: and for all there are degrees of reward and punishment for what they have done and it is so that he may fully compensate them for their deeds and they will not be wronged

ayahs.t: And the Day those who disbelieved are exposed to the Fire [it will be said], "You exhausted your pleasures during your worldly life and enjoyed them, so this Day you will be awarded the punishment of [extreme] humiliation because you were arrogant upon the earth without right and because you were defiantly disobedient."
ayahs.n: and the day those who disbelieved are exposed to the fire it will be said you exhausted your pleasures during your worldly life and enjoyed them so this day you will be awarded the punishment of extreme humiliation because you were arrogant upon the earth without right and because you were defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: And mention, [O Muhammad], the brother of 'Aad, when he warned his people in the [region of] al-Ahqaf - and warners had already passed on before him and after him - [saying], "Do not worship except Allah. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a terrible day."
ayahs.n: and mention o muhammad the brother of aad when he warned his people in the region of al ahqaf and warners had already passed on before him and after him saying do not worship except allah indeed i fear for you the punishment of a terrible day

ayahs.t: They said, "Have you come to delude us away from our gods? Then bring us what you promise us, if you should be of the truthful."
ayahs.n: they said have you come to delude us away from our gods then bring us what you promise us if you should be of the truthful

ayahs.t: He said, "Knowledge [of its time] is only with Allah, and I convey to you that with which I was sent; but I see you [to be] a people behaving ignorantly."
ayahs.n: he said knowledge of its time is only with allah and i convey to you that with which i was sent but i see you to be a people behaving ignorantly

ayahs.t: And when they saw it as a cloud approaching their valleys, they said, "This is a cloud bringing us rain!" Rather, it is that for which you were impatient: a wind, within it a painful punishment,
ayahs.n: and when they saw it as a cloud approaching their valleys they said this is a cloud bringing us rain rather it is that for which you were impatient a wind within it a painful punishment

ayahs.t: Destroying everything by command of its Lord. And they became so that nothing was seen [of them] except their dwellings. Thus do We recompense the criminal people.
ayahs.n: destroying everything by command of its lord and they became so that nothing was seen of them except their dwellings thus do we recompense the criminal people

ayahs.t: And We had certainly established them in such as We have not established you, and We made for them hearing and vision and hearts. But their hearing and vision and hearts availed them not from anything [of the punishment] when they were [continually] rejecting the signs of Allah; and they were enveloped by what they used to ridicule.
ayahs.n: and we had certainly established them in such as we have not established you and we made for them hearing and vision and hearts but their hearing and vision and hearts availed them not from anything of the punishment when they were continually rejecting the signs of allah and they were enveloped by what they used to ridicule

ayahs.t: And We have already destroyed what surrounds you of [those] cities, and We have diversified the signs [or verses] that perhaps they might return [from disbelief].
ayahs.n: and we have already destroyed what surrounds you of those cities and we have diversified the signs or verses that perhaps they might return from disbelief

ayahs.t: Then why did those they took besides Allah as deities by which to approach [Him] not aid them? But they had strayed from them. And that was their falsehood and what they were inventing.
ayahs.n: then why did those they took besides allah as deities by which to approach him not aid them but they had strayed from them and that was their falsehood and what they were inventing

ayahs.t: And [mention, O Muhammad], when We directed to you a few of the jinn, listening to the Qur'an. And when they attended it, they said, "Listen quietly." And when it was concluded, they went back to their people as warners.
ayahs.n: and mention o muhammad when we directed to you a few of the jinn listening to the quran and when they attended it they said listen quietly and when it was concluded they went back to their people as warners

ayahs.t: They said, "O our people, indeed we have heard a [recited] Book revealed after Moses confirming what was before it which guides to the truth and to a straight path.
ayahs.n: they said o our people indeed we have heard a recited book revealed after moses confirming what was before it which guides to the truth and to a straight path

ayahs.t: O our people, respond to the Messenger of Allah and believe in him; Allah will forgive for you your sins and protect you from a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: o our people respond to the messenger of allah and believe in him allah will forgive for you your sins and protect you from a painful punishment

ayahs.t: But he who does not respond to the Caller of Allah will not cause failure [to Him] upon earth, and he will not have besides Him any protectors. Those are in manifest error."
ayahs.n: but he who does not respond to the caller of allah will not cause failure to him upon earth and he will not have besides him any protectors those are in manifest error

ayahs.t: Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and earth and did not fail in their creation, is able to give life to the dead? Yes. Indeed, He is over all things competent.
ayahs.n: do they not see that allah who created the heavens and earth and did not fail in their creation is able to give life to the dead yes indeed he is over all things competent

ayahs.t: And the Day those who disbelieved are exposed to the Fire [it will be said], "Is this not the truth?" They will say, "Yes, by our Lord." He will say, "Then taste the punishment because you used to disbelieve."
ayahs.n: and the day those who disbelieved are exposed to the fire it will be said is this not the truth they will say yes by our lord he will say then taste the punishment because you used to disbelieve

ayahs.t: So be patient, [O Muhammad], as were those of determination among the messengers and do not be impatient for them. It will be - on the Day they see that which they are promised - as though they had not remained [in the world] except an hour of a day. [This is] notification. And will [any] be destroyed except the defiantly disobedient people?
ayahs.n: so be patient o muhammad as were those of determination among the messengers and do not be impatient for them it will be on the day they see that which they are promised as though they had not remained in the world except an hour of a day this is notification and will any be destroyed except the defiantly disobedient people

ayahs.t: Those who disbelieve and avert [people] from the way of Allah - He will waste their deeds.
ayahs.n: those who disbelieve and avert people from the way of allah he will waste their deeds

ayahs.t: And those who believe and do righteous deeds and believe in what has been sent down upon Muhammad - and it is the truth from their Lord - He will remove from them their misdeeds and amend their condition.
ayahs.n: and those who believe and do righteous deeds and believe in what has been sent down upon muhammad and it is the truth from their lord he will remove from them their misdeeds and amend their condition

ayahs.t: That is because those who disbelieve follow falsehood, and those who believe follow the truth from their Lord. Thus does Allah present to the people their comparisons.
ayahs.n: that is because those who disbelieve follow falsehood and those who believe follow the truth from their lord thus does allah present to the people their comparisons

ayahs.t: So when you meet those who disbelieve [in battle], strike [their] necks until, when you have inflicted slaughter upon them, then secure their bonds, and either [confer] favor afterwards or ransom [them] until the war lays down its burdens. That [is the command]. And if Allah had willed, He could have taken vengeance upon them [Himself], but [He ordered armed struggle] to test some of you by means of others. And those who are killed in the cause of Allah - never will He waste their deeds.
ayahs.n: so when you meet those who disbelieve in battle strike their necks until when you have inflicted slaughter upon them then secure their bonds and either confer favor afterwards or ransom them until the war lays down its burdens that is the command and if allah had willed he could have taken vengeance upon them himself but he ordered armed struggle to test some of you by means of others and those who are killed in the cause of allah never will he waste their deeds

ayahs.t: He will guide them and amend their condition
ayahs.n: he will guide them and amend their condition

ayahs.t: And admit them to Paradise, which He has made known to them.
ayahs.n: and admit them to paradise which he has made known to them

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, if you support Allah, He will support you and plant firmly your feet.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed if you support allah he will support you and plant firmly your feet

ayahs.t: But those who disbelieve - for them is misery, and He will waste their deeds.
ayahs.n: but those who disbelieve for them is misery and he will waste their deeds

ayahs.t: That is because they disliked what Allah revealed, so He rendered worthless their deeds.
ayahs.n: that is because they disliked what allah revealed so he rendered worthless their deeds

ayahs.t: Have they not traveled through the land and seen how was the end of those before them? Allah destroyed [everything] over them, and for the disbelievers is something comparable.
ayahs.n: have they not traveled through the land and seen how was the end of those before them allah destroyed everything over them and for the disbelievers is something comparable

ayahs.t: That is because Allah is the protector of those who have believed and because the disbelievers have no protector.
ayahs.n: that is because allah is the protector of those who have believed and because the disbelievers have no protector

ayahs.t: Indeed, Allah will admit those who have believed and done righteous deeds to gardens beneath which rivers flow, but those who disbelieve enjoy themselves and eat as grazing livestock eat, and the Fire will be a residence for them.
ayahs.n: indeed allah will admit those who have believed and done righteous deeds to gardens beneath which rivers flow but those who disbelieve enjoy themselves and eat as grazing livestock eat and the fire will be a residence for them

ayahs.t: And how many a city was stronger than your city [Makkah] which drove you out? We destroyed them; and there was no helper for them.
ayahs.n: and how many a city was stronger than your city makkah which drove you out we destroyed them and there was no helper for them

ayahs.t: So is he who is on clear evidence from his Lord like him to whom the evil of his work has been made attractive and they follow their [own] desires?
ayahs.n: so is he who is on clear evidence from his lord like him to whom the evil of his work has been made attractive and they follow their own desires

ayahs.t: Is the description of Paradise, which the righteous are promised, wherein are rivers of water unaltered, rivers of milk the taste of which never changes, rivers of wine delicious to those who drink, and rivers of purified honey, in which they will have from all [kinds of] fruits and forgiveness from their Lord, like [that of] those who abide eternally in the Fire and are given to drink scalding water that will sever their intestines?
ayahs.n: is the description of paradise which the righteous are promised wherein are rivers of water unaltered rivers of milk the taste of which never changes rivers of wine delicious to those who drink and rivers of purified honey in which they will have from all kinds of fruits and forgiveness from their lord like that of those who abide eternally in the fire and are given to drink scalding water that will sever their intestines

ayahs.t: And among them, [O Muhammad], are those who listen to you, until when they depart from you, they say to those who were given knowledge, "What has he said just now?" Those are the ones of whom Allah has sealed over their hearts and who have followed their [own] desires.
ayahs.n: and among them o muhammad are those who listen to you until when they depart from you they say to those who were given knowledge what has he said just now those are the ones of whom allah has sealed over their hearts and who have followed their own desires

ayahs.t: And those who are guided - He increases them in guidance and gives them their righteousness.
ayahs.n: and those who are guided he increases them in guidance and gives them their righteousness

ayahs.t: Then do they await except that the Hour should come upon them unexpectedly? But already there have come [some of] its indications. Then what good to them, when it has come, will be their remembrance?
ayahs.n: then do they await except that the hour should come upon them unexpectedly but already there have come some of its indications then what good to them when it has come will be their remembrance

ayahs.t: So know, [O Muhammad], that there is no deity except Allah and ask forgiveness for your sin and for the believing men and believing women. And Allah knows of your movement and your resting place.
ayahs.n: so know o muhammad that there is no deity except allah and ask forgiveness for your sin and for the believing men and believing women and allah knows of your movement and your resting place

ayahs.t: Those who believe say, "Why has a surah not been sent down? But when a precise surah is revealed and fighting is mentioned therein, you see those in whose hearts is hypocrisy looking at you with a look of one overcome by death. And more appropriate for them [would have been]
ayahs.n: those who believe say why has a surah not been sent down but when a precise surah is revealed and fighting is mentioned therein you see those in whose hearts is hypocrisy looking at you with a look of one overcome by death and more appropriate for them would have been

ayahs.t: Obedience and good words. And when the matter [of fighting] was determined, if they had been true to Allah, it would have been better for them.
ayahs.n: obedience and good words and when the matter of fighting was determined if they had been true to allah it would have been better for them

ayahs.t: So would you perhaps, if you turned away, cause corruption on earth and sever your [ties of] relationship?
ayahs.n: so would you perhaps if you turned away cause corruption on earth and sever your ties of relationship

ayahs.t: Those [who do so] are the ones that Allah has cursed, so He deafened them and blinded their vision.
ayahs.n: those who do so are the ones that allah has cursed so he deafened them and blinded their vision

ayahs.t: Then do they not reflect upon the Qur'an, or are there locks upon [their] hearts?
ayahs.n: then do they not reflect upon the quran or are there locks upon their hearts

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who reverted back [to disbelief] after guidance had become clear to them - Satan enticed them and prolonged hope for them.
ayahs.n: indeed those who reverted back to disbelief after guidance had become clear to them satan enticed them and prolonged hope for them

ayahs.t: That is because they said to those who disliked what Allah sent down, "We will obey you in part of the matter." And Allah knows what they conceal.
ayahs.n: that is because they said to those who disliked what allah sent down we will obey you in part of the matter and allah knows what they conceal

ayahs.t: Then how [will it be] when the angels take them in death, striking their faces and their backs?
ayahs.n: then how will it be when the angels take them in death striking their faces and their backs

ayahs.t: That is because they followed what angered Allah and disliked [what earns] His pleasure, so He rendered worthless their deeds.
ayahs.n: that is because they followed what angered allah and disliked what earns his pleasure so he rendered worthless their deeds

ayahs.t: Or do those in whose hearts is disease think that Allah would never expose their [feelings of] hatred?
ayahs.n: or do those in whose hearts is disease think that allah would never expose their feelings of hatred

ayahs.t: And if We willed, We could show them to you, and you would know them by their mark; but you will surely know them by the tone of [their] speech. And Allah knows your deeds.
ayahs.n: and if we willed we could show them to you and you would know them by their mark but you will surely know them by the tone of their speech and allah knows your deeds

ayahs.t: And We will surely test you until We make evident those who strive among you [for the cause of Allah] and the patient, and We will test your affairs.
ayahs.n: and we will surely test you until we make evident those who strive among you for the cause of allah and the patient and we will test your affairs

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who disbelieved and averted [people] from the path of Allah and opposed the Messenger after guidance had become clear to them - never will they harm Allah at all, and He will render worthless their deeds.
ayahs.n: indeed those who disbelieved and averted people from the path of allah and opposed the messenger after guidance had become clear to them never will they harm allah at all and he will render worthless their deeds

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, obey Allah and obey the Messenger and do not invalidate your deeds.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed obey allah and obey the messenger and do not invalidate your deeds

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who disbelieved and averted [people] from the path of Allah and then died while they were disbelievers - never will Allah forgive them.
ayahs.n: indeed those who disbelieved and averted people from the path of allah and then died while they were disbelievers never will allah forgive them

ayahs.t: So do not weaken and call for peace while you are superior; and Allah is with you and will never deprive you of [the reward of] your deeds.
ayahs.n: so do not weaken and call for peace while you are superior and allah is with you and will never deprive you of the reward of your deeds

ayahs.t: [This] worldly life is only amusement and diversion. And if you believe and fear Allah, He will give you your rewards and not ask you for your properties.
ayahs.n: this worldly life is only amusement and diversion and if you believe and fear allah he will give you your rewards and not ask you for your properties

ayahs.t: If He should ask you for them and press you, you would withhold, and He would expose your unwillingness.
ayahs.n: if he should ask you for them and press you you would withhold and he would expose your unwillingness

ayahs.t: Here you are - those invited to spend in the cause of Allah - but among you are those who withhold [out of greed]. And whoever withholds only withholds [benefit] from himself; and Allah is the Free of need, while you are the needy. And if you turn away, He will replace you with another people; then they will not be the likes of you.
ayahs.n: here you are those invited to spend in the cause of allah but among you are those who withhold out of greed and whoever withholds only withholds benefit from himself and allah is the free of need while you are the needy and if you turn away he will replace you with another people then they will not be the likes of you

ayahs.t: Indeed, We have given you, [O Muhammad], a clear conquest
ayahs.n: indeed we have given you o muhammad a clear conquest

ayahs.t: That Allah may forgive for you what preceded of your sin and what will follow and complete His favor upon you and guide you to a straight path
ayahs.n: that allah may forgive for you what preceded of your sin and what will follow and complete his favor upon you and guide you to a straight path

ayahs.t: And [that] Allah may aid you with a mighty victory.
ayahs.n: and that allah may aid you with a mighty victory

ayahs.t: It is He who sent down tranquillity into the hearts of the believers that they would increase in faith along with their [present] faith. And to Allah belong the soldiers of the heavens and the earth, and ever is Allah Knowing and Wise.
ayahs.n: it is he who sent down tranquillity into the hearts of the believers that they would increase in faith along with their present faith and to allah belong the soldiers of the heavens and the earth and ever is allah knowing and wise

ayahs.t: [And] that He may admit the believing men and the believing women to gardens beneath which rivers flow to abide therein eternally and remove from them their misdeeds - and ever is that, in the sight of Allah, a great attainment -
ayahs.n: and that he may admit the believing men and the believing women to gardens beneath which rivers flow to abide therein eternally and remove from them their misdeeds and ever is that in the sight of allah a great attainment

ayahs.t: And [that] He may punish the hypocrite men and hypocrite women, and the polytheist men and polytheist women - those who assume about Allah an assumption of evil nature. Upon them is a misfortune of evil nature; and Allah has become angry with them and has cursed them and prepared for them Hell, and evil it is as a destination.
ayahs.n: and that he may punish the hypocrite men and hypocrite women and the polytheist men and polytheist women those who assume about allah an assumption of evil nature upon them is a misfortune of evil nature and allah has become angry with them and has cursed them and prepared for them hell and evil it is as a destination

ayahs.t: And to Allah belong the soldiers of the heavens and the earth. And ever is Allah Exalted in Might and Wise.
ayahs.n: and to allah belong the soldiers of the heavens and the earth and ever is allah exalted in might and wise

ayahs.t: Indeed, We have sent you as a witness and a bringer of good tidings and a warner
ayahs.n: indeed we have sent you as a witness and a bringer of good tidings and a warner

ayahs.t: That you [people] may believe in Allah and His Messenger and honor him and respect the Prophet and exalt Allah morning and afternoon.
ayahs.n: that you people may believe in allah and his messenger and honor him and respect the prophet and exalt allah morning and afternoon

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who pledge allegiance to you, [O Muhammad] - they are actually pledging allegiance to Allah. The hand of Allah is over their hands. So he who breaks his word only breaks it to the detriment of himself. And he who fulfills that which he has promised Allah - He will give him a great reward.
ayahs.n: indeed those who pledge allegiance to you o muhammad they are actually pledging allegiance to allah the hand of allah is over their hands so he who breaks his word only breaks it to the detriment of himself and he who fulfills that which he has promised allah he will give him a great reward

ayahs.t: Those who remained behind of the bedouins will say to you, "Our properties and our families occupied us, so ask forgiveness for us." They say with their tongues what is not within their hearts. Say, "Then who could prevent Allah at all if He intended for you harm or intended for you benefit? Rather, ever is Allah, with what you do, Acquainted.
ayahs.n: those who remained behind of the bedouins will say to you our properties and our families occupied us so ask forgiveness for us they say with their tongues what is not within their hearts say then who could prevent allah at all if he intended for you harm or intended for you benefit rather ever is allah with what you do acquainted

ayahs.t: But you thought that the Messenger and the believers would never return to their families, ever, and that was made pleasing in your hearts. And you assumed an assumption of evil and became a people ruined."
ayahs.n: but you thought that the messenger and the believers would never return to their families ever and that was made pleasing in your hearts and you assumed an assumption of evil and became a people ruined

ayahs.t: And whoever has not believed in Allah and His Messenger - then indeed, We have prepared for the disbelievers a Blaze.
ayahs.n: and whoever has not believed in allah and his messenger then indeed we have prepared for the disbelievers a blaze

ayahs.t: And to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. He forgives whom He wills and punishes whom He wills. And ever is Allah Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: and to allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth he forgives whom he wills and punishes whom he wills and ever is allah forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: Those who remained behind will say when you set out toward the war booty to take it, "Let us follow you." They wish to change the words of Allah. Say, "Never will you follow us. Thus did Allah say before." So they will say, "Rather, you envy us." But [in fact] they were not understanding except a little.
ayahs.n: those who remained behind will say when you set out toward the war booty to take it let us follow you they wish to change the words of allah say never will you follow us thus did allah say before so they will say rather you envy us but in fact they were not understanding except a little

ayahs.t: Say to those who remained behind of the bedouins, "You will be called to [face] a people of great military might; you may fight them, or they will submit. So if you obey, Allah will give you a good reward; but if you turn away as you turned away before, He will punish you with a painful punishment."
ayahs.n: say to those who remained behind of the bedouins you will be called to face a people of great military might you may fight them or they will submit so if you obey allah will give you a good reward but if you turn away as you turned away before he will punish you with a painful punishment

ayahs.t: There is not upon the blind any guilt or upon the lame any guilt or upon the ill any guilt [for remaining behind]. And whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger - He will admit him to gardens beneath which rivers flow; but whoever turns away - He will punish him with a painful punishment.
ayahs.n: there is not upon the blind any guilt or upon the lame any guilt or upon the ill any guilt for remaining behind and whoever obeys allah and his messenger he will admit him to gardens beneath which rivers flow but whoever turns away he will punish him with a painful punishment

ayahs.t: Certainly was Allah pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you, [O Muhammad], under the tree, and He knew what was in their hearts, so He sent down tranquillity upon them and rewarded them with an imminent conquest
ayahs.n: certainly was allah pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you o muhammad under the tree and he knew what was in their hearts so he sent down tranquillity upon them and rewarded them with an imminent conquest

ayahs.t: And much war booty which they will take. And ever is Allah Exalted in Might and Wise.
ayahs.n: and much war booty which they will take and ever is allah exalted in might and wise

ayahs.t: Allah has promised you much booty that you will take [in the future] and has hastened for you this [victory] and withheld the hands of people from you - that it may be a sign for the believers and [that] He may guide you to a straight path.
ayahs.n: allah has promised you much booty that you will take in the future and has hastened for you this victory and withheld the hands of people from you that it may be a sign for the believers and that he may guide you to a straight path

ayahs.t: And [He promises] other [victories] that you were [so far] unable to [realize] which Allah has already encompassed. And ever is Allah, over all things, competent.
ayahs.n: and he promises other victories that you were so far unable to realize which allah has already encompassed and ever is allah over all things competent

ayahs.t: And if those [Makkans] who disbelieve had fought you, they would have turned their backs [in flight]. Then they would not find a protector or a helper.
ayahs.n: and if those makkans who disbelieve had fought you they would have turned their backs in flight then they would not find a protector or a helper

ayahs.t: [This is] the established way of Allah which has occurred before. And never will you find in the way of Allah any change.
ayahs.n: this is the established way of allah which has occurred before and never will you find in the way of allah any change

ayahs.t: And it is He who withheld their hands from you and your hands from them within [the area of] Makkah after He caused you to overcome them. And ever is Allah of what you do, Seeing.
ayahs.n: and it is he who withheld their hands from you and your hands from them within the area of makkah after he caused you to overcome them and ever is allah of what you do seeing

ayahs.t: They are the ones who disbelieved and obstructed you from al-Masjid al-Haram while the offering was prevented from reaching its place of sacrifice. And if not for believing men and believing women whom you did not know - that you might trample them and there would befall you because of them dishonor without [your] knowledge - [you would have been permitted to enter Makkah]. [This was so] that Allah might admit to His mercy whom He willed. If they had been apart [from them], We would have punished those who disbelieved among them with painful punishment
ayahs.n: they are the ones who disbelieved and obstructed you from al masjid al haram while the offering was prevented from reaching its place of sacrifice and if not for believing men and believing women whom you did not know that you might trample them and there would befall you because of them dishonor without your knowledge you would have been permitted to enter makkah this was so that allah might admit to his mercy whom he willed if they had been apart from them we would have punished those who disbelieved among them with painful punishment

ayahs.t: When those who disbelieved had put into their hearts chauvinism - the chauvinism of the time of ignorance. But Allah sent down His tranquillity upon His Messenger and upon the believers and imposed upon them the word of righteousness, and they were more deserving of it and worthy of it. And ever is Allah, of all things, Knowing.
ayahs.n: when those who disbelieved had put into their hearts chauvinism the chauvinism of the time of ignorance but allah sent down his tranquillity upon his messenger and upon the believers and imposed upon them the word of righteousness and they were more deserving of it and worthy of it and ever is allah of all things knowing

ayahs.t: Certainly has Allah showed to His Messenger the vision in truth. You will surely enter al-Masjid al-Haram, if Allah wills, in safety, with your heads shaved and [hair] shortened, not fearing [anyone]. He knew what you did not know and has arranged before that a conquest near [at hand].
ayahs.n: certainly has allah showed to his messenger the vision in truth you will surely enter al masjid al haram if allah wills in safety with your heads shaved and hair shortened not fearing anyone he knew what you did not know and has arranged before that a conquest near at hand

ayahs.t: It is He who sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth to manifest it over all religion. And sufficient is Allah as Witness.
ayahs.n: it is he who sent his messenger with guidance and the religion of truth to manifest it over all religion and sufficient is allah as witness

ayahs.t: Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah; and those with him are forceful against the disbelievers, merciful among themselves. You see them bowing and prostrating [in prayer], seeking bounty from Allah and [His] pleasure. Their mark is on their faces from the trace of prostration. That is their description in the Torah. And their description in the Gospel is as a plant which produces its offshoots and strengthens them so they grow firm and stand upon their stalks, delighting the sowers - so that Allah may enrage by them the disbelievers. Allah has promised those who believe and do righteous deeds among them forgiveness and a great reward.
ayahs.n: muhammad is the messenger of allah and those with him are forceful against the disbelievers merciful among themselves you see them bowing and prostrating in prayer seeking bounty from allah and his pleasure their mark is on their faces from the trace of prostration that is their description in the torah and their description in the gospel is as a plant which produces its offshoots and strengthens them so they grow firm and stand upon their stalks delighting the sowers so that allah may enrage by them the disbelievers allah has promised those who believe and do righteous deeds among them forgiveness and a great reward

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, do not put [yourselves] before Allah and His Messenger but fear Allah. Indeed, Allah is Hearing and Knowing.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed do not put yourselves before allah and his messenger but fear allah indeed allah is hearing and knowing

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet or be loud to him in speech like the loudness of some of you to others, lest your deeds become worthless while you perceive not.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed do not raise your voices above the voice of the prophet or be loud to him in speech like the loudness of some of you to others lest your deeds become worthless while you perceive not

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who lower their voices before the Messenger of Allah - they are the ones whose hearts Allah has tested for righteousness. For them is forgiveness and great reward.
ayahs.n: indeed those who lower their voices before the messenger of allah they are the ones whose hearts allah has tested for righteousness for them is forgiveness and great reward

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who call you, [O Muhammad], from behind the chambers - most of them do not use reason.
ayahs.n: indeed those who call you o muhammad from behind the chambers most of them do not use reason

ayahs.t: And if they had been patient until you [could] come out to them, it would have been better for them. But Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.n: and if they had been patient until you could come out to them it would have been better for them but allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, if there comes to you a disobedient one with information, investigate, lest you harm a people out of ignorance and become, over what you have done, regretful.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed if there comes to you a disobedient one with information investigate lest you harm a people out of ignorance and become over what you have done regretful

ayahs.t: And know that among you is the Messenger of Allah. If he were to obey you in much of the matter, you would be in difficulty, but Allah has endeared to you the faith and has made it pleasing in your hearts and has made hateful to you disbelief, defiance and disobedience. Those are the [rightly] guided.
ayahs.n: and know that among you is the messenger of allah if he were to obey you in much of the matter you would be in difficulty but allah has endeared to you the faith and has made it pleasing in your hearts and has made hateful to you disbelief defiance and disobedience those are the rightly guided

ayahs.t: [It is] as bounty from Allah and favor. And Allah is Knowing and Wise.
ayahs.n: it is as bounty from allah and favor and allah is knowing and wise

ayahs.t: And if two factions among the believers should fight, then make settlement between the two. But if one of them oppresses the other, then fight against the one that oppresses until it returns to the ordinance of Allah. And if it returns, then make settlement between them in justice and act justly. Indeed, Allah loves those who act justly.
ayahs.n: and if two factions among the believers should fight then make settlement between the two but if one of them oppresses the other then fight against the one that oppresses until it returns to the ordinance of allah and if it returns then make settlement between them in justice and act justly indeed allah loves those who act justly

ayahs.t: The believers are but brothers, so make settlement between your brothers. And fear Allah that you may receive mercy.
ayahs.n: the believers are but brothers so make settlement between your brothers and fear allah that you may receive mercy

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, let not a people ridicule [another] people; perhaps they may be better than them; nor let women ridicule [other] women; perhaps they may be better than them. And do not insult one another and do not call each other by [offensive] nicknames. Wretched is the name of disobedience after [one's] faith. And whoever does not repent - then it is those who are the wrongdoers.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed let not a people ridicule another people perhaps they may be better than them nor let women ridicule other women perhaps they may be better than them and do not insult one another and do not call each other by offensive nicknames wretched is the name of disobedience after ones faith and whoever does not repent then it is those who are the wrongdoers

ayahs.t: O you who have believed, avoid much [negative] assumption. Indeed, some assumption is sin. And do not spy or backbite each other. Would one of you like to eat the flesh of his brother when dead? You would detest it. And fear Allah; indeed, Allah is Accepting of repentance and Merciful.
ayahs.n: o you who have believed avoid much negative assumption indeed some assumption is sin and do not spy or backbite each other would one of you like to eat the flesh of his brother when dead you would detest it and fear allah indeed allah is accepting of repentance and merciful

ayahs.t: O mankind, indeed We have created you from male and female and made you peoples and tribes that you may know one another. Indeed, the most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous of you. Indeed, Allah is Knowing and Acquainted.
ayahs.n: o mankind indeed we have created you from male and female and made you peoples and tribes that you may know one another indeed the most noble of you in the sight of allah is the most righteous of you indeed allah is knowing and acquainted

ayahs.t: The bedouins say, "We have believed." Say, "You have not [yet] believed; but say [instead], 'We have submitted,' for faith has not yet entered your hearts. And if you obey Allah and His Messenger, He will not deprive you from your deeds of anything. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful."
ayahs.n: the bedouins say we have believed say you have not yet believed but say instead we have submitted for faith has not yet entered your hearts and if you obey allah and his messenger he will not deprive you from your deeds of anything indeed allah is forgiving and merciful

ayahs.t: The believers are only the ones who have believed in Allah and His Messenger and then doubt not but strive with their properties and their lives in the cause of Allah. It is those who are the truthful.
ayahs.n: the believers are only the ones who have believed in allah and his messenger and then doubt not but strive with their properties and their lives in the cause of allah it is those who are the truthful

ayahs.t: Say, "Would you acquaint Allah with your religion while Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and Allah is Knowing of all things?"
ayahs.n: say would you acquaint allah with your religion while allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth and allah is knowing of all things

ayahs.t: They consider it a favor to you that they have accepted Islam. Say, "Do not consider your Islam a favor to me. Rather, Allah has conferred favor upon you that He has guided you to the faith, if you should be truthful."
ayahs.n: they consider it a favor to you that they have accepted islam say do not consider your islam a favor to me rather allah has conferred favor upon you that he has guided you to the faith if you should be truthful

ayahs.t: Indeed, Allah knows the unseen [aspects] of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is Seeing of what you do.
ayahs.n: indeed allah knows the unseen aspects of the heavens and the earth and allah is seeing of what you do

ayahs.t: Qaf. By the honored Qur'an...
ayahs.n: qaf by the honored quran

ayahs.t: But they wonder that there has come to them a warner from among themselves, and the disbelievers say, "This is an amazing thing.
ayahs.n: but they wonder that there has come to them a warner from among themselves and the disbelievers say this is an amazing thing

ayahs.t: When we have died and have become dust, [we will return to life]? That is a distant return."
ayahs.n: when we have died and have become dust we will return to life that is a distant return

ayahs.t: We know what the earth diminishes of them, and with Us is a retaining record.
ayahs.n: we know what the earth diminishes of them and with us is a retaining record

ayahs.t: But they denied the truth when it came to them, so they are in a confused condition.
ayahs.n: but they denied the truth when it came to them so they are in a confused condition

ayahs.t: Have they not looked at the heaven above them - how We structured it and adorned it and [how] it has no rifts?
ayahs.n: have they not looked at the heaven above them how we structured it and adorned it and how it has no rifts

ayahs.t: And the earth - We spread it out and cast therein firmly set mountains and made grow therein [something] of every beautiful kind,
ayahs.n: and the earth we spread it out and cast therein firmly set mountains and made grow therein something of every beautiful kind

ayahs.t: Giving insight and a reminder for every servant who turns [to Allah].
ayahs.n: giving insight and a reminder for every servant who turns to allah

ayahs.t: And We have sent down blessed rain from the sky and made grow thereby gardens and grain from the harvest
ayahs.n: and we have sent down blessed rain from the sky and made grow thereby gardens and grain from the harvest

ayahs.t: And lofty palm trees having fruit arranged in layers -
ayahs.n: and lofty palm trees having fruit arranged in layers

ayahs.t: As provision for the servants, and We have given life thereby to a dead land. Thus is the resurrection.
ayahs.n: as provision for the servants and we have given life thereby to a dead land thus is the resurrection

ayahs.t: The people of Noah denied before them, and the companions of the well and Thamud
ayahs.n: the people of noah denied before them and the companions of the well and thamud

ayahs.t: And 'Aad and Pharaoh and the brothers of Lot
ayahs.n: and aad and pharaoh and the brothers of lot

ayahs.t: And the companions of the thicket and the people of Tubba'. All denied the messengers, so My threat was justly fulfilled.
ayahs.n: and the companions of the thicket and the people of tubba all denied the messengers so my threat was justly fulfilled

ayahs.t: Did We fail in the first creation? But they are in confusion over a new creation.
ayahs.n: did we fail in the first creation but they are in confusion over a new creation

ayahs.t: And We have already created man and know what his soul whispers to him, and We are closer to him than [his] jugular vein
ayahs.n: and we have already created man and know what his soul whispers to him and we are closer to him than his jugular vein

ayahs.t: When the two receivers receive, seated on the right and on the left.
ayahs.n: when the two receivers receive seated on the right and on the left

ayahs.t: Man does not utter any word except that with him is an observer prepared [to record].
ayahs.n: man does not utter any word except that with him is an observer prepared to record

ayahs.t: And the intoxication of death will bring the truth; that is what you were trying to avoid.
ayahs.n: and the intoxication of death will bring the truth that is what you were trying to avoid

ayahs.t: And the Horn will be blown. That is the Day of [carrying out] the threat.
ayahs.n: and the horn will be blown that is the day of carrying out the threat

ayahs.t: And every soul will come, with it a driver and a witness.
ayahs.n: and every soul will come with it a driver and a witness

ayahs.t: [It will be said], "You were certainly in unmindfulness of this, and We have removed from you your cover, so your sight, this Day, is sharp."
ayahs.n: it will be said you were certainly in unmindfulness of this and we have removed from you your cover so your sight this day is sharp

ayahs.t: And his companion, [the angel], will say, "This [record] is what is with me, prepared."
ayahs.n: and his companion the angel will say this record is what is with me prepared

ayahs.t: [Allah will say], "Throw into Hell every obstinate disbeliever,
ayahs.n: allah will say throw into hell every obstinate disbeliever

ayahs.t: Preventer of good, aggressor, and doubter,
ayahs.n: preventer of good aggressor and doubter

ayahs.t: Who made [as equal] with Allah another deity; then throw him into the severe punishment."
ayahs.n: who made as equal with allah another deity then throw him into the severe punishment

ayahs.t: His [devil] companion will say, "Our Lord, I did not make him transgress, but he [himself] was in extreme error."
ayahs.n: his devil companion will say our lord i did not make him transgress but he himself was in extreme error

ayahs.t: [Allah] will say, "Do not dispute before Me, while I had already presented to you the warning.
ayahs.n: allah will say do not dispute before me while i had already presented to you the warning

ayahs.t: The word will not be changed with Me, and never will I be unjust to the servants."
ayahs.n: the word will not be changed with me and never will i be unjust to the servants

ayahs.t: On the Day We will say to Hell, "Have you been filled?" and it will say, "Are there some more,"
ayahs.n: on the day we will say to hell have you been filled and it will say are there some more

ayahs.t: And Paradise will be brought near to the righteous, not far,
ayahs.n: and paradise will be brought near to the righteous not far

ayahs.t: [It will be said], "This is what you were promised - for every returner [to Allah] and keeper [of His covenant]
ayahs.n: it will be said this is what you were promised for every returner to allah and keeper of his covenant

ayahs.t: Who feared the Most Merciful unseen and came with a heart returning [in repentance].
ayahs.n: who feared the most merciful unseen and came with a heart returning in repentance

ayahs.t: Enter it in peace. This is the Day of Eternity."
ayahs.n: enter it in peace this is the day of eternity

ayahs.t: They will have whatever they wish therein, and with Us is more.
ayahs.n: they will have whatever they wish therein and with us is more

ayahs.t: And how many a generation before them did We destroy who were greater than them in [striking] power and had explored throughout the lands. Is there any place of escape?
ayahs.n: and how many a generation before them did we destroy who were greater than them in striking power and had explored throughout the lands is there any place of escape

ayahs.t: Indeed in that is a reminder for whoever has a heart or who listens while he is present [in mind].
ayahs.n: indeed in that is a reminder for whoever has a heart or who listens while he is present in mind

ayahs.t: And We did certainly create the heavens and earth and what is between them in six days, and there touched Us no weariness.
ayahs.n: and we did certainly create the heavens and earth and what is between them in six days and there touched us no weariness

ayahs.t: So be patient, [O Muhammad], over what they say and exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting,
ayahs.n: so be patient o muhammad over what they say and exalt allah with praise of your lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting

ayahs.t: And [in part] of the night exalt Him and after prostration.
ayahs.n: and in part of the night exalt him and after prostration

ayahs.t: And listen on the Day when the Caller will call out from a place that is near -
ayahs.n: and listen on the day when the caller will call out from a place that is near

ayahs.t: The Day they will hear the blast [of the Horn] in truth. That is the Day of Emergence [from the graves].
ayahs.n: the day they will hear the blast of the horn in truth that is the day of emergence from the graves

ayahs.t: Indeed, it is We who give life and cause death, and to Us is the destination
ayahs.n: indeed it is we who give life and cause death and to us is the destination

ayahs.t: On the Day the earth breaks away from them [and they emerge] rapidly; that is a gathering easy for Us.
ayahs.n: on the day the earth breaks away from them and they emerge rapidly that is a gathering easy for us

ayahs.t: We are most knowing of what they say, and you are not over them a tyrant. But remind by the Qur'an whoever fears My threat.
ayahs.n: we are most knowing of what they say and you are not over them a tyrant but remind by the quran whoever fears my threat

ayahs.t: By those [winds] scattering [dust] dispersing
ayahs.n: by those winds scattering dust dispersing

ayahs.t: And those [clouds] carrying a load [of water]
ayahs.n: and those clouds carrying a load of water

ayahs.t: And those [ships] sailing with ease
ayahs.n: and those ships sailing with ease

ayahs.t: And those [angels] apportioning [each] matter,
ayahs.n: and those angels apportioning each matter

ayahs.t: Indeed, what you are promised is true.
ayahs.n: indeed what you are promised is true

ayahs.t: And indeed, the recompense is to occur.
ayahs.n: and indeed the recompense is to occur

ayahs.t: By the heaven containing pathways,
ayahs.n: by the heaven containing pathways

ayahs.t: Indeed, you are in differing speech.
ayahs.n: indeed you are in differing speech

ayahs.t: Deluded away from the Qur'an is he who is deluded.
ayahs.n: deluded away from the quran is he who is deluded

ayahs.t: Destroyed are the falsifiers
ayahs.n: destroyed are the falsifiers

ayahs.t: Who are within a flood [of confusion] and heedless.
ayahs.n: who are within a flood of confusion and heedless

ayahs.t: They ask, "When is the Day of Recompense?"
ayahs.n: they ask when is the day of recompense

ayahs.t: [It is] the Day they will be tormented over the Fire
ayahs.n: it is the day they will be tormented over the fire

ayahs.t: [And will be told], "Taste your torment. This is that for which you were impatient."
ayahs.n: and will be told taste your torment this is that for which you were impatient

ayahs.t: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and springs,
ayahs.n: indeed the righteous will be among gardens and springs

ayahs.t: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.
ayahs.n: accepting what their lord has given them indeed they were before that doers of good

ayahs.t: They used to sleep but little of the night,
ayahs.n: they used to sleep but little of the night

ayahs.t: And in the hours before dawn they would ask forgiveness,
ayahs.n: and in the hours before dawn they would ask forgiveness

ayahs.t: And from their properties was [given] the right of the [needy] petitioner and the deprived.
ayahs.n: and from their properties was given the right of the needy petitioner and the deprived

ayahs.t: And on the earth are signs for the certain [in faith]
ayahs.n: and on the earth are signs for the certain in faith

ayahs.t: And in yourselves. Then will you not see?
ayahs.n: and in yourselves then will you not see

ayahs.t: And in the heaven is your provision and whatever you are promised.
ayahs.n: and in the heaven is your provision and whatever you are promised

ayahs.t: Then by the Lord of the heaven and earth, indeed, it is truth - just as [sure as] it is that you are speaking.
ayahs.n: then by the lord of the heaven and earth indeed it is truth just as sure as it is that you are speaking

ayahs.t: Has there reached you the story of the honored guests of Abraham? -
ayahs.n: has there reached you the story of the honored guests of abraham

ayahs.t: When they entered upon him and said, "[We greet you with] peace." He answered, "[And upon you] peace, [you are] a people unknown.
ayahs.n: when they entered upon him and said we greet you with peace he answered and upon you peace you are a people unknown

ayahs.t: Then he went to his family and came with a fat [roasted] calf
ayahs.n: then he went to his family and came with a fat roasted calf

ayahs.t: And placed it near them; he said, "Will you not eat?"
ayahs.n: and placed it near them he said will you not eat

ayahs.t: And he felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not," and gave him good tidings of a learned boy.
ayahs.n: and he felt from them apprehension they said fear not and gave him good tidings of a learned boy

ayahs.t: And his wife approached with a cry [of alarm] and struck her face and said, "[I am] a barren old woman!"
ayahs.n: and his wife approached with a cry of alarm and struck her face and said i am a barren old woman

ayahs.t: They said, "Thus has said your Lord; indeed, He is the Wise, the Knowing."
ayahs.n: they said thus has said your lord indeed he is the wise the knowing

ayahs.t: [Abraham] said, "Then what is your business [here], O messengers?"
ayahs.n: abraham said then what is your business here o messengers

ayahs.t: They said, "Indeed, we have been sent to a people of criminals
ayahs.n: they said indeed we have been sent to a people of criminals

ayahs.t: To send down upon them stones of clay,
ayahs.n: to send down upon them stones of clay

ayahs.t: Marked in the presence of your Lord for the transgressors."
ayahs.n: marked in the presence of your lord for the transgressors

ayahs.t: So We brought out whoever was in the cities of the believers.
ayahs.n: so we brought out whoever was in the cities of the believers

ayahs.t: And We found not within them other than a [single] house of Muslims.
ayahs.n: and we found not within them other than a single house of muslims

ayahs.t: And We left therein a sign for those who fear the painful punishment.
ayahs.n: and we left therein a sign for those who fear the painful punishment

ayahs.t: And in Moses [was a sign], when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority.
ayahs.n: and in moses was a sign when we sent him to pharaoh with clear authority

ayahs.t: But he turned away with his supporters and said," A magician or a madman."
ayahs.n: but he turned away with his supporters and said a magician or a madman

ayahs.t: So We took him and his soldiers and cast them into the sea, and he was blameworthy.
ayahs.n: so we took him and his soldiers and cast them into the sea and he was blameworthy

ayahs.t: And in 'Aad [was a sign], when We sent against them the barren wind.
ayahs.n: and in aad was a sign when we sent against them the barren wind

ayahs.t: It left nothing of what it came upon but that it made it like disintegrated ruins.
ayahs.n: it left nothing of what it came upon but that it made it like disintegrated ruins

ayahs.t: And in Thamud, when it was said to them, "Enjoy yourselves for a time."
ayahs.n: and in thamud when it was said to them enjoy yourselves for a time

ayahs.t: But they were insolent toward the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they were looking on.
ayahs.n: but they were insolent toward the command of their lord so the thunderbolt seized them while they were looking on

ayahs.t: And they were unable to arise, nor could they defend themselves.
ayahs.n: and they were unable to arise nor could they defend themselves

ayahs.t: And [We destroyed] the people of Noah before; indeed, they were a people defiantly disobedient.
ayahs.n: and we destroyed the people of noah before indeed they were a people defiantly disobedient

ayahs.t: And the heaven We constructed with strength, and indeed, We are [its] expander.
ayahs.n: and the heaven we constructed with strength and indeed we are its expander

ayahs.t: And the earth We have spread out, and excellent is the preparer.
ayahs.n: and the earth we have spread out and excellent is the preparer

ayahs.t: And of all things We created two mates; perhaps you will remember.
ayahs.n: and of all things we created two mates perhaps you will remember

ayahs.t: So flee to Allah. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.
ayahs.n: so flee to allah indeed i am to you from him a clear warner

ayahs.t: And do not make [as equal] with Allah another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.
ayahs.n: and do not make as equal with allah another deity indeed i am to you from him a clear warner

ayahs.t: Similarly, there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said, "A magician or a madman."
ayahs.n: similarly there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said a magician or a madman

ayahs.t: Did they suggest it to them? Rather, they [themselves] are a transgressing people.
ayahs.n: did they suggest it to them rather they themselves are a transgressing people

ayahs.t: So leave them, [O Muhammad], for you are not to be blamed.
ayahs.n: so leave them o muhammad for you are not to be blamed

ayahs.t: And remind, for indeed, the reminder benefits the believers.
ayahs.n: and remind for indeed the reminder benefits the believers

ayahs.t: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.
ayahs.n: and i did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship me

ayahs.t: I do not want from them any provision, nor do I want them to feed Me.
ayahs.n: i do not want from them any provision nor do i want them to feed me

ayahs.t: Indeed, it is Allah who is the [continual] Provider, the firm possessor of strength.
ayahs.n: indeed it is allah who is the continual provider the firm possessor of strength

ayahs.t: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a portion [of punishment] like the portion of their predecessors, so let them not impatiently urge Me.
ayahs.n: and indeed for those who have wronged is a portion of punishment like the portion of their predecessors so let them not impatiently urge me

ayahs.t: And woe to those who have disbelieved from their Day which they are promised.
ayahs.n: and woe to those who have disbelieved from their day which they are promised

ayahs.t: By the mount
ayahs.n: by the mount

ayahs.t: And [by] a Book inscribed
ayahs.n: and by a book inscribed

ayahs.t: In parchment spread open
ayahs.n: in parchment spread open

ayahs.t: And [by] the frequented House
ayahs.n: and by the frequented house

ayahs.t: And [by] the heaven raised high
ayahs.n: and by the heaven raised high

ayahs.t: And [by] the sea filled [with fire],
ayahs.n: and by the sea filled with fire

ayahs.t: Indeed, the punishment of your Lord will occur.
ayahs.n: indeed the punishment of your lord will occur

ayahs.t: Of it there is no preventer.
ayahs.n: of it there is no preventer

ayahs.t: On the Day the heaven will sway with circular motion
ayahs.n: on the day the heaven will sway with circular motion

ayahs.t: And the mountains will pass on, departing -
ayahs.n: and the mountains will pass on departing

ayahs.t: Then woe, that Day, to the deniers,
ayahs.n: then woe that day to the deniers

ayahs.t: Who are in [empty] discourse amusing themselves.
ayahs.n: who are in empty discourse amusing themselves

ayahs.t: The Day they are thrust toward the fire of Hell with a [violent] thrust, [its angels will say],
ayahs.n: the day they are thrust toward the fire of hell with a violent thrust its angels will say

ayahs.t: "This is the Fire which you used to deny.
ayahs.n: this is the fire which you used to deny

ayahs.t: Then is this magic, or do you not see?
ayahs.n: then is this magic or do you not see

ayahs.t: [Enter to] burn therein; then be patient or impatient - it is all the same for you. You are only being recompensed [for] what you used to do."
ayahs.n: enter to burn therein then be patient or impatient it is all the same for you you are only being recompensed for what you used to do

ayahs.t: Indeed, the righteous will be in gardens and pleasure,
ayahs.n: indeed the righteous will be in gardens and pleasure

ayahs.t: Enjoying what their Lord has given them, and their Lord protected them from the punishment of Hellfire.
ayahs.n: enjoying what their lord has given them and their lord protected them from the punishment of hellfire

ayahs.t: [They will be told], "Eat and drink in satisfaction for what you used to do."
ayahs.n: they will be told eat and drink in satisfaction for what you used to do

ayahs.t: They will be reclining on thrones lined up, and We will marry them to fair women with large, [beautiful] eyes.
ayahs.n: they will be reclining on thrones lined up and we will marry them to fair women with large beautiful eyes

ayahs.t: And those who believed and whose descendants followed them in faith - We will join with them their descendants, and We will not deprive them of anything of their deeds. Every person, for what he earned, is retained.
ayahs.n: and those who believed and whose descendants followed them in faith we will join with them their descendants and we will not deprive them of anything of their deeds every person for what he earned is retained

ayahs.t: And We will provide them with fruit and meat from whatever they desire.
ayahs.n: and we will provide them with fruit and meat from whatever they desire

ayahs.t: They will exchange with one another a cup [of wine] wherein [results] no ill speech or commission of sin.
ayahs.n: they will exchange with one another a cup of wine wherein results no ill speech or commission of sin

ayahs.t: There will circulate among them [servant] boys [especially] for them, as if they were pearls well-protected.
ayahs.n: there will circulate among them servant boys especially for them as if they were pearls well protected

ayahs.t: And they will approach one another, inquiring of each other.
ayahs.n: and they will approach one another inquiring of each other

ayahs.t: They will say, "Indeed, we were previously among our people fearful [of displeasing Allah].
ayahs.n: they will say indeed we were previously among our people fearful of displeasing allah

ayahs.t: So Allah conferred favor upon us and protected us from the punishment of the Scorching Fire.
ayahs.n: so allah conferred favor upon us and protected us from the punishment of the scorching fire

ayahs.t: Indeed, we used to supplicate Him before. Indeed, it is He who is the Beneficent, the Merciful."
ayahs.n: indeed we used to supplicate him before indeed it is he who is the beneficent the merciful

ayahs.t: So remind [O Muhammad], for you are not, by the favor of your Lord, a soothsayer or a madman.
ayahs.n: so remind o muhammad for you are not by the favor of your lord a soothsayer or a madman

ayahs.t: Or do they say [of you], "A poet for whom we await a misfortune of time?"
ayahs.n: or do they say of you a poet for whom we await a misfortune of time

ayahs.t: Say, "Wait, for indeed I am, with you, among the waiters."
ayahs.n: say wait for indeed i am with you among the waiters

ayahs.t: Or do their minds command them to [say] this, or are they a transgressing people?
ayahs.n: or do their minds command them to say this or are they a transgressing people

ayahs.t: Or do they say, "He has made it up"? Rather, they do not believe.
ayahs.n: or do they say he has made it up rather they do not believe

ayahs.t: Then let them produce a statement like it, if they should be truthful.
ayahs.n: then let them produce a statement like it if they should be truthful

ayahs.t: Or were they created by nothing, or were they the creators [of themselves]?
ayahs.n: or were they created by nothing or were they the creators of themselves

ayahs.t: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? Rather, they are not certain.
ayahs.n: or did they create the heavens and the earth rather they are not certain

ayahs.t: Or have they the depositories [containing the provision] of your Lord? Or are they the controllers [of them]?
ayahs.n: or have they the depositories containing the provision of your lord or are they the controllers of them

ayahs.t: Or have they a stairway [into the heaven] upon which they listen? Then let their listener produce a clear authority.
ayahs.n: or have they a stairway into the heaven upon which they listen then let their listener produce a clear authority

ayahs.t: Or has He daughters while you have sons?
ayahs.n: or has he daughters while you have sons

ayahs.t: Or do you, [O Muhammad], ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?
ayahs.n: or do you o muhammad ask of them a payment so they are by debt burdened down

ayahs.t: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down?
ayahs.n: or have they knowledge of the unseen so they write it down

ayahs.t: Or do they intend a plan? But those who disbelieve - they are the object of a plan.
ayahs.n: or do they intend a plan but those who disbelieve they are the object of a plan

ayahs.t: Or have they a deity other than Allah? Exalted is Allah above whatever they associate with Him.
ayahs.n: or have they a deity other than allah exalted is allah above whatever they associate with him

ayahs.t: And if they were to see a fragment from the sky falling, they would say, "[It is merely] clouds heaped up."
ayahs.n: and if they were to see a fragment from the sky falling they would say it is merely clouds heaped up

ayahs.t: So leave them until they meet their Day in which they will be struck insensible -
ayahs.n: so leave them until they meet their day in which they will be struck insensible

ayahs.t: The Day their plan will not avail them at all, nor will they be helped.
ayahs.n: the day their plan will not avail them at all nor will they be helped

ayahs.t: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a punishment before that, but most of them do not know.
ayahs.n: and indeed for those who have wronged is a punishment before that but most of them do not know

ayahs.t: And be patient, [O Muhammad], for the decision of your Lord, for indeed, you are in Our eyes. And exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord when you arise.
ayahs.n: and be patient o muhammad for the decision of your lord for indeed you are in our eyes and exalt allah with praise of your lord when you arise

ayahs.t: And in a part of the night exalt Him and after [the setting of] the stars.
ayahs.n: and in a part of the night exalt him and after the setting of the stars

ayahs.t: By the star when it descends,
ayahs.n: by the star when it descends

ayahs.t: Your companion [Muhammad] has not strayed, nor has he erred,
ayahs.n: your companion muhammad has not strayed nor has he erred

ayahs.t: Nor does he speak from [his own] inclination.
ayahs.n: nor does he speak from his own inclination

ayahs.t: It is not but a revelation revealed,
ayahs.n: it is not but a revelation revealed

ayahs.t: Taught to him by one intense in strength -
ayahs.n: taught to him by one intense in strength

ayahs.t: One of soundness. And he rose to [his] true form
ayahs.n: one of soundness and he rose to his true form

ayahs.t: While he was in the higher [part of the] horizon.
ayahs.n: while he was in the higher part of the horizon

ayahs.t: Then he approached and descended
ayahs.n: then he approached and descended

ayahs.t: And was at a distance of two bow lengths or nearer.
ayahs.n: and was at a distance of two bow lengths or nearer

ayahs.t: And he revealed to His Servant what he revealed.
ayahs.n: and he revealed to his servant what he revealed

ayahs.t: The heart did not lie [about] what it saw.
ayahs.n: the heart did not lie about what it saw

ayahs.t: So will you dispute with him over what he saw?
ayahs.n: so will you dispute with him over what he saw

ayahs.t: And he certainly saw him in another descent
ayahs.n: and he certainly saw him in another descent

ayahs.t: At the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary -
ayahs.n: at the lote tree of the utmost boundary

ayahs.t: Near it is the Garden of Refuge -
ayahs.n: near it is the garden of refuge

ayahs.t: When there covered the Lote Tree that which covered [it].
ayahs.n: when there covered the lote tree that which covered it

ayahs.t: The sight [of the Prophet] did not swerve, nor did it transgress [its limit].
ayahs.n: the sight of the prophet did not swerve nor did it transgress its limit

ayahs.t: He certainly saw of the greatest signs of his Lord.
ayahs.n: he certainly saw of the greatest signs of his lord

ayahs.t: So have you considered al-Lat and al-'Uzza?
ayahs.n: so have you considered al lat and al uzza

ayahs.t: And Manat, the third - the other one?
ayahs.n: and manat the third the other one

ayahs.t: Is the male for you and for Him the female?
ayahs.n: is the male for you and for him the female

ayahs.t: That, then, is an unjust division.
ayahs.n: that then is an unjust division

ayahs.t: They are not but [mere] names you have named them - you and your forefathers - for which Allah has sent down no authority. They follow not except assumption and what [their] souls desire, and there has already come to them from their Lord guidance.
ayahs.n: they are not but mere names you have named them you and your forefathers for which allah has sent down no authority they follow not except assumption and what their souls desire and there has already come to them from their lord guidance

ayahs.t: Or is there for man whatever he wishes?
ayahs.n: or is there for man whatever he wishes

ayahs.t: Rather, to Allah belongs the Hereafter and the first [life].
ayahs.n: rather to allah belongs the hereafter and the first life

ayahs.t: And how many angels there are in the heavens whose intercession will not avail at all except [only] after Allah has permitted [it] to whom He wills and approves.
ayahs.n: and how many angels there are in the heavens whose intercession will not avail at all except only after allah has permitted it to whom he wills and approves

ayahs.t: Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter name the angels female names,
ayahs.n: indeed those who do not believe in the hereafter name the angels female names

ayahs.t: And they have thereof no knowledge. They follow not except assumption, and indeed, assumption avails not against the truth at all.
ayahs.n: and they have thereof no knowledge they follow not except assumption and indeed assumption avails not against the truth at all

ayahs.t: So turn away from whoever turns his back on Our message and desires not except the worldly life.
ayahs.n: so turn away from whoever turns his back on our message and desires not except the worldly life

ayahs.t: That is their sum of knowledge. Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who strays from His way, and He is most knowing of who is guided.
ayahs.n: that is their sum of knowledge indeed your lord is most knowing of who strays from his way and he is most knowing of who is guided

ayahs.t: And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth - that He may recompense those who do evil with [the penalty of] what they have done and recompense those who do good with the best [reward] -
ayahs.n: and to allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth that he may recompense those who do evil with the penalty of what they have done and recompense those who do good with the best reward

ayahs.t: Those who avoid the major sins and immoralities, only [committing] slight ones. Indeed, your Lord is vast in forgiveness. He was most knowing of you when He produced you from the earth and when you were fetuses in the wombs of your mothers. So do not claim yourselves to be pure; He is most knowing of who fears Him.
ayahs.n: those who avoid the major sins and immoralities only committing slight ones indeed your lord is vast in forgiveness he was most knowing of you when he produced you from the earth and when you were fetuses in the wombs of your mothers so do not claim yourselves to be pure he is most knowing of who fears him

ayahs.t: Have you seen the one who turned away
ayahs.n: have you seen the one who turned away

ayahs.t: And gave a little and [then] refrained?
ayahs.n: and gave a little and then refrained

ayahs.t: Does he have knowledge of the unseen, so he sees?
ayahs.n: does he have knowledge of the unseen so he sees

ayahs.t: Or has he not been informed of what was in the scriptures of Moses
ayahs.n: or has he not been informed of what was in the scriptures of moses

ayahs.t: And [of] Abraham, who fulfilled [his obligations] -
ayahs.n: and of abraham who fulfilled his obligations

ayahs.t: That no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another
ayahs.n: that no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another

ayahs.t: And that there is not for man except that [good] for which he strives
ayahs.n: and that there is not for man except that good for which he strives

ayahs.t: And that his effort is going to be seen -
ayahs.n: and that his effort is going to be seen

ayahs.t: Then he will be recompensed for it with the fullest recompense
ayahs.n: then he will be recompensed for it with the fullest recompense

ayahs.t: And that to your Lord is the finality
ayahs.n: and that to your lord is the finality

ayahs.t: And that it is He who makes [one] laugh and weep
ayahs.n: and that it is he who makes one laugh and weep

ayahs.t: And that it is He who causes death and gives life
ayahs.n: and that it is he who causes death and gives life
